entries.farsi: گفت آرى ليك كو دور يزيد / كى بده‏ست اين غم چه دير اينجا رسيد
entries.english: “Yes,” said he; “but where is the epoch of Yazíd? When did this grievous tragedy occur? How late has it arrived here!

entries.farsi: چشم كوران آن خسارت را بديد / گوش كران آن حكايت را شنيد
entries.english: The eyes of the blind have seen that loss; the ears of the deaf have heard that story.

entries.farsi: خفته بوده‏ستيد تا اكنون شما / كه كنون جامه دريديت از عزا
entries.english: Have you been asleep till now, that now you have rent your garments in mourning?

entries.farsi: پس عزا بر خود كنيد اى خفتگان / ‏ز انكه بد مرگى است اين خواب گران
entries.english: Then, O sleepers mourn for yourselves, for this heavy slumber is an evil death.

entries.farsi: روح سلطانى ز زندانى بجست / ‏جامه چه دْرانيم و چون خاييم دست
entries.english: A royal spirit escaped from a prison: why should we rend our garments and how should we gnaw our hands?

entries.farsi: چون كه ايشان خسرو دين بوده‏اند / وقت شادى شد چو بشكستند بند
entries.english: Since they were monarchs of the religion, it was the hour of joy when they broke their bonds.

entries.farsi: سوى شادُروان دولت تاختند / كنده و زنجير را انداختند
entries.english: They sped towards the pavilion of empire; they cast off their fetters and chains.

entries.farsi: روز ملك است و گش و شاهنشهى / ‏گر تو يك ذره از ايشان آگهى
entries.english: It is the day of kingship and pride and sovereignty, if you have an atom of knowledge of them.

entries.farsi: ور نه‏اى آگه برو بر خود گرى / ‏ز انكه در انكار نقل و محشرى
entries.english: And if you have not knowledge, go, weep for yourself, for you are disbelieving in the removal and in the assembly at the Last Judgement.

entries.farsi: بر دل و دين خرابت نوحه كن / ‏كه نمى‏بيند جز اين خاك كهن
entries.english: Mourn for your corrupt heart and religion, for it sees nothing but this old earth.

entries.farsi: ور همى‏بيند چرا نبود دليل / پشت دار و جان سپار و چشم سير
entries.english: Or if it is seeing, why is it not brave and supporting and self-sacrificing and fully contented?

entries.farsi: در رخت كو از مى دين فرخى / ‏گر بديدى بحر كو كف سخى
entries.english: In your countenance where is the happiness of the wine of religion? If you have beheld the Ocean, where is the bounteous hand?

entries.farsi: آن كه جو ديد آب را نكند دريغ / ‏خاصه آن كاو ديد آن دريا و ميغ
entries.english: He that has beheld the River does not grudge water, especially he that has beheld that Sea and Clouds.”

sections.title_fa: تمثيل مرد حريص نابيننده رزاقى حق را و خزاين رحمت او را به مورى كه در خرمنگاه بزرگ با دانه‏ى گندم مى‏كوشد و مى‏جوشد و مى‏لرزد و به تعجيل مى‏كشد و سعت آن خرمن را نمى‏بيند
sections.title_en: Comparison of the covetous man, who does not see the all-providingness of God and the stores of His mercy, to an ant struggling with a single grain of wheat on a great threshing-floor and showing violent agitation and trembling and dragging it hurriedly along,
unconscious of the amplitude of the threshing-floor.

entries.farsi: مور بر دانه بدان لرزان شود / كه ز خرمنهاى خوش اعمى بود
entries.english: The ant trembles for a grain because it is blind to the goodly threshing-floors.

entries.farsi: مى‏كشد آن دانه را با حرص و بيم / ‏كه نمى‏بيند چنان چاش كريم
entries.english: It drags a grain along greedily and fearfully, for it does not see such a noble stack of winnowed wheat.

entries.farsi: صاحب خرمن همى‏گويد كه هى / ‏اى ز كورى پيش تو معدوم شى
entries.english: The Owner of the threshing-floor is saying, “Hey, you who in your blindness deem nothing something,

entries.farsi: تو ز خرمنهاى ما آن ديده‏اى / ‏كه در آن دانه به جان پيچيده‏اى
entries.english: Have you regarded that as belonging to My threshing-floors, so that you are devoted with your soul to that grain?”

entries.farsi: آدمى ديده‏ست باقى گوشت و پوست / ‏هر چه چشمش ديده است آن چيز اوست
entries.english: O you, who in semblance are a mote, look at Saturn; you are a lame ant: go, look at Solomon.

entries.farsi: اى به صورت ذره كيوان را ببين / ‏مور لنگى رو سليمان را ببين
entries.english: You are not this body: you are that Eye. If you have beheld the spirit, you are delivered from the body.

entries.farsi: تو نه‏اى اين جسم تو آن ديده‏اى / ‏وارهى از جسم گر جان ديده‏اى
entries.english: Man is eye: the rest is flesh and skin: whatsoever his eye has beheld, he is that thing.

entries.farsi: كوه را غرقه كند يك خم ز نم / ‏چشم خم چون باز باشد سوى يم
entries.english: A jar will submerge a mountain with water when the eye of the jar is open to the Sea.

entries.farsi: چون به دريا راه شد از جان خم / ‏خم با جيحون بر آورد اشتلم
entries.english: When from the soul of the jar a channel is made to the Sea, the jar will overwhelm the Oxus.

entries.farsi: ز آن سبب قُلْ گفته‏ى دريا بود / هر چه نطق احمدى گويا بود
entries.english: For that reason whatever the speech of Ahmad may utter, the words are uttered by the Sea.

entries.farsi: گفته‏ى او جمله در بحر بوذ / كه دلش را بود در دريا نفوذ
entries.english: All his words were pearls of the Sea, for his heart had a passage into the Sea.

entries.farsi: داد دريا چون ز خم ما بود / چه عجب گر ماهيى دريا بود
entries.english: Since the bounty of the Sea is through our jar, what wonder the Sea should be in a Fish?

entries.farsi: چشم حس افسرد بر نقش ممر / تش ممر مى‏بينى و او مستقر
entries.english: The sensual eye is fixed on the form of the thoroughfare: you are regarding it as a thoroughfare, but he as a permanent abode.

entries.farsi: اين دويى اوصاف ديد احول است / ‏ور نه اول آخر آخر اول است
entries.english: This dualism is characteristic of the eye that sees double; but the first is the last and the last is the first.

entries.farsi: هى ز چه معلوم گردد اين ز بعث / ‏بعث را جو كم كن اندر بعث بحث
entries.english: Listen, by what means is this made known? By means of the resurrection. Seek to experience resurrection: do not dispute concerning resurrection.

entries.farsi: شرط روز بعث اول مردن است / ‏ز انكه بعث از مرده زنده كردن است
entries.english: The condition of the Day of Resurrection is to die first, for ba‘th (resurrection) signifies “to raise to life from the dead.”

entries.farsi: از كجا جوييم علم از ترك علم / ‏از كجا جوييم سلم از ترك سلم
entries.english: Hence the entire world has taken the wrong way, for they are afraid of nonexistence, though it is the refuge.

entries.farsi: جمله عالم زين غلط كردند راه / ‏كز عدم ترسند و آن آمد پناه
entries.english: Where shall we seek true knowledge? In renouncing knowledge. Where shall we seek peace? From renouncing peace.

entries.farsi: از كجا جوييم هست از ترك هست / ‏از كجا جوييم سيب از ترك دست
entries.english: Where shall we seek existence? From renouncing existence. Where shall we seek the apple? From renouncing the hand.

entries.farsi: هم تو تانى كرد يا نعم المعين / ‏ديده‏ى معدوم بين را هست بين
entries.english: O best Helper, only You can make the eye that regards the non-existent to regard that which is existent.

entries.farsi: ديده‏اى كاو از عدم آمد پديد / ذات هستى را همه معدوم ديد
entries.english: The eye that was produced from non-existence regarded the Essence of Being as wholly non-existent;

entries.farsi: اين جهان منتظم محشر شود / گر دو ديده مبدل و انور شود
entries.english: If two eyes are transformed and illumined, this well-ordered world becomes the scene of the Last Judgement.

entries.farsi: ز آن نمايد اين حقايق ناتمام / ‏كه بر اين خامان بود فهمش حرام
entries.english: These realities are shown forth imperfectly because the apprehension of them is forbidden to these raw ones.

entries.farsi: نعمت جنات خوش بر دوزخى / ‏شد محرم گر چه حق آمد سخى
entries.english: Although God is munificent, the enjoyment of the delightful gardens of Paradise is forbidden to him who is destined for Hell.

entries.farsi: در دهانش تلخ آيد شهد خلد / چون نبود از وافيان در عهد خلد
entries.english: The honey of Paradise becomes bitter in his mouth, since he was not one of them that faithfully keep the covenant of everlasting life.

entries.farsi: مر شما را نيز در سوداگرى / ‏دست كى جنبد چو نبود مشترى
entries.english: You also who are engaged in commerce— how should your hands move when there is no buyer?

entries.farsi: كى نظاره اهل بخريدن بود / آن نظاره گول گرديدن بود
entries.english: How should looking-on be capable of buying? The fool’s looking-on is loitering.

entries.farsi: از ملولى كاله مى‏خواهد ز تو / نيست آن كس مشترى و كاله جو
entries.english: Continually asking, “How much is this?” and “How much is that?” for the sake of pastime and mockery.

entries.farsi: پرس پرسان كاين به چند و آن به چند / از پى تعبير وقت و ريش‏خند
entries.english: From boredom he asks you your goods: that person is not a buyer and customer.

entries.farsi: كاله را صد بار ديد و باز داد / جامه كى پيمود او پيمود باد
entries.english: He inspects the article a hundred times and hands it back: when did he measure a piece of cloth? He measured wind.

entries.farsi: كو قدوم و كر و فر مشترى / ‏كو مزاح گنگلى سرسرى
entries.english: What a distance between the approach and bargaining of a purchaser and the pleasantries of a silly joker?

entries.farsi: چون كه در ملكش نباشد حبه‏اى / ‏جز پى گنگل چه جويد جبه‏اى
entries.english: Since there is not a mite in his possession, how should he seek a coat except in jest?

entries.farsi: در تجارت نيستش سرمايه‏اى / ‏پس چه شخص زشت او چه سايه‏اى
entries.english: He has no capital for trading: what, then, is the difference between his ill-favoured person and a shadow?

entries.farsi: مايه در بازار اين دنيا زر است / ‏مايه آن جا عشق و دو چشم تر است
entries.english: The capital for the market of this world is gold; there the capital is love and two eyes wet.

entries.farsi: هر كه او بى‏مايه‏ى بازار رفت / ‏عمر رفت و باز گشت او خام تفت
entries.english: Whoever went to market without any capital, his life passed and he speedily returned in disappointment.

entries.farsi: هى كجا بودى برادر هيچ جا / هى چه پختى بهر خوردن هيچ با
entries.english: “Oh, where have you been, brother?” “Nowhere.” “Oh, what have you cooked to eat?” “No soup.”

entries.farsi: مشترى شو تا بجنبد دست من / ‏لعل زايد معدن آبست من
entries.english: Become a buyer, that my hand may move, and that my pregnant mine may bring forth the ruby.

entries.farsi: مشترى گر چه كه سست و بارد است / ‏دعوت دين كن كه دعوت وارد است
entries.english: Though the buyer is slack and lukewarm, call to the religion, for the call has come down.

entries.farsi: باز پران كن حمام روح گير / در ره دعوت طريق نوح گير
entries.english: Let the falcon fly and catch the spiritual dove: in calling take the way of Noah.

entries.farsi: خدمتى مى‏كن براى كردگار / با قبول و رد خلقانت چه كار
entries.english: Perform an act of service for the Creator’s sake: what have you to do with being accepted or rejected by the people?

sections.title_fa: داستان آن شخص كه بر در سرايى نيم شب سحورى مى‏زد همسايه او را گفت كه آخر نيم شب است سحر نيست و ديگر آن كه در اين سراى كسى نيست بهر كى مى‏زنى، و جواب گفتن مطرب او را
sections.title_en: Story of the person who was giving the drum-call for the sahúr at the gate of a certain palace
at midnight. A neighbour said to him, “Why, it is midnight, it is not dawn; and besides, there is no one in this palace: for whose sake are you drumming?”— and the minstrel’s reply to him.

entries.farsi: آن يكى مى‏زد سحورى بر درى / ‏درگهى بود و رواق مهترى
entries.english: A certain man was drumming at a certain gate to announce the sahúr: it was a court-house and the pavilion of a grandee.

entries.farsi: اوال وقت سحر زن اين سحور / نيم شب نبود گه اين شرو شور
entries.english: He was beating his drum vigorously at midnight, someone said to him, “O you who are seeking support,

entries.farsi: ديگر آن كه فهم كن اى بو الهوس / ‏كه در اين خانه درون خود هست كس
entries.english: Firstly, give this call to the sahúr at daybreak: midnight is not the time for this disturbance;

entries.farsi: نيم شب مى‏زد سحورى را به جد / گفت او را قائلى كاى مستمد
entries.english: And secondly, observe, O man of vain desire, whether in fact there is any one inside this house at midnight.

entries.farsi: كس در اينجا نيست جز ديو و پرى / ‏روزگار خود چه ياوه مى‏برى
entries.english: There is nobody here except demons and spirits: why are you trifling your time away?

entries.farsi: بهر گوشى مى‏زنى دف گوش كو / هوش بايد تا بداند هوش كو
entries.english: You are beating your tambourine for the sake of an ear: where is the ear? Intelligence is needed in order to know: where is the intelligence?”

entries.farsi: گفت گفتى بشنو از چاكر جواب / ‏تا نمانى در تحير و اضطراب
entries.english: He replied, “You have said: hear the answer from your servant that you may not remain in bewilderment and confusion.

entries.farsi: گر چه هست اين دم بر تو نيم شب / ‏نزد من نزديك شد صبح طرب
entries.english: Although in your opinion this moment is midnight, in my view the dawn of delight is near at hand.

entries.farsi: هر شكستى پيش من پيروز شد / جمله شبها پيش چشمم روز شد
entries.english: In my sight every defeat has been turned to victory, in my eyes all nights have been turned to day.

entries.farsi: پيش تو خون است آب رود نيل / ‏نزد من خون نيست آب است اى نبيل
entries.english: To you the water of the river Nile seems blood; to me it is not blood, it is water, O noble one.

entries.farsi: در حق تو آهن است آن و رخام / ‏پيش داود نبى موم است و رام
entries.english: In regard to you, that is iron or marble, to the prophet David it is wax and tractable.

entries.farsi: پيش تو كه بس گران است و جماد / مطرب است او پيش داود اوستاد
entries.english: To you the mountain is exceedingly heavy and inanimate, to David it is a master-musician.

entries.farsi: پيش تو آن سنگ ريزه ساكت است / ‏پيش احمد او فصيح و قانت است
entries.english: To you the gravel is silent; to Ahmad it is eloquent and making supplication.

entries.farsi: پيش تو استون مسجد مرده‏اى است / ‏پيش احمد عاشقى دل برده‏اى است
entries.english: To you the pillar of the mosque is a dead thing; to Ahmad it is a lover who has lost his heart.

entries.farsi: آن چه گفتى كاندر اين خانه و سرا / نيست كس چون مى‏زنى اين طبل را
entries.english: To the ordinary all the particles of the world seem dead, but before God they are possessed of knowledge and submissive.

entries.farsi: جمله اجزاى جهان پيش عوام / ‏مرده و پيش خدا دانا و رام
entries.english: As for your saying, ‘there is nobody in this house and palace: why are you beating this drum?’—

entries.farsi: بهر حق اين خلق زرها مى‏دهند / صد اساس خير و مسجد مى‏نهند
entries.english: This people are giving sums of gold for God’s sake, founding hundreds of pious institutions and mosques,

entries.farsi: مال و تن در راه حج دور دست / ‏خوش همى‏بازند چون عشاق مست
entries.english: And, like intoxicated lovers, gladly risking their property and lives on their way to the distant Pilgrimage:

entries.farsi: هيچ مى‏گويند كان خانه تهى است / ‏بلكه صاحب خانه جان مختبى است
entries.english: Do they ever say, ‘The House is empty’? Nay, the Lord of the House is the Spirit invisible.

entries.farsi: پر همى‏بيند سراى دوست را / آن كه از نور اله استش ضيا
entries.english: He that is illumined by the Light of God deems the House of the Beloved to be full.

entries.farsi: بس سراى پر ز جمع و انبهى / ‏پيش چشم عاقبت بينان تهى
entries.english: In the eyes of those who see the end, many a palace filled with a crowd and throng is empty.

entries.farsi: هر كه را خواهى تو در كعبه بجو / تا برويد در زمان او پيش رو
entries.english: Seek in the Ka‘ba whomever you please, that he may at once grow before your face.

entries.farsi: صورتى كاو فاخر و عالى بود / او ز بيت اهلل كى خالى بود
entries.english: How should the form, which is splendid and sublime, be absent from the House of God?

entries.farsi: او بود حاضر منزه از رتاج / ‏باقى مردم براى احتياج
entries.english: He is present, exempt from exclusion, the rest of mankind on account of need.

entries.farsi: هيچ مى‏گويند كاين لبيكها / بى‏ندايى مى‏كنيم آخر چرا
entries.english: Do they ever say, ‘We are crying Labbayka without any response. Pray, why?

entries.farsi: بلكه توفيقى كه لبيك آورد / هست هر لحظه ندايى از احد
entries.english: Nay, the Divine blessing which causes Labbayka is a response from the One at every moment.

entries.farsi: من به بو دانم كه اين قصر و سرا / بزم جان افتاد و خاكش كيميا
entries.english: I know by intuition that this pavilion and palace is the banquet of the soul, and that its dust is an elixir.

entries.farsi: مس خود را بر طريق زير و بم / ‏تا ابد بر كيميايش مى‏زنم
entries.english: I will strike my copper on its elixir unto everlasting in the mode of treble and bass,

entries.farsi: تا بجوشد زين چنين ضرب سحور / در در افشانى و بخشايش بحور
entries.english: That, from playing the sahúr tune in this fashion, the seas may surge to scatter pearls and bounty.

entries.farsi: خلق در صف قتال و كارزار / جان همى‏بازند بهر كردگار
entries.english: Men hazard their lives in the line of battle and in fighting for the Creator’s sake.

entries.farsi: آن يكى اندر بال ايوب‏وار / و آن دگر در صابرى يعقوب‏وار
entries.english: One is like Job in tribulation; another like Jacob in patience.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران خلق تشنه و مستمند / بهر حق از طمع جهدى مى‏كنند
entries.english: Hundreds of thousands of people, thirsty and sorrowful, are doing some sore toil for God’s sake in desire.

entries.farsi: من هم از بهر خداوند غفور / مى‏زنم بر در به اوميدش سحور
entries.english: I too, for the merciful Lord’s sake and in hope of Him, am drumming the sahúr call at the gate.”

entries.farsi: مشترى خواهى كه از وى زر برى / ‏به ز حق كى باشد اى دل مشترى
entries.english: you want a customer from whom you will get gold, how should there be a better customer than God, O heart?

entries.farsi: مى‏خرد از مالت انبانى نجس / ‏مى‏دهد نور ضميرى مقتبس
entries.english: He buys a dirty bag from your goods, and gives an inner light that borrows.

entries.farsi: مى‏ستاند اين يخ جسم فنا / مى‏دهد ملكى برون از وهم ما
entries.english: He receives the ice of this mortal body, and gives a kingdom beyond our imagination.

entries.farsi: مى‏ستاند قطره‏ى چندى ز اشك / ‏مى‏دهد كوثر كه آرد قند رشك
entries.english: He receives a few tear-drops, and gives a Kawthar that sugar shows jealousy.

entries.farsi: مى‏ستاند آه پر سودا و دود / مى‏دهد هر آه را صد جاه سود
entries.english: He receives sighs full of melancholy and vaporous gloom, and gives for every sigh a hundred gainful dignities.

entries.farsi: باد آهى كابر اشك چشم راند / مر خليلى را بدان اواه خواند
entries.english: Because of the wind of sighs that drove onward the tearful cloud, He has called a Abraham awwáh (full of sighs).

entries.farsi: هين در اين بازار گرم بى‏نظير / كهنه‏ها بفروش و ملك نقد گير
entries.english: Listen, sell your old rags in this brisk incomparable market, and receive the sterling kingdom.

entries.farsi: ور ترا شكى و ريبى ره زند / تاجران انبيا را كن سند
entries.english: And if any doubt and suspicion waylay you, rely upon the traders, the prophets.

entries.farsi: بس كه افزود آن شهنشه بختشان / ‏كوه نتواند كشيدن رختشان
entries.english: Inasmuch as the Emperor increased their fortune exceedingly, no mountain can carry their merchandise.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى احد احد گفتن بالل در حر حجاز از محبت مصطفى عليه السلام در آن چاشتگاهها كه خواجه‏اش از تعصب جهود به شاخه‏ى خارش مى‏زد پيش آفتاب حجاز، و از زخم خون از تن بالل بر مى‏جوشيد از او احد احد مى‏جست بى‏قصد او چنان كه از دردمندان ديگر ناله جهد بى‏قصد، زيرا كه از درد عشق ممتلى بود اهتمام دفع درد خار را مدخل نبود، همچون سحره‏ى فرعون و جرجيس و
‏غيرهم ال يعد و ال يحصى
sections.title_en: The Story of Bilal’s crying “One! One!” in the heat of the Hejaz, from his love for Mustafá, on whom be peace, in the forenoons when his master, by Jewish fanaticism, used to flog him

with a thorny branch under the sun of the Hijaz; and how at blow the blood spurted from Bilal’s body, and “One! One!” escaped involuntarily, just as sobs escape involuntarily from others stricken with grief, because he was full of the passion of love there was no room for any care about relieving the pain of the thorns to enter. Like Pharaoh’s magicians and Jirjís
and others innumerable and beyond computation.

entries.farsi: تن فداى خار مى‏كرد آن بالل / ‏خواجه‏اش مى‏زد براى گوشمال
entries.english: That Bilal was devoting his body to the thorns: his master was flogging him by way of correction,

entries.farsi: كه چرا تو ياد احمد مى‏كنى / ‏بنده‏ى بد منكر دين منى
entries.english: Saying, “Why do you celebrate Ahmad? Wicked slave, you disbelieve in my religion!”

entries.farsi: مى‏زد اندر آفتابش او به خار / او احد مى‏گفت بهر افتخار
entries.english: He was beating him in the sun with thorns he cried boasting “One!”

entries.farsi: تا كه صديق آن طرف بر مى‏گذشت / ‏آن احد گفتن به گوش او برفت
entries.english: Till those cries of “One!” reached the ears of the Siddíq, who was passing in that neighbourhood.

entries.farsi: چشم او پر آب شد دل پر عنا / ز آن احد مى‏يافت بوى آشنا
entries.english: His eyes became filled with tears and his heart with trouble, from that “One!” he caught the scent of a loving friend.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن خلوت بديدش پند داد / كز جهودان خفيه مى‏دار اعتقاد
entries.english: Afterwards he saw him in private and admonished him, saying, “Keep your belief hidden from the Jews.

entries.farsi: عالم السر است پنهان دار كام / ‏گفت كردم توبه پيشت اى همام
entries.english: He knows secrets: conceal your desire.” He said, “I repent before you, O prince.”

entries.farsi: روز ديگر از پگه صديق تفت / ‏آن طرف از بهر كارى مى‏برفت
entries.english: Early next day, the Siddíq was going quickly in that district on account of some affair,

entries.farsi: باز احد بشنيد و ضرب زخم خار / بر فروزيد از دلش سوز و شرار
entries.english: He again heard “One!” and blows inflicted by the thorns: flames and sparks of fire were kindled in his heart.

entries.farsi: باز پندش داد باز او توبه كرد / عشق آمد توبه‏ى او را بخورد
entries.english: He admonished him once more, and once more he repented; Love came and consumed his repentance.

entries.farsi: توبه كردن زين نمط بسيار شد / عاقبت از توبه او بيزار شد
entries.english: There was much repenting of this sort, at last he became quit of repentance,

entries.farsi: فاش كرد اسپرد تن را در بال / كاى محمد اى عدوى توبه‏ها
entries.english: And proclaimed and yielded up his body to tribulation, crying, “O Mohammed, O enemy of vows of repentance,

entries.farsi: اى تن من وى رگ من پر ز تو / توبه را گنجا كجا باشد در او
entries.english: O you with whom my body and my veins are filled— how should there be room therein for repentance?—

entries.farsi: توبه را زين پس ز دل بيرون كنم / ‏از حيات خلد توبه چون كنم
entries.english: Henceforth I will banish repentance from this heart: how should I repent of the life everlasting?”

entries.farsi: عشق قهار است و من مقهور عشق / ‏چون شكر شيرين شدم از شور عشق
entries.english: Love is the All-subduer, and I am subdued by Love: by Love’s bitterness I have been made sweet as sugar.

entries.farsi: برگ كاهم پيش تو اى تند باد / من چه دانم كه كجا خواهم فتاد
entries.english: O fierce Wind, before You I am a straw: how can I know where I shall fall?

entries.farsi: گر هاللم گر باللم مى‏دوم / ‏مقتدى آفتابت مى‏شوم
entries.english: Whether I am Bilal or the new moon, I am running on and following the course of Your sun.

entries.farsi: ماه را با زفتى و زارى چه كار / در پى خورشيد پويد سايه‏وار
entries.english: What has the moon to do with stoutness and thinness? She runs at the heels of the sun, like a shadow.

entries.farsi: با قضا هر كاو قرارى مى‏دهد / ريش‏خند سبلت خود مى‏كند
entries.english: Anyone who offers to make a settlement with destiny is mocking at his own moustache.

entries.farsi: كاه برگى پيش باد آن گه قرار / رستخيزى و آنگهانى عزم كار
entries.english: A straw in the face of the wind, and then a settlement! A Resurrection, and then the resolve to act!

entries.farsi: گربه در انبانم اندر دست عشق / ‏يك دمى بالا و يك دم پست عشق
entries.english: In the hand of Love I am like a cat in a bag, now lifted high and now flung low by Love.

entries.farsi: او همى‏گرداندم بر گرد سر / نه به زير آرام دارم نه ز بر
entries.english: He is whirling me round His head: I have no rest either below or aloft.

entries.farsi: عاشقان در سيل تند افتاده‏اند / بر قضاى عشق دل بنهاده‏اند
entries.english: The lovers have fallen into a fierce torrent: they have set their hearts on the ordinance of Love.

entries.farsi: همچو سنگ آسيا اندر مدار / روز و شب گردان و نالان بى‏قرار
entries.english: Like the millstone turning, day and night, in revolution and moaning incessantly.

entries.farsi: گردشش بر جوى جويان شاهد است / ‏تا نگويد كس كه آن جو راكد است
entries.english: Its turning is evidence for those who seek the River, lest anyone should say that the River is motionless.

entries.farsi: گر نمى‏بينى تو جو را در كمين / گردش دوالب گردونى ببين
entries.english: If you see not the hidden River, see the turning of the celestial water-wheel.

entries.farsi: چون قرارى نيست گردون را از او / اى دل اختروار آرامى مجو
entries.english: Since the heavens have no rest from Him, you, O heart, like a star, seek no rest.

entries.farsi: گر زنى در شاخ دستى كى هلد / هر كجا پيوند سازى بگسلد
entries.english: If you lay hold of a branch, how should He let? Wherever you make an attachment, He will break it.

entries.farsi: گر نمى‏بينى تو تدوير قدر / در عناصر جوشش و گردش نگر
entries.english: If you see not the revolutionary action of the decree, look at the surging and whirling in the elements;

entries.farsi: ز انكه گردشهاى آن خاشاك و كف / ‏باشد از غليان بحر با شرف
entries.english: For the whirling of the sticks and straws and foam are caused by the boiling of the noble Sea.

entries.farsi: باد سر گردان ببين اندر خروش / ‏پيش امرش موج دريا بين به جوش
entries.english: See the giddy wind howling; see the billows surging at His command.

entries.farsi: آفتاب و ماه دو گاو خراس / ‏گرد مى‏گردند و مى‏دارند پاس
entries.english: The sun and moon are two mill-oxen, going round and round and keeping watch.

entries.farsi: اختران هم خانه خانه مى‏دوند / مركب هر سعد و نحسى مى‏شوند
entries.english: The stars likewise run from house to house and convey every good and evil fortune.

entries.farsi: اختران چرخ گر دورند هى / ‏وين حواست كاهلند و سست پى
entries.english: Listen, though the stars of heaven are far away and your senses are dull and slack,

entries.farsi: اختران چشم و گوش و هوش ما / شب كجايند و به بيدارى كجا
entries.english: Where are our stars—eye, ear, and mind—at night, and where when we are awake?

entries.farsi: گاه در سعد و وصال و دل خوشى / ‏گاه در نحس و فراق و بى‏هشى
entries.english: Now in good luck and union and happiness; now in ill-luck and separation and insensibility.

entries.farsi: ماه گردون چون در اين گرديدن است / ‏گاه تاريك و زمانى روشن است
entries.english: Since the moon of heaven is in making this circuit, she is sometimes dark and sometimes bright.

entries.farsi: گه بهار و صيف همچون شهد و شير / گه سياستگاه برف و زمهرير
entries.english: Sometimes it is spring and summer, as honey and milk; sometimes a place of punishment by snow and piercing cold.

entries.farsi: چون كه كليات پيش او چو گوست / ‏سخره و سجده كن چوگان اوست
entries.english: Seeing that before Him universals are like a ball, subject and prostrating themselves before His bat,

entries.farsi: تو كه يك جزوى دال زين صد هزار / چون نباشى پيش حكمش بى‏قرار
entries.english: How should you, O heart, which are one of these hundred thousand particulars, not be in restless movement at His decree?

entries.farsi: چون ستورى باش در حكم امير / گه در آخور حبس گاهى در مسير
entries.english: Be at the disposal of the Prince, like a horse, now confined in the stable, now going.

entries.farsi: چون كه بر ميخت ببندد بسته باش / ‏چون كه بگشايد برو برجسته باش
entries.english: When He fastens you to a peg, be fastened; when He frees you, go, be exultant.

entries.farsi: آفتاب اندر فلك كژ مى‏جهد / در سيه رويى خسوفش مى‏دهد
entries.english: When the sun in heaven jumps crookedly, He causes it to be eclipsed in black disgrace,

entries.farsi: كز ذنب پرهيز كن هين هوش دار / تا نگردى تو سيه رو ديگ‏وار
entries.english: Saying, “Avoid the Dragon’s Tail: listen, take heed, lest you become black of face like a cooking-pot.”

entries.farsi: ابر را هم تازيانه‏ى آتشين / ‏مى‏زنندش كانچنان رو نه چنين
entries.english: The cloud, too, is lashed with a whip of fire, “Go that way, do not go this way!

entries.farsi: بر فالن وادى ببار اين سوم بار / گوشمالش مى‏دهد كه گوش دار
entries.english: Rain upon such and such a valley, do not rain in this quarter”: He reprimands it, saying, “Give ear!

entries.farsi: عقل تو از آفتابى بيش نيست / ‏اندر آن فكرى كه نهى آمد مه ايست
entries.english: Your reason is not superior to a sun: do not stay in a thought that has been forbidden.

entries.farsi: كژ منه اى عقل تو هم گام خويش / ‏تا نيايد آن خسوف رو به پيش
entries.english: O Reason, do not you too step crookedly, lest that eclipse of face befall.

entries.farsi: چون گنه كمتر بود نيم آفتاب / ‏منخسف بينى و نيمى نور تاب
entries.english: When sin is less, you will see half the sun eclipsed and half radiant,

entries.farsi: كه به قدر جرم مى‏گيرم ترا / اين بود تقرير در داد و جزا
entries.english: For I punish you in proportion to your sin: this is the principle laid down for justice and retribution.

entries.farsi: خواه نيك و خواه بد فاش و ستير / بر همه اشيا سميعيم و بصير
entries.english: Whether good or bad or open or secret, I am He that overhears and oversees all things.”

entries.farsi: زين گذر كن اى پدر نوروز شد / خلق از خالق خوش پدفوز شد
entries.english: Leave this topic, O father: New Year’s Day is come: the creatures have had their mouths made sweet by the Creator.

entries.farsi: باز آمد آب جان در جوى ما / باز آمد شاه ما در كوى ما
entries.english: The spiritual Water has returned into our river-bed, our King has returned into our street.

entries.farsi: مى‏خرامد بخت و دامن مى‏كشد / نوبت توبه شكستن مى‏زند
entries.english: Fortune is strutting and trailing her skirt and beating the drums to break vows of repentance.

entries.farsi: توبه را بار دگر سيالب برد / فرصت آمد پاسبان را خواب برد
entries.english: Once more the flood-water has swept repentance away: the opportunity has arrived, the watchman is overcome by sleep.

entries.farsi: هر خمارى مست گشت و باده خورد / رخت را امشب گرو خواهيم كرد
entries.english: Every toper has drunk the wine and is intoxicated: to-night we will pawn all our belongings.

entries.farsi: ز آن شراب لعل جان جان فزا / لعل اندر لعل اندر لعل ما
entries.english: From the ruby wine of the life-increasing Spirit we are ruby within ruby within ruby.

entries.farsi: باز خرم گشت مجلس دل فروز / خيز دفع چشم بد اسپند سوز
entries.english: Once more the assembly-place has become flourishing and heart-illuminating: arise and burn rue-seed to keep off the evil eye.

entries.farsi: نعره مستان خوش مى‏آيدم / ‏تا ابد جانا چنين مى‏بايدم
entries.english: The cries of the joyous drunk lovers are coming to me: O Beloved, I want it to continue like this forever.

entries.farsi: نك هاللى با باللى يار شد / زخم خار او را گل و گلنار شد
entries.english: Lo, a new moon (hilálí) has been united with a Bilal: the blows of the thorns have become to him roses and pomegranate-flowers.

entries.farsi: تن به پيش زخم خار آن جهود / جان من مست و خراب آن ودود
entries.english: “If my body is a sieve from the blows of the thorns, my soul and body are a rose-garden of felicity.

entries.farsi: گر ز زخم خار تن غربال شد / جان و جسم گلشن اقبال شد
entries.english: My body is exposed to the blows of the Jew’s thorns, my spirit is intoxicated and ravished by that Loving One.

entries.farsi: بوى جانى سوى جانم مى‏رسد / بوى يار مهربانم مى‏رسد
entries.english: The scent of a Soul is coming towards my soul: the scent of my loving Friend is coming to me.”

entries.farsi: از سوى معراج آمد مصطفى / بر باللش حبذا لى حبذا
entries.english: Mustafá came from the Ascension, and pronounced on his Bilal the blessing, “How dear to me, how dear!”

entries.farsi: چون كه صديق از بالل دم درست / ‏اين شنيد از توبه‏ى او دست شست
entries.english: On hearing this from Bilal, in whose speech there was no guile, the Siddíq washed his hands of urging him to repent.

sections.title_fa: باز گردانيدن صديق واقعه‏ى بالل را و ظلم جهودان را بر وى و احد احد گفتن او و افزون شدن كينه‏ى جهودان و قصه كردن آن قضيه پيش مصطفى عليه الصالة و السلام و مشورت در خريدن او ‏از جهودان

sections.title_en: How the Siddíq, may God be pleased with him, recalled what had happened to Bilal, may God be pleased with him, and his maltreatment by the Jews and his crying “One! One!” and the Jews becoming more incensed; and how he told the story of the affair to Mustafá, on whom be peace, and consulted him as to buying him from the Jews.

entries.farsi: باز سلطان است ز آن جغدان به رنج / ‏در حدث مدفون شدست آن زفت گنج
entries.english: Afterwards the Siddíq related to Mustafá the plight of the faithful Bilal,

entries.farsi: بعد از آن صديق پيش مصطفى / گفت حال آن بالل با وفا
entries.english: Saying, “That heaven-surveying nimble of blessed wing is at this time in love and in your net.

entries.farsi: كان فلك پيماى ميمون بال چست / ‏اين زمان در عشق و اندر دام تست
entries.english: The Sultan’s falcon is tormented by those owls; that grand treasure is buried in filth.

entries.farsi: جغدها بر باز استم مى‏كنند / پر و بالش بى‏گناهى مى‏كنند
entries.english: The owls are doing violence to the falcon: they are tearing out his plumes and feathers though he is innocent.

entries.farsi: جرم او اين است كاو باز است و بس / ‏غير خوبى جرم يوسف چيست پس
entries.english: His only crime is this, that he is a falcon: after all, what is Joseph’s crime except beauty?

entries.farsi: جغد را ويرانه باشد زاد و بود / هستشان بر باز ز آن خشم جهود
entries.english: The owl’s origin and existence is the wilderness; that is the cause of their Jewish anger against the falcon.

entries.farsi: كه چرا مى ياد آرى ز آن ديار / يا ز قصر و ساعد آن شهريار
entries.english: ‘Why are you making mention of yonder land, or of the palace and wrist of the Emperor?

entries.farsi: در ده جغدان فضولى مى‏كنى / فتنه و تشويش در مى‏افگنى
entries.english: You are behaving impudently in the owls’ village; you are introducing dissension and disturbance.

entries.farsi: مسكن ما را كه شد رشك اثير / تو خرابه خوانى و نام حقير
entries.english: Our dwelling-place, which is the envy of the empyrean, you call a wilderness and give it the name of “vile.”

entries.farsi: شيد آوردى كه تا جغدان ما / مر ترا سازند شاه و پيشوا
entries.english: You have employed hypocrisy in order that our owls may make you king and leader.

entries.farsi: وهم و سودايى در ايشان مى‏تنى / ‏نام اين فردوس ويران مى‏كنى
entries.english: You are instilling into them a vain imagination and a mad fancy: you are giving the name “ruin” to this Paradise.

entries.farsi: بر سرت چندان زنيم اى بد صفات / ‏كه بگويى ترك شيد و ترهات
entries.english: We will beat your head so long, O of evil qualities, that you will renounce this hypocrisy and nonsense.’

entries.farsi: پيش مشرق چار ميخش مى‏كنند / تن برهنه شاخ خارش مى‏زنند
entries.english: They are crucifying him, his face to the East, and flogging his naked body with a thorny branch.

entries.farsi: از تنش صد جاى خون بر مى‏جهد / او احد مى‏گويد و سر مى‏نهد
entries.english: The blood is spurting from his body in a hundred places, he is crying ‘One!’ and bowing his head.

entries.farsi: پندها دادم كه پنهان دار دين / ‏سر بپوشان از جهودان لعين
entries.english: I admonished him often, saying, ‘Keep your religion hidden, and conceal your secret from the accursed Jews.’

entries.farsi: عاشق است او را قيامت آمده ست / ‏تا در توبه بر او بسته شده ست
entries.english: He is a lover: to him the resurrection has come, so that the door of repentance has been shut on him.”

entries.farsi: عاشقى و توبه يا امكان صبر / اين محالى باشد اى جان بس سطبر
entries.english: Loverhood and repentance or the possibility of patience— this, O soul, is a very enormous absurdity.

entries.farsi: توبه كرم و عشق همچون اژدها / توبه وصف خلق و آن وصف خدا
entries.english: Repentance is a worm, while Love is like a dragon: repentance is an attribute of Man, while that is an attribute of God.

entries.farsi: ز انكه آن حسن زر اندود آمده ست / ‏ظاهرش نور اندرون دود آمده ست
entries.english: Love is of the attributes of God who wants nothing: love for anything besides Him is unreal,

entries.farsi: عشق ز اوصاف خداى بى‏نياز / عاشقى بر غير او باشد مجاز
entries.english: Because that is a gilded beauty: its outside is light, it is smoke within.

entries.farsi: چون رود نور و شود پيدا دخان / ‏بفسرد عشق مجازى آن زمان
entries.english: When the light goes and the smoke becomes visible, at that moment the unreal love is frozen up.

entries.farsi: وا رود آن حسن سوى اصل خود / جسم ماند گنده و رسوا و بد
entries.english: That beauty returns to its source; the body is left—foul-smelling, shameful, and ugly.

entries.farsi: نور مه راجع شود هم سوى ماه / ‏وا رود عكسش ز ديوار سياه
entries.english: The moonlight is returning to the moon: its reflection goes off the black wall;

entries.farsi: قلب را كه زر ز روى او بجست / ‏باز گشت آن زر به كان خود نشست
entries.english: And then, the water and clay are left without that ornament, the wall, moonless, becomes as a devil.

entries.farsi: پس بماند آب و گل بى‏آن نگار / گردد آن ديوار بى‏مه ديووار
entries.english: When the gold flies from the surface of the base coin that gold returns to its mine and settles;

entries.farsi: پس مس رسوا بماند دودوش / ‏زو سيه روتر بماند عاشقش
entries.english: Then the shamefully exposed copper is left like smoke, and its lover is left looking blacker than it.

entries.farsi: عشق بينايان بود بر كان زر / الجرم هر روز باشد بيشتر
entries.english: The love of them that have insight is on the gold-mine; necessarily it is greater every day,

entries.farsi: ز انكه كان را در زرى نبود شريك / ‏مرحبا اى كان زر ال شك فيك
entries.english: Because the mine has no partner in golden properties. Hail, O Gold-mine, You concerning whom there is no doubt!

entries.farsi: هر كه قلبى را كند انباز كان / ‏وا رود زر تا به كان المكان
entries.english: If anyone lets a base coin become a companion with the Mine, the gold goes back to the Mine beyond locality,

entries.farsi: عاشق و معشوق مرده ز اضطراب / ‏مانده ماهى رفته ز آن گرداب آب
entries.english: The lover and his beloved are dead in agony: the fish is left, the water is gone from the whirlpool.

entries.farsi: عشق ربانى است خورشيد كمال / ‏امر نور اوست خلقان چون ظالل
entries.english: The Divine Love is the Sun of perfection: the Word is its light, the creatures are as shadows.

entries.farsi: مصطفى زين قصه چون خوش بر شكفت / ‏رغبت افزون گشت او را هم به گفت
entries.english: When Mustafá expanded with joy this story, his desire to speak increased also.

entries.farsi: مستمع چون يافت همچون مصطفى / هر سر مويش زبانى شد جدا
entries.english: Since he found a listener like Mustafá, every hair of him became a separate tongue.

entries.farsi: مصطفى گفتش كه اكنون چاره چيست / ‏گفت اين بنده مر او را مشترى است
entries.english: Mustafá said to him, “Now what is the remedy?” He replied, “This servant is going to buy him.

entries.farsi: هر بها كه گويد او را مى‏خرم / ‏در زيان و حيف ظاهر ننگرم
entries.english: I will buy him at whatever price he may name: I will not regard the apparent loss and the extortion;

entries.farsi: كاو اسير اللهَّ فى الارض آمده ست / ‏سخره‏ى خشم عدو اللهَّ شده ست
entries.english: For he is God’s captive on the earth, and he has become subjected to the anger of God’s enemy.”

sections.title_fa: وصيت كردن مصطفى عليه السلام صديق را كه چون بالل را مشترى مى‏شوى هر آينه ايشان از ستيز بر خواهند فزود بهاى او را، مرا در اين فضيلت شريك خود كن وكيل من باش و نيم بها از من ‏بستان

sections.title_en: How Mustafá, on whom be peace, enjoined the Siddíq, may God be pleased with him, saying, “Since you are going to purchase Bilal, they will certainly raise his price by wrangling: make me your partner in this merit, be my agent, and receive from me half the purchase-money.”

entries.farsi: مصطفى گفتش كه اى اقبال جو / اندر اين من مى‏شوم انباز تو
entries.english: Mustafá said to him, “O seeker of fortune, I will be your partner in this.

entries.farsi: تو وكيلم باش نيمى بهر من / ‏مشترى شو قبض كن از من ثمن
entries.english: Be my agent, buy a half share on my account, and receive the payment from me.”

entries.farsi: گفت صد خدمت كنم رفت آن زمان / ‏سوى خانه‏ى آن جهود بى‏امان
entries.english: He replied, “I will do my utmost to serve you.” Then he went to the house of the merciless Jew.

entries.farsi: گفت با خود كز كف طفالن گهر / بس توان آسان خريدن اى پدر
entries.english: He said to himself, “From the hands of children one can buy pearls very cheaply, O father.”

entries.farsi: عقل و ايمان را از اين طفالن گول / ‏مى‏خرد با ملك دنيا ديو غول
entries.english: From these foolish children the ghoul-like Devil is buying their reason and faith in exchange for the kingdom of this world.

entries.farsi: آن چنان زينت دهد مردار را / كه خرد ز ايشان دو صد گلزار را
entries.english: He decks out the carcass so finely that he buys from them two hundred rose-gardens.

entries.farsi: آن چنان مهتاب پيمايد به سحر / كز خسان صد كيسه بربايد به سحر
entries.english: By magic he produces such moonshine that by means of magic he carries off from worthless folk a hundred purses.

entries.farsi: انبياشان تاجرى آموختند / پيش ايشان شمع دين افروختند
entries.english: The prophets taught them to trade and lighted the candle of the religion before them;

entries.farsi: ديو و غول ساحر از سحر و نبرد / انبيا را در نظرشان زشت كرد
entries.english: By means of magic and in despite the devilish and ghoulish Magician caused the prophets to appear ugly in their eyes.

entries.farsi: اين گهر از هر دو عالم برتر است / ‏هين بخر زين طفل جاهل كاو خر است
entries.english: By sorcery the Foe causes ugliness, so that divorce takes place between the wife and husband.

entries.farsi: ديده‏هاشان را به سحرى دوختند / تا چنين جوهر به خس بفروختند
entries.english: Their eyes have been sealed by a enchantment, so that they have sold such a pearl for rubbish.

entries.farsi: زشت گرداند به جادويى عدو / تا طالق افتد ميان جفت و شو
entries.english: This pearl is superior to both the worlds: listen, buy from this ignorant child, for he is an ass.

entries.farsi: پيش خر خر مهره و گوهر يكى است / ‏آن اشك را در در و دريا شكى است
entries.english: To the ass a shell and a pearl are alike: the ass has a doubt concerning the pearl and the Sea.

entries.farsi: منكر بحر است و گوهرهاى او / كى بود حيوان در و پيرايه جو
entries.english: He disbelieves in the Sea and its pearls: how should an animal be a seeker of pearls and adornments?

entries.farsi: در سر حيوان خدا ننهاده است / ‏كاو بود در بند لعل و در پرست
entries.english: God has not put it into the animal’s head to be engrossed with rubies and devoted to pearls.

entries.farsi: مر خران را هيچ ديدى گوشوار / گوش و هوش خر بود در سبزه زار
entries.english: Have you ever seen asses with ear-rings? The ear and mind of the ass are on the meadow.

entries.farsi: احسن التقويم در و التين بخوان / ‏كه گرامى گوهر است اى دوست جان
entries.english: Read in Wa’l-Tín, We created Man in the best proportion, for the spirit, O friend is a precious pearl.

entries.farsi: احسن التقويم از عرش او فزون / ‏احسن التقويم از فكرت برون
entries.english: In the best proportion surpasses the empyrean: in the best proportion is beyond thought.

entries.farsi: گر بگويم قيمت اين ممتنع / ‏من بسوزم هم بسوزد مستمع
entries.english: If I declare the value of this inaccessible, I shall be consumed, and the hearer too will be consumed.

entries.farsi: لب ببند اينجا و خر اين سو مران / ‏رفت اين صديق سوى آن خران
entries.english: At this point close your lips and proceed no further. This Siddíq went to those asses.

entries.farsi: حلقه‏ى در زد چو در را بر گشود / رفت بى‏خود در سراى آن جهود
entries.english: He knocked the door-ring, and when the Jew opened the door he went into his house, beside himself.

entries.farsi: بى‏خود و سر مست و پر آتش نشست / كاين ولى اللهَّ را چون مى‏زنى
entries.english: He sat down, beside himself and furious and full of fire: from his mouth leaped many bitter words—

entries.farsi: ‏اين چه حقد است اى عدوى روشنى / گر ترا صدقى است اندر دين خود
entries.english: “Why are you beating this friend of God? What hatred is this, O enemy of the Light?

entries.farsi: ظلم بر صادق دلت چون مى‏دهد / اى تو در دين جهودى ماده‏اى
entries.english: If you are steadfast in your own religion, how is your heart consenting to maltreat him who is steadfast?

entries.farsi: ‏كاين گمان دارى تو بر شه زاده‏اى / در همه ز آيينه‏ى كژ ساز خود
entries.english: O you effeminate in Judaism, who imputes this to a prince!

entries.farsi: منگر اى مردود نفرين ابد / آن چه آن دم از لب صديق جست
entries.english: Do not view all in the distorting mirror of your selfhood, O you who are banned with an everlasting curse!”

entries.farsi: ‏گر بگويم گم كنى تو پاى و دست / آن ينابيع الحكم همچون فرات
entries.english: If I should tell what burst from the lips of the Siddíq at that moment, you would lose foot and hand.

entries.farsi: ‏از دهان او دوان از بى‏جهات / همچو از سنگى كه آبى شد روان
entries.english: From beyond spatial relations, the fountains of wisdom as the Euphrates were running from his mouth,

entries.farsi: ‏نه ز پهلو مايه دارد نه از ميان / اسپر خود كرده حق آن سنگ را
entries.english: As from the rock where water gushed, having no source of supply in side or interior;

entries.farsi: بر گشاده آب مينا رنگ را / همچنانك از چشمه‏ى چشم تو نور
entries.english: God made that rock a shield for Himself and opened the blue crystalline water,

entries.farsi: او روان كرده ست بى‏بخل و فتور / نه ز پيه آن مايه دارد نه ز پوست
entries.english: Even as He has caused the light to flow from the fountain of your eye without stint or abatement:

entries.farsi: ‏روى پوشى كرد در ايجاد دوست / در خالى گوش باد جاذبش
entries.english: It has no source of supply either in the fat or in the coating; the Beloved made a veil when bringing light into existence.

entries.farsi: ‏مدرك صدق كالم و كاذبش / آن چه باد است اندر آن خرد استخوان
entries.english: The attracting air in the cavity of the ear apprehends that which is spoken, true or false.

entries.farsi: ‏كاو پذيرد حرف و صوت قصه خوان / استخوان و باد رو پوشست و بس
entries.english: What is that air within that little bone, which receives the words and sounds uttered by the story-teller?

entries.farsi: ‏در دو عالم غير يزدان نيست كس / مستمع او قائل او بى‏احتجاب
entries.english: The bone and the air are only a veil: in the two worlds there is none except God.

entries.farsi: ‏ز آنك الاذنان من الرأس اى مثاب / گفت رحمت گر همى‏آيد بر او
entries.english: He is the hearer, He is the speaker, unveiled; for the ears belong to the head, O you who have merited the Divine recompense.

entries.farsi: زر بده بستانش اى اكرام خو / از منش واخر چو مى‏سوزد دلت
entries.english: He said, “If you are feeling pity for him, give gold and take him, O man of generous disposition.

entries.farsi: ‏بى‏مئونت حل نگردد مشكلت / گفت صد خدمت كنم پانصد سجود
entries.english: Since your heart is burning , ransom him from me: your difficulty will not be solved without expense.”

entries.farsi: بنده‏اى دارم نكو لكن جهود / تن سپيد و دل سياهستش بگير
entries.english: He replied, “I will perform a hundred services and five hundred prostrations. I have a handsome slave, but a Jew;

entries.farsi: در عوض ده تن سياه و دل منير / پس فرستاد و بياورد آن همام
entries.english: He has a white body, but a black heart: take, and give in exchange that one whose body is black but whose heart is illumined.”

entries.farsi: ‏بود الحق سخت زيبا آن غالم / آن چنان كه ماند حيران آن جهود
entries.english: Then the chieftain sent to fetch him: in truth that slave was exceedingly comely,

entries.farsi: آن دل چون سنگش از جا رفت زود / حالت صورت پرستان اين بود
entries.english: So that the Jew was dumbfounded: at once his stony heart inclined.

entries.farsi: سنگشان از صورتى مومين بود / باز كرد استيزه و راضى نشد
entries.english: This is what happens to form-worshippers: their stone is pale by a form.

entries.farsi: تا كه راضى گشت حرص آن جهود
entries.english: Again he wrangled and would not be satisfied, saying, “Without any evasion, give more than this.”

entries.farsi: كه بر اين افزون بده بى‏هيچ بد / يك نصاب نقره هم بر وى فزود
entries.english: He offered him in addition two hundred dirhems (nisab) of silver, so that the Jew’s greed was satisfied.

sections.title_fa: خنديدن جهود و پنداشتن كه صديق مغبون است در اين عقد
sections.title_en: How the Jew laughed and imagined that the Siddíq had been swindled in this bargain.

entries.farsi: قهقهه زد آن جهود سنگ دل / ‏از سر افسوس و طنز و غش و غل
entries.english: The stony-hearted Jew guffawed jeeringly and mockingly in malice and spite.

entries.farsi: گفت صديقش كه اين خنده چه بود / در جواب پرسش او خنده فزود
entries.english: The Siddíq said to him, “Why this laughter?” In reply to the question he laughed more loudly,

entries.farsi: من ز استيزه نمى‏جوشيد مى / ‏خود به عشر اينش بفروشيد مى
entries.english: And said, “Had it not been for the earnestness and ardour shown by you in the purchase of this black slave,

entries.farsi: گفت اگر جدت نبودى و غرام / ‏در خريدارى اين اسود غالم
entries.english: I would not have wrangled excitedly: indeed I would have sold him for a tenth of this,

entries.farsi: كاو به نزد من نيرزد نيم دانگ / ‏تو گران كردى بهايش را به بانگ
entries.english: For in my opinion he is not worth half a dáng; you made his price heavy by clamour.”

entries.farsi: پس جوابش داد صديق اى غبى / ‏گوهرى دادى به جوزى چون صبى
entries.english: Then the Siddíq answered him, “O simpleton, you have given away a pearl in exchange for a walnut, like a boy;

entries.farsi: كاو به نزد من همى‏ارزد دو كون / ‏من به جانش ناظرستم تو به لون
entries.english: For in my opinion he is worth the two worlds: I am regarding his spirit, you his colour.

entries.farsi: زر سرخ است او سيه تاب آمده / ‏از براى رشك اين احمق كنده
entries.english: He is red gold that has been made black polished iron on account of the enviousness of this abode of fools.

entries.farsi: ديده‏ى اين هفت رنگ جسمها / درنيابد زين نقاب آن روح را
entries.english: The eye that sees these seven bodily colours cannot perceive the spirit because of this veil.

entries.farsi: گر مكيسى كرديى در بيع بيش / ‏دادمى من جمله ملك و مال خويش
entries.english: If you had haggled in the sale more, I would have given the whole of my property and riches;

entries.farsi: ور مكاس افزوديى من ز اهتمام / ‏دامنى زر كردمى از غير وام
entries.english: And if you had increased your demands, I would have borrowed a skirt full of gold in my anxiety.

entries.farsi: حقه‏ى سر بسته جهل تو بداد / زود بينى كه چه غبنت اوفتاد
entries.english: You gave up easily because you got cheaply: you did not see the pearl, you did not split the casket.

entries.farsi: سهل دادى ز انكه ارزان يافتى / ‏در نديدى حقه را نشكافتى
entries.english: Your folly gave a sealed casket: you will soon see what a swindle has befallen you.

entries.farsi: حقه‏ى پر لعل را دادى به باد / همچو زنگى در سيه رويى تو شاد
entries.english: You have given away a casket full of rubies and, like the negro, you are rejoicing in your blackness of face.

entries.farsi: عاقبت وا حسرتا گويى بسى / ‏بخت و دولت را فرو شد خود كسى
entries.english: In the end you will utter many a ‘woe is me!’ Does anyone, truthfully, sell fortune and felicity?

entries.farsi: بخت با جامه‏ى غالمانه رسيد / چشم بد بختت بجز ظاهر نديد
entries.english: Fortune came in the garb of a slave, your unlucky eye saw only the surface.

entries.farsi: او نمودت بندگى خويشتن / ‏خوى زشتت كرد با او مكر و فن
entries.english: He showed unto you his slavery: your wicked nature practiced cunning and deceit with him.

entries.farsi: اين سيه اسرار تن اسپيد را / بت پرستانه بگير اى ژاژخا
entries.english: O driveller, take idolatrously this whose secret thoughts are black though his body is white.

entries.farsi: اين ترا و آن مرا برديم سود / هين لكم دين وَ لِيَ دِينِ اى جهود
entries.english: This one for you, that one for me: we have profited. Listen, unto you religion and unto me religion, O Jew.”

entries.farsi: خود سزاى بت پرستان اين بود / جلش اطلس اسب او چوبين بود
entries.english: Truly this is proper for idolaters: his horse-cloth is satin his horse is made of wood.

entries.farsi: همچو گور كافران پر دود و نار / وز برون بر بسته صد نقش و نگار
entries.english: It is like the tomb of infidels—full of smoke and fire, on the outside it is decked with a hundred designs and ornaments;

entries.farsi: همچو مال ظالمان بيرون جمال / و ز درونش خون مظلوم و وبال
entries.english: Like the wealth of tyrants—fair externally, within it the blood of the oppressed and woe;

entries.farsi: چون منافق از برون صوم و صالت / ‏و ز درون خاك سياه بى‏نبات
entries.english: Like the hypocrite externally fasting and prayer, inwardly black loam without vegetation;

entries.farsi: همچو ابرى خاليى پر قر و قر / نه در او نفع زمين نه قوت بر
entries.english: Like a cloud empty, full of thunderclaps, wherein is neither benefit to the earth nor nourishment for the wheat;

entries.farsi: بعد از آن بگرفت او دست بالل / ‏آن ز زخم ضرس محنت چون خالل
entries.english: Like a promise of guile and lying words, of which the end is shameful though its beginning is splendid.

entries.farsi: همچو وعده‏ى مكر و گفتار دروغ / ‏آخرش رسوا و اول با فروغ
entries.english: Afterwards he took the hand of Bilal, who was as a toothpick from the blows inflicted by the tooth of tribulation.

entries.farsi: شد خاللى در دهانى راه يافت / ‏جانب شيرين زبانى مى‏شتافت
entries.english: He became a toothpick and found his way into a mouth: he was hastening towards a man of sweet tongue.

entries.farsi: تا به ديرى بى‏خود و بى‏خويش ماند / چون به خويش آمد ز شادى اشك راند
entries.english: When that wounded one beheld the face of Mustafá, he fell down in a swoon, he fell on his back.

entries.farsi: چون بديد آن خسته روى مصطفى / خر مغشيا فتاد او بر قفا
entries.english: For a long time he remained unconscious and beside himself: when he came to himself, he shed tears for joy.

entries.farsi: مصطفايش در كنار خود كشيد / كس چه داند بخششى كاو را رسيد
entries.english: Mustafá clasped him to his bosom: how should anyone know the bounty that was bestowed on him?

entries.farsi: چون بود مسى كه بر اكسير زد / مفلسى بر گنج پر توفير زد
entries.english: How is it with a piece of copper that has touched the elixir? How with an insolvent who has hit upon an ample treasure?

entries.farsi: ماهى پژمرده در بحر اوفتاد / كاروان گم شده زد بر رشاد
entries.english: A parched fish fell into the sea, a caravan that had lost its way struck the right road.

entries.farsi: آن خطاباتى كه گفت آن دم نبى / ‏گر زند بر شب بر آيد از شبى
entries.english: If the words which the Prophet addressed at that moment should fall upon Night, it would cease from being night;

entries.farsi: روز روشن گردد آن شب چون صباح / ‏من نتانم باز گفت آن اصطالح
entries.english: Night would become day radiant as dawn: I cannot express that mystic allocution.

entries.farsi: خود تو دانى كافتابى در حمل / ‏تا چه گويد با نبات و با دقل
entries.english: You yourself know what a sun, in Aries, speaks to the plants and the date-palms;

entries.farsi: خود تو دانى هم كه آن آب زالل / ‏مى چه گويد با رياحين و نهال
entries.english: You yourself, too, know what the limpid water is saying to the sweet herbs and the sapling.

entries.farsi: صنع حق با جمله اجزاى جهان / ‏چون دم و حرف است از افسونگران
entries.english: The doing of God towards all the particles of the world is like the words breathed by enchanters.

entries.farsi: جذب يزدان با اثرها و سبب / ‏صد سخن گويد نهان بى‏حرف و لب
entries.english: The Divine attraction holds a hundred discourses with the effects and secondary causes, without a word or a lip.

entries.farsi: نه كه تاثير از قدر معمول نيست / ليك تاثيرش از او معقول نيست
entries.english: Not that the production of effects by the Divine decree is not actual; but His production of effects thereby is inconceivable to reason.

entries.farsi: چون مقلد بود عقل اندر اصول / ‏دان مقلد در فروعش اى فضول
entries.english: Since reason has learned by rote in regard to the fundamentals, know O trifler that it learns by rote in regard to the copies.

entries.farsi: گر بپرسد عقل چون‏باشد مرام / ‏گو چنان كه تو ندانى و السلام
entries.english: If reason should ask how the aim may be, say, “In a manner that you know not, and farewell!”

sections.title_fa: معاتبه‏ى مصطفى عليه الصالة و السلام با صديق كه ترا وصيت كردم كه به شركت من بخر تو چرا بهر خود تنها خريدى و عذر او
sections.title_en: How Mustafá, on whom be peace, reproached the Siddíq, may God be pleased with him, saying, “I enjoined you to buy in partnership with me: why has you bought for yourself alone?”
and his excuse.

entries.farsi: گفت اى صديق آخر گفتمت / ‏كه مرا انباز كن در مكرمت
entries.english: He said, “Why, O Siddíq, I told you to make me the partner in generosity.”

entries.farsi: گفت ما دو بندگان كوى تو / كردمش آزاد من بر روى تو
entries.english: He replied, “We are two slaves in your street: I set him free for your sake.

entries.farsi: تو مرا مى‏دار بنده و يار غار / هيچ آزادى نخواهم زينهار
entries.english: Keep me as your slave and loyal friend: I want no freedom, beware!

entries.farsi: كه مرا از بندگيت آزادى است / ‏بى‏تو بر من محنت و بى‏دادى است
entries.english: For my freedom consists in being your slave: without you, tribulation and injustice are on me.

entries.farsi: اى جهان را زنده كرده ز اصطفا / خاص كرده عام را خاصه مرا
entries.english: O you who through being the chosen has brought the world to life and has made the common folk to be the elect, especially me,

entries.farsi: خوابها مى‏ديد جانم در شباب / ‏كه سالمم كرد قرص آفتاب
entries.english: In my youth my spirit used to dream that the orb of the sun greeted to me,

entries.farsi: از زمينم بر كشيد او بر سما / همره او گشته بودم ز ارتقا
entries.english: And lifted me up from earth to heaven: by mounting high I had become its fellow-traveller.

entries.farsi: گفتم اين ماخوليا بود و محال / ‏هيچ گردد مستحيلى وصف حال
entries.english: I said, ‘this is an hallucination and absurd: how should absurdity ever become actuality?’

entries.farsi: چون ترا ديدم بديدم خويش را / آفرين آن آينه‏ى خوش كيش را
entries.english: When I beheld you I beheld myself: blessings on that mirror goodly in its ways!

entries.farsi: چون ترا ديدم محالم حال شد / جان من مستغرق اجلال شد
entries.english: When I beheld you, the absurd became real for me: my spirit was submerged in the Glory.

entries.farsi: چون ترا ديدم خود اى روح البالد / مهر اين خورشيد از چشمم فتاد
entries.english: When I beheld you, O Spirit of the world, truly love for this sun fell from my eye.

entries.farsi: گشت عالى همت از تو چشم من / ‏جز به خوارى ننگرد اندر چمن
entries.english: By you my eye was endowed with lofty aspiration: it looks not on the garden save with contempt.

entries.farsi: نور جستم خود بديدم نور نور / حور جستم خود بديدم رشك حور
entries.english: I sought light: truly I beheld the Light of light. I sought the houri: truly I beheld an object of envy to the houri.

entries.farsi: يوسفى جستم لطيف و سيم تن / ‏يوسفستانى بديدم در تو من
entries.english: I sought a Joseph comely and with limbs silver: in you I beheld an assembly of Josephs.

entries.farsi: در پى جنت بدم در جستجو / جنتى بنمود از هر جزو تو
entries.english: I was in searching after Paradise: from every part of you a Paradise appeared.

entries.farsi: هست اين نسبت به من مدح و ثنا / هست اين نسبت به تو قدح و هجا
entries.english: In relation to me this is praise and eulogy; in relation to you this is abusive and satirical,

entries.farsi: قدح او را حق به مدحى بر گرفت / ‏گر تو هم رحمت كنى نبود شگفت
entries.english: Like the praise given to God by the simple shepherd in the presence of Moses the Kalím—

entries.farsi: كه بجويم اشپشت شيرت دهم / ‏چارقت دوزم من و پيشت نهم
entries.english: ‘I will seek out Your lice, I will give You milk, I will stitch Your shoes and lay them before You.’

entries.farsi: همچو مدح مرد چوپان سليم / ‏مر خدا را پيش موساى كليم
entries.english: God accepted his harsh language as an expression of praise: if you also have mercy, it will be no marvel.

entries.farsi: رحم فرما بر قصور فهمها / اى وراى عقلها و وهمها
entries.english: Have mercy upon the failure of minds, O you who are beyond understandings and conceptions.”

entries.farsi: ايها العشاق اقبال جديد / از جهان كهنه‏ى نو كن رسيد
entries.english: O lovers, new fortune has arrived from the old World that makes new,

entries.farsi: ز آن جهان كاو چاره‏ى بى‏چاره جوست / ‏صد هزاران نادره‏ى دنيا در اوست
entries.english: From the World that is seeking a remedy for them that have no remedy: hundreds of thousands of wonders of the world are in it.

entries.farsi: ابشروا يا قوم إذ جاء الفرج / ‏افرحوا يا قوم قد زال الحرج
entries.english: Rejoice, O people, since the relief has come; be glad, O people: the distress is removed.

entries.farsi: آفتابى رفت در كازه‏ى هالل / ‏در تقاضا كه ارحنا يا بالل
entries.english: A Sun went into the hut of the new-moon, making urgent demands and saying, “Refresh us, O Bilal!

entries.farsi: زير لب مى‏گفتى از بيم عدو / كورى او بر مناره رو بگو
entries.english: From fear of the foe you were wont to speak under your breath: to his confusion, go up into the minaret and speak.”

entries.farsi: مى‏دمد در گوش هر غمگين بشير / خيز اى مدبر ره اقبال گير
entries.english: The announcer of glad news is shouting in the ear of every sorrowful one, “Arise, O unlucky man, and take the road to fortune.

entries.farsi: اى در اين حبس و در اين گند و شپش / ‏هني كه تا كس نشنود رستى خمش
entries.english: O you that are in this prison and amidst this stench and these lice, beware lest anyone hears! You have escaped: be silent!”

entries.farsi: چون كنى خامش كنون اى يار من / ‏كز بن هر مو بر آمد طبل زن
entries.english: How should you keep silence now, O my beloved, when a drummer has appeared from the root of every hair?

entries.farsi: آن چنان كر شد عدوى رشك خو / گويد اين چندين دهل را بانگ كو
entries.english: The jealous foe has become so deaf he says, “Where is the sound of all these drums?”

entries.farsi: مى‏زند بر روش ريحان كه طرى است / ‏او ز كورى گويد اين آسيب چيست
entries.english: The fresh sweet basil is touching his face, in his blindness he says, “What is this annoyance?”

entries.farsi: مى‏شكنجد حور دستش مى‏كشد / كور حيران كز چه دردم مى‏كند
entries.english: The houri is nipping his hand and drawing: the blind man is distraught and says, “Wherefore is he hurting me?

entries.farsi: اين كشاكش چيست بر دست و تنم / ‏خفته‏ام بگذار تا خوابى كنم
entries.english: What is this having my hand and body pulled hither and thither? I am asleep, let me sleep awhile.”

entries.farsi: آن كه در خوابش همى‏جويى وى است / ‏چشم بگشا كان مه نيكو پى است
entries.english: He whom you seek in your slumbers, this is He! Open yours eye, it is that auspicious Moon.

entries.farsi: ز آن بالها بر عزيزان بيش بود / كان جتمش يار با خوبان فزود
entries.english: Tribulations were more upon dear ones because the Beloved showed more coquettishness towards the beauteous.

entries.farsi: الغ با خوبان كند در هر رهى / ‏نيز كوران را بشوراند گهى
entries.english: He sports with the beauteous ones in every path; sometimes, too, he throws the blind into frenzy.

entries.farsi: خويش را يك دم بدين كوران دهد / تا غريو از كوى كوران بر جهد
entries.english: For a moment He gives Himself to the blind, so that a great uproar arises from the street of the blind.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى هالل كه بنده‏ى مخلص بود خداى را، صاحب بصيرت بى‏تقليد، پنهان شده در بندگى مخلوقان جهت مصلحت نه از عجز، چنان كه لقمان و يوسف از روى ظاهر و غير ايشان، بنده‏اى سايس بود ،اميرى را و آن امير مسلمان بود اما كور ليك چونى به وهم درنارد داند اعمى كه مادرى دارد
اگر با اين دانش تعظيم اين مادر كند ممكن بود كه از عمى خالص يابد كه إذا اراد اللهَّ بعبد خيرا فتح عينى قلبه ‏ليبصره بهما الغيب
sections.title_en: Story of Hilal, who was a devoted servant to God. Possessed of spiritual insight and was not a mere imitator. He had concealed himself in being a slave to creatures, not from helplessness but for good reason, as Luqman and Joseph and others in appearance.

He was a groom in the service of a certain Amír, and that Amír was a Moslem, but blind. “The blind man knows that he has a mother, but he cannot conceive what she is like.” If, having this knowledge, he show reverence towards his mother, it is possible that he may gain deliverance from blindness, for when God wills good unto a servant
He opens the eyes of his heart, that He may let him see the Invisible with them.

entries.farsi: چون شنيدى بعض اوصاف بالل / ‏بشنو اكنون قصه‏ى ضعف هالل
entries.english: Since you have heard some of the qualities of Bilal, now hear the story of the emaciation of Hilal.

entries.farsi: از بالل او پيش بود اندر روش / ‏خوى بد را بيش كرده بد كشش
entries.english: He was more advanced than Bilal in the Way: he had mortified his evil nature more.

entries.farsi: نه چو تو پس رو كه هر دم پس‏ترى / ‏سوى سنگى مى‏روى از گوهرى
entries.english: Not a backslider like you, for at every moment you are farther back: you are moving away from the state of the pearl towards the state of the stone.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كان خواجه را مهمان رسيد / خواجه از ايام و سالش بر رسيد
entries.english: It is like the case of the guest who came to a certain Khwaja: the Khwaja inquired concerning his days and years.

entries.farsi: گفت عمرت چند سال است اى پسر / باز گو و در مدزد و بر شمر
entries.english: He asked, “How many years have you lived, my lad? Say out and don’t hide away but count up.”

entries.farsi: گفت هجده هفده يا خود شانزده / ‏يا كه پانزده اى برادر خوانده
entries.english: He replied, “Eighteen, seventeen, or sixteen, or fifteen, O adoptive brother.”

entries.farsi: گفت واپس واپس اى خيره‏سرت / ‏باز مى‏رو تا به كس مادرت
entries.english: “Backward, backward,” said he, “O giddy-headed one”; “keep going back until you came out of your mother!”

sections.title_fa: ‏حكايت در تقرير همين سخن
sections.title_en: Story in exposition of the same topic.

entries.farsi: آن يكى اسبى طلب كرد از امير / گفت رو آن اسب اشهب را بگير
entries.english: A certain man begged an Amír to give him a horse: he said, “Go and take that grey horse.”

entries.farsi: گفت آن را من نخواهم گفت چون / ‏گفت او واپس رواست و بس حرون
entries.english: He replied, “I don’t want that one.” “Why not?” he asked. “It goes backward and is very restive,” said he;

entries.farsi: سخت پس پس مى‏رود او سوى بن / ‏گفت دمش را به سوى خانه كن
entries.english: “It goes back, back very hard in the direction of its rump.” He replied, “Turn its tail towards home!”

entries.farsi: دم اين استور نفست شهوت است / ‏زين سبب پس پس رود آن خود پرست
entries.english: The tail of this beast you are riding, your carnal soul, is lust; hence that self-worshipper goes back, back.

entries.farsi: شهوت او را كه دم آمد ز بن / ‏اى مبدل شهوت عقبيش كن
entries.english: O changer, make its lust, which is the tail, to be entirely lust for the world hereafter.

entries.farsi: چون ببندى شهوتش را از رغيف / ‏سر كند آن شهوت از عقل شريف
entries.english: When you bind its lust from the loaf, that lust puts forth its head from noble reason.

entries.farsi: همچو شاخى كه ببرى از درخت / ‏سر كند قوت ز شاخ نيك بخت
entries.english: As, when you lop off a branch from a tree, vigour is imparted to the well-conditioned branches.

entries.farsi: چون كه كردى دم او را آن طرف / ‏گر رود پس پس رود تا مكتنف
entries.english: When you have turned its tail in that direction, if it goes backward, it goes to the place of shelter.

entries.farsi: حبذا اسبان رام پيش رو / نه سپس رو نه حرونى را گرو
entries.english: How excellent are the docile horses which go forward, not backward, and are not given over to restiveness,

entries.farsi: هست هفصد ساله راه آن حقب / ‏كه بكرد او عزم در سيران حب
entries.english: Going hot-foot, like the body of Moses the Kalím, to which to the two seas as the breadth of a blanket!

entries.farsi: گرم رو چون جسم موساى كليم / ‏تا به بحرينش چو پهناى گليم
entries.english: Seven hundred years is the duration of the journey on which he set out in the path of Love, for an age.

entries.farsi: همت سير تنش چون اين بود / سير جانش تا به عليين بود
entries.english: Since the aspiration on his journey in the body is this, his journey in the spirit must be unto the highest Paradise.

entries.farsi: شهسواران در سباقت تاختند / خر بطان در پايگه انداختند
entries.english: The kingly knights sped forward in advance; the boobies unloaded in the stable-yard.

sections.title_fa: ‏مثل
sections.title_en: Parable

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه كاروانى مى‏رسيد / در دهى آمد درى را باز ديد
entries.english: It is like the caravaneers arrived and entered a village and found a certain door open.

entries.farsi: آن يكى گفت اندر اين برد العجوز / تا بيندازيم اينجا چند روز
entries.english: One said, “During this spell of cold weather let us unload here for a few days.”

entries.farsi: بانگ آمد نه بينداز از برون / ‏و آنگهانى اندر آ تو اندرون
entries.english: A voice cried, “Nay, unload outside, and then come indoors!”

entries.farsi: هم برون افكن هر آنچ افكندنى است / ‏در ميا با آن كه اين مجلس سنى است
entries.english: Drop outside everything that ought to be dropped: do not come in with it, for this assembly-place is of high dignity.”

entries.farsi: بد هالل استاد دل جان روشنى / ‏سايس و بنده‏ى امير مومنى
entries.english: Hilal was a spiritual adept and a man of illumined soul, the groom and slave of a Moslem Amír.

entries.farsi: سايسى كردى در آخور آن غالم / ‏ليك سلطان سالطين بنده نام
entries.english: The youth served as a groom in the stable, but a king of kings and a slave in name.

entries.farsi: آن امير از حال بنده بى‏خبر / كه نبودش جز بليسانه نظر
entries.english: The Amír was ignorant of his slave’s condition, for he had no discernment but of the sort possessed by Iblís.

entries.farsi: آب و گل مى‏ديد و در وى گنج نه / ‏پنج و شش مى‏ديد و اصل پنج نه
entries.english: He saw the clay, but not the treasure in it: he saw the five and the six, but not the source of the five.

entries.farsi: رنگ طين پيدا و نور دين نهان / ‏هر پيمبر اين چنين بد در جهان
entries.english: The colour of clay is manifest; the light of religion is hidden: such was every prophet in the world.

entries.farsi: آن مناره ديد و در وى مرغ نى / ‏بر مناره شاه بازى پر فنى
entries.english: One saw the minaret, but not the bird upon it, upon the minaret a fully accomplished royal falcon;

entries.farsi: و آن دوم مى‏ديد مرغى پر زنى / ‏ليك موى اندر دهان مرغ نى
entries.english: And a second saw a bird flapping its wings, but not the hair in the bird’s mouth;

entries.farsi: و آن كه او ينظر بنور اهلل بود / هم ز مرغ و هم ز مو آگاه بود
entries.english: But that one who was seeing by the light of God was aware both of the bird and of the hair,

entries.farsi: آن يكى گل ديد نقشين در وحل / ‏و آن دگر گل ديد پر علم و عمل
entries.english: And said, “Pray, direct your eye towards the hair: till you see the hair, the knot will not be untied.”

entries.farsi: تن مناره علم و طاعت همچو مرغ / ‏خواه سيصد مرغ گير و يا دو مرغ
entries.english: The one saw in the mud figured clay, while the other saw clay replete with knowledge and works.

entries.farsi: گفت آخر چشم سوى موى نه / ‏تا نبينى مو بنگشايد گره
entries.english: The body is the minaret; knowledge and obedience are like the bird: suppose three hundred birds or two birds, whichever you please.

entries.farsi: مرد اوسط مرغ بين است او و بس / ‏غير مرغى مى‏نبيند پيش و پس
entries.english: The middle man sees the bird only: neither before nor behind does he see anything but a bird.

entries.farsi: موى آن نورى است پنهان آن مرغ / ‏كه بد آن پاينده باشد جان مرغ
entries.english: The hair is the hidden light belonging to the bird, whereby the soul of the bird is enduring.

entries.farsi: مرغ كان موى است در منقار او / هيچ عاريت نباشد كار او
entries.english: The works of the bird in whose beak is that hair are never counterfeit.

entries.farsi: علم او از جان او جوشد مدام / ‏پيش او نه مستعار آمد نه وام
entries.english: Its knowledge gushes perpetually from its soul: it has nothing that is borrowed and no debt.

sections.title_fa: رنجور شدن اين هالل و بى‏خبرى خواجه‏ى او از رنجورى او از تحقير و ناشناخت، و واقف شدن دل مصطفى عليه الصالة و السلام از رنجورى و حال او و افتقاد و عيادت رسول عليه السلام اين هالل را
sections.title_en: How this Hilal fell ill, and how his master was unaware of his being ill, because he despised him and did not recognise; and how the heart of Mustafá, on whom be peace, came to know of his illness and his state, and how the Prophet, on whom be peace, inquired after this Hilal and went to see him.

entries.farsi: از قضا رنجور و ناقص شد هالل / ‏مصطفى را وحى شد غماز حال
entries.english: By destiny Hilal became ill and weak: inspiration acquainted Mustafá with his condition.

entries.farsi: بد ز رنجوريش خواجه‏ش بى‏خبر / كه بر او بد كساد و بى‏خطر
entries.english: His master was unaware of his illness, for in his eyes he was worth little and without importance.

entries.farsi: آن كه كس بود و شهنشاه كسان / ‏عقل صد چون قلزمش هر جا رسان
entries.english: A well-doer lay in the stable for nine days, and none took notice of his plight.

entries.farsi: خفته نه روز اندر آخور محسنى / ‏هيچ كس از حال او آگاه نى
entries.english: He who was a personage and the Emperor of personages, he whose oceanic mind reaches every place—

entries.farsi: مصطفى بهر هالل با شرف / ‏رفت از بهر عيادت آن طرف
entries.english: To him came the inspiration: God’s Mercy sympathised, saying, “Such-and-such a one who longs for you is fallen sick.”

entries.farsi: وحيش آمد رحم حق غم خوار شد / كه فالن مشتاق تو بيمار شد
entries.english: Mustafá went thither to pay a visit to the noble Hilal.

entries.farsi: در پى خورشيد وحى آن مه دوان / ‏و آن صحابه در پيش چون اختران
entries.english: The Moon was running behind the Sun of inspiration, while the Companions followed behind him, like the stars.

entries.farsi: ماه مى‏گويد كه اصحابى نجوم / ‏للسرى قدوه و للطاغى رجوم
entries.english: The Moon is saying, “My Companions are stars— a model for the night-journey, and missiles hurled at the disobedient.”

entries.farsi: مير را گفتند كان سلطان رسيد / او ز شادى بى‏دل و جان بر جهيد
entries.english: The Amír was told of the arrival of that Sultan, he sprang up, beside himself with joy;

entries.farsi: بر گمان آن ز شادى زد دو دست / ‏كان شهنشه بهر آن مير آمده ست
entries.english: He clapped his hands joyously, thinking that the Emperor had come on his account.

entries.farsi: پس زمين بوس و سالم آورد او / كرد رخ را از طرب چون ورد او
entries.english: When the Amír came down from the upper chamber, he was ready to lavish his soul on the messenger as a reward.

entries.farsi: چون فرود آمد ز غرفه آن امير / جان همى‏افشاند پا مزد بشير
entries.english: Then he kissed the earth and gave the salaam: in his delight he made his countenance like a rose.

entries.farsi: گفت بسم اللهَّ مشرف كن وطن / ‏تا كه فردوسى شود اين انجمن
entries.english: “In God’s name,” he said, “bestow honour on the house, so that this assembly-place may become a Paradise,

entries.farsi: تا فزايد قصر من بر آسمان / ‏كه بديدم قطب دوران زمان
entries.english: And that my palace may surpass heaven, saying, ‘I have seen the Pole on which Time revolves.’”

entries.farsi: گفتش از بهر عتاب آن محترم / ‏من براى ديدن تو نامدم
entries.english: The venerable Prophet said to him by way of rebuke, “I have not come to visit you.”

entries.farsi: گفت روحم آن تو خود روح چيست / ‏هين بفرما كاين تجشم بهر كيست
entries.english: He replied, “My spirit belongs to you—what, indeed, is my spirit? Oh, say on whose account is this solicitude?—

entries.farsi: تا شوم من خاك پاى آن كسى / ‏كه به باغ لطف تست‏اش مغرسى
entries.english: That I may become dust for the feet of the person who is planted in the orchard of your favour.”

entries.farsi: پس بگفتش كان هالل عرش كو / همچو مهتاب از تواضع فرش كو
entries.english: Then he said to him, “Where is that New-moon of the highest heaven? Where is he that in his humility is spread as moonbeams?—

entries.farsi: آن شهى در بندگى پنهان شده / ‏بهر جاسوسى به دنيا آمده
entries.english: That king who is disguised as a slave and has come to this world for the purpose of spying?

entries.farsi: تو مگو كاو بنده و آخورچى ماست / ‏اين بدان كه گنج در ويرانه‏هاست
entries.english: Do not say, ‘He is my slave and stableman’: know this, that he is a treasure in ruins.

entries.farsi: اى عجب چون است از سقم آن هالل / ‏كه هزاران بدر هستش پاى مال
entries.english: Oh, I wonder to what state he has been reduced by sickness— that New moon by which thousands of full-moons are trodden underfoot.”

entries.farsi: صحبت او با ستور و استر است / ‏سايس است و منزلش اين آخور است
entries.english: He said, “I have no knowledge of his illness, but he has not been at the palace-gate for several days.

entries.farsi: گفت از رنجش مرا آگاه نيست / ‏ليك روز چند بر درگاه نيست
entries.english: He keeps company with the horses and mules: he is a groom, and this stable is his dwelling-place.”

sections.title_fa: در آمدن مصطفى عليه السلام از بهر عيادت هالل در ستورگاه آن امير و نواختن مصطفى هالل را
sections.title_en: How Mustafá, on whom be peace, came into the Amír’s stable to see the sick Hilal, and how he caressed Hilal, may God be pleased with him!

entries.farsi: بود آخور مظلم و زشت و پليد / وين همه برخاست چون الفت رسيد
entries.english: The Prophet went eagerly into the stable to look for him and began to search.

entries.farsi: رفت پيغمبر به رغبت بهر او / اندر آخور و آمد اندر جستجو
entries.english: The stable was dark, foul, and dirty; all this vanished when friendship arrived.

entries.farsi: بوى پيغمبر ببرد آن شير نر / همچنان كه بوى يوسف را پدر
entries.english: That fierce lion scented the Prophet just as the scent of Joseph was perceived by his father.

entries.farsi: موجب ايمان نباشد معجزات / ‏بوى جنسيت كند جذب صفات
entries.english: Miracles are not the cause of religious faith; it is the scent of homogeneity that attracts qualities.

entries.farsi: معجزات از بهر قهر دشمن است / ‏بوى جنسيت پى دل بردن است
entries.english: Miracles are for the purpose of subjugating the foe: the scent of homogeneity is for the winning of hearts.

entries.farsi: قهر گردد دشمن اما دوست نى / ‏دوست كى گردد ببسته گردنى
entries.english: A foe is subjugated, but not a friend: how should a friend have his neck bound?

entries.farsi: اندر آمد او ز خواب از بوى او / گفت سرگين دان درون زين گونه بو
entries.english: He was awakened from sleep by his scent: he said, “A stable full of dung, and this kind of scent within it!”

entries.farsi: از ميان پاى استوران بديد / دامن پاك رسول بى‏نديد
entries.english: Through the legs of the riding-beasts he saw the holy skirt of the peerless Prophet,

entries.farsi: پس ز كنج آخور آمد غژغژان / ‏روى بر پايش نهاد آن پهلوان
entries.english: And that hero came creeping out of a corner in the stable and laid his face upon his feet.

entries.farsi: پس پيمبر روى بر رويش نهاد / بر سر و بر چشم و رويش بوسه داد
entries.english: Then the Prophet laid his face against his face and kissed his head and eyes and cheeks.

entries.farsi: گفت يا ربا چه پنهان گوهرى / ‏اى غريب عرش چونى خوشترى
entries.english: “O Lord,” he cried, “what a hidden pearl you are! How are you, O heavenly stranger? Are you better?”

entries.farsi: گفت چون باشد خود آن شوريده خواب / ‏كه در آيد در دهانش آفتاب
entries.english: He said, “One whose sleep was disturbed, how for truth is he when the Sun comes into his mouth?

entries.farsi: چون بود آن تشنه‏اى كاو گل چرد / آب بر سر بنهدش خوش مى‏برد
entries.english: The thirsty man who eats clay, how is he the Water lays him on its head and bears him happily along?

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه مصطفى صلى اللهَّ عليه و آله شنيد كه عيسى عليه السلام بر روى آب رفت فرمود لو ازداد يقينه لمشى على الهواء
sections.title_en: Explanation of, that Mustafá, on whom be peace, hearing that Jesus, on whom be peace, walked
on the water, said, ‘If his faith had increased, he would have walked on the air.’

entries.farsi: همچو عيسى بر سرش گيرد فرات / ‏كايمنى از غرقه در آب حيات
entries.english: How is he whom the vast river takes on its head, like Jesus, saying, ‘In the Water of Life you are safe from drowning’?”

entries.farsi: گويد احمد گر يقينش افزون بدى / ‏خود هوايش مركب و مأمون بدى
entries.english: Ahmad says, “Had faith been greater, even the air would have carried him safely,

entries.farsi: همچو من كه بر هوا راكب شدم / ‏در شب معراج مستصحب شدم
entries.english: Like me, who rode upon the air on the night of the Ascension and sought communion.”
