entries.farsi: ليك داند هر كه او را منظر است / ‏كه فغان اين سرى هم ز آن سر است
entries.english: One mouth is wailing unto you: it lets a shrill note fall on the air;

entries.farsi: يك دهان نالان شده سوى شما / هاى و هويى در فكنده در هوا
entries.english: But everyone who has insight knows that the lamentation at this end is from that end.

entries.farsi: دمدمه‏ى اين ناى از دمهاى اوست / ‏هاى و هوى روح از هيهاى اوست
entries.english: The noise of this reed is from his breaths: the spirit’s outcry is from his outcry.

entries.farsi: با كه خفتى و ز چه پهلو خاستى / ‏كاين چنين پر جوش چون درياستى
entries.english: If the reed had no converse with his lip, the reed would not fill the world with sugar.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى با لبش نى را سمر / نى جهان را پر نكردى از شكر
entries.english: With whom have you slept and from what side have you risen, that you are so full of agitation, like the sea?

entries.farsi: يا ابيت عند ربى خوانده‏اى / ‏در دل درياى آتش رانده‏اى
entries.english: Or have you recited, “I pass the night with my Lord,” and plunged into the heart of the sea of fire?

entries.farsi: نعره‏ى يا نار كونى باردا / عصمت جان تو گشت اى مقتدا
entries.english: The shout, “O fire, be cool,” became a protection to your spirit, O exemplar.

entries.farsi: اى ضياء الحق حسام دين و دل / ‏كى توان اندود خورشيدى به گل
entries.english: O Radiance of God, Husam religious and spiritual, how can a sun be daubed over with clay?

entries.farsi: در دل كه لعلها دالل تست / ‏باغها از خنده مالامال تست
entries.english: These lumps of clay attempted to cover up your sun.

entries.farsi: قصد كردستند اين گل پاره‏ها / كه بپوشانند خورشيد ترا
entries.english: The rubies in the mountain’s heart are your brokers; the orchards in laughter are filled to the brim with you.

entries.farsi: محرم مرديت را كو رستمى / ‏تا ز صد خرمن يكى جو گفتمى
entries.english: For one familiar with your manhood, where is a Rustam that I might tell a single barley-corn out of hundred stacks?

entries.farsi: چون بخواهم كز سرت آهى كنم / ‏چون على سر را فرو چاهى كنم
entries.english: When I wish to sigh forth your secret, like Alí I put my head down into a well.

entries.farsi: مست گشتم خويش بر غوغا زنم / ‏چه چه باشد خيمه بر صحرا زنم
entries.english: Since his brothers have vindictive hearts, the bottom of the well is best for my Joseph.

entries.farsi: چون كه اخوان را دل كينه‏ور است / ‏يوسفم را قعر چاه اوليتر است
entries.english: I have become intoxicated; I will set about making a row: what of the well? I will pitch my tent in the open plain.

entries.farsi: بر كف من نه شراب آتشين / ‏وانگه آن كر و فر مستانه بين
entries.english: Put the fiery wine in my hand, and then behold the pomp and glory that is enjoyed by the drunken!

entries.farsi: منتظر گو باش بى‏گنج آن فقير / ز انكه ما غرقيم اين دم در عصير
entries.english: Bid the fakir wait without the treasure, for at this moment we are drowned in the syrup.

entries.farsi: از خدا خواه اى فقير اين دم پناه / ‏از من غرقه شده يارى مخواه
entries.english: Now, O fakir, seek refuge with God: do not seek help from me who am drowned;

entries.farsi: كه مرا پرواى آن اسناد نيست / ‏از خود و از ريش خويشم ياد نيست
entries.english: For I have no concern with lending support: I have no recollection of myself and my own beard.

entries.farsi: باد سبلت كى بگنجد و آب رو / در شرابى كه نگنجد تار مو
entries.english: How should there be room for wind of the moustache and water of the face in the wine in which there is no room for a single hair?

entries.farsi: در ده اى ساقى يكى رطلى گران / ‏خواجه را از ريش و سبلت وارهان
entries.english: Hand a heavy goblet, O cup-bearer: deliver the Khwaja from his beard and moustache.

entries.farsi: نخوتش بر ما سبالى مى‏زند / ليك ريش از رشك ما بر مى‏كند
entries.english: His arrogance is curling a moustache at us, but he is tearing out his beard in envy of us.

entries.farsi: مات او و مات او و مات او / كه همى‏دانيم تزويرات او
entries.english: He is mated by Him, mated by Him, mated by Him, for we are acquainted with his impostures.

entries.farsi: از پس صد سال آنچ آيد از او / پير مى‏بيند معين مو به مو
entries.english: The Pír is seeing distinctly, hair by hair, what will become of him after a hundred years.

entries.farsi: اندر آيينه چه بيند مرد عام / ‏كه نبيند پير اندر خشت خام
entries.english: What does the common man see in the mirror that the Pír does not see in the crude brick?

entries.farsi: آن چه لحيانى به خانه‏ى خود نديد / هست بر كوسه يكايك آن پديد
entries.english: That which the bushy-bearded man never saw in his own house is apparent at once to him who has but a few hairs on his chin.

entries.farsi: رو به دريايى كه ماهى زاده‏اى / همچو خس در ريش چون افتاده‏اى
entries.english: Go to the Sea of whose fish you are born: how have you fallen, like rubbish, into the beard?

entries.farsi: خس نه‏اى دور از تو رشك گوهرى / ‏در ميان موج و بحر اوليترى
entries.english: You are not rubbish—far be it from you! You are an object of envy to the pearl: you have the best right amidst the waves and the sea.

entries.farsi: بحر وحدان است جفت و زوج نيست / ‏گوهر و ماهيش غير موج نيست
entries.english: It is the Sea of Unity: there is no fellow or consort: its pearls and fishes are not other than its waves.

entries.farsi: اى محال و اى محال اشراك او / دور از آن دريا و موج پاك او
entries.english: Oh, absurd, absurd to make anything its partner. Far be it from that Sea and its pure waves!

entries.farsi: نيست اندر بحر شرك و پيچ پيچ / ‏ليك با احول چه گويم هيچ هيچ
entries.english: In the Sea there is no partnership or perplexity; but what can I say to him that sees double? Nothing, nothing.

entries.farsi: چون كه جفت احوالنيم اى شمن / ‏الزم آيد مشركانه دم زدن
entries.english: Since we are the mates of those who see double, O idolater, it is necessary to speak in the fashion of him who attributes a partner.

entries.farsi: آن يكيى ز آن سوى وصف است و حال / ‏جز دويى نآيد به ميدان مقال
entries.english: That Unity is beyond description and condition: nothing comes into the arena of speech except duality.

entries.farsi: يا چو احول اين دويى را نوش كن / ‏يا دهان بر دوز و خوش خاموش كن
entries.english: Either, like the double-seeing man, drink in this duality, or close your mouth and be very silent;

entries.farsi: يا به نوبت گه سكوت وگه كالم / ‏احوالنه طبل مى‏زن و السلام
entries.english: Or in turns, now silence now speech: beat the drum like him that sees double, and peace!

entries.farsi: چون ببينى محرمى گو سر جان / ‏گل ببينى نعره زن چون بلبالن
entries.english: When you see a confidant, declare the mystery of the Spirit: you see the rose, sing loud like nightingales.

entries.farsi: چون ببينى مشك پر مكر و مجاز / لب ببند و خويشتن را خنب ساز
entries.english: When you see a water-skin full of deceit and falsehood, shut your lips and make yourself like a jar;

entries.farsi: دشمن آب است پيش او مجنب / ‏ور نه سنگ جهل او بشكست خنب
entries.english: He is an enemy to the water: in his presence do not move, else the stone of his ignorance breaks the jar.

entries.farsi: با سياستهاى جاهل صبر كن / خوش مدارا كن به عقل من لدن
entries.english: Patiently endure the punishments inflicted by the ignorant man: give him fair words and dissemble with the reason that is divinely inspired.

entries.farsi: صبر با نااهل اهالن را جالست / ‏صبر صافى مى‏كند هر جا دلى است
entries.english: Patience to the unworthy is the means of polishing the worthy: wherever a heart exists, patience purifies it.

entries.farsi: آتش نمرود ابراهيم را / صفوت آيينه آمد در جال
entries.english: The fire of Nimrod was the means of making pure the mirror of Abraham in polishing.

entries.farsi: جور كفر نوحيان و صبر نوح / ‏نوح را شد صيقل مرآت روح
entries.english: The iniquitous unbelief of Noah’s people and the patience of Noah were instrumental in polishing the mirror of Noah’s spirit.

sections.title_fa: ‏حكايت مريد شيخ حسن خرقانى قدس اللهَّ سره
sections.title_en: Story of the disciple of Shaykh Hasan Kharraqání, may God sanctify his spirit!

entries.farsi: رفت درويشى ز شهر طالقان / ‏بهر صيت بو الحسن تا خارقان
entries.english: A dervish went from the town of Talaqan because of the fame of Abu ’l- Husayn of Kharaqan.

entries.farsi: كوهها ببريد و وادى دراز / بهر ديد شيخ با صدق و نياز
entries.english: He traversed the mountains and the long valley to visit the Shaykh who was endowed with sincerity and fervent supplication.

entries.farsi: آن چه در ره ديد از رنج و ستم / ‏گر چه در خورد است كوته مى‏كنم
entries.english: Although the afflictions and injuries which he suffered on the road are deserving, I will abridge.

entries.farsi: چون به مقصد آمد از ره آن جوان / ‏خانه‏ى آن شاه را جست او نشان
entries.english: When the young man reached the end of his journey, he asked to be directed to the house of that king.

entries.farsi: چون به صد حرمت بزد حلقه‏ى درش / ‏زن برون كرد از در خانه سرش
entries.english: As soon as he knocked at his door with a hundred reverences, the wife put forth her head from the door of the house,

entries.farsi: كه چه مى‏خواهى بگو اى ذو الكرم / ‏گفت بر قصد زيارت آمدم
entries.english: Saying, “What do you want? Tell, kind sir.” He replied, “I have come with the intention of paying a visit.”

entries.farsi: خنده‏اى زد زن كه خه خه ريش بين / ‏اين سفر گيرى و اين تشويش بين
entries.english: The wife gave a laugh. “Ha, ha,” she exclaimed, “look at your beard, look at this undertaking of a journey and this trouble!

entries.farsi: خود ترا كارى نبود آن جايگاه / ‏كه به بى‏هوده كنى اين عزم راه
entries.english: Was there nothing for you to do in the place that you should idly set out upon this expedition?

entries.farsi: اشتهاى گول گردى آمدت / ‏يا ملولى وطن غالب شدت
entries.english: Did you feel a craving to indulge in foolish sight-seeing, or were you overcome by disgust with your home?

entries.farsi: يا مگر ديوت دو شاخه بر نهاد / بر تو وسواس سفر را در گشاد
entries.english: Or, perchance, the Devil laid on you a two-forked barnacle and let loose upon you the temptation to travel.”

entries.farsi: گفت نافرجام و فحش و دمدمه / ‏من نتانم باز گفتن آن همه
entries.english: She uttered unseemly and foul and silly words: I cannot relate all of them.

entries.farsi: از مثل و ز ريش‏خند بى‏حساب / ‏آن مريد افتاد از غم در نشيب
entries.english: The disciple was thrown into a painful state of dejection by her parables and countless mockeries.

sections.title_fa: ‏پرسيدن آن وارد از حرم شيخ كه شيخ كجاست كجا جويم و جواب نافرجام گفتن حرم
sections.title_en: How the new-comer asked the Shaykh’s wife, “Where is the Shaykh? Where shall I look for him?” and the rude answer given by the Shaykh’s wife.

entries.farsi: اشكش از ديده بجست و گفت او / با همه آن شاه شيرين نام كو
entries.english: Tears burst from his eyes, and he said, “Nevertheless, where is that king of sweet name?”

entries.farsi: گفت آن سالوس زراق تهى / ‏دام گوالن و كمند گمرهى
entries.english: She replied, “That vain hypocritical impostor, a trap for fools and a noose for error—

entries.farsi: صد هزاران خام ريشان همچو تو / اوفتاده از وى اندر صد عتو
entries.english: Hundreds of thousands of callow simpletons like you have fallen, through him, into a hundred types of rebelliousness.

entries.farsi: گر نبينيش و سالمت وا روى / ‏خير تو باشد نگردى زو غوى
entries.english: If you should not see him and return in safety, it will be good for you: you will not be led astray by him.

entries.farsi: الف‏كيشى كاسه ليسى طبل‏خوار / بانگ طبلش رفته اطراف ديار
entries.english: A braggart, a lick-platter, a parasite: the noise of his drum has reached the remotest parts of the world.

entries.farsi: سبطى‏اند اين قوم و گوساله پرست / ‏در چنين گاوى چه مى‏مالند دست
entries.english: These folk are Israelites and worshippers of the calf: why do they fondle such a cow?

entries.farsi: جيفه الليل است و بطال النهار / هر كه او شد غره‏ى اين طبل‏خوار
entries.english: Anyone who is duped by this parasite is a carcass by night and a good-for nothing by day.

entries.farsi: هشته‏اند اين قوم صد علم و كمال / ‏مكر و تزويرى گرفته كينست حال
entries.english: These folk have abandoned a hundred kinds of knowledge and perfection and have embraced a deceit and imposture, saying, ‘This is ecstasy.’

entries.farsi: آل موسى كو دريغا تا كنون / ‏عابدان عجل را ريزند خون
entries.english: Alas, where are the family of Moses that now they might shed the blood of the calf-worshippers

entries.farsi: شرع و تقوى را فگنده سوى پشت / ‏كو عمر كو امر معروفى درشت
entries.english: Who have cast religion and piety behind their backs? Where is ‘Umar? Where is a stern command to act righteously?

entries.farsi: كاين اباحت زين جماعت فاش شد / رخصت هر مفسد قالش شد
entries.english: For the licence practised by these people has become notorious: it is an indulgence enjoyed by every scoundrel evil-doer.

entries.farsi: كو ره پيغمبر و اصحاب او / كو نماز و سبحه و آداب او
entries.english: Where is the Way of the Prophet and his Companions? Where are his ritual prayer and rosary and observances?”

sections.title_fa: ‏جواب گفتن مريد و زجر كردن مريد آن طعانه را از كفر و بى‏هوده گفتن
sections.title_en: How the disciple answered that railing woman and bade her refrain from her unbelief and idle talk.

entries.farsi: بانگ زد بر وى جوان و گفت بس / ‏روز روشن از كجا آمد عسس
entries.english: The youth cried out at her and said, “Enough! In bright daylight where did the night-patrol come from?

entries.farsi: نور مردان مشرق و مغرب گرفت / ‏آسمانها سجده كردند از شگفت
entries.english: The splendour of the men has overspread the East and the West: the heavens have bowed low in amazement.

entries.farsi: آفتاب حق بر آمد از حمل / ‏زير چادر رفت خورشيد از خجل
entries.english: The Sun of God has risen from the Ram: the sun has gone, shamefaced, under the veil.

entries.farsi: ترهات چون تو ابليسى مرا / كى بگرداند ز خاك اين سرا
entries.english: How should the nonsense of a devil like you turn me back from the dust of this abode?

entries.farsi: من به بادى نامدم همچون سحاب / ‏تا به گردى باز گردم زين جناب
entries.english: I have not come by a wind like a cloud, that I should be turned back from this presence by a dust.

entries.farsi: عجل با آن نور شد قبله‏ى كرم / ‏قبله بى‏آن نور شد كفر و صنم
entries.english: By virtue of that Light the calf becomes a qibla of grace; without that Light the qibla becomes infidelity and an idol.

entries.farsi: هست اباحت كز هوا آمد ضالل / ‏هست اباحت كز خدا آمد كمال
entries.english: The licence that comes from self-will is error; the licence that comes from God is perfection.

entries.farsi: كفر ايمان گشت و ديو اسالم يافت / ‏آن طرف كآن نور بى‏اندازه تافت
entries.english: In that quarter where the illimitable Light has shone, infidelity has become faith and the Devil has attained unto Islam.

entries.farsi: سجده آدم را بيان سبق اوست / ‏سجده آرد مغز را پيوست پوست
entries.english: He is a theatre for the manifestation of the Glory, and he is the real beloved: he has carried off the prize from all the Cherubim.

entries.farsi: مظهر عز است و محبوب بحق / ‏از همه كروبيان برده سبق
entries.english: The worship of Adam is clear evidence of his superiority: the husk always bows down to the kernel.

entries.farsi: شمع حق را پف كنى تو اى عجوز / هم تو سوزى هم سرت اى گنده پوز
entries.english: O old woman, you puff God’s candle, you will be burnt, you and your head at the same time, O foul-mouthed one.

entries.farsi: كى شود دريا ز پوز سگ نجس / ‏كى شود خورشيد از پف منطمس
entries.english: How should the sea be defiled by a dog’s muzzle? How should the sun be extinguished by a puff?

entries.farsi: حكم بر ظاهر اگر هم مى‏كنى / ‏چيست ظاهرتر بگو زين روشنى
entries.english: Even if you judge by appearances, tell, what is more apparent than this Light?

entries.farsi: جمله ظاهرها به پيش اين ظهور / باشد اندر غايت نقص و قصور
entries.english: In comparison with this appearance all apparent things are in the utmost degree of imperfection and default.

entries.farsi: هر كه بر شمع خدا آرد پفو / شمع كى ميرد بسوزد پوز او
entries.english: If anyone puffs at God’s candle, how should the candle be extinguished? His jaws and nose will be burnt.

entries.farsi: چون تو خفاشان بسى بينند خواب / ‏كاين جهان ماند يتيم از آفتاب
entries.english: Bats like you often dream that this world will be left orphaned of the Sun.

entries.farsi: موجهاى تيز درياهاى روح / ‏هست صد چندان كه بد طوفان نوح
entries.english: The fierce waves of the seas of the Spirit are a hundred times as many as was the Flood of Noah;

entries.farsi: ليك اندر چشم كنعان موى رست / نوح و كشتى را بهشت و كوه جست
entries.english: But hair grew in the eye of Canaan: he forsook Noah and the Ark and sought the mountain.

entries.farsi: كوه و كنعان را فرو برد آن زمان / نيم موجى تا به قعر امتهان
entries.english: Then half a wave swept the mountain and Canaan down into the abyss of dishonour.

entries.farsi: شب روان و همرهان مه به تگ / ‏ترك رفتن كى كنند از بانگ سگ
entries.english: The moon scatters her light and the dog bays: how should the dog feed on the light of the moon?

entries.farsi: مه فشاند نور و سگ وع وع كند / سگ ز نور ماه كى مرتع كند
entries.english: Those who travel by night and move swiftly with the moon on her way, how should they relinquish their journey because of the dog’s yelping?

entries.farsi: جزو سوى كل دوان مانند تير / كى كند وقف از پى هر گنده پير
entries.english: The part is speeding like an arrow towards the Whole: how should it stop on account of any old hag?

entries.farsi: جان شرع و جان تقوى عارف است / معرفت محصول زهد سالف است
entries.english: The gnostic is the soul of religion and the soul of piety: gnosis is the result of past asceticism.

entries.farsi: زهد اندر كاشتن كوشيدن است / ‏معرفت آن كشت را روييدن است
entries.english: Asceticism is the labour of sowing; gnosis is the growth of the seed.

entries.farsi: پس چو تن باشد جهاد و اعتقاد / جان اين كشتن نبات است و حصاد
entries.english: Therefore the hard struggle and his firm religious conviction are like the body, the soul of this sowing is the growth and its harvesting.

entries.farsi: امر معروف او و هم معروف اوست / ‏كاشف اسرار و هم مكشوف اوست
entries.english: He is both the command to do right and the right; he is both the revealer of mysteries and that which is revealed.

entries.farsi: شاه امروزينه و فرداى ماست / ‏پوست بنده‏ى مغز نغزش دايماست
entries.english: He is our king to-day and to-morrow: the husk is forever a slave to his goodly kernel,

entries.farsi: چون انا الحق گفت شيخ و پيش برد / پس گلوى جمله كوران را فشرد
entries.english: When the Shaykh said ‘I am God’ and carried it through, he throttled all the blind.

entries.farsi: چون اناى بنده ال شد از وجود / پس چه ماند تو بينديش اى جحود
entries.english: When a man’s ‘I’ is negated from existence, then what remains? Consider O denier.

entries.farsi: گر ترا چشمى است بگشا درنگر / بعد ال آخر چه مى‏ماند دگر
entries.english: If you have an eye, open it and look! After ‘not,’ why, what else remains?

entries.farsi: اى بريده آن لب و حلق و دهان / ‏كه كند تف سوى مه يا آسمان
entries.english: Oh, the lips and throat and mouth cut off that spit at the moon or the sky!

entries.farsi: تف به رويش باز گردد بى‏شكى / ‏تف سوى گردون نيابد مسلكى
entries.english: Without any doubt his spittle will recoil upon his face: spittle can find no path to heaven.

entries.farsi: تا قيامت تف بر او بارد ز رب / ‏همچو تبت بر روان بو لهب
entries.english: Spittle from the Lord rains upon him till the Resurrection, just as rains (tabbat) upon the spirit of Bu Lahab.

entries.farsi: طبل و رايت هست ملك شهريار / سگ كسى كه خواند او را طبل‏خوار
entries.english: Drum (tabl) and banner are the possession of the king: anyone who calls him a parasite (tabl-khwar) is a cur.

entries.farsi: آسمانها بنده‏ى ماه وى‏اند / شرق و مغرب جمله نان خواه وى‏اند
entries.english: The heavens are a slave to his moon: the whole East and West is begging him for bread;

entries.farsi: ز انكه لوالك است بر توقيع او / جمله در انعام و در توزيع او
entries.english: For lawláka is on his sign-manual: all are in his bounty and distribution.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى او نيابيدى فلك / ‏گردش و نور و مكانى ملك
entries.english: If he did not exist, Heaven would not have gained circling motion and light and being the abode of the angels;

entries.farsi: گر نبودى او نيابيدى بحار / هيبت و ماهى و در شاهوار
entries.english: If he did not exist, the seas would not have gained the awe and fish and regal pearls;

entries.farsi: گر نبودى او نيابيدى زمين / ‏در درونه گنج و بيرون ياسمين
entries.english: If he did not exist, the earth would not have gained treasure within and jasmine without.

entries.farsi: رزقها هم رزق خواران وى‏اند / ميوه‏ها لب خشك باران وى‏اند
entries.english: Our means of sustenance are eating the means of sustenance bestowed by him: the fruits are dry-lipped for his rain.

entries.farsi: هين كه معكوس است در امر اين گره / ‏صدقه بخش خويش را صدقه بده
entries.english: Take heed, for in the command this knot is upside down. Give alms to him who gives alms to you.

entries.farsi: از فقير استت همه زر و حرير / هين غنى را ده زكاتى اى فقير
entries.english: All gold and silk comes to you from the poor man: listen, give an alms to the rich man, O you who are poor.

entries.farsi: چون تو ننگى جفت آن مقبول روح / ‏چون عيال كافر اندر عقد نوح
entries.english: A disgrace like you, married to that man whose spirit is accepted, resembles the unbelieving wedded wife of Noah.

entries.farsi: دادمى آن نوح را از تو خالص / ‏تا مشرف گشتمى من در قصاص
entries.english: Were it not for your relationship to this house, I would tear you to pieces at this moment.

entries.farsi: گر نبودى نسبت تو زين سرا / پاره پاره كردمى اين دم ترا
entries.english: I would deliver that Noah from you, in order that I might be ennobled in retaliation.

entries.farsi: ليك با خانه‏ى شهنشاه زمن / ‏اين چنين گستاخيى نآيد ز من
entries.english: But such disrespect to the house of the emperor of the world cannot be shown by me.

entries.farsi: رو دعا كن كه سگ اين موطنى / ‏ور نه اكنون كردمى من كردنى
entries.english: Go and thank God that you are the dog of this dwelling-place, otherwise I would do now what ought to be done.”

sections.title_fa: ‏واگشتن مريد از وثاق شيخ و پرسيدن از مردم و نشان دادن ايشان كه شيخ به فالن بيشه رفته است
sections.title_en: How the disciple turned back from the Shaykh’s house and questioned the people, and how they directed him, saying, “The Shaykh has gone to such and such a forest.”

entries.farsi: بعد از آن پرسان شد او از هر كسى / ‏شيخ را مى‏جست از هر سو بسى
entries.english: Afterwards he began to inquire of every one and sought the Shaykh for a long while in every quarter.

entries.farsi: پس كسى گفتش كه آن قطب ديار / رفت تا هيزم كشد از كوهسار
entries.english: Then somebody said to him, “That Qutb of the world has gone to fetch fire wood from the hilly country.”

entries.farsi: آن مريد ذو الفقار انديش تفت / ‏در هواى شيخ سوى بيشه رفت
entries.english: The disciple, whose thoughts were Dhu ’l-faqár, ran quickly to the forest in eager desire for the Shaykh.

entries.farsi: ديو مى‏آورد پيش هوش مرد / وسوسه‏ى تا خفيه گردد مه ز گرد
entries.english: The Devil was introducing to the man’s mind an evil suggestion, in order that the Moon might be concealed by dust,

entries.farsi: كاين چنين زن را چرا اين شيخ دين / ‏دارد اندر خانه يار و همنشين
entries.english: Namely, “Why should this Shaykh of the religion keep in his house a woman like this as his mate and companion?

entries.farsi: باز او الحول مى‏كرد آتشين / ‏كاعتراض من بر او كفر است و كين
entries.english: Whence familiarity between opposite and opposite? Whence a nasnas (anthropoid ape) is with the Imam of mankind?”

entries.farsi: ضد را با ضد ايناس از كجا / با امام الناس نسناس از كجا
entries.english: Then again he was exclaiming fervidly, “God help me! My impugning him is infidelity and enmity.

entries.farsi: من كه باشم با تصرفهاى حق / ‏كه بر آرد نفس من اشكال و دق
entries.english: Who am I, in view of God’s exercising control, that my carnal soul should raise difficulties and objections?”

entries.farsi: باز نفسش حمله مى‏آورد زود / زين تعرف در دلش چون كاه دود
entries.english: But soon his carnal soul was returning to the attack— in consequence of this acquaintance smoke in his straw-like heart—

entries.farsi: كه چه نسبت ديو را با جبرئيل / ‏كه بود با او به صحبت هم مقيل
entries.english: Saying, “What affinity has the Devil with Gabriel, that she should be his bedfellow in intercourse?

entries.farsi: چون تواند ساخت با آزر خليل / ‏چون تواند ساخت با ره زن دليل
entries.english: How can Khalíl agree with Ázar? How can a guide agree with a brigand?”

sections.title_fa: ‏يافتن مريد مراد را و مالقات او با شيخ نزديك آن بيشه
sections.title_en: How the disciple gained his wish and met the Shaykh near the forest.

entries.farsi: اندر اين بود او كه شيخ نامدار / زود پيش افتاد بر شيرى سوار
entries.english: He was in this when suddenly the renowned Shaykh appeared before him, riding on a lion.

entries.farsi: تازيانه‏ش مار نر بود از شرف / ‏مار را بگرفته چون خرزن به كف
entries.english: The roaring lion carried his faggots, while that blessed one sat on the top of them.

entries.farsi: شير غران هيزمش را مى‏كشيد / بر سر هيزم نشسته آن سعيد
entries.english: Because of the honour his whip was a fierce serpent: he had grasped the serpent in his hand, like an ass-goad.

entries.farsi: تو يقين مى‏دان كه هر شيخى كه هست / ‏هم سوارى مى‏كند بر شير مست
entries.english: Know for certain that likewise every Shaykh that exists is riding on a furious lion.

entries.farsi: گر چه آن محسوس و اين محسوس نيست / ‏ليك آن بر چشم جان ملبوس نيست
entries.english: Although that and this are not perceived by the senses, yet it is not concealed from the spiritual eye.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران شير زير را نشان / ‏پيش ديده‏ى غيب دان هيزم كشان
entries.english: Under their thighs a hundred thousand lions carrying faggots are before the eye that knows the Unseen;

entries.farsi: ليك يك يك را خدا محسوس كرد / تا كه بيند نيز او كه نيست مرد
entries.english: But God has made them visible singly, in order that even he who is not a man may behold them.

entries.farsi: ديدش از دور و بخنديد آن خديو / گفت آن را مشنو اى مفتون ز ديو
entries.english: That prince saw him from afar and laughed and said, “O you who are tempted, do not listen to it from the Devil.”

entries.farsi: از ضمير او بدانست آن جليل / ‏هم ز نور دل بلى نعم الدليل
entries.english: The venerable knew his secret thought by the light of the heart: yea, it is an excellent guide.

entries.farsi: خواند بر وى يك به يك آن ذو فنون / ‏آن چه در ره رفت بر وى تا كنون
entries.english: The master of mystical sciences recited to him in detail all that had befallen him on his journey until now.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن در مشكل انكار زن / ‏بر گشاد آن خوش سراينده دهن
entries.english: Afterwards that man of sweet discourse opened his mouth on the difficult matter of his wife’s disbelief,

entries.farsi: كآن تحمل از هواى نفس نيست / ‏آن خيال نفس تست آن جا مه‏ايست
entries.english: Saying, “My long-suffering is not from sensual desire; that is a vain fancy of your carnal soul: do not take that standpoint.

entries.farsi: گرنه صبرم مى‏كشيدى بار زن / ‏كى كشيدى شير نر بيگار من
entries.english: Unless my patience had endured the burden of my wife, how should the fierce lion have endured the labour of me?

entries.farsi: اشتران بختيم اندر سبق / ‏مست و بى‏خود زير محملهاى حق
entries.english: I am Bactrian camels, in advance, intoxicated and beside myself under the panniers of God.

entries.farsi: من نيم در امر و فرمان نيم خام / ‏تا بينديشم من از تشنيع عام
entries.english: I am not half-raw in the order and command, that I should take any thought of revilement by the public.

entries.farsi: عام ما و خاص ما فرمان اوست / ‏جان ما بر رو دوان جويان اوست
entries.english: My public and my private is His command: my spirit is running on its face in search of Him.

entries.farsi: فردى ما جفتى ما نه از هواست / ‏جان ما چون مهره در دست خداست
entries.english: My being single or wedded is not on account of sensual desire: my spirit is like a die in the hand of God.

entries.farsi: ناز آن ابله كشيم و صد چو او / نه ز عشق رنگ و نه سوداى بو
entries.english: I endure the disdain of that foolish and a hundred like her, neither from love of colour nor passion for scent.

entries.farsi: اين قدر خود درس شاگردان ماست / ‏كر و فر ملحمه‏ى ما تا كجاست
entries.english: This much, indeed, is the lesson learned by my disciples; unto what place the forward and backward movement of my battle!

entries.farsi: تا كجا آن جا كه جا را راه نيست / ‏جز سنا برق مه اهلل نيست
entries.english: Unto what place? Unto the place where Place finds no admittance, and where nothing exists save the lightning-flash of the Moon of Allah.

entries.farsi: از همه اوهام و تصويرات دور / نور نور نور نور نور نور
entries.english: Far beyond all conceptions and imaginations, the Light of light of light of light of light of light.”

entries.farsi: بهر تو گر پست كردم گفت و گو / تا بسازى با رفيق زشت خو
entries.english: If I have made my discourse low for your sake, in order that you may put up with an ill-natured companion,

entries.farsi: تا كشى خندان و خوش بار حرج / ‏از پى الصبر مفتاح الفرج
entries.english: And smilingly and cheerfully bear the burden of distress, because patience is the key to relief from pain.

entries.farsi: كانبيا رنج خسان بس ديده‏اند / از چنين ماران بسى پيچيده‏اند
entries.english: When you put up with the vileness of these vile folk you will attain unto the light of the sunnas;

entries.farsi: چون بسازى با خسى اين خسان / ‏گردى اندر نور سنتها رسان
entries.english: For the prophets have often suffered affliction from the vile: often have they writhed in anguish on account of such snakes.

entries.farsi: بى‏ز ضدى ضد را نتوان نمود / و آن شه بى‏مثل را ضدى نبود
entries.english: Since in eternity it was the will and decree of God, the Forgiver, to reveal and manifest Himself,

entries.farsi: چون مراد و حكم يزدان غفور / بود در قدمت تجلى و ظهور
entries.english: Nothing can be shown without a contrary; and there was no contrary to that incomparable King.

sections.title_fa: ًحكمت در إِنِّي جاعِ لٌ فِي الأَْرْضِ خَلِيفَة
sections.title_en: The purpose in, “Lo, I will place a viceroy in the earth.”

entries.farsi: پس خليفه ساخت صاحب سينه‏اى / ‏تا بود شاهيش را آيينه‏اى
entries.english: Therefore He made a viceroy, one having a heart; to the end that he might be a mirror for His sovereignty;

entries.farsi: پس صفاى بى‏حدودش داد او / وانگه از ظلمت ضدش بنهاد او
entries.english: So He endowed him with infinite purity, and then set up against him a contrary of darkness.

entries.farsi: در ميان آن دو لشكرگاه زفت / ‏چالش و پيكار آن چه رفت رفت
entries.english: He made two banners, white and black: one Adam, the other the Iblís of the Way.

entries.farsi: دو علم بر ساخت اسپيد و سياه / ‏آن يكى آدم دگر ابليس راه
entries.english: Between those two mighty camps combat and strife, and there came to pass what came to pass.

entries.farsi: همچنان دور دوم هابيل شد / ضد نور پاك او قابيل شد
entries.english: Likewise in the second period Abel arose, and Cain became the antagonist of his pure light.

entries.farsi: همچنان اين دو علم از عدل و جور / تا به نمرود آمد اندر دور دور
entries.english: Even so these two banners of justice and iniquity till in the course of time the period of Nimrod arrived.

entries.farsi: ضد ابراهيم گشت و خصم او / و آن دو لشكر كين گزار و جنگ جو
entries.english: He became the antagonist and adversary of Abraham, and those two armies waged war and sought battle.

entries.farsi: چون درازى جنگ آمد ناخوشش / ‏فيصل آن هر دو آمد آتشش
entries.english: When He was displeased with the prolongation of the strife, His fire became the decision between the twain.

entries.farsi: پس حكم كرد آتشى را و نكر / تا شود حل مشكل آن دو نفر
entries.english: So He caused a fire to be His arbiter and servant, in order that the difficulty of those two persons might be solved.

entries.farsi: دور دور و قرن و قرن اين دو فريق / ‏تا به فرعون و به موساى شفيق
entries.english: These two parties from period to period and from generation to generation, down to Pharaoh and God fearing Moses,

entries.farsi: سالها اندر ميانشان حرب بود / چون ز حد رفت و ملولى مى‏فزود
entries.english: Between whom there was war for years. When it passed bounds and was causing excessive weariness,

entries.farsi: آب دريا را حكم سازيد حق / ‏تا كه ماند كى برد زين دو سبق
entries.english: God made the water of the sea His arbiter, that it might be left which of these two should prevail.

entries.farsi: هم نكر سازيد از بهر ثمود / صيحه‏اى كه جانشان را در ربود
entries.english: So till the period and time of Mustafá, with Abu Jahl, the general of the army of iniquity.

entries.farsi: همچنان تا دور و طور مصطفى / با ابو جهل آن سپهدار جفا
entries.english: Moreover He appointed a servant for Thamud, the Cry that took away their lives.

entries.farsi: هم نكر سازيد بر قارون ز كين / ‏در حليمى اين زمين پوشيد كين
entries.english: Moreover He appointed a servant for the people of ‘Ad, one that rises quickly and moves rapidly, that is, the Wind.

entries.farsi: هم نكر سازيد بهر قوم عاد / زود خيز تيز رو يعنى كه باد
entries.english: Moreover He appointed a discerning servant for Qarun: He endured the graciousness of the Earth with enmity,

entries.farsi: تا حليمى زمين شد جمله قهر / برد قارون را و گنجش را به قعر
entries.english: So that the graciousness of the Earth turned entirely to wrath, and she bore Qarun and his treasure down to the abyss.

entries.farsi: لقمه‏اى را كاو ستون اين تن است / ‏دفع تيغ جوع نان چون جوشن است
entries.english: In the case of the food that is a pillar for this body, bread is like a breastplate to repel the sword of hunger;

entries.farsi: چون كه حق قهرى نهد در نان تو / چون خناق آن نان بگيرد در گلو
entries.english: When God puts a wrath into your bread, that bread will stick in your throat as if it were inflamed.

entries.farsi: اين لباسى كه ز سرما شد مجير / حق دهد او را مزاج زمهرير
entries.english: This garment that protects you from the cold— God gives it the temperature of intense frost,

entries.farsi: تا شود بر تنت اين جبه‏ى شگرف / ‏سرد همچون يخ گزنده همچو برف
entries.english: So that this greatcoat on your body becomes cold as ice and biting as snow.

entries.farsi: تا گريزى از وشق هم از حرير / زو پناه آرى به سوى زمهرير
entries.english: In order that you may flee from the fox-fur and silk and take refuge from them with the intense cold.

entries.farsi: تو دو قله نيستى يك قله‏اى / ‏غافل از قصه‏ى عذاب ظله‏اى
entries.english: You are not the two qullas (ewers), you are one ewer: you have forgotten the chastisement inflicted by an overshadowing cloud.

entries.farsi: امر حق آمد به شهرستان و ده / ‏خانه و ديوار را سايه مده
entries.english: In town and village, to house and wall came the command of God, “Give no shade!

entries.farsi: مانع باران مباش و آفتاب / ‏تا بدان مرسل شدند امت شتاب
entries.english: Do not ward off the rain and the sun!” so that the people went in haste to that Apostle (Shu‘ayb),

entries.farsi: كه بمرديم اغلب اى مهتر امان / ‏باقى‏اش از دفتر تفسير خوان
entries.english: Crying, “We are dead for the most part: mercy, O Prince!” Read the rest of it in the book of commentary.

entries.farsi: چون عصا را مار كرد آن چست دست / ‏گر ترا عقلى است آن نكته بس است
entries.english: Since that deft-handed One made the rod a serpent, that instance is enough if you have any intelligence.

entries.farsi: تو نظر دارى و ليك امعانش نيست / ‏چشمه‏ى افسرده است و كرده‏ايست
entries.english: You possess consideration, but it does not go deep: it is a frozen spring and has stopped.

entries.farsi: زين همى‏گويد نگارنده‏ى فكر / كه بكن اى بنده امعان نظر
entries.english: Hence the Artist who depicts thoughts is saying, “Consider deeply, O servant.”

entries.farsi: آن نمى‏خواهد كه آهن كوب سرد / ليك اى پوالد بر داود گرد
entries.english: He does not mean, “Beat cold iron,” but “O steel, and devote yourself to David.”

entries.farsi: تن بمردت سوى اسرافيل ران / ‏دل فسردت روبه خورشيد روان
entries.english: If your body is dead, resort to Isráfíl; if your heart is frozen, repair to the sun of the Spirit.

entries.farsi: در خيال از بس كه گشتى مكتسى / ‏نك به سوفسطايى بد ظن رسى
entries.english: Inasmuch as you have wrapped yourself in the garment of phantasy, lo, you will reach the evil-minded sophist.

entries.farsi: او خود از لب خرد معزول بود / شد ز حس معزول و محروم از وجود
entries.english: Truly he was dispossessed of the kernel Reason: he was dispossessed of perception and deprived of experience.

entries.farsi: هين سخن خا نوبت لب‏خوايى است / ‏گر بگويى خلق را رسوايى است
entries.english: Listen, O mouther, it is the hour for mumbling: if you speak to the people, it is a shameful exposure.

entries.farsi: چيست امعان چشمه را كردن روان / ‏چون ز تن جان رست گويندش روان
entries.english: What is im’n ? Causing the spring to flow: when the spirit (jan) has escaped from the body, they call it rawán.

entries.farsi: آن حكيمى را كه جان از بند تن / ‏باز رست و شد روان اندر چمن
entries.english: The philosopher whose spirit was delivered from the bondage of the body and began to wander (rawan)in the garden

entries.farsi: دو لقب را او بر اين هر دو نهاد / بهر فرق اى آفرين بر جانش باد
entries.english: Bestowed two titles on these two in order to distinguish. Oh, may his spirit be blest!

entries.farsi: در بيان آن كه بر فرمان رود / گر گلى را خار خواهد آن شود
entries.english: Showing that if he who walks according to the command wishes a rose to become a thorn, it will become that.

sections.title_fa: معجزه‏ى هود عليه السلام در تخلص مومنان امت به وقت نزول باد
sections.title_en: The evidentiary miracle of Hud, on whom be peace, in the deliverance of the true believers of the community at the moment when the Wind descended.

entries.farsi: باد طوفان بود و كشتى لطف هو / بس چنين كشتى و طوفان دارد او
entries.english: All the true believers, from the violence of the pernicious Wind, seated themselves in the circle.

entries.farsi: مومنان از دست باد ضايره / ‏جمله بنشستند اندر دايره
entries.english: The Wind was the Flood, and His grace was the ship: He has many such arks and floods.

entries.farsi: پادشاهى را خدا كشتى كند / تا به حرص خويش بر صفها زند
entries.english: God makes a king to be an ark, to the end that he, by selfishness, may assault the ranks.

entries.farsi: قصد شه آن نه كه خلق ايمن شوند / قصدش آن كه ملك گردد پاى بند
entries.english: The king’s aim is not that the people should become safe; his aim is that his kingdom should become a fetter.

entries.farsi: آن خر آسى مى‏دود قصدش خالص / ‏تا بيابد او ز زخم آن دم مناص
entries.english: The ass that turns the mill is running along: its aim is release, so that it may gain refuge from blows at that moment.

entries.farsi: قصد او آن نه كه آبى بر كشد / يا كه كنجد را بدان روغن كند
entries.english: Its aim is not to draw some water or thereby to make sesame into oil.

entries.farsi: گاو بشتابد ز بيم زخم سخت / ‏نه براى بردن گردون و رخت
entries.english: The ox hurries for fear of hard blows, not for the purpose of taking the cart and baggage;

entries.farsi: ليك دادش حق چنين خوف وجع / ‏تا مصالح حاصل آيد در تبع
entries.english: But God put such fear of pain in him, to the end that good results might be achieved in consequence.

entries.farsi: همچنان هر كاسبى اندر دكان / ‏بهر خود كوشد نه اصالح جهان
entries.english: Similarly, every shopkeeper works for himself, not for the improvement of the world.

entries.farsi: هر يكى بر درد جويد مرهمى / ‏در تبع قايم شده زين عالمى
entries.english: Every one seeks a plaster for his pain, and in consequence of this a whole world is set in order.

entries.farsi: حق ستون اين جهان از ترس ساخت / ‏هر يكى از ترس جان در كار باخت
entries.english: God made of fear the pillar of this world: because of fear everyone has devoted himself to work.

entries.farsi: اين همه ترسنده‏اند از نيك و بد / هيچ ترسنده نترسد خود ز خود
entries.english: Praise be to God that on this wise He has made a fear to be the architect and improvement of the world.

entries.farsi: حمد ايزد را كه ترسى را چنين / ‏كرد او معمار اصالح زمين
entries.english: All these are afraid of good and evil: none that is afraid is himself frightened by himself.

entries.farsi: پس حقيقت بر همه حاكم كسى است / ‏كه قريب است او اگر محسوس نيست
entries.english: In reality, then, the ruler over all is that One who is near, though He is not perceived by the senses.

entries.farsi: هست او محسوس اندر مكمنى / ‏ليك محسوس حس اين خانه نى
entries.english: He is perceived in a certain hiding-place, but not perceived by the sense of this house.

entries.farsi: آن حسى كه حق بر آن حس مظهر است / ‏نيست حس اين جهان آن ديگر است
entries.english: The sense to which God is manifested is not the sense of this world; it is another.

entries.farsi: حس حيوان گر بديدى آن صور / بايزيد وقت بودى گاو و خر
entries.english: If the animal sense perceived those forms an ox or an ass would be the Bayazid of the time.

entries.farsi: آن كه تن را مظهر هر روح كرد / وان كه كشتى را براق نوح كرد
entries.english: He who made the body to be the theatre in which every spirit is manifested, He who made the Ark to be the Buraq of Noah,

entries.farsi: گر بخواهد عين كشتى را به خو / او كند طوفان تو اى نور جو
entries.english: He, if He will, makes a very ark in character to be a flood for you, O seeker of light.

entries.farsi: هر دمت طوفان و كشتى اى مقل / ‏با غم و شاديت كرد او متصل
entries.english: At every moment, O man of little means, He has conjoined with your grief and gladness an ark and a flood.

entries.farsi: گر نبينى كشتى و دريا به پيش / ‏لرزه‏ها بين در همه اجزاى خويش
entries.english: If you do not perceive the ark and the sea before you, consider the tremors in all your limbs.

entries.farsi: چون نبيند اصل ترسش را عيون / ‏ترس دارد از خيال گونه گون
entries.english: Since his eyes do not perceive the source of his fear, he is affrighted by diverse kinds of phantasy.

entries.farsi: ز انكه آن دم بانك اشتر مى‏شنيد / كور را گوش است آيينه، نه ديد
entries.english: A drunk boor strikes a blind man with his fist: the blind man thinks it is a kicking camel,

entries.farsi: مشت بر اعمى زند يك جلف مست / ‏كور پندارد لگد زن اشتر است
entries.english: Because at that moment he heard a camel’s cry: the ear, not the eye, is the mirror for the blind.

entries.farsi: باز گويد كور نه اين سنگ بود / يا مگر از قبه‏اى پر طنگ بود
entries.english: Then again the blind man says, “No, it was a stone, or perhaps it was from an echoing dome.”

entries.farsi: اين نبود و او نبود و آن نبود / آن كه او ترس آفريد اينها نمود
entries.english: It was neither this nor that nor that: He who created fear produced these.

entries.farsi: ترس و لرزه باشد از غيرى يقين / ‏هيچ كس از خود نترسد اى حزين
entries.english: Certainly fear and trembling are by another: nobody is frightened by himself, O sorrowful man.

entries.farsi: آن حكيمك وهم خواند ترس را / فهم كژ كردست او اين درس را
entries.english: The miserable philosopher calls fear “imagination” (wahm): he has wrongly understood this lesson.

entries.farsi: كى دروغى قيمت آرد بى‏ز راست / ‏در دو عالم هر دروغ از راست خاست
entries.english: How should there be any imagination without reality? How should any false coin pass without a genuine one?

entries.farsi: هيچ وهمى بى‏حقيقت كى بود / هيچ قلبى بى‏صحيحى كى رود
entries.english: How should a lie fetch a price without truth? Every lie in both worlds has arisen from truth.

entries.farsi: راست را ديد او رواجى و فروغ / ‏بر اميد آن روان كرد او دروغ
entries.english: He saw the currency and prestige enjoyed by truth: he set going the lie in hope of the same.

entries.farsi: اى دروغى كه ز صدقت اين نواست / ‏شكر نعمت گو مكن انكار راست
entries.english: O lie, whose fortune is from veracity, give thanks for the bounty and do not deny the truth!

entries.farsi: از مفلسف گويم و سوداى او / يا ز كشتيها و درياهاى او
entries.english: Shall I speak of the philosopher and his mad fancy, or of His ships and seas?

entries.farsi: بل ز كشتيهاش كآن پند دل است / ‏گويم از كل جزو در كل داخل است
entries.english: Nay, of His arks, which are the spiritual counsel; I will speak of the whole: the part is included in the whole.

entries.farsi: هر ولى را نوح و كشتيبان شناس / ‏صحبت اين خلق را طوفان شناس
entries.english: Know every saint to be a Noah and captain of the Ark; know companionship with these people to be the Flood.

entries.farsi: كم گريز از شير و اژدرهاى نر / ز آشنايان و ز خويشان كن حذر
entries.english: Do not flee from lions and fierce dragons, beware of friends and kinsmen.

entries.farsi: در تالقى روزگارت مى‏برند / يادهاشان غايبى‏ات مى‏چرند
entries.english: They waste your time face to face, and your recollections of them devour your absence.

entries.farsi: نشف كرد از تو خيال آن وشات / ‏شبنمى كه دارى از بحر الحيات
entries.english: Like a thirsty ass, the image of each one is licking up the sherbet of thought from the carafe of the body.

entries.farsi: چون خر تشنه خيال هر يكى / ‏از قف تن فكر را شربت مكى
entries.english: The image of those talebearers has sucked out of you the dew that you have from the Sea of Life.

entries.farsi: پس نشان نشف آب اندر غصون / ‏آن بود كآن مى‏نجنبد در ركون
entries.english: The sign, then, of the absorption of the water in the boughs is that they are not moved to sway.

entries.farsi: عضو حر شاخ تر تازه بود / مى‏كشى هر سو كشيده مى‏شود
entries.english: The limb of him who is free is a moist fresh bough: you pull it in any direction, it is pulled.

entries.farsi: گر سبد خواهى توانى كردنش / ‏هم توانى كرد چنبر گردنش
entries.english: If you want a basket, you can make it; you can also make its neck a hoop;

entries.farsi: چون شد آن ناشف ز نشف بيخ خود / نآيد آن سويى كه امرش مى‏كشد
entries.english: When it has been sucked dry by the draining of its root, it does not come in the direction to which command is pulling it.

entries.farsi: پس بخوان قامُوا كُسالى‏ از نبى / ‏چون نيابد شاخ از بيخش طبى
entries.english: Recite, then, from the Qur’an they stand up languidly, when the bough gets no medicinal treatment from its root.

entries.farsi: آتشى ديدى كه سوزد هر نهال / ‏آتش جان بين كز او سوزد خيال
entries.english: This symbol is fiery, I will cut it short and resume the fakir and the treasure and the circumstances connected with it.

entries.farsi: آتشين است اين نشان كوته كنم / ‏بر فقير و گنج و احوالش زنم
entries.english: You have seen the fire that burns every sapling; see the fire of the Spirit by which phantasy is burnt.

entries.farsi: نه خيال و نه حقيقت را امان / ‏زين چنين آتش كه شعله زد ز جان
entries.english: Neither for phantasy nor for reality is there any protection against a fire like this which flamed forth from the Spirit.

entries.farsi: خصم هر شير آمد و هر روبه او / ‏كُلُّ شَيْ‏ءٍ هالِكٌ إِلاَّ وجهه
entries.english: He is the adversary of every lion and every fox: everything is perishing except His Face.

entries.farsi: در وجوه و وجه او رو خرج شو / چون الف در بسم در رو درج شو
entries.english: Go into His aspects and Face, become spent: go in, become enveloped, like the alif in bism.

entries.farsi: آن الف در بسم پنهان كرد ايست / ‏هست او در بسم و هم در بسم نيست
entries.english: In bism the alif has stayed hidden: it is in bism and also it is not in bism.

entries.farsi: همچنين جمله‏ى حروف گشته مات / ‏وقت حذف حرف از بهر صالت
entries.english: Such is the case with all the letters that disappear when they are elided for the purpose of conjunctions.

entries.farsi: او صله‏ست و بى و سين زو وصل يافت / ‏وصل بى و سين الف را بر نتافت
entries.english: The suppressed alif in bism is a sila and through it the b and the s have attained to union: the union of the b and the s could not bear the alif.

entries.farsi: چون كه حرفى بر نتابد اين وصال / ‏واجب آيد كه كنم كوته مقال
entries.english: Since this union cannot bear a single letter, it behooves me to cut short the discourse.

entries.farsi: چون يكى حرفى فراق سين و بى است / ‏خامشى اينجا مهم تر واجبى است
entries.english: Since a single letter is the cause of separation between the s and the b, here silence is a most urgent duty.

entries.farsi: چون الف از خود فنا شد مكتنف / ‏بى و سين بى‏او همى‏گويند الف
entries.english: When the alif has passed away from self-existence, taking shelter, the b and the s say “alif” without it.

entries.farsi: ما رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رَمَيْتَ بى‏وى است / ‏همچنين قال اللهَّ از صمتش بجست
entries.english: You did not throw when you threw are without him; likewise God said sprang from his silence.

entries.farsi: تا بود دار و ندارد او عمل / ‏چون كه شد فانى كند دفع علل
entries.english: So long as a drug exists, it has no effect; it removes diseases when it has perished.

entries.farsi: گر شود بيشه قلم دريا مداد / مثنوى را نيست پايانى اميد
entries.english: If the forest should become pens and the ocean ink, there is no hope of bringing the Masnavi to an end.

entries.farsi: چار چوب خشت زن تا خاك هست / ‏مى‏دهد تقطيع شعرش نيز دست
entries.english: So long as the Brick-maker’s mould is earth, the scansion of its poetry, too, will be kept up.

entries.farsi: چون نماند خاك و بودش جف كند / خاك سازد بحر او چون كف كند
entries.english: When earth remains no more and He dries its existence, His sea when it foams will make earth.

entries.farsi: چون نماند بيشه و سر در كشد / بيشه‏ها از عين دريا سر كشد
entries.english: When the forest remains no more and disappears, forests will raise their heads from the essence of the Sea.

entries.farsi: بهر اين گفت آن خداوند فرج / ‏حدثوا عن بحرنا إذ ال حرج
entries.english: Hence that Lord of relief said, “Relate Traditions from our Sea, since there is no harm.”

entries.farsi: باز گرد از بحر و رو در خشك نه / ‏هم ز لعبت گو كه كودك راست به
entries.english: Turn back from the Sea and set your face towards dry land: talk only of the plaything, for it is better for the child,

entries.farsi: تا ز لعبت اندك اندك در صبا / جانش گردد با يم عقل آشنا
entries.english: So that in his boyhood, little by little beyond the plaything, his spirit may become acquainted with the ocean of Reason.

entries.farsi: عقل از آن بازى همى‏يابد صبى / ‏گر چه با عقل است در ظاهر ابى
entries.english: By means of that play the boy is acquiring reason, though superficially it is repugnant.

entries.farsi: كودك ديوانه بازى كى كند / جزو بايد تا كه كل را فى كند
entries.english: How can a demented child play? There must be a part in order that it may attain to the whole.

sections.title_en: Returning to the Story of the dome and the treasure.

entries.farsi: بانگ او تو نشنوى من بشنوم / ‏ز انكه در اسرار هم راز وى‏ام
entries.english: Lo, the idea of that fakir with “Come! Come!” has rendered me totally unable.

entries.farsi: نك خيال آن فقيرم بى‏ريا / عاجز آورد از بيا و از بيا
entries.english: You do not hear his cry; I hear it, because I am his confidant in my inmost thoughts.

entries.farsi: طالب گنجش مبين خود گنج اوست / ‏دوست كى باشد به معنى غير دوست
entries.english: Do not regard him as a seeker of the treasure; he is the treasure himself, how should the lover in reality be other than the beloved?

entries.farsi: سجده خود را مى‏كند هر لحظه او / سجده پيش آينه‏ست از بهر رو
entries.english: At even moment he is bowing down to himself: the bowing is in front of the mirror for the sake of the face.

entries.farsi: گر بديدى ز آينه او يك پشيز / بى‏خيالى زو نماندى هيچ چيز
entries.english: If he saw in the mirror a single mite without any phantasy, nothing would be left of him.

entries.farsi: هم خيالاتش هم او فانى شدى / ‏دانش او محو نادانى شدى
entries.english: Both his phantasies and he would vanish: his know ledge would be obliterated in nescience.

entries.farsi: دانشى ديگر ز نادانى ما / سر بر آوردى عيان كه انى انا
entries.english: From our nescience another knowledge would rise into clear view, saying, “Lo, I am.”

entries.farsi: اسْ جُدُوا لآِ دَمَ ندا آمد همى / ‏كآدميد و خويش بينيدش دمى
entries.english: The call was coming—”Bow down to Adam, for you are Adam, and for a moment see yourselves to be him.”

entries.farsi: احولى از چشم ايشان دور كرد / تا زمين شد عين چرخ الجورد
entries.english: He uncrossed their eyes, so that the earth became identical with the azure heavens.

entries.farsi: ال اله گفت و الا اللهَّ گفت / ‏گشت ال الا اللهَّ و وحدت شكفت
entries.english: He said, “There is no god,” and He said, “except God”: not became except God, and Unity blossomed forth.

entries.farsi: آن حبيب و آن خليل با رشد / وقت آن آمد كه گوش ما كشد
entries.english: The time has come for that righteous beloved and dear friend to pull my ear

entries.farsi: سوى چشمه كه دهان زينها بشو / آن چه پوشيديم از خلقان مگو
entries.english: Towards the fountain, saying, “Wash your mouth clean of these things: do not tell that which we have concealed from the people.

entries.farsi: ور بگويى خود نگردد آشكار / تو به قصد كشف گردى جرم دار
entries.english: And if you tell, it will not become manifest, you will be guilty of attempting to reveal it.

entries.farsi: صورت درويش و نقش گنج گو / رنج كيشند اين گروه از رنج گو
entries.english: But, mark, I am compassing them about: I am at once the speaker and the hearer of this.

entries.farsi: ليك من اينك بر ايشان مى‏تنم / ‏قائل اين سامع اين هم منم
entries.english: Tell of the form of the dervish and the picture of the treasure. These folk are addicted to trouble: tell of trouble.

entries.farsi: چشمه‏ى رحمت بر ايشان شد حرام / ‏مى‏خورند از زهر قاتل جام جام
entries.english: The fountain of Mercy has become unlawful to them: they are drinking cup after cup of deadly poison.

entries.farsi: خاكها پر كرده دامن مى‏كشند / تا كنند اين چشمه‏ها را خشك بند
entries.english: Having filled their skirts with clods, they are taking them along in order to make a dam for these fountains.

entries.farsi: كى شود اين چشمه‏ى دريا مدد / مكتبس زين مشت خاك نيك و بد
entries.english: How should this fountain, which is replenished by the Sea, be stopped up by this good or bad folk’s handful of earth?

entries.farsi: قوم معكوس‏اند اندر مشتها / خاك خوار و آب را كرده رها
entries.english: But it says, ‘With you, I am closed; without you, I continue unto everlasting.’ ”

entries.farsi: ليك گويد با شما من بسته‏ام / ‏بى‏شما من تا ابد پيوسته‏ام
entries.english: The folk are perverted in their appetites: eating earth and have left the water.

entries.farsi: ضد طبع انبيا دارند خلق / ‏اژدها را متكا دارند خلق
entries.english: The people have a nature opposite to that of the prophets: the people deem the dragon an object of reliance.

entries.farsi: بر چه بگشادى بدل اين ديده‏ها / يك به يك بئس البدل دان آن ترا
entries.english: Inasmuch as you have known the eye-bandage whereby God seals, do you know at all to what you have shut your eyes?

entries.farsi: چشم بند ختم چون دانسته‏اى / ‏هيچ دانى از چه ديده بسته‏اى
entries.english: To what instead have you opened these eyes? Know that in every respect it is a bad exchange for you.

entries.farsi: ليك خورشيد عنايت تافته ست / ‏آيسان را از كرم دريافته ست
entries.english: But the sun of favour has shone and has graciously succoured them that despair.

entries.farsi: نرد بس نادر ز رحمت باخته / ‏عين كفران را انابت ساخته
entries.english: He in His mercy has played a very marvellous game of backgammon: He has made the essence of ingratitude to be a turning in repentance.

entries.farsi: هم از اين بد بختى خلق آن جواد / منفجر كرده دو صد چشمه‏ى وداد
entries.english: Even from this ill-fatedness of the people that Bounteous One has caused two hundred fountains of love to burst.

entries.farsi: از سواد شب برون آرد نهار / و ز كف معسر بروياند يسار
entries.english: He gives to the rose-bud a source in the thorn; He gives to the snake-stone, from the snake, an ornamental quality.

entries.farsi: غنچه را از خار سرمايه دهد / مهره را از مار پيرايه دهد
entries.english: He brings forth day from the blackness of night and makes ease to grow from the hand of him who suffers hardship.

entries.farsi: آرد سازد ريگ را بهر خليل / ‏كوه با داود گردد هم رسيل
entries.english: He makes sand into flour for Khalil; the mountain becomes an accompanist to David.

entries.farsi: كوه با وحشت در آن ابر ظلم / ‏بر گشايد بانگ چنگ و زير و بم
entries.english: The solitary mountain amidst that cloud of darkness opens the music of the harp and treble and bass,

entries.farsi: خيز اى داود از خلقان نفير / ترك آن كردى عوض از ما بگير
entries.english: ” Arise, O David, you shunner of the people! You have abandoned that: receive compensation from me.”

sections.title_fa: انابت آن طالب گنج به حق تعالى بعد از طلب بسيار و عجز و اضطرار كه اى ولى الاظهار تو كن اين نهان را آشكار
sections.title_en: How the seeker of the treasure, after having searched much and having been reduced
to helplessness and despair, turned to God most High, saying, “O You to whom manifestation belongs, do You make this hidden thing evident!”

entries.farsi: گفت آن درويش اى داناى راز / از پى اين گنج كردم ياوه تاز
entries.english: The dervish said, “O Knower of the secret, I have run about in vain for the sake of this treasure.

entries.farsi: زيو حرص و آز و مستعجل تگى / ‏نى تانى جست و نى آهستگى
entries.english: The devil of greed and cupidity and hurry sought neither deliberation nor calmness.

entries.farsi: خود نگفتم چون در اين ناموقنم / ‏ز آن گره زن اين گره را حل كنم
entries.english: I have not gained a morsel from any pot: I have blackened my hand and burnt my mouth.

entries.farsi: من ز ديگى لقمه‏اى نندوختم / ‏كف سيه كردم دهان را سوختم
entries.english: Truly, I did not say, ‘Since I have no certainty in this, I will untie this knot by Him who ties knots.’”

entries.farsi: قول حق را هم ز حق تفسير جو / هين مگو ژاژ از گمان اى سخت رو
entries.english: Seek the exposition of God’s Word from God: do not talk nonsense from opinion, O hard man.

entries.farsi: آن گره كاو زد همو بگشايدش / ‏مهره كاو انداخت او بربايدش
entries.english: The knot which He tied He also will loosen: the die which He cast He will take off.

entries.farsi: گر چه آسانت نمود آن سان سخن / ‏كى بود آسان رموز من لدن
entries.english: Although words of that sort seemed to you to be easy, how should the esoteric symbols be easy?

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب توبه كردم زين شتاب / ‏چون تو در بستى تو كن هم فتح باب
entries.english: He said, “O Lord, I repent of this haste: since You have shut the door, do You also open the door.

entries.farsi: بر سر خرقه شدن بار دگر / در دعاكردن بدم هم بى‏هنر
entries.english: To go once more to the patched frock: even in making invocation to God: I was devoid of merit.

entries.farsi: كو هنر كو من كجا دل مستوى / ‏اين همه عكس تو است و خود توى
entries.english: How have I any independent merit or personality or heart? All these are the reflection of You, and You Yourself are.

entries.farsi: هر شبى تدبير و فرهنگم به خواب / ‏همچو كشتى غرقه مى‏گردد ز آب
entries.english: Every night in sleep my forethought and knowledge become: like a ship overwhelmed by the water.

entries.farsi: خود نه من مى‏مانم و نه آن هنر / تن چو مردارى فتاده بى‏خبر
entries.english: Neither do I myself remain nor that merit: my body lies unconscious like a carcass.

entries.farsi: تا سحر جمله‏ى شب آن شاه على / ‏خود همى‏گويد الستى و بلى
entries.english: The whole night until dawn that exalted King is Himself uttering an ‘Alast’ and ‘Yea.’

entries.farsi: كو بلى گو جمله را سيالب برد / يا نهنگى خورد كل را كرد و مرد
entries.english: Where is anyone to say ‘Yea’? The flood has swept them all away, or a leviathan has swallowed them all piecemeal.

entries.farsi: آفتاب شرق شب را طى كند / اين نهنگ آن خورده‏ها را قى كند
entries.english: At morning tide, when He draws His sheeny sword from the scabbard of the darkness of night,

entries.farsi: صبحدم چون تيغ گوهر دار خود / از نيام ظلمت شب بر كشد
entries.english: And the orient sun rolls up night, this leviathan spews out all that it swallowed,

entries.farsi: رسته چون يونس ز معده‏ى آن نهنگ / ‏منتشر گرديم اندر بو و رنگ
entries.english: And we, delivered like Jonah from the belly of that leviathan, are dispersed into scent and colour.

entries.farsi: خلق چون يونس مسبح آمدند / كاندر آن ظلمات پر راحت شدند
entries.english: Like Jonah, the people give praise, because they were restful in that darkness.

entries.farsi: هر يكى گويد به هنگام سحر / چون ز بطن حوت شب آيد به در
entries.english: At the hour of dawn each one says, when he comes forth from the belly of the Fish, Night,

entries.farsi: كاى كريمى كه در آن ليل وحش / ‏گنج رحمت بنهى و چندين چشش
entries.english: ‘O Gracious One who deposits in lonesome Night the treasure of Mercy and all these delicious experiences!

entries.farsi: چشم تيز و گوش تازه تن سبك / ‏از شب همچون نهنگ ذو الحبك
entries.english: By means of Night, which resembles the scaly leviathan, the eye keen, the ear fresh, and the body nimble?

entries.farsi: از مقامات وحش رو زين سپس / ‏هيچ نگريزيم ما با چون تو كس
entries.english: Henceforth, with One like You, we will never flee from positions of fearful aspect.

entries.farsi: موسى آن را نار ديد و نور بود / زنگيى ديديم شب را حور بود
entries.english: Moses deemed that to be fire, but it was light: we regarded Night as a Negro, but it was a houri.

entries.farsi: بعد از اين ما ديده خواهيم از تو بس / ‏تا نپوشد بحر را خاشاك و خس
entries.english: After this, we beg of You the eye, in order that sticks and straws may not conceal the Sea.

entries.farsi: ساحران را چشم چون رست از عما / كف زنان بودند بى‏اين دست و پا
entries.english: When the eyes of magicians were delivered from blindness, they were clapping their hands, deprived of these hands and feet.

entries.farsi: چشم بند خلق جز اسباب نيست / ‏هر كه لرزد بر سبب ز اصحاب نيست
entries.english: What bandages the people’s eyes is nothing but means: whoever trembles for means is not one of the Comrades.

entries.farsi: ليك حق اصحاب و نااصحاب را / در گشاد و برد تا صدر سرا
entries.english: But, O my comrades, God has opened the door to the Comrades and led them to the high-seat in the palace.

entries.farsi: با كفش نامستحق و مستحق / ‏معتقان رحمت‏اند از بند رق
entries.english: Through His hand the unworthy and the worthy are freed by Mercy from the bonds of servitude.

entries.farsi: در عدم ما مستحقان كى بديم / ‏كه بر اين جان و بر اين دانش زديم
entries.english: During non-existence how were we worthy to attain to this spirituality and knowledge?

entries.farsi: اى بكرده يار هر اغيار را / وى بداده خلعت گل خار را
entries.english: O You who have made every stranger friend, and O You who have given the rose as a robe of honour to the thorn,

entries.farsi: خاك ما را ثانيا پاليز كن / ‏هيچ نى را بار ديگر چيز كن
entries.english: Sift our dust a second time; make nothing to be something once more!

entries.farsi: اين دعا تو امر كردى ز ابتدا / ‏ور نه خاكى را چه زهره‏ى اين بدى
entries.english: You commanded this invocation from the beginning; else how should a creature of dust have dared this?

entries.farsi: چون دعامان امر كردى اى عجاب / ‏اين دعاى خويش را كن مستجاب
entries.english: Since—oh, wonder!—you commanded us to invoke You, cause this invocation of You to be answered favourably.

entries.farsi: شب شكسته كشتى فهم و حواس / ‏نه اميدى مانده نه خوف و نه ياس
entries.english: Night has wrecked the ship of understanding and senses: no hope is left, nor fear nor despair.

entries.farsi: آن يكى را كرده پر نور جلال / ‏و آن دگر را كرده پر وهم و خيال
entries.english: God has borne me into the sea of Mercy: with what specialty He will fill me and send me.

entries.farsi: برده در درياى رحمت ايزدم / ‏تا ز چه فن پر كند بفرستدم
entries.english: He fills one with the light of Majesty, while He fills another with imagination and fancy.

entries.farsi: گر به خويشم هيچ راى و فن بدى / ‏راى و تدبيرم به حكم من بدى
entries.english: If I had any judgement and skill by myself, my judgement and forethought would be under my control;

entries.farsi: شب نرفتى هوش بى‏فرمان من / ‏زير دام من بدى مرغان من
entries.english: At night my consciousness would not go without my bidding, and my birds would be under my trap.

entries.farsi: بودمى آگه ز منزلهاى جان / ‏وقت خواب و بى‏هشى و امتحان
entries.english: I should be aware of the stages of the soul at the time of sleep and unconsciousness and tribulation.

entries.farsi: چون كفم زين حل و عقد او تهى است / ‏اى عجب اين معجبى من ز كيست
entries.english: Inasmuch as my hand is empty by this power of His to loosen and bind, oh, I wonder, from whom comes this self-conceit of mine?

entries.farsi: ديده را ناديده خود انگاشتم / ‏باز زنبيل دعا برداشتم
entries.english: I have even deemed that what I saw was not seen’ and I have again held up the basket of invocation.

entries.farsi: چون الف چيزى ندارم اى كريم / ‏جز دلى دل تنگ‏تر از چشم ميم
entries.english: Like alif, I possess nothing, O Gracious One, except a heart more constricted with anguish than the eye of mim.

entries.farsi: اين الف وين ميم ام بود ماست / ‏ميم ام تنگ است الف زو نر گداست
entries.english: This alif and this mim are the mother (umm) of our existence: the mim of umm is narrow, and the alif is from it a sturdy beggar.

entries.farsi: آن الف چيزى ندارد غافلى است / ‏ميم دل تنگ آن زمان عاقلى است
entries.english: ‘alif possesses nothing’ is forgetfulness; the distressful mim is the time of rationality.

entries.farsi: در زمان بى‏هشى خود هيچ من / ‏در زمان هوش اندر پيچ من
entries.english: During the time of unconsciousness I am nothing at all; during the time of consciousness I am in torment.

entries.farsi: خود ندارم هيچ به سازد مرا / كه ز وهم دارم است اين صد عنا
entries.english: Do not lay another nothing upon a nothing like this; do not put the name of ‘fortune’ upon a torment like this.

entries.farsi: هيچ ديگر بر چنين هيچى منه / ‏نام دولت بر چنين پيچى منه
entries.english: Truly ‘I possess nothing’ suits me better, since these hundred troubles arise from imagining that I possess.

entries.farsi: در ندارم هم تو داراييم كن / ‏رنج ديدم راحت افزاييم كن
entries.english: Just in the state where I possess nothing You act in sovereign fashion towards me. I have suffered pain: Increase my pleasure.

entries.farsi: هم در آب ديده عريان بيستم / ‏بر در تو چون كه ديده نيستم
entries.english: I will just stand naked in tears at Your gate, since I have no sight.

entries.farsi: آب ديده‏ى بنده‏ى بى‏ديده را / سبزه‏اى بخش و نباتى زين چرا
entries.english: Bestow on the tears of Your sightless slave verdure and vegetation from this pasture;

entries.farsi: ور نمانم آب آبم ده ز عين / ‏همچو عينين نبى هطالتين
entries.english: And if I leave no tears, Give me tears from an eye like the two streaming eyes of the Prophet.

entries.farsi: او چو آب ديده جست از جود حق / ‏با چنان اقبال و اجلال و سبق
entries.english: Since he, with all that high fortune and majesty and pre-eminence, sought tears from the bounty of God,

entries.farsi: چون نباشم ز اشك خون باريك ريس / ‏من تهى دست قصور كاسه ليس
entries.english: How should not I, an empty-handed destitute lick-platter, spin fine webs of blood-stained tears?

entries.farsi: چون چنان چشم اشك را مفتون بود / اشك من بايد كه صد جيحون بود
entries.english: Inasmuch as an eye like that is enamoured of tears, it behooves my tears to be a hundred great rivers.”

entries.farsi: قطره‏اى ز آن زين دو صد جيحون به است / ‏كه بدان يك قطره انس و جن برست
entries.english: A single drop of those is better than these two hundred great rivers, for by that single drop mankind and the Jinn were saved.

entries.farsi: چون كه باران جست آن روضه‏ى بهشت / ‏چون نجويد آب شوره خاك زشت
entries.english: Since that Garden of Paradise sought rain, how should not the foul briny soil seek water?

entries.farsi: اى اخى دست از دعاكردن مدار / با اجابت يا رد اويت چه كار
entries.english: O comrade, do not refrain from invoking: what business have you with His acceptance of rejection?

entries.farsi: نان كه سد و مانع اين آب بود / دست از آن نان مى‏ببايد شست زود
entries.english: Since bread was the barrier and obstacle to this water, you must quickly wash your hands of that bread.

entries.farsi: خويش را موزون و چست و سخته كن / ‏ز آب ديده نان خود را پخته كن
entries.english: Make yourself harmonious and congruous and balanced: let your bread be baked well with tears.

sections.title_fa: ‏آواز دادن هاتف مر طالب گنج را و اعالم كردن از حقيقت اسرار آن
sections.title_en: How the Voice from heaven called to the seeker of the treasure and acquainted him with the truth of the mysteries thereof.

entries.farsi: اندر اين بود او كه الهام آمدش / ‏كشف شد اين مشكالت از ايزدش
entries.english: He was in this when inspiration came to him and these difficulties were solved for him by God,

entries.farsi: كاو بگفتت در كمان تيرى بنه / ‏كى بگفتندت كه اندر كش تو زه
entries.english: Saying, “It told you to put an arrow to the bow, when were you told to pull the bowstring?

entries.farsi: او نگفتت كه كمان را سخت كش / ‏در كمان نه گفت او نه پر كنش
entries.english: It did not tell you to draw the bow hard: it bade you put to the bow, not ‘shoot with your full strength.’

entries.farsi: از فضولى تو كمان افراشتى / ‏صنعت قواسيى برداشتى
entries.english: You, from vanity, raised the bow aloft and brought to a high pitch the art of archery.

entries.farsi: ترك اين سخته كمانى رو بگو / در كمان نه تير و پريدن مجو
entries.english: Go; renounce this skill in drawing the strong-bow: put the arrow to the bow and do not seek to draw to the full extent.

entries.farsi: چون بيفتد بر كن آن جا مى‏طلب / ‏زور بگذار و به زارى جو ذهب
entries.english: When it falls, dig up the spot and search: abandon strength and seek the gold by means of piteous supplication.”

entries.farsi: آن چه حق است اقرب از حبل الوريد / تو فگنده تير فكرت را بعيد
entries.english: That which is real is nearer than the neck-artery; you have shot the arrow of thought far afield.

entries.farsi: اى كمان و تيرها بر ساخته / ‏صيد نزديك و تو دور انداخته
entries.english: O you who have provided yourself with bow and arrows, the prey is near and you have shot far.

entries.farsi: هر كه دور اندازتر او دورتر / وز چنين گنج است او مهجورتر
entries.english: The farther one shoots, the farther away and more separated is he from a treasure like this.

entries.farsi: فلسفى خود را از انديشه بكشت / ‏گو بدو كاو راست سوى گنج پشت
entries.english: The philosopher killed himself with thinking: let him run on, for his back is turned towards the treasure.

entries.farsi: گو بدو چندان كه افزون مى‏دود / از مراد دل جداتر مى‏شود
entries.english: Let him run on: the more he runs, the more remote does he become from the object of his heart’s desire.

entries.farsi: جاهَدُوا فِينا بگفت آن شهريار / جاهدوا عنا نگفت اى بى‏قرار
entries.english: That King said, “those who have striven in Us”: He did not say, “those who have striven away from Us,” O restless one,

entries.farsi: همچو كنعان كاو ز ننگ نوح رفت / ‏بر فراز قله‏ى آن كوه زفت
entries.english: As Canaan, who in disdain of Noah went up to the top of that great mountain.

entries.farsi: هر چه افزونتر همى‏جست او خالص / ‏سوى كه مى‏شد جداتر از مناص
entries.english: The more he sought deliverance towards the mountain, the more was he separated from the place of refuge,

entries.farsi: همچو اين درويش بهر گنج و كان / ‏هر صباحى سخت‏تر جستى كمان
entries.english: Like this dervish for the sake of the treasure and the mine sought the bow more strongly every morning,

entries.farsi: اين مثل اندر زمانه جانى است / ‏جان نادانان به رنج ارزانى است
entries.english: And the more strongly he gripped the bow each time, the worse luck he had in respect of the treasure and the mark.

entries.farsi: هر كمانى كاو گرفتى سخت‏تر / بود از گنج و نشان بد بخت‏تر
entries.english: This parable is of vital import in the world: the soul of the ignorant is worthy of pain.

entries.farsi: آن دكان بالاى استاد اى نگار / گنده و پر كژدم است و پر ز مار
entries.english: Inasmuch as the ignoramus disdains his teacher, consequently he goes and opens a new shop.

entries.farsi: ز انكه جاهل ننگ دارد ز اوستاد / الجرم رفت و دكانى نو گشاد
entries.english: O picture, that shop, over the teacher, is stinking and full of scorpions and snakes.

entries.farsi: زود ويران كن دكان و باز گرد / سوى سبزه و گلبنان و آب خورد
entries.english: Quickly lay waste that shop and turn back to the greenery and the rose-tress and the watering-place;

entries.farsi: ته چو كنعان كاو ز كبر و ناشناخت / ‏از كه عاصم سفينه‏ى فوز ساخت
entries.english: Not like Canaan, who from pride and ignorance made of the “protecting” mountain a ship of safety.

entries.farsi: علم تير اندازى‏اش آمد حجاب / ‏و آن مراد او را بده حاضر به جيب
entries.english: His knowledge of archery became a veil to him, while he had that object of desire present in his bosom.

entries.farsi: اى بسا علم و ذكاوات و فطن / ‏گشته ره رو را چو غول و راه زن
entries.english: Oh, how often have knowledge and keen wits and understandings become as the ghoul or brigand to the wayfarer!

entries.farsi: بيشتر اصحاب جنت ابلهند / تا ز شر فيلسوفى مى‏رهند
entries.english: Most of those destined for Paradise are simpletons, so that they escape from the mischief of philosophy.

entries.farsi: خويش را عريان كن از فضل و فضول / ‏تا كند رحمت به تو هر دم نزول
entries.english: Strip yourself of learning and vanity, in order that mercy may descend on you at every moment.

entries.farsi: زيركى ضد شكست است و نياز / زيركى بگذار و با گولى بساز
entries.english: Cleverness is the opposite of abasement and supplication: give up cleverness and sort with stupidity.

entries.farsi: زيركى دان دام برد و طمع و گاز / تا چه خواهد زيركى را پاك باز
entries.english: Know that cleverness is a trap for gaining victory and ambition and a scarecrow: why should the pure devotee wish to be clever?

entries.farsi: يركان با صنعتى قانع شده / ‏ابلهان از صنع در صانع شده
entries.english: The clever ones are content with an ingenious device; the simple ones have gone from the artifice to rest in the Artificer,

entries.farsi: ز انكه طفل خرد را مادر نهاد / دست و پا باشد نهاده بر كنار
entries.english: Because at breakfast time a mother will have laid the little child’s hands and feet on her bosom.

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن سه مسافر مسلمان و ترسا و جهود كه به منزل قوتى يافتند و ترسا و جهود سير بودند گفتند اين قوت را فردا خوريم مسلمان صايم بود گرسنه ماند از آن كه مغلوب بود
sections.title_en: Story of the three travellers—a Moslem, a Christian, and a Jew— who obtained some food at a hostelry. The Christian and the Jew had already eaten their fill, so they said, “Let us eat this food
to-morrow.” The Moslem was fasting, and he remained hungry because he was overpowered.

entries.farsi: ك حكايت بشنو اينجا اى پسر / تا نگردى ممتحن اندر هنر
entries.english: Here listen to a story, O son, in order that you may not suffer affliction in talent.

entries.farsi: آن جهود و مومن و ترسا مگر / همرهى كردند با هم در سفر
entries.english: As it happened, a Jew and a true believer and a Christian travelled together on a journey.

entries.farsi: با دو گمره همره آمد مومنى / ‏چون خرد با نفس و با آهرمنى
entries.english: A true believer travelled along with two miscreants, like reason with a carnal soul and Devil.

entries.farsi: در قفس افتند زاغ و جغد و باز / جفت شد در حبس پاك و بى‏نماز
entries.english: In travel the man of Merv and the man of Rayy meet one another as companions on the road and at table.

entries.farsi: مرغزى و رازى افتند از سفر / همره و هم سفره پيش همدگر
entries.english: Crow and owl and falcon come into the cage: the holy and the irreligious become mates in prison.

entries.farsi: كرده منزل شب به يك كاروانسرا / اهل شرق و اهل غرب و ما ورا
entries.english: At night Easterners and Westerners and Transoxanians make their abode in the same caravanseray.

entries.farsi: مانده در كاروانسرا خرد و شگرف / ‏روزها با هم ز سرما و ز برف
entries.english: Small and great remain together for days in the caravanseray because of frost and snow.

entries.farsi: چون گشاده شد ره و بگشاد بند / بگسلند و هر يكى جايى روند
entries.english: As soon as the road is opened and the obstacle removed, they separate and everyone goes in a direction.

entries.farsi: چون قفس را بشكند شاه خرد / جمع مرغان هر يكى سويى پرد
entries.english: When sovereign Reason breaks the cage, all the birds fly away, each one to a quarter.

entries.farsi: پر گشايد پيش از اين پر شوق و باد / در هواى جنس خود سوى معاد
entries.english: Before this each one, full of longing and lament, spreads its wings towards its destination, in desire for its mate.

entries.farsi: پر گشايد هر دمى با اشك و آه / ‏ليك پريدن ندارد روى و راه
entries.english: At every moment it spreads its wings with tears and sighs, but it has no room or way to fly.

entries.farsi: راه شد هر يك پرد مانند باد / سوى آن كز ياد آن پر مى‏گشاد
entries.english: Way is made, each one flies like the wind towards that in remembrance of which it spread its wings.

entries.farsi: آن طرف كه بود اشك و آه او / چون كه فرصت يافت باشد راه او
entries.english: Its way, when it gains the opportunity, is towards the region whither its tears and sighs were.

entries.farsi: در تن خود بنگر اين اجزاى تن / ‏از كجاها گرد آمد در بدن
entries.english: Consider your own body: from what places were these corporeal parts collected in the body—

entries.farsi: آبى و خاكى و بادى و آتشى / ‏عرشى و فرشى و رومى و كشى
entries.english: Watery and earthen and airy and fiery, celestial and terrestrial, of Rum and of Kash.

entries.farsi: از اميد عود هر يك بسته طرف / ‏اندر اين كاروانسرا از بيم برف
entries.english: In this caravanseray one and all, from fear of the snow, have closed their eyes to the hope of returning.

entries.farsi: برف گوناگون جمود هر جماد / در شتاى بعد آن خورشيد داد
entries.english: The various snows are the congealing of every inanimate thing in the winter of farness from that Sun of justice.

entries.farsi: چون بتابد تف آن خورشيد خشم / ‏كوه گردد گاه ريگ و گاه پشم
entries.english: When the heat of the angry Sun flames, the mountain becomes now sand and now wool.

entries.farsi: در گداز آيد جمادات گران / ‏چون گداز تن به وقت نقل جان
entries.english: The gross inanimate things dissolve, like the dissolution of the body at the hour of the spirit’s departure.

entries.farsi: چون رسيدند اين سه همره منزلى / ‏هديه‏شان آورد حلوا مقبلى
entries.english: When these three fellow-travellers arrived at a certain hostelry, a man of fortune brought them halwá as a gift.

entries.farsi: برد حلوا پيش آن هر سه غريب / ‏محسنى از مطبخ انى قريب
entries.english: A benefactor brought to the three strangers halwá from the kitchen of Lo, I am near.

entries.farsi: نان گرم و صحن حلواى عسل / ‏برد آن كه در ثوابش بود امل
entries.english: One who had expectation of the reward brought warm bread and a dish of halwá made with honey.

entries.farsi: الكياسه و الادب الهل المدر / الضيافة و القرى الهل الوبر
entries.english: Intelligence and culture are characteristic of townsmen; hospitality and entertainment are characteristic of tent-dwellers.

entries.farsi: الضيافة للغريب و القرى / ودع الرحمن فى اهل القرى
entries.english: The Merciful has implanted hospitality to strangers and entertainment in the villagers.

entries.farsi: كل يوم فى القرى ضيف حديث / ‏ما له غير الاله من مغيث
entries.english: Every day in the villages there is a new guest who has none to help him except God.

entries.farsi: كل ليل فى القرى وفد جديد / ما لهم ثم سوى اللهَّ محيد
entries.english: Every night in the villages are new-comers who have no refuge there save God.

entries.farsi: تخمه بودند آن دو بيگانه ز خور / بود صايم روز آن مومن مگر
entries.english: The two aliens were surfeited with food and suffering from indigestion; the true believer, as it happened, was fasting day.

entries.farsi: چون نماز شام آن حلوا رسيد / بود مومن مانده در جوع شديد
entries.english: At the evening prayer, when the halwá arrived, the true believer was reduced to extreme hunger.

entries.farsi: آن دو كس گفتند ما از خور پريم / ‏امشبش بنهيم و فردايش خوريم
entries.english: The two said, “We have eaten our fill: let us put it away to-night and eat it tomorrow.

entries.farsi: صبر گيريم امشب از خور تن زنيم / ‏بهر فردا لوت را پنهان كنيم
entries.english: To-night let us practise self-denial and refrain from food; let us hide the dainty for to-morrow.”

entries.farsi: پس بدو گفتند زين حكمت‏گرى / ‏قصد تو آن است تا تنها خورى
entries.english: The true believer said, “Let this be eaten to-night; let us put away self-denial till to-morrow.”

entries.farsi: گفت مومن امشب اين خورده شود / صبر را بنهيم تا فردا بود
entries.english: Then they said to him, “Your purpose in this wisdom-mongering is that you may eat it by yourself.”

entries.farsi: گفت اى ياران نه كه ما سه تنيم / ‏چون خالف افتاد تا قسمت كنيم
entries.english: “O my friends,” said he, “are not we three persons? Since disagreement has occurred, let us share.

entries.farsi: هر كه خواهد قسم خود بر جان زند / هر كه خواهد قسم خود پنهان كند
entries.english: Let him who wishes take his own share to his heart; let him who wishes put his share in hiding.”
