entries.farsi: آن دو گفتندش ز قسمت در گذر / گوش كن قسام فى النار از خبر
entries.english: The two said to him, “Abandon sharing: give ear to ‘The sharer is in Hell-fire’ from the Traditions.”

entries.farsi: گفت قسام آن بود كاو خويش را / كرد قسمت بر هوا و بر خدا
entries.english: He replied, “The sharer is he that has shared himself between sensuality and God.”

entries.farsi: ملك حق و جمله قسم اوستى / ‏قسم ديگر را دهى دو گوستى
entries.english: You are God’s property and His share entirely: you give the share to another, you are a dualist.

entries.farsi: اين اسد غالب شدى هم بر سگان / ‏گر نبودى نوبت آن بد رگان
entries.english: This lion would have prevailed over the curs, if it had not been the turn of those evil-natured ones.

entries.farsi: قصدشان آن كآن مسلمان غم خورد / شب بر او در بى‏نوايى بگذرد
entries.english: It was their intention that the Moslem should suffer pain and pass the night in want of food.

entries.farsi: بود مغلوب او به تسليم و رضا / گفت سمعا طاعه اصحابنا
entries.english: He was overpowered: he said, with resignation and acquiescence, “My friends, I hear and obey.”

entries.farsi: پس بخفتند آن شب و برخاستند / بامدادان خويش را آراستند
entries.english: So they slept that night, and in the morning they rose and dressed themselves,

entries.farsi: روى شستند و دهان و هر يكى / ‏داشت اندر ورد راه و مسلكى
entries.english: And washed their faces and mouths; and each one had a method and practice in his devotions.

entries.farsi: يك زمانى هر كسى آورد رو / سوى ورد خويش از حق فضل جو
entries.english: For a while each one applied himself to his devotions, seeking favour from God.

entries.farsi: مومن و ترسا جهود و گبر و مغ / ‏جمله را رو سوى آن سلطان الغ
entries.english: True believer and Christian, Jew and Guebre and Magi— the faces of them all are towards that mighty Sultan.

entries.farsi: بلكه سنگ و خاك و كوه و آب را / هست واگشت نهانى با خدا
entries.english: Nay, stone and earth and mountain and water have their invisible recourse to God.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد هر سه يار / رو به هم كردند آن دم ياروار
entries.english: This topic is infinite. At that time the three companions looked on one another friendlily,

entries.farsi: آن يكى گفتا كه هر يك خواب خويش / ‏آن چه ديد او دوش گو آور به پيش
entries.english: And one said, “Let each relate what he dreamed last night.

entries.farsi: هر كه خوابش بهتر اين را او خورد / قسم هر مفضول را افضل برد
entries.english: Let him who had the best dream eat this: let the most excellent carry off the share of every one that is excelled.”

entries.farsi: آن كه اندر عقل بالاتر رود / خوردن او خوردن جمله بود
entries.english: He who mounts highest in reason— his eating is the eating of all.

entries.farsi: فوق آمد جان پر انوار او / باقيان را بس بود تيمار او
entries.english: His luminous spirit is supreme: it is enough for the rest of them to tend him.

entries.farsi: عاقالن را چون بقا آمد ابد / پس به معنى اين جهان باقى بود
entries.english: Since those endowed with reason endure forever, in reality this world is enduring forever.

entries.farsi: پس جهود آورد آن چه ديده بود / تا كجا شب روح او گرديده بود
entries.english: Then the Jew related his dream whither his spirit had wandered during the night.

entries.farsi: گفت در ره موسى‏ام آمد به پيش / ‏گربه بيند دنبه اندر خواب خويش
entries.english: He said, “Moses met me on the way, ‘the cat sees a fat sheep’s tail in her dreams.’

entries.farsi: در پى موسى شدم تا كوه طور / هر سه‏مان گشتيم ناپيدا ز نور
entries.english: I followed Moses to Mt Sinai: in the Light all three of us vanished.

entries.farsi: هر سه سايه محو شد ز آن آفتاب / ‏بعد از آن ز آن نور شد يك فتح باب
entries.english: All three shadows disappeared in the Sun; after that, there came from the Light an opening of the door.

entries.farsi: نور ديگر از دل آن نور رست / ‏پس ترقى جست آن ثانيش چست
entries.english: From the heart of that Light another Light sprang up, and then the second quickly sought to transcend it.

entries.farsi: هم من و هم موسى و هم كوه طور / هر سه گم گشتيم ز آن اشراق نور
entries.english: Both I and Moses and also Mt Sinai, we were lost, all three, in that effulgence of the Light.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن ديدم كه كه سه شاخ شد / چون كه نور حق در او نفاخ شد
entries.english: After that, I saw the mountain break into three pieces when the Light of God surged upon it.

entries.farsi: وصف هيبت چون تجلى زد بر او / مى‏گسست از هم همى‏شد سو به سو
entries.english: When the Attribute of Majesty was revealed to it, it burst asunder in every direction.

entries.farsi: آن يكى شاخ كه آمد سوى يم / ‏گشت شيرين آب تلخ همچو سم
entries.english: One piece of the mountain fell towards the sea, and the water bitter as poison was made sweet.

entries.farsi: آن يكى شاخش فرو شد در زمين / ‏چشمه‏ى دارو برون آمد معين
entries.english: One piece thereof sank into the earth, and a medicinal spring of running water gushed forth,

entries.farsi: آن يكى شاخ دگر پريد زود / تا جوار كعبه كه عرفات بود
entries.english: So that its water became a cure for all the sick by the blessedness of the goodly revelation.

entries.farsi: كه شفاى جمله رنجوران شد آب / ‏از همايونى وحى مستطاب
entries.english: The other piece flew at once to the neighbourhood of the Ka‘ba where ‘Arafat was.

entries.farsi: باز از آن صعقه چو با خود آمدم / ‏طور بر جا بد نه افزون و نه كم
entries.english: When I came back to myself out of that swoon, Sinai was in its place, neither greater nor less;

entries.farsi: ليك زير پاى موسى همچو يخ / ‏مى‏گدازيد او نماندش شاخ و شخ
entries.english: But under the foot of Moses it was melting like ice: no spur or peak of it remained.

entries.farsi: با زمين هموار شد كه از نهيب / ‏گشت بالايش از آن هيبت نشيب
entries.english: The mountain was levelled to the earth by terror: it was turned upside down by that awful Majesty.

entries.farsi: باز با خود آمدم ز آن انتشار / باز ديدم طور و موسى برقرار
entries.english: After that scattering I came to myself again and saw that Sinai and Moses were unchanged,

entries.farsi: و آن بيابان سر به سر در ذيل كوه / ‏پر خاليق شكل موسى در وجوه
entries.english: And that the desert skirting the mountain was filled from end to end with people resembling Moses in their faces.

entries.farsi: چون عصا و خرقه‏ى او خرقه‏شان / ‏جمله سوى طور خوش دامن كشان
entries.english: Their mantles were like his staff and mantle: all were speeding joyously towards Sinai.

entries.farsi: جمله كفها در دعا افراخته / ‏نغمه‏ى أَرِنِي به هم در ساخته
entries.english: All had lifted their hands in prayer and struck up together the tune of let me see You.

entries.farsi: باز آن غشيان چو از من رفت زود / صورت هر يك دگرگونم نمود
entries.english: Again, as soon as the trance departed from me, the form of each one seemed to me to be diverse.

entries.farsi: انبيا بودند ايشان اهل ود / اتحاد انبيايم فهم شد
entries.english: They were the prophets endowed with love: the unity of the prophets was apprehended by me.

entries.farsi: باز امالكى همى‏ديدم شگرف / ‏صورت ايشان بد از اجرام برف
entries.english: Again, I beheld some mighty angels: their outward form was of bodies of snow;

entries.farsi: حلقه‏ى ديگر ماليك مستعين / ‏صورت ايشان به جمله آتشين
entries.english: And another circle of angels asking help: their outward form was wholly of fire.”

entries.farsi: زين نسق مى‏گفت آن شخص جهود / بس جهودى كآخرش محمود بود
entries.english: On this wise did the Jew tell: there is many a Jew whose end was praiseworthy.

entries.farsi: هيچ كافر را به خوارى منگريد / كه مسلمان مردنش باشد اميد
entries.english: Do not regard any infidel with contempt, for there may be hope of his dying a Moslem.

entries.farsi: چه خبر دارى ز ختم عمر او / كه بگردانى از او يك باره رو
entries.english: What knowledge have you of the close of his life that you should once avert your face from him?

entries.farsi: بعد از آن ترسا در آمد در كالم / ‏كه مسيحم رو نمود اندر منام
entries.english: Afterwards the Christian began to speak, saying, “The Messiah appeared to me in my dream.

entries.farsi: من شدم با او به چارم آسمان / ‏مركز و مثواى خورشيد جهان
entries.english: I went with him to the Fourth Heaven, the centre and abode of the sun of this world.

entries.farsi: خود عجبهاى قالع آسمان / ‏نسبتش نبود به آيات جهان
entries.english: Truly, the marvels of the citadels of Heaven have no relation to the wonders of the world.

entries.farsi: هر كسى دانند اى فخر البنين / ‏كه فزون باشد فن چرخ از زمين
entries.english: Everyone knows, O pride of the sons, that the artifice of the celestial sphere exceeds the earth.”

sections.title_fa: ‏حكايت اشتر و گاو و قچ كه در راه بند گياه يافتند هر يكى مى‏گفت من خورم
sections.title_en: Story of the camel and the ox and the ram who found a bunch of grass on the road, and each said, “I will eat it.”

entries.farsi: اشتر و گاو و قچى در پيش راه / ‏يافتند اندر روش بندى گياه
entries.english: Whilst a camel, ox, and ram were going along, they found a bunch of grass in front of the road.

entries.farsi: گفت قچ بخش ار كنيم اين را يقين / ‏هيچ كس از ما نگردد سير از اين
entries.english: The ram said, “If we divide this, certainly none of us will get his fill of it;

entries.farsi: ليك عمر هر كه باشد بيشتر / اين علف او راست اولى گو بخور
entries.english: But whichever of us has lived longest has the best right to this fodder: let him eat;

entries.farsi: كه اكابر را مقدم داشتن / ‏آمده‏ست از مصطفى اندر سنن
entries.english: For to give the foremost place to the seniors has come from Mustafá among the practices observed by him,

entries.farsi: گر چه پيران را در اين دور لئام / ‏در دو موضع پيش مى‏دارند عام
entries.english: Although, at this time when vile men hold sway, the vulgar put forward the elders on two occasions,

entries.farsi: يا در آن لوتى كه آن سوزان بود / يا بر آن پل كز خلل ويران بود
entries.english: Either in food that is burning hot, or on a bridge that is by cracks in a state of ruin.

entries.farsi: خدمت شيخى بزرگى قايدى / ‏عام نآرد بى‏قرينه‏ى فاسدى
entries.english: The vulgar do not pay homage to a venerable Shaykh and leader without some mischievous idea associated.

entries.farsi: خيرشان اين است چه بود شرشان / ‏قبحشان را باز دان از فرشان
entries.english: This is their good: what must their evil be? Distinguish their foulness from their fairness.”

sections.title_en: Parable

entries.farsi: سوى جامع مى‏شد آن يك شهريار / خلق را مى‏زد نقيب و چوب دار
entries.english: A king was going to the congregational mosque, and the marshals and mace-bearers were beating the people off.

entries.farsi: آن يكى را سر شكستى چوب زن / ‏و آن دگر را بر دريدى پيرهن
entries.english: The wielder of the stick would break the head of one and tear to bits the shirt of another.

entries.farsi: در ميانه بى‏دلى ده چوب خورد / بى‏گناهى كه برو از راه برد
entries.english: A poor wretch amidst the throng received ten blows with the stick without any offence. “Begone,” they cried, “get out of the way!”

entries.farsi: خون چكان رو كرد با شاه و بگفت / ‏ظلم ظاهر بين چه پرسى از نهفت
entries.english: Dripping blood, he turned his face to the king and said, “Behold the manifest iniquity: why ask of that which is hidden?

entries.farsi: خير تو اين است جامع مى‏روى / ‏تا چه باشد شر و وزرت اى غوى
entries.english: This is your good: you are going to the mosque; what must your evil and burden be, O misguided one?”

entries.farsi: يك سالمى نشنود پير از خسى / ‏تا نپيچد عاقبت از وى بسى
entries.english: The Pír never hears a salaam from a base fellow without being exceedingly tormented by him in the end.

entries.farsi: گرگ دريابد ولى را به بود / ز انكه دريابد ولى را نفس بد
entries.english: If a wolf catch a saint, it is better than that the saint should be caught by the wicked carnal soul,

entries.farsi: ز انكه گرگ ار چه كه بس استمگرى است / ‏ليكش آن فرهنگ و كيد و مكر نيست
entries.english: Because, though the wolf does great violence, yet it has not the same knowledge and craft and cunning;

entries.farsi: ور نه كى اندر فتادى او به دام / ‏مكر اندر آدمى باشد تمام
entries.english: Else how should it fall into the trap? Cunning is complete in man.

entries.farsi: هر يكى تاريخ عمر ابدا كنيد / پيرتر اولى است باقى تن زنيد
entries.english: The ram said to the ox and the camel, “O comrades, since such a chance has come to us,

entries.farsi: گفت قچ با گاو و اشتر اى رفاق / ‏چون چنين افتاد ما را اتفاق
entries.english: Let each declare the date of his life: the oldest has the best right, let the others suffer in silence.

entries.farsi: گفت قچ مرج من اندر آن عهود / با قچ قربان اسماعيل بود
entries.english: In those times,” said the ram, “my pasturage was with the ram that was sacrificed for Ishmael.”

entries.farsi: گاو گفتا بوده‏ام من سال خورد / جفت آن گاوى كش آدم جفت كرد
entries.english: The ox said, “I am the advanced in years, coupled with the ox that Adam yoked.

entries.farsi: جفت آن گاوم كش آدم جد خلق / ‏در زراعت بر زمين مى‏كرد فلق
entries.english: I am the yoke-fellow of the ox with which Adam, the forefather of mankind, used to plough the earth in sowing.”

entries.farsi: چون شنيد از گاو و قچ اشتر شگفت / ‏سر فرو آورد و آن را بر گرفت
entries.english: When the camel heard the ox and the ram he was amazed: he lowered his head and picked up that.

entries.farsi: در هوا برداشت آن بند قصيل / ‏اشتر بختى سبك بى‏قال و قيل
entries.english: Promptly, without any palaver, the Bactrian camel raised the bunch of fresh barley in the air,

entries.farsi: كه مرا خود حاجت تاريخ نيست / كاين چنين جسمى و عالى گردنى است
entries.english: Saying, “I, in sooth, need no chronology, since I have such a body and high neck.

entries.farsi: خود همه كس داند اى جان پدر / كه نباشم از شما من خردتر
entries.english: Indeed everyone knows, O father’s darling, that I am not smaller than you.

entries.farsi: داند اين را هر كه ز اصحاب نهاست / ‏كه نهاد من فزون تر از شماست
entries.english: Whoever is one of those possessed of intelligence knows this, that my nature is superior to yours.”

entries.farsi: جملگان دانند كاين چرخ بلند / هست صد چندان كه اين خاك نژند
entries.english: “All know that this lofty heaven is a hundred times as great as this low earth.

entries.farsi: كو گشاد رقعه‏هاى آسمان / ‏كو نهاد بقعه‏هاى خاكدان
entries.english: How can the wide expanse of the celestial domains be compared with the character of the terrestrial regions?”

sections.title_fa: ‏جواب گفتن مسلمان آن چه ديد به يارانش جهود و ترسا و حسرت خوردن ايشان
sections.title_en: How the Moslem in reply told his companions, the Jew and the Christian, what he had seen, and how they were disappointed.

entries.farsi: پس مسلمان گفت اى ياران من / ‏پيشم آمد مصطفى سلطان من
entries.english: Then the Moslem said, “O my friends, Mustafá came to me, my sovereign,

entries.farsi: پس مرا گفت آن يكى بر طور تاخت / ‏با كليم حق و نرد عشق باخت
entries.english: And said to me, ‘that one has sped to Sinai with him to whom God spoke, and has played the game of love;

entries.farsi: خيز اى پس مانده‏ى ديده ضرر / بارى آن حلوا و يخنى را بخور
entries.english: And the other has been carried by Jesus, the Lord of happy star, to the zenith of the Fourth Heaven.

entries.farsi: و آن دگر را عيسى صاحب قران / ‏برد بر اوج چهارم آسمان
entries.english: Arise, O you who have been left behind and have suffered injury, at least eat up the sweetmeat and comfit!

entries.farsi: آن هنرمندان پر فن راندند / نامه‏ى اقبال و منصب خواندند
entries.english: Those (two) talented and accomplished men have pushed forward and have read the book of fortune and honour.

entries.farsi: آن دو فاضل فضل خود دريافتند / اى سليم گول واپس مانده هين
entries.english: Those two eminent men have attained to their eminence and because of their talents have mingled with the angels.

entries.farsi: ‏بر جه و بر كاسه‏ى حلوا نشين / پس بگفتندش كه آن گه تو حريص
entries.english: Listen, O foolish simpleton who have been left behind, jump up and seat yourself beside the bowl of halwá!’”

entries.farsi: ‏اى عجب خوردى ز حلواى خبيص / گفت چون فرمود آن شاه مطاع
entries.english: Thereupon they said to him, “Then, you greedy fellow, have you made a meal of the halwá and khabís? Oh, an astonishing thing!”

entries.farsi: ‏من كه بودم تا كنم ز آن امتناع / تو جهود از امر موسى سركشى
entries.english: He replied, “When that sovereign who is obeyed gave the order, who was I that I should resist it?

entries.farsi: ‏گر بخواند در خوشى يا ناخوشى / تو مسيحى هيچ از امر مسيح
entries.english: Will you, Jew, rebel against the command of Moses if he summons you in a fair cause or a foul?

entries.farsi: ‏خورده‏ام حلوا و اين دم سر خوشم / پس بگفتندش كه و اهلل خواب راست
entries.english: Can you, Christian, ever spurn the command of Christ for good or evil?

entries.farsi: ‏سر توانى تافت در خير و قبيح / من ز فخر انبيا سرچون كشم
entries.english: How, should I rebel against the Glory of the prophets? I have eaten the halwá and now I am happy.”

entries.farsi: ‏تو بديدى وين به از صد خواب ماست / خواب تو بيدارى است اى بو بطر
entries.english: Then they said to him, “By God, you have dreamed a true dream, and it is better than a hundred dreams of ours.

entries.farsi: كه به بيدارى عيانستش اثر / در گذر از فضل و از جلدى و فن
entries.english: Your dreaming is waking, O gleeful one, for its effect is made evident by waking.”

entries.farsi: ‏كار خدمت دارد و خلق حسن / بهر اين آوردمان يزدان برون
entries.english: Abandon eminence and energy and skill: what matters is service and a goodly disposition.

entries.farsi: ‏ما خلقت اإلنس إال يعبدون / سامرى را آن هنر چه سود كرد
entries.english: For this God brought us forth: “I did not create mankind except to serve Me.”

entries.farsi: كآن فن از باب اللهش مردود كرد / چه كشيد از كيميا قارون ببين
entries.english: How did that knowledge profit Sámirí, whom the skill banished from God’s door?

entries.farsi: ‏كه فرو بردش به قعر خود زمين / بو الحكم آخر چه بر بست از هنر
entries.english: What did Qárún gain by his alchemy? See how the earth bore him down to its abyss.

entries.farsi: سر نگون رفت او ز كفران در سقر / خود هنر آن دان كه ديد آتش عيان
entries.english: What, after all, did Bu ’l-Hakam get from knowledge? On account of his unbelief he went headlong into Hell.

entries.farsi: نه گپ دل على النار الدخان / اى دليلت گنده‏تر پيش لبيب
entries.english: Know that knowledge consists in seeing fire plainly, not in prating that smoke is evidence of fire.

entries.farsi: ‏در حقيقت از دليل آن طبيب / چون دليلت نيست جز اين اى پسر
entries.english: O you whose evidence in the eyes of the Sage is really more stinking than the evidence of the physician,

entries.farsi: گوه مى‏خور در گميزى مى‏نگر / اى دليل تو مثال آن عصا
entries.english: Since you have no evidence but this, O son, eat dung and inspect urine!

entries.farsi: ‏در كفت دل على عيب العمى / غلغل و طاق و طرنب و گير و دار
entries.english: O you whose evidence is like the staff in your hand indicate that you suffer from blindness,

entries.farsi: كه نمى‏بينم مرا معذور دار / سيد ترمد كه آن جا شاه بود
entries.english: Noise and pompous talk and assumption of authority, “I cannot see: excuse me.”

sections.title_fa: منادى كردن سيد ملك ترمد كه هر كه در سه يا چهار روز به سمرقند روند به فالن مهم خلعت و اسب و غالم و كنيزك و چندين زر دهم، و شنيدن دلقك خبر اين منادى در ده و آمدن به اوالقى نزد شاه كه من ‏بارى نتوانم رفتن
sections.title_en: How the Sayyid, the King of Tirmid, proclaimed that he would give robes of honour

and horses and slave-boys and slave-girls and a large sum in gold to anyone who would go on urgent business to Samarkand in three or four days; and how Dalqak, having heard the news of this proclamation in the country, came post-haste to the king, saying, “I, at all events, cannot go.”

entries.farsi: مسخره‏ى او دلقك آگاه بود / داشت كارى در سمرقند او مهم
entries.english: The sagacious Dalqak was the buffoon of the Sayyid of Tirmid, who reigned in that place.

entries.farsi: ‏جست الاقى تا شود او مستتم / زد منادى هر كه اندر پنج روز
entries.english: He had an urgent affair in Samarkand, and wanted a courier in order that he might conclude it.

entries.farsi: آردم ز آن جا خبر بدهم كنوز / دلقك اندر ده بد و آن را شنيد
entries.english: He proclaimed that he would bestow treasures on any one who should bring him news from there in five days.

entries.farsi: بر نشست و تا به ترمذ مى‏دويد / مركبى دو اندر آن ره شد سقط
entries.english: Dalqak was in the country and heard of that: he mounted and galloped to Tirmid.

entries.farsi: از دوانيدن فرس را ز آن نمط / پس به ديوان در دويد از گرد راه
entries.english: Two horses dropped on the way because of his galloping in that manner.

entries.farsi: شورشى در وهم آن سلطان فتاد / خاص و عام شهر را دل شد ز دست
entries.english: Then, from the dust of the road, he ran into the council-chamber and demanded admission to the king at an untimely hour.

entries.farsi: ‏وقت ناهنگام ره جست او به شاه / فجفجى در جمله‏ى ديوان فتاد
entries.english: A whispered rumour arose in the council and the Sultan became agitated.

entries.farsi: ‏تا چه تشويش و بال حادث شده‏ست / يا عدوى قاهرى در قصد ماست
entries.english: The hearts of the nobles and populace of the city were stricken with panic, what disturbance and calamity had occurred,

entries.farsi: ‏يا باليى مهلكى از غيب خاست / كه زده دلقك به سيران درشت
entries.english: “Either a conquering enemy is about to attack us or a deadly calamity has emerged from the Unseen,

entries.farsi: ‏چند اسب تازى اندر راه كشت / جمع گشته بر سراى شاه خلق
entries.english: For Dalqak, riding hard from the country has killed several Arab horses on the way.”

entries.farsi: غلغل و تشويش در ترمد فتاد / آن يكى دو دست بر زانو زنان
entries.english: The people gathered at the king’s palace, wondering why Dalqak had come in such a hurry.

entries.farsi: ‏تا چرا آمد چنين اشتاب دلق / از شتاب او و فحش اجتهاد
entries.english: Because of his haste and the enormity of his exertions, tumult and commotion arose in Tirmid;

entries.farsi: ‏و آن دگر از وهم وا ويال كنان / از نفير و فتنه و خوف نكال
entries.english: One man beating both hands against his knee, while another, from presentiment of evil, was uttering woeful cries.

entries.farsi: ‏هر دلى رفته به صد كوى خيال / هر كسى فالى همى‏زد از قياس
entries.english: On account of the hubbub and distraction and the dread of punishment every heart went to a hundred streets of phantasy.

entries.farsi: ‏تا چه آتش اوفتاد اندر پالس / راه جست و راه دادش شاه زود
entries.english: Everyone was taking an omen by conjecture what had set the rug on fire.

entries.farsi: چون زمين بوسيد گفتش هى چه بود / هر كه مى‏پرسيد حالى ز آن ترش
entries.english: He sought admission and the king at once granted it to him. When he kissed the earth, the king said to him, “Hey, what’s the matter?”

entries.farsi: ‏دست بر لب مى‏نهاد او كه خمش / وهم مى‏افزود زين فرهنگ او
entries.english: Whenever anyone asked that sour-faced man for some particulars, he laid his hand on his lips as though to say “Hush!”

entries.farsi: جمله در تشويش گشته دنگ او / كرد اشارت دلق كاى شاه كرم
entries.english: Apprehension was increased by his gravity: all were perplexed and dumbfounded by him.

entries.farsi: ‏يك دمى بگذار تا من دم زنم / تا كه باز آيد به من عقلم دمى
entries.english: Dalqak made a gesture, as though to say, “O gracious king, let me have a moment to take breath,

entries.farsi: ‏كه فتادم در عجايب عالمى / بعد يك ساعت كه شه از وهم و ظن
entries.english: That my wits may once come back to me, for I am fallen into a marvellous state.”

entries.farsi: ‏تلخ گشتش هم گلو و هم دهن / كه نديده بود دلقك را چنين
entries.english: After a little while, during which both the throat and the mouth of the king were made bitter by foreboding and surmise—

entries.farsi: ‏شاه را او شاد و خندان داشتى / آن چنان خندانش كردى در نشست
entries.english: Because he had never seen Dalqak like this; for there was no companion more agreeable to him than he;

entries.farsi: ‏كه از او خوشتر نبودش همنشين / دايما دستان و الغ افراشتى
entries.english: He was always bringing up stories and jests and keeping the king in merriment and laughter.

entries.farsi: ‏كه گرفتى شه شكم را با دو دست / كه ز زور خنده خوى كردى تنش
entries.english: When sitting he used to make him laugh so that the king would grip his belly with both hands;

entries.farsi: ‏رو در افتادى ز خنده كردنش / باز امروز اين چنين زرد و ترش
entries.english: And his body sweated from the violence of his laughter and he would fall on his face with laughing.

entries.farsi: ‏دست بر لب مى‏زند كاى شه خمش / وهم در وهم و خيال اندر خيال
entries.english: To-day, on the contrary, he, pale and grim like this, is laying his hand on his lips as though to say, “Hush, O King!”

entries.farsi: ‏شاه را تا خود چه آيد از نكال / كه دل شه با غم و پرهيز بود
entries.english: Foreboding on foreboding and fancy on fancy to the king what chastisement would come,

entries.farsi: ز انكه خوارزمشاه بس خون‏ريز بود / بس شهان آن طرف را كشته بود
entries.english: For the king’s heart was anxious and alarmed because the Khwárizmsháh was very bloodthirsty,

entries.farsi: يا به حيله يا به سطوت آن عنود / اين شه ترمد از او در وهم بود
entries.english: And that perverse had killed many kings in that region either by craft or violence.

entries.farsi: و ز فن دلقك خود آن وهمش فزود / گفت زوتر باز گو تا حال چيست
entries.english: This King of Tirmid was apprehensive of him, and his apprehension was increased by the artifice of Dalqak.

entries.farsi: اين چنين آشوب و شور تو ز كيست / گفت من در ده شنيدم آن كه شاه
entries.english: He said, “Be quick! Tell what is the matter. Who is the cause of your being so perturbed and agitated?”

entries.farsi: ‏زد منادى بر سر هر شاه راه / كه كسى خواهم كه تازد در سه روز
entries.english: He replied, “I heard in the country that the king had proclaimed on every highway

entries.farsi: تا سمرقند و دهم او را كنوز / من شتابيدم بر تو بهر آن
entries.english: That he required someone to run to Samarkand in three days and would bestow treasures.

entries.farsi: ‏تا بگويم كه ندارم آن توان / اين چنين جستى نيايد از چو من
entries.english: I hurried to you in order to say that I am not able to do it.

entries.farsi: ‏بارى اين اوميد را بر من متن / گفت شه لعنت بر اين زوديت باد
entries.english: For one like me such agility is impossible: at all events do not expect this of me.”

entries.farsi: كه دو صد تشويش در شهر اوفتاد / از براى اين قدر اى خام ريش
entries.english: “Curse your hurry!” cried the king; “for a hundred confusions have arisen in the city.

entries.farsi: ‏آتش افكندى در اين مرج و حشيش / همچو اين خامان با طبل و علم
entries.english: For this trifle, O half-baked fool, you have set fire to this meadow and hay?”

entries.farsi: ‏كه الاقانيم در فقر و عدم / الف شيخى در جهان انداخته
entries.english: Like these raw persons with drum and banner, saying, “We are couriers in poverty and non-existence,”

entries.farsi: ‏خويشتن را بايزيدى ساخته / هم ز خود سالك شده واصل شده
entries.english: Boast far and wide of being Shaykhs and make out that they have the rank of Bayazid,

entries.farsi: ‏محفلى واكرده در دعوى‏كده / خانه‏ى داماد پر آشوب و شر
entries.english: And, having travelled away from themselves and become united, open a secret religious gathering in the abode of pretension.

entries.farsi: قوم دختر را نبوده زين خبر / ولوله كه كار نيمى راست شد
entries.english: The bridegroom’s house is full of turmoil and trouble; the girl’s family knows nothing about it.

entries.farsi: شرطهايى كه ز سوى ماست شد / خانه‏ها را روفتيم آراستيم
entries.english: An outcry, saying, “Half the affair is concluded: the conditions that are on our side have been fulfilled.

entries.farsi: ‏زين هوس سر مست و خوش برخاستيم / ز آن طرف آمد يكى پيغام نى
entries.english: We have swept and garnished the rooms and have risen up intoxicated and glad with this ardent desire.”

entries.farsi: يك جوابى ز آن حواليتان رسيد / نى و ليكن يار ما زين آگه است
entries.english: Has any message come from over there? “No.” Has any bird come here from that roof? “No.”

entries.farsi: ‏مرغى آمد اين طرف ز آن بام نى / زين رسالات مزيد اندر مزيد
entries.english: After these missives one on the top of another, has any answer reached you from that neighbourhood?

entries.farsi: ‏ز انكه از دل سوى دل ال بد ره است / پس از آن يارى كه اوميد شماست
entries.english: “No; but our Friend is acquainted with this, because inevitably there is a way from heart to heart.”

entries.farsi: ‏از جواب نامه ره خالى چراست / صد نشان است از سرار و از جهار
entries.english: Why, then, is the way devoid of answer to letter from the Friend who is your hope?

entries.farsi: ليك بس كن پرده زين در بر مدار / باز رو تا قصه‏ى آن دلق گول
entries.english: There are a hundred signs, secret and manifest; but desist, do not lift the curtain from this door.

entries.farsi: ‏كه بال بر خويش آورد از فضول / پس وزيرش گفت اى حق را ستن
entries.english: Return to the story of that foolish Dalqak who brought tribulation on himself by his silly meddling.

entries.farsi: ‏بشنو از بنده‏ى كمينه يك سخن / دلقك از ده بهر كارى آمده‏ست
entries.english: Afterwards the vizier said to him, “O Pillar of the Truth, hear a word from your humble slave.

entries.farsi: ‏راى او گشت و پشيمانش شده‏ست / ز آب و روغن كهنه را نو مى‏كند
entries.english: Dalqak came from the country on some enterprise; his mind is changed and he has repented.

entries.farsi: او به مسخرگى برون شو مى‏كند / غمد را بنمود و پنهان كرد تيغ
entries.english: He is making the old new with water and oil, he is evading by means of buffoonery.

entries.farsi: ‏بايد افشردن مر او را بى‏دريغ / پسته را يا جوز را تا نشكنى
entries.english: He has displayed the scabbard and concealed the sword: he must be tortured without mercy.

entries.farsi: ‏نه نمايد دل نه بدهد روغنى / مشنو اين دفع وى و فرهنگ او
entries.english: Unless you break the pistachio or walnut, it will neither reveal its heart nor give any oil.

entries.farsi: در نگر در ارتعاش و رنگ او / گفت حق سيماهم فى وجههم
entries.english: Do not listen to this skilful defence of his; look at his trembling and his colour.

entries.farsi: ‏ز انكه غماز است سيما و منم / اين معاين هست ضد آن خبر
entries.english: God has said, ‘Their mark is on their faces,’ for the mark is an informer and tell-tale.

entries.farsi: كه به شر بسرشته آمد اين بشر / گفت دلقك با فغان و با خروش
entries.english: This ocular evidence is opposed to that story, for this mankind is moulded of evil.”

entries.farsi: كآن نباشد حق و صادق اى امير / إِنَّ بَعْضَ الظَّ نِّ إِثْمٌ است اى وزير
entries.english: “O Sahib,” cried Dalqak, wailing and sobbing, “do not endeavour to shed the blood of this miserable wretch.

entries.farsi: ‏صاحبا در خون اين مسكين مكوش / بس گمان و وهم آيد در ضمير
entries.english: Many a thought and fancy that is not real and true comes into the mind, O Prince.

entries.farsi: نيست استم راست خاصه بر فقير / شه نگيرد آن كه مى‏رنجاندش
entries.english: Truly, some suspicion is a sin, O Vizier: injustice is not right, especially to a poor man.

entries.farsi: ‏از چه گيرد آن كه مى‏خنداندش / گفت صاحب پيش شه جا گير شد
entries.english: The king does not chastise one who vexes him: wherefore should he chastise one who makes him laugh?”

entries.farsi: كاشف اين مكر و اين تزوير شد / گفت دلقك را سوى زندان بريد
entries.english: The words of the Sahib impressed the king, and he resolved to clear up this deceit and imposture.

entries.farsi: چاپلوس و زرق او را كم خريد / مى‏زنيدش چون دهل اشكم تهى
entries.english: “Take Dalqak to prison,” he said, “and pay no attention to his wheedling and hypocrisy.

entries.farsi: ‏تا دهل‏وار او دهدمان آگهى / تر و خشك و پر و تى باشد دهل
entries.english: Beat him, empty-bellied like a drum, that like a drum he may give us information.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه گيرد اين دلها قرار / چون طمانينه‏ست صدق با فروغ
entries.english: The drum is wet or dry or full or empty, its sound informs us of everything.

entries.farsi: ‏بانگ او آگه كند ما را ز كل / تا بگويد سر خود از اضطرار
entries.english: In order that he may be compelled to declare the secret, so that these hearts will be reassured.

entries.farsi: ‏دل نيارامد به گفتار دروغ / كذب چون خس باشد و دل چون دهان
entries.english: Since the shining truth is tranquil, the heart will not be calmed by lying words.

entries.farsi: ‏خس نگردد در دهان هرگز نهان / تا در او باشد زبانى مى‏زند
entries.english: Falsehood is like a straw, and the heart like a mouth: a straw never becomes hidden in the mouth.

entries.farsi: تا بد آن‏اش از دهان بيرون كند / خاصه كه در چشم افتد خس ز باد
entries.english: So long as it is there, he keeps moving his tongue, in order that thereby he may eject it from his mouth.

entries.farsi: چشم افتد در نم و بند و گشاد / ما پس اين خس را زنيم اكنون لگد
entries.english: Especially, when a straw by the wind falls into the eye, the eye begins to water and shut and open.

entries.farsi: تا دهان و چشم از اين خس وارهد / گفت دلقك اى ملك آهسته باش
entries.english: We, therefore, now kick this straw, i n order that our mouth and eye may be delivered from this straw.”

entries.farsi: ‏روى حلم و مغفرت را كم خراش / تا بدين حد چيست تعجيل نقم
entries.english: Dalqak said, “O King, be calm: do not scratch the face of clemency and forgiveness.

entries.farsi: ‏من نمى‏پرم به دست تو درم / آن ادب كه باشد از بهر خدا
entries.english: Why such an excessive haste to take revenge? I cannot fly away; I am in your hand.

entries.farsi: اندر آن مستعجلى نبود روا / و انچه باشد طبع و خشم عارضى
entries.english: It is not right to be haste in correction that is for God’s sake;

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏شتابد تا نگردد مرتضى / ترسد ار آيد رضا خشمش رود
entries.english: That which is humour and casual anger, he is in a hurry lest he should become content.

entries.farsi: انتقام و ذوق آن فايت شود / شهوت كاذب شتابد در طعام
entries.english: He is afraid that, if contentment comes and his anger goes: his revenge and the pleasure of it will be lost.

entries.farsi: ‏خوف فوت ذوق هست آن خود سقام / اشتها صادق بود تاخير به
entries.english: False appetite makes haste to the food for fear of missing the pleasure: that is sickness indeed.

entries.farsi: ‏تا ببينى رخنه را بندش كنى / تا از آن رخنه برون نايد بال
entries.english: The appetite be true, it is better to delay, in order that it may be digested without difficulty.

entries.farsi: ‏تا گواريده شود آن بى‏گره / تو پى دفع باليم مى‏زنى
entries.english: Will you beat me for the purpose of averting a calamity, to the end that you may see the crevice and block it up,

entries.farsi: غير آن رخنه بسى دارد قضا / چاره‏ى دفع بال نبود ستم
entries.english: So that the calamity will not issue from that crevice? Destiny has many a crevice besides that one.

entries.farsi: ‏داو مرضاك بصدقه يا فتى / صدقه نبود سوختن درويش را
entries.english: Violence is not the means of averting calamity: the means is beneficence and pardon and kindness.

entries.farsi: ‏چاره احسان باشد و عفو و كرم / گفت الصدقة مرد للبال
entries.english: He said, ‘Alms is a means of averting calamity: cure your diseased ones by alms, O youth.’

entries.farsi: كور كردن چشم حلم انديش را / گفت شه نيكوست خير و موقعش
entries.english: It is not alms-giving to burn a poor man and to blind the eye that meditates on forbearance.”

entries.farsi: ليك چون خيرى كنى در موضعش / موضع رخ شه نهى ويرانى است
entries.english: The king replied, “Charity and the occasion for it are excellent, but when you perform an act of charity in its place.

entries.farsi: ‏موضع شه اسب هم نادانى است / در شريعت هم عطا هم زجر هست
entries.english: You put the king in the rook’s place, it is ruin; likewise, the horse in the king’s place, it is the act of an ignoramus.

entries.farsi: ‏شاه را صدر و فرس را درگه است / عدل چه بود وضع اندر موضعش
entries.english: Both bounty and severity are in the religious Law: for the king the throne, for the horse the gate.

entries.farsi: ‏ظلم چه بود وضع در ناموقعش / نيست باطل هر چه يزدان آفريد
entries.english: What is justice? To put in its place. What is injustice? To put it in its wrong place.

entries.farsi: از غضب و ز حلم و ز نصح و مكيد / خير مطلق نيست زينها هيچ چيز
entries.english: Nothing is vain that God created, anger or forbearance or sincere counsel or guile.

entries.farsi: شر مطلق نيست زينها هيچ نيز / نفع و ضر هر يكى از موضع است
entries.english: None of these things is absolutely good, nor is any of them absolutely evil.

entries.farsi: ‏علم از اين رو واجبست و نافع است / اى بسا زجرى كه بر مسكين رود
entries.english: The usefulness and harm of each depend on the place: for this reason knowledge is necessary and useful.

entries.farsi: در ثواب از نان و حلوا به بود / ز انكه حلوا بى‏اوان صفرا كند
entries.english: Oh, many a punishment inflicted on a poor fellow is more meritorious than bread and sweetmeat,

entries.farsi: سيلى‏اش از خبث مستنقا كند / سيليى در وقت بر مسكين بزن
entries.english: For sweetmeat unseasonably causes yellow bile, slaps purge him of wickedness.

entries.farsi: ‏كه رهاند آنش از گردن زدن / زخم در معنى فتد از خوى بد
entries.english: Give the poor fellow a slap in season: it will save him from beheading.

entries.farsi: چوب بر گرد اوفتد نه بر نمد / بزم و زندان هست هر بهرام را
entries.english: The blow is really inflicted because of evil disposition: the stick falls on the dust, not on the felt.

entries.farsi: بزم مخلص را و زندان خام را / شق بايد ريش را مرهم كنى
entries.english: Every Bahram has a banquet-hall and a prison: the banquet is for the sincere and the prison for the half-baked.

entries.farsi: ‏چرك را در ريش مستحكم كنى / تا خورد مر گوشت را در زير آن
entries.english: A sore wants lancing and you apply a poultice to it, you will establish the pus in the sore,

entries.farsi: ‏نيم سودى باشد و پنجه زيان / گفت دلقك من نمى‏گويم گذار
entries.english: So that it will eat away the flesh underneath: will be a half of profit and fifty losses.”

entries.farsi: من همى‏گويم تحريى بيار / هين ره صبر و تانى در مبند
entries.english: Dalqak said, “I am not saying, ‘Let pass’; I am saying, ‘Take some care to investigate.’

entries.farsi: صبر كن انديشه مى‏كن روز چند / در تانى بر يقينى بر زنى
entries.english: Listen, do not bar the road of patience and deliberation: be patient, reflect for a few days.

entries.farsi: ‏گوشمال من به ايقانى كنى / در روش يَمْشِي مُكِبًّا خود چرا
entries.english: In deliberation you will hit upon a certainty, you will chastise me with a sure conviction.”

entries.farsi: چون همى شايد شدن در استوا / مشورت كن با گروه صالحان
entries.english: Why, indeed, walks falling in wayfaring, when it behooves him to walk in an upright posture?

entries.farsi: كز تشاور سهو و كژ كمتر رود / اين خردها چون مصابيح انور است
entries.english: Take counsel with the company of the righteous: note the command to the Prophet, “Consult them.”

entries.farsi: ‏بر پيمبر امر شاوِرْهُمْ بدان / أَمْرُهُمْ شُورى‏ براى اين بود
entries.english: Their affair is consultation are to this purpose, for owing to consultation mistakes and errors occur less.

entries.farsi: ‏بيست مصباح از يكى روشن‏تر است / بو كه مصباحى فتد اندر ميان
entries.english: These intellects are luminous like lamps: twenty lamps are brighter than one.

entries.farsi: ‏سفلى و علوى به هم آميخته‏ست / گفت سِيرُوا مى‏طلب اندر جهان
entries.english: There may happen to be amongst them a lamp that has become aflame with the light of Heaven,

entries.farsi: ‏مشتعل گشته ز نور آسمان / غيرت حق پرده‏اى انگيخته‏ست
entries.english: The jealousy of God has produced a veil and has mingled the low and the lofty together.

entries.farsi: ‏بخت و روزى را همى‏كن امتحان / در مجالس مى‏طلب اندر عقول
entries.english: He has said, “Travel”: always seek in the world and try your fortune and lot.

entries.farsi: ‏آن چنان عقلى كه بود اندر رسول / ز انكه ميراث از رسول آن است و بس
entries.english: In assembly-places always be seeking amidst the intellects such an intellect as is in the Prophet,

entries.farsi: ‏كه ببيند غيبها از پيش و پس / در بصرها مى‏طلب هم آن بصر
entries.english: For the only heritage from the Prophet is that which perceives the unseen things before and behind.

entries.farsi: كه نتابد شرح آن اين مختصر / بهر اين كردست منع آن باشكوه
entries.english: Amidst the eyes, too, always be seeking that eye which this epitome has not the power to describe.

entries.farsi: كآن نظر بخت است و اكسير بقا / در ميان صالحان يك اصلحى است
entries.english: Hence the majestic has forbidden monkery and going to live as a hermit in the mountains,

entries.farsi: ‏از ترهب وز شدن خلوت به كوه / تا نگردد فوت اين نوع التقا
entries.english: In order that this kind of meeting should not be lost; for to be looked on by them is fortune and an elixir of immortality.

entries.farsi: ‏بر سر توقيعش از سلطان صحى است / كآن دعا شد با اجابت مقترن
entries.english: Amongst the righteous there is one the most righteous: his diploma verified by the Sultan’s hand a sahh,

entries.farsi: ‏كفو او نبود كبار انس و جن / در مرى‏اش آن كه حلو و حامض است
entries.english: That the prayer is linked with acceptance, the greatest of men and Jinn are not his peers.

entries.farsi: ‏حجت ايشان بر حق داحض است / كه چو ما او را به خود افراشتيم
entries.english: Those who are sweet or sour in contention with him, in God’s sight their argument is null,

entries.farsi: ‏عذر و حجت از ميان برداشتيم / قبله را چون كرد دست حق عيان
entries.english: For, “As We have exalted him by Ourselves, We have done away with plea and argument .”

entries.farsi: پس تحرى بعد از اين مردود دان / هين بگردان از تحرى رو و سر
entries.english: Since the Hand of God has made the Qibla manifest, henceforth deem searching to be disallowed.

entries.farsi: كه پديد آمد معاد و مستقر / يك زمان زين قبله گر ذاهل شوى
entries.english: Listen; avert your face and head from searching, now that the Destination and Dwelling-place has come into view.

entries.farsi: ‏سخره‏ى هر قبله‏ى باطل شوى / چون شوى تمييز ده را ناسپاس
entries.english: If you forget this Qibla for one moment, you will become in thrall to every worthless qibla.

entries.farsi: ‏بجهد از تو خطرت قبله شناس / گر از اين انبار خواهى بر و بر
entries.english: When you show ingratitude to him that gives you discernment, the thought that recognises the Qibla will dart away from you.

entries.farsi: نيم ساعت هم ز هم دردان مبر / كه در آن دم كه ببرى زين معين
entries.english: If you desire benefit and wheat from this Barn, do not part, even for half an hour, from those who sympathise,

entries.farsi: ‏مبتال گردى تو با بئس القرين / از قضا موشى و چغزى با وفا
entries.english: For at the moment when you part from this helper you will be afflicted with an evil comrade

sections.title_fa: حكايت تعلق موش با چغز و بستن پاى هر دو به رشته‏ى دراز و بر كشيدن زاغ موش را و معلق شدن ‏چغز و ناليدن او و پشيمانى او از تعلق با غير جنس و با جنس خود ناساختن
sections.title_en: Story of the attachment between the mouse and the frog: how they tied their legs together with a long string, and how a raven carried off the mouse,
and how the frog was suspended and lamented and repented of having attached himself to an animal of a different species instead of sorting with one of his own kind.

entries.farsi: بر لب جو گشته بودند آشنا / هر دو تن مربوط ميقاتى شدند
entries.english: As it happened, a mouse and a faithful frog had become friends on the bank of a river.

entries.farsi: هر صباحى گوشه‏اى مى‏آمدند / نرد دل با همدگر مى‏باختند
entries.english: Both of them were bound to a tryst: every morning they would come into a nook,

entries.farsi: از وساوس سينه مى‏پرداختند / هر دو را دل از تالقى متسع
entries.english: They played heart-and-soul with one another and emptied their breasts of evil thoughts.

entries.farsi: همدگر را قصه خوان و مستمع / ازگويان با زبان و بى‏زبان
entries.english: The hearts of both swelled from meeting: they recited stories and listened to each other,

entries.farsi: ‏الجماعة رحمه را تاويل دان / آن اشر چون جفت آن شاد آمدى
entries.english: Telling secrets with and without tongue, knowing how to interpret, “A united party is a mercy.”

entries.farsi: ‏پنج ساله قصه‏اش ياد آمدى / جوش نطق از دل نشان دوستى است
entries.english: Whenever the exultant consorted with the merry, a five years’ tale would come into his mind.

entries.farsi: ‏بستگى نطق از بى‏الفتى است / دل كه دل بر ديد كى ماند ترش
entries.english: Flow of speech from the heart is a sign of friendship; obstruction of speech arises from lack of intimacy.

entries.farsi: ‏بلبلى گل ديد كى ماند خمش / ماهى بريان ز آسيب خضر
entries.english: The heart that has seen the sweetheart, how should it remain bitter? A nightingale has seen the rose, how should he remain silent?

entries.farsi: زنده شد در بحر گشت او مستقر / يار را با يار چون بنشسته شد
entries.english: At the touch of Khadir the roasted fish came to life and took its abode in the sea.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران لوح سر دانسته شد / لوح محفوظى است پيشانى يار
entries.english: To the friend, when he is seated beside his Friend, a hundred thousand tablets of mystery are made known.

entries.farsi: راز كونينش نمايد آشكار / هادى راهست يار اندر قدوم
entries.english: The brow of the Friend is a Guarded Tablet: to him it reveals plainly the secret of the two worlds.

entries.farsi: ‏مصطفى زين گفت اصحابى نجوم / نجم اندر ريگ و دريا رهنماست
entries.english: The Friend is the guide on the way during advance: hence Mustafá said, “My Companions are the stars.”

entries.farsi: ‏چشم اندر نجم نه كو مقتداست / چشم را با روى او مى‏دار جفت
entries.english: The star shows the way in sands and on the sea: fix your eye on the Star, for he is the one to be followed.

entries.farsi: ‏گرد منگيزان ز راه بحث و گفت / ز انكه گردد نجم پنهان ز آن غبار
entries.english: Keep your eye always paired with his face: do not stir up dust by way of discussion and argument,

entries.farsi: چشم بهتر از زبان با عثار / تا بگويد او كه وحى استش شعار
entries.english: Because the Star will be hidden by that dust: the eye is better than the stumbling tongue.

entries.farsi: كآن نشاند گرد و ننگيزد غبار / چون شد آدم مظهر وحى و وداد
entries.english: In order that he may speak whose innermost garment is inspiration which lays the dust and does not stir up trouble.

entries.farsi: ناطقه‏ى او علم الاسماء گشاد / نام هر چيزى چنان كه هست آن
entries.english: When Adam became the theatre of inspiration and love, his rational soul revealed the knowledge of the Names.

entries.farsi: ‏از صحيفه‏ى دل روى گشتش زبان / فاش مى‏گفتى زبان از رويتش
entries.english: His tongue, from the page of his heart, recited the name of everything as it is.

entries.farsi: ‏جمله را خاصيت و ماهيتش / آن چنان نامى كه اشيا را سزد
entries.english: Through his vision his tongue was divulging the properties and essences of all things.

entries.farsi: نه چنان كه هيز را خواند اسد / نوح نه صد سال در راه سوى
entries.english: Such a name as fits the things, not so as to call a catamite a lion.

entries.farsi: ‏بود هر روزيش تذكير نوى / لعل او گويا ز ياقوت القلوب
entries.english: Nine hundred years Noah in the straightway, and every day he had a new sermon to preach.

entries.farsi: ‏نه رساله خوانده نه قوت القلوب / وعظ را نآموخته هيچ از شروح
entries.english: His ruby drew its eloquence from the corundum in the hearts: he had not read the Risála or the Qútu ’l-qulúb.

entries.farsi: ‏بلكه ينبوع كشوف و شرح روح / ز آن ميى كآن مى چو نوشيده شود
entries.english: He had never learned to preach from commentaries; nay, from the fountain of revelations and from the exposition by the spirit—

entries.farsi: آب نطق از گنگ جوشيده شود / طفل نو زاده شود حبر فصيح
entries.english: From the wine that when it is quaffed the water of speech gushes from the dumb,

entries.farsi: ‏حكمت بالغ بخواند چون مسيح / از كهى كه يافت ز آن مى خوش لبى
entries.english: And the new-born child becomes an eloquent divine and, like the Messiah, recites mature wisdom.

entries.farsi: ‏صد غزل آموخت داود نبى / مله مرغان ترك كرده جيك جيك
entries.english: The prophet David learned a hundred odes from the mountain that gained from that wine sweet song.

entries.farsi: ‏هم زبان و يار داود مليك / چه عجب كه مرغ گردد مست او
entries.english: All the birds left off chirping and joined their voices with King David as accompanists.

entries.farsi: چون شنود آهن نداى دست او / صرصرى بر عاد قتالى شده
entries.english: What wonder that a bird should be enraptured by him, since iron obeyed the call of his hand?

entries.farsi: ‏مر سليمان را چو حمالى شده / صرصرى مى‏برد بر سر تخت شاه
entries.english: A roaring wind became murderous to Ad, to Solomon it became as a carrier.

entries.farsi: ‏هر صباح و هر مسا يك ماهه راه / هم شده حمال و هم جاسوس او
entries.english: A roaring wind carried on its head the throne of the king a month’s journey every morn and eve.

entries.farsi: گفت غايب را كنان محسوس او / باد دم كه گفت غايب يافتى
entries.english: It became both a carrier and a spy for him, making the talk of the absent to be apprehended by him.

entries.farsi: ‏سوى گوش آن ملك بشتافتى / كه فالنى اين چنين گفت اين زمان
entries.english: The waft of air that caught the words of the absent would hasten to the ear of the king,

entries.farsi: ‏اى سليمان مه صاحب قران / اين سخن پايان ندارد گفت موش
entries.english: Saying, “Such-and-such a one said so-and-so just now, O mighty Solomon of auspicious fortune!”

sections.title_fa: تدبير كردن موش به چغز كه من نمى‏توانم بر تو آمدن به وقت حاجت در آب، ميان ما وصلتى بايد كه چون من بر لب جو آيم ترا توانم خبر كردن و تو چون بر سر سوراخ موش خانه آيى مرا توانى خبر ‏كردن الى آخره

sections.title_en: How the mouse made an arrangement with the frog, saying, “I cannot come to you in the water when I want. There must be some means of communication between us, so that when I come to the river-bank I may be able to let you know, and when you come to the mouse-hole you may be able to let me know, etc.”

entries.farsi: ‏چغز را روزى كه اى مصباح هوش / وقتها خواهم كه گويم با تو راز
entries.english: This topic is endless. One day the mouse said to the frog, “O lamp of intelligence,

entries.farsi: تو درون آب دارى ترك تاز / بر لب جو من ترا نعره زنان
entries.english: At times I wish to talk with you in secret, and you are leaping playfully in the water.

entries.farsi: ‏نشنوى در آب ناله‏ى عاشقان / من بدين وقت معين اى دلير
entries.english: I am on the river-bank, crying aloud for you, you in the water do not hear the wailing of lovers.

entries.farsi: مى‏نگردم از محاكات تو سير / پنج وقت آمد نماز و رهنمون
entries.english: At this appointed time, O brave, I never become weary of conversing with you.”

entries.farsi: ‏عاشقان را فى صاله دائمون / نه به پنج آرام گيرد آن خمار
entries.english: The prayer is five times, but the guide for lovers is, they who are in prayer continually.

entries.farsi: كه در آن سرهاست نى پانصد هزار / نيست زر غبا وظيفه‏ى عاشقان
entries.english: The hangover that is in those heads is not relieved by five nor by five hundred thousand.

entries.farsi: ‏ز انكه بى‏دريا ندارند انس جان / آب اين دريا كه هايل بقعه‏اى است
entries.english: “Visit once a week” is not the ration for lovers; the soul of the sincere has an intense craving to drink.

entries.farsi: ‏سخت مستسقى است جان صادقان / نيست زر غبا وظيفه‏ى ماهيان
entries.english: “Visit once a week” is not the ration for fishes, since they feel no spiritual joy without the Sea.

entries.farsi: ‏با خمار ماهيان خود جرعه‏اى است / يك دم هجران بر عاشق چو سال
entries.english: Notwithstanding the crop-sickness of the fishes, the water of this Sea, which is a tremendous place, is but a single draught.

entries.farsi: در پى هم اين و آن چون روز و شب / روز بر شب عاشق است و مضطر است
entries.english: To the lover one moment of separation is as a year; to him a year’s uninterrupted union is a fancy.

entries.farsi: ‏وصل سالى متصل پيشش خيال / عشق مستسقى است مستسقى طلب
entries.english: Love craves to drink and seeks him who craves to drink: this and that are at each other’s heels, like Day and Night.

entries.farsi: ‏چون ببينى شب بر او عاشق‏تر است / نيستشان از جستجو يك لحظه‏ايست
entries.english: Day is in love with Night and has lost control of itself; when you look, Night is more in love with it.

entries.farsi: ‏از پى همشان يكى دم ايست نيست / اين گرفته پاى آن آن گوش اين
entries.english: Never for one instant do they cease from seeking; never for one moment do they cease from pursuing each other.

entries.farsi: ‏اين بر آن مدهوش و آن بى‏هوش اين / در دل معشوق جمله عاشق است
entries.english: This one has caught the foot of that one, and that one the ear of this one: this one is distraught with that one, and that one is beside itself for this one.

entries.farsi: ‏در دل عذرا هميشه وامق است / در دل عاشق بجز معشوق نيست
entries.english: In the heart of the beloved the lover is all: Wámiq is always in the heart of Adhrá.

entries.farsi: ‏در ميانشان فارق و فاروق نيست / بر يكى اشتر بود اين دو درا
entries.english: In the lover’s heart is naught but the beloved: there is nothing to separate and divide them.

entries.farsi: پس چه زر غبا بگنجد اين دو را / هيچ كس با خويش زر غبا نمود
entries.english: These two bells are on one camel: how, then, in regard to these two should, “Visit once a week,” be admissible?

entries.farsi: هيچ كس با خود به نوبت يار بود / آن يكيى نه كه عقلش فهم كرد
entries.english: Did anyone pay recurring visits to himself? Was any one a companion to himself at regular intervals?

entries.farsi: فهم اين موقوف شد بر مرگ مرد / ور به عقل ادراك اين ممكن بدى
entries.english: That is not the oneness that reason apprehends: the apprehension of this depends on a man’s dying;

entries.farsi: ‏بى‏ضرورت چون بگويد نفس كش
entries.english: And if it were possible to perceive this by means of reason, wherefore should self-violence have become a duty?

entries.farsi: ‏قهر نفس از بهر چه واجب شدى / با چنان رحمت كه دارد شاه هش
entries.english: How, with such mercy as He has, would the King of intellect say unnecessarily “Kill yourself ”?

sections.title_fa: ‏مبالغه كردن موش در البه و زارى و وصلت جستن از چغز آبى
sections.title_en: How the mouse exerted himself to the utmost in supplication and humble entreaty and besought the water-frog to grant him access.

entries.farsi: روز نور و مكسب و تابم تويى / ‏شب قرار و سلوت و خوابم تويى
entries.english: He said, “O dear and affectionate friend, without your face I have not a moment’s rest.

entries.farsi: گفت كاى يار عزيز مهر كار / من ندارم بى‏رخت يك دم قرار
entries.english: By day you are my light and acquisition and strength; by night you are my rest and comfort and sleep.

entries.farsi: از مروت باشد ار شادم كنى / ‏وقت و بى‏وقت از كرم يادم كنى
entries.english: It would be a generous act if you would make me happy and kindly remember me early and late.

entries.farsi: در شبانه روزى وظيفه‏ى چاشتگاه / ‏راتبه كردى وصال اى نيك خواه
entries.english: During a day and night you have allowed me breakfast-time for access, O well-wisher.

entries.farsi: پانصد استسقاستم اندر جگر / با هر استسقا قرين جوع البقر
entries.english: I feel in my liver five hundred cravings for drink, and bulimia is joined with every craving.

entries.farsi: اين فقير بى‏ادب نادر خور است / ‏ليك لطف عام تو ز آن برتر است
entries.english: You, O prince, art unconcerned with my passion: pay the poor-tax on your high estate, look on poor wretch.

entries.farsi: بى‏نيازى از غم من اى امير / ده زكات جاه و بنگر در فقير
entries.english: This poor unmannerly wretch is not worthy; but your universal grace is superior to that.

entries.farsi: مى‏نجويد لطف عام تو سند / آفتابى بر حدثها مى‏زند
entries.english: Your universal grace requires no support: a sun strikes on excrement.

entries.farsi: نور او را ز آن زيانى نابده / ‏و آن حدث از خشكى هيزم شده
entries.english: Its light suffers no loss thereby, and the ordure is made dry and fuel,

entries.farsi: تا حدث در گلخنى شد نور يافت / ‏در در و ديوار حمامى بتافت
entries.english: So that the ordure goes into a bath-furnace, is converted into light, and illumines the door and wall of a bath-house.

entries.farsi: بود آاليش شد آرايش كنون / ‏چون بر او بر خواند خورشيد آن فسون
entries.english: It was defilement, now it has become an adornment, since the sun chanted that spell upon it.

entries.farsi: شمس هم معده‏ى زمين را گرم كرد / تا زمين باقى حدثها را بخورد
entries.english: The sun also warms the belly of the earth, so that the earth consumes the remaining excrement.

entries.farsi: با حدث كه بدترين است اين كند / كش نبات و نرگس و نسرين كند
entries.english: They become a part of the earth, and herbage springs up from them: even so doth God wipe out evil actions.

entries.farsi: جزو خاكى گشت و رست از وى نبات / ‏هكذا يمحو الاله السيئات
entries.english: To ordure, which is the worst, He does this, that He makes it herbage and narcissus and eglantine.

entries.farsi: تا به نسرين مناسك در وفا / حق چه بخشد در جزا و در عطا
entries.english: What God bestows in recompense and bounty on the eglantines of devotion faithfully.

entries.farsi: ون خبيثان را چنين خلعت دهد / طيبين را تا چه بخشد در رصد
entries.english: Since He confers such a robe of honour on the wicked, what He bestows on the righteous in the place where He waits.

entries.farsi: آن دهد حقشان كه ال عين رأت / ‏كه نگنجد در زبان و در لغت
entries.english: God gives them what no eye has beheld; that which is not comprehensible in any tongue or language.

entries.farsi: ما كه‏ايم اين را بيا اى يار من / ‏روز من روشن كن از خلق حسن
entries.english: Who are we to this? Come, my friend, make my day bright with goodly disposition.

entries.farsi: منگر اندر زشتى و مكروهى‏ام / ‏كه ز پر زهرى چو مار كوهى‏ام
entries.english: Do not regard my ugliness and hatefulness, though I am as venomous as a mountain-snake.

entries.farsi: اى كه من زشت و خصالم جمله زشت / ‏چون شوم گل چون مرا او خار كشت
entries.english: Oh, I am ugly and all my qualities are ugly: since He planted me as a thorn, how should I become a rose?

entries.farsi: در كمال زشتى‏ام من منتهى / ‏لطف تو در فصل و در فن منتهى
entries.english: Bestow on the thorn the springtide of the rose’s beauty: bestow on this snake the loveliness of the peacock!

entries.farsi: نو بهار حسن گل ده خار را / زينت طاوس ده اين مار را
entries.english: I have reached the limit in perfection of ugliness: your grace has reached the limit in excellence and accomplishment.

entries.farsi: حاجت اين منتهى ز آن منتهى / ‏تو بر آر اى حسرت سرو سهى
entries.english: Do you grant the boon sought by this consummate one from that consummate one, O you who are the envy of the tall cypress.

entries.farsi: چون بميرم فضل تو خواهد گريست / ‏از كرم گر چه ز حاجت او برى است
entries.english: When I die, your bounty, though it is exempt from need, will weep for kindness’ sake.

entries.farsi: بر سر گورم بسى خواهد نشست / ‏خواهد از چشم لطيفش اشك جست
entries.english: It will sit beside my grave a long while: tears will gush from its gracious eye.

entries.farsi: نوحه خواهد كرد بر محرومى‏ام / ‏چشم خواهد بست از مظلومى‏ام
entries.english: It will mourn for my deprivation; it will shut its eyes to my abjectness.

entries.farsi: اندكى ز آن لطفها اكنون بكن / ‏حلقه‏اى در گوش من كن ز آن سخن
entries.english: Bestow a little of those favours now, put a few of those words as a ring into my ear!

entries.farsi: آن كه خواهى گفت تو با خاك من / ‏بر فشان بر مدرك غمناك من
entries.english: That which you will say to my dust— strew it upon my sorrowful perception!”

sections.title_fa: البه كردن موش مر چغز را كه بهانه مينديش و در نسيه مينداز انجاح اين حاجت مرا كه فى التاخير آفات و الصوفى ابن الوقت و ابن دست از دامن پدر باز ندارد و آب مشفق صوفى كه وقت است او را به نگرش به فردا محتاج نگرداند چندانش مستغرق دارد در گلزار سريع الحسابى خويش نه چون عوام، منتظر مستقبل نباشد، نهرى باشد نه دهرى كه ال صباح عند اهلل و ال مساء، ماضى و مستقبل و ازل و ابد آن جا نباشد، آدم سابق و دجال مسبوق نباشد كه اين رسوم در خطه‏ى عقل جزوى است و روح حيوانى، در عالم المكان و ال زمان اين رسوم نباشد پس او ابن وقتى است كه ال يفهم منه الا
‏نفى تفرقه الازمنه چنان كه از اللهَّ واحد فهم شود نفى دويى نى حقيقت واحدى
sections.title_en: How the mouse humbly entreated the frog, saying, “Do not think of pretexts and do not defer the fulfillment of this request of mine, for ‘there are dangers in delay,’ and ‘the Sufi is the son

of the moment.’” A son does not withdraw his hand from the skirt of his father, and the Sufi’s kind father, who is the “moment,” does not let him be reduced to the necessity of looking to the morrow keeps him all the while absorbed, unlike the common folk, in the garden of his (the father’s) swift reckoning. He does not wait for the future. He is of the River, not of Time, for “with God is neither morn nor eve”: there the past and the future and time without beginning and time without end do not exist: Adam is not prior nor is Dajjal posterior. these terms belong to the domain of the particular reason and the animal soul: they are not in the non-spatial and non-temporal world. Therefore he is the son of that “moment”
by which is to be understood only a denial of the division of times, just as “God is One” is to be understood as a denial of duality, not as the real nature of unity.

entries.farsi: صوفيى را گفت خواجه‏ى سيم پاش / اى قدمهاى ترا جانم فراش
entries.english: A certain Khwaja, accustomed to scatter silver, said to a Sufi, “O you for whose feet my soul is a carpet,

entries.farsi: يك درم خواهى تو امروز اى شهم / ‏يا كه فردا چاشتگاهى سه درم
entries.english: Would you like one dirhem to-day, my king, or three dirhems at breakfast-time tomorrow?”

entries.farsi: گفت دى نيم درم راضى‏ترم / ‏ز انكه امروز اين و فردا صد درم
entries.english: He replied, “I am more pleased with half a dirhem yesterday than with this to-day and a hundred dirhems to-morrow.”

entries.farsi: سيلى نقد از عطاى نسيه به / ‏نك قفا پيشت كشيدم نقد ده
entries.english: “A slap in cash is better than a donation on credit: lo, I put the nape of my neck before you: give the cash!

entries.farsi: خاصه آن سيلى كه از دست تو است / ‏كه قفا و سيلى‏اش مست تو است
entries.english: Especially as the slap is from your hand, for both the nape and the slap inflicted on it are intoxicated with you.

entries.farsi: هين بيا اى جان جان و صد جهان / ‏خوش غنيمت دار نقد اين زمان
entries.english: Listen, come, O soul of my soul and of a hundred worlds, gladly take the opportunity of the cash of this moment.

entries.farsi: در مدزد آن روى مه از شب روان / ‏سر مكش زين جوى اى آب روان
entries.english: Do not stealthily remove your moon-like face from the night-travellers; do not withdraw yourself from this river-bed, O flowing water,

entries.farsi: تا لب جو خندد از آب معين / ‏لب لب جو سر بر آرد ياسمين
entries.english: But flow in order that the river-bank may laugh by the running water, and that jasmines may rear their heads on each brim of the river.”

entries.farsi: چون ببينى بر لب جو سبزه مست / ‏پس بدان از دور كآنجا آب هست
entries.english: When you see that greenery is fresh on the river-brim, then know from afar that water is there.

entries.farsi: گفت سيماهم وجوه كردگار / كه بود غماز باران سبزه‏زار
entries.english: The Maker has said, “Their mark is on their faces,” for the verdant orchard tells a tale of rain.

entries.farsi: گر ببارد شب نبيند هيچ كس / ‏كه بود در خواب هر نفس و نفس
entries.english: If it rains during the night, no one sees, for every soul and breath is asleep;

entries.farsi: تازگى هر گلستان جميل / ‏هست بر باران پنهانى دليل
entries.english: The freshness of every beauteous rose-garden is evidence of the rain hidden.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كن از عطا و از قسم / ‏كه گه و بى‏گه به خدمت مى‏رسم
entries.english: “O comrade, I am of the earth, you art of the water; but you are the king of mercy and munificence.

entries.farsi: اى اخى من خاكيم تو آبيى / ‏ليك شاه رحمت و وهابيى
entries.english: By way of bounty and dispensing so act that I may attain to serving you early and late.

entries.farsi: آمدن در آب بر من بسته شد / ز انك تركيبم ز خاكى رسته شد
entries.english: I am always calling you on the river-bank with my soul, I never experience the mercy of response.

entries.farsi: بر لب جو من به جان مى‏خوانمت / ‏مى‏نبينم از اجابت مرحمت
entries.english: Entrance into the water is barred against me because my frame has grown from a piece of earth.

entries.farsi: يا رسولى يا نشانى كن مدد / تا ترا از بانگ من آگه كند
entries.english: Use the aid either of a messenger or a token to make you aware of my cry.”

entries.farsi: بحث كردند اندر اين كار آن دو يار / آخر آن بحث آن آمد قرار
entries.english: The two friends debated on this: at the close of the debate it was settled

entries.farsi: كه به دست آرند يك رشته‏ى دراز / تا ز جذب رشته گردد كشف راز
entries.english: That they should procure a long string, in order that by pulling the string the secret should be revealed.

entries.farsi: يك سرى بر پاى اين بنده‏ى دو تو / بست بايد ديگرش بر پاى تو
entries.english: “One end must be tied to the foot of this slave double, and the other to your foot,

entries.farsi: تا بهم آييم زين فن ما دو تن / ‏اندر آميزيم چون جان با بدن
entries.english: That by this device we two persons may come together and mingle as the soul with the body.”

entries.farsi: موش تن ز آن ريسمان بازش كشد / چند تلخى زين كشش جان مى‏چشد
entries.english: The body is like a string on the foot of the soul, drawing it from Heaven to earth.

entries.farsi: هست تن چون ريسمان بر پاى جان / مى‏كشاند بر زمينش ز آسمان
entries.english: When the frog-like soul escapes from the mouse-like body into the water, the sleep of unconsciousness, it enters into a happy state;

entries.farsi: چغز جان در آب خواب بى‏هشى / ‏رسته از موش تن آيد در خوشى
entries.english: The mouse-like body pulls it back with that string: how much bitterness does the soul taste from this pulling!

entries.farsi: گر نبودى جذب موش گنده مغز / عيشها كردى درون آب چغز
entries.english: Were it not for the pulling of the scatter-brained mouse, the frog would have enjoyed himself in the water.

entries.farsi: باقى‏اش چون روز برخيزى ز خواب / ‏بشنوى از نور بخش آفتاب
entries.english: You will hear the rest of it from the light-giving of the Sun when you rise from slumber on the Day.

entries.farsi: يك سر رشته گره بر پاى من / ‏ز آن سر ديگر تو پا بر عقده زن
entries.english: “Tie one end of the string on my foot and the other end on your,

entries.farsi: تا توانم من در اين خشكى كشيد / مر ترا نك شد سر رشته پديد
entries.english: That I may be able to pull you to this dry land: lo, the end of the string is clear.

entries.farsi: تلخ آمد بر دل چغز اين حديث / ‏كه مرا در عقده آرد اين خبيث
entries.english: This news was disagreeable to the heart of the frog, “This wicked fellow will bring me into a tangle.”

entries.farsi: هر كراهت در دل مرد بهى / ‏چون در آيد از فنى نبود تهى
entries.english: Whenever a feeling of repugnance comes into the heart of a good man, it is not devoid of some significance.

entries.farsi: وصف حق دان آن فراست را نه وهم / ‏نور دل از لوح كل كردست فهم
entries.english: Deem that sagacity to be a Divine attribute, not a suspicion: the light of the heart has apprehended from the Universal Tablet.

entries.farsi: امتناع پيل از سيران به بيت / ‏با جد آن پيلبان و بانگ هيت
entries.english: The refusal of the Elephant to march against the House notwithstanding the driver’s efforts and cries of “Come on!”

entries.farsi: جانب كعبه نرفتى پاى پيل / ‏با همه لت نه كثير و نه قليل
entries.english: In spite of all blows the Elephant’s feet would not move, either much or little, towards the Ka‘ba.

entries.farsi: گفتيى خود خشك شد پاهاى او / يا بمرد آن جان صول افزاى او
entries.english: You would have said that its legs were paralysed or that its impetuous spirit was dead.

entries.farsi: چون كه كردندى سرش سوى يمن / ‏پيل نر صد اسبه گشتى گام زن
entries.english: Whenever they turned its head towards Yemen, the fierce Elephant would begin to stride with the speed of a hundred horses.

entries.farsi: حس پيل از زخم غيب آگاه بود / چون بود حس ولى با ورود
entries.english: The Elephant’s perception was aware of the blow from the Unseen, how must the perception of the saint with inspiration be!

entries.farsi: از پدر چون خواستندش دادران / ‏تا برندش سوى صحرا يك زمان
entries.english: Is it not that the prophet Jacob, that man of holy nature, for Joseph’s sake to all his brethren—

entries.farsi: نه كه يعقوب نبى آن پاك خو / بهر يوسف با همه اخوان او
entries.english: When the brothers begged their father to give him to them, that they might take him to the country for a while,

entries.farsi: جمله گفتندش مينديش از ضرر / يك دو روزش مهلتى ده اى پدر
entries.english: They all said to him, “Do not be afraid of harm: give him one or two days’ time, O father;

entries.farsi: كه چرا ما را نمى‏دارى امين / ‏يوسف خود را به سيران و ظعين
entries.english: For why will not you entrust your Joseph to us in going about and traveling,

entries.farsi: تا بهم در مرجها بازى كنيم / ‏ما در اين دعوت امين و محسنيم
entries.english: That we may play together in the meadows? In this request we are trustworthy and beneficent”—

entries.farsi: گفت اين دانم كه نقلش از برم / مى‏فروزد در دلم درد و سقم
entries.english: Did not he say, “I know this, that his being removed from me is kindling grief and sickness in my heart;

entries.farsi: اين دلم هرگز نمى‏گويد دروغ / ‏كه ز نور عرش دارد دل فروغ
entries.english: This heart of mine never lies, for my heart is illumined by the light of the highest heaven”?

entries.farsi: آن دليل قاطعى بد بر فساد / و ز قضا آن را نكرد او اعتداد
entries.english: That was a decisive proof of wickedness, but by destiny he took no account.

entries.farsi: در گذشت از وى نشانى آن چنان / ‏كه قضا در فلسفه بود آن زمان
entries.english: Intimation like that passed away from him, because Destiny was at that moment engaged in philosophy.

entries.farsi: اين عجب نبود كه كور افتد به چاه / ‏بو العجب افتادن بيناى راه
entries.english: It is no wonder that a blind man should fall into a pit, the falling of one who can see the way is beyond all wonder.

entries.farsi: اين قضا را گونه‏گون تصريفهاست / ‏چشم بندش يفعل اللهَّ ما يشاست
entries.english: This Destiny employs diverse shifts: its eye-binding spell is God does what He pleases.

entries.farsi: هم بداند هم نداند دل فنش / ‏موم گردد بهر آن مهر آهنش
entries.english: The heart knows and yet knows not its artfulness: its iron becomes as wax for the seal.

entries.farsi: گوييا دل گويدى كه ميل او / چون در اين شد هر چه افتد باش گو
entries.english: It is as though the heart should say, “Since its inclination is turned to this, whatever may happen, let it come!”

entries.farsi: خويش را زين هم مغفل مى‏كند / در عقالش جان معقل مى‏كند
entries.english: Accordingly it makes itself heedless of this and binds its soul fast in the shackle thereof.

entries.farsi: يك بال از صد باليش واخرد / يك هبوطش بر معارجها برد
entries.english: If that exalted one is checkmated in this, it is not checkmate, it is tribulation.

entries.farsi: گر شود مات اندر اين آن بو العال / آن نباشد مات باشد ابتال
entries.english: A single tribulation redeems him from a hundred tribulations; a single fall takes him up on the ladders.

entries.farsi: خام شوخى كه رهانيدش مدام / ‏از خمار صد هزاران زشت خام
entries.english: The half-baked saucy fellow, whom the wine has relieved from the surfeit of intoxication with a hundred thousand wicked half-baked persons like himself,

entries.farsi: عاقبت او پخته و استاد شد / جست از رق جهان و آزاد شد
entries.english: Finally becomes mature and adept: he escapes from enslavement to this world and is made free.

entries.farsi: از شراب اليزالى گشت مست / ‏شد مميز از خاليق باز رست
entries.english: He is made drunken with the everlasting wine, he becomes discerning, and is delivered from created beings,

entries.farsi: ز اعتقاد سست پر تقليدشان / ‏وز خيال ديده‏ى بى‏ديدشان
entries.english: From their weak conventional faith and from the illusions of their unseeing eyes.

entries.farsi: اى عجب چه فن زند ادراكشان / ‏پيش جزر و مد بحر بى‏نشان
entries.english: Oh, what device can their mental perception employ, I wonder, against the ebb and flow of the trackless Sea?

entries.farsi: ز آن بيابان اين عمارتها رسيد / ملك و شاهى و وزارتها رسيد
entries.english: From that Desert came these signs of cultivation and prosperity; came empires, kingships and governments.

entries.farsi: ز آن بيابان عدم مشتاق شوق / ‏مى‏رسند اندر شهادت جوق جوق
entries.english: Yearning with desire they come in troops from the Desert of Non-existence into the visible world.

entries.farsi: كاروان بر كاروان زين باديه / ‏مى‏رسد در هر مسا و غاديه
entries.english: Caravan on caravan, they arrive from this Desert every evening and morning.

entries.farsi: آيد و گيرد وثاق ما گرو / كه رسيدم نوبت ما شد تو رو
entries.english: They come and seize our houses in distress, saying, “I have arrived, it is my turn, leave!”

entries.farsi: چون پسر چشم خرد را بر گشاد / زود بابا رخت برگردون نهاد
entries.english: When the son has opened the eye of reason, the father at once puts his baggage in the cart.

entries.farsi: جاده‏ى شاه است آن زين سو روان / ‏و آن از آن سو صادران و واردان
entries.english: It is the King’s highway—departing and arriving, one going in this direction, another in that direction.

entries.farsi: نيك بنگر ما نشسته مى‏رويم / ‏مى‏نبينى قاصد جاى نويم
entries.english: Consider well! We, sitting still, are marching: don’t you see that we are bound for a new place?

entries.farsi: بهر حالى مى‏نگيرى راس مال / ‏بلكه از بهر غرضها در مال
entries.english: You do not get your capital for any present need; nay, but for your ultimate purposes.

entries.farsi: پس مسافر اين بود اى ره پرست / ‏كه مسير و روش در مستقبل است
entries.english: The traveller, then, O devotee of the Way, is he whose march and face are towards the future,

entries.farsi: همچنانك از پرده‏ى دل بى‏كالل / ‏دم به دم در مى‏رسد خيل خيال
entries.english: Even as the troops of Phantasy are at every moment arriving unwearied through the curtains of the heart.

entries.farsi: گرنه تصويرات از يك مغرسند / در پى هم سوى دل چون مى‏رسند
entries.english: If ideas are not from one Plantation, how are they coming to the heart on each other’s heels?

entries.farsi: جوق جوق اسپاه تصويرات ما / سوى چشمه‏ى دل شتابان از ظما
entries.english: Company after company, the army of our ideas, by thirst, is speeding towards the fountain of the heart.

entries.farsi: جره‏ها پر مى‏كنند و مى‏روند / دايما پيدا و پنهان مى‏شوند
entries.english: They fill their jars and go: they are continually appearing and vanishing.

entries.farsi: سعد ديدى شكر كن ايثار كن / ‏نحس ديدى صدقه و استغفار كن
entries.english: Regard thoughts as stars of the sky revolving in the sphere of another heaven.

entries.farsi: فكرها را اختران چرخ دان / ‏داير اندر چرخ ديگر آسمان
entries.english: You have experienced good fortune, give thanks and do works of charity; you have experienced bad fortune, give alms and ask pardon.

entries.farsi: ما كه‏ايم اين را بيا اى شاه من / ‏طالعم مقبل كن و چرخى بزن
entries.english: Who am I in relation to this? Come, O my King, make my ruling star auspicious and wheel once.

entries.farsi: روح را تابان كن از انوار ماه / ‏كه ز آسيب ذنب جان شد سياه
entries.english: Illumine my spirit with moonbeams, for my soul is blackened by contact with the Tail.

entries.farsi: از خيال و وهم و ظن بازش رهان / از چه و جور رسن بازش رهان
entries.english: Deliver it from fancy and vain imagination and opinion; deliver it from the well and the tyranny of the rope,

entries.farsi: تا ز دل دارى خوب تو دلى / ‏پر بر آرد بر پرد ز آب و گلى
entries.english: In order that through Your goodly lovingkindness a heart may lift its wings and soar up from a water and earth.

entries.farsi: اى عزيز مصر و در پيمان درست / ‏يوسف مظلوم در زندان تست
entries.english: O Prince of Egypt and faithful keeper of your promise, the wronged Joseph is in your prison.

entries.farsi: در خالص او يكى خوابى ببين / ‏زود كاهلل يُحِبُّ المحسنين
entries.english: Quickly dream a dream of his release, for God loves the beneficent.

entries.farsi: هفت گاو الغرى پر گزند / هفت گاو فربهش را مى‏خورند
entries.english: The seven noxious lean cows are devouring its seven fat cows.

entries.farsi: قحط از مصرش بر آمد اى عزيز / هين مباش اى شاه اين را مستجيز
entries.english: The seven dry, ugly, and unapproved ears of corn are feeding on its fresh ears.

entries.farsi: هفت خوشه‏ى خشك زشت ناپسند / سنبالت تازه‏اش را مى‏چرند
entries.english: Famine has arisen in its Egypt, O mighty Potentate: listen, O King, do not continue to sanction this.

entries.farsi: يوسفم در حبس تو اى شه نشان / ‏هين ز دستان زنانم وارهان
entries.english: Let my Joseph sit in Your prison, O King: come, deliver me from the wiles of the women.

entries.farsi: از سوى عرشى كه بودم مربط او / شهوت مادر فگندم كه اهبطوا
entries.english: My mother’s lust caused me to fall from the highest heaven which was my tethering-place, for, Fall down!

entries.farsi: پس فتادم ز آن كمال مستتم / ‏از فن زالى به زندان رحم
entries.english: So by the artfulness of a crone I fell from complete perfection into the prison of the womb.

entries.farsi: اول و آخر هبوط من ز زن / ‏چون كه بودم روح و چون گشتم بدن
entries.english: She brings the spirit from the highest heaven to the Hatím: great must be the craft of women.

entries.farsi: روح را از عرش آرد در حطيم / ‏الجرم كيد زنان باشد عظيم
entries.english: My first and my last fall were caused by woman, since I was spirit— and how have I become body?

entries.farsi: بشنو اين زارى يوسف در عثار / يا بر آن يعقوب بى‏دل رحم آر
entries.english: Hearken to this lament of Joseph in his lapse, or take pity on that distraught Jacob.

entries.farsi: ناله از اخوان كنم يا از زنان / ‏كه فگندندم چو آدم از جنان
entries.english: Shall I complain of my brethren or of the women who have cast me, like Adam, from the gardens?

entries.farsi: ز آن مثال برگ دى پژمرده‏ام / ‏كز بهشت وصل گندم خورده‏ام
entries.english: I am withered like leaves in December because I have eaten the wheat from the Paradise of union.

entries.farsi: چون بديدم لطف و اكرام ترا / و آن سالم سلم و پيغام ترا
entries.english: When I saw Your graciousness and kindness and Your greeting of peace and Your message,

entries.farsi: من سپند از چشم بد كردم پديد / در سپندم نيز چشم بد رسيد
entries.english: I produced rue against the evil eye; the evil eye reached even my rue.

entries.farsi: دافع هر چشم بد از پيش و پس / چشمهاى پر خمار تست و بس
entries.english: Only Your languishing eyes are able to avert every evil eye in front or behind.

entries.farsi: چشم بد را چشم نيكويت شها / مات و مستاصل كند نعم الدوا
entries.english: Your good eye, O King, defeats and extirpates the evil eye: how excellent it is as a remedy!

entries.farsi: بل ز چشمت كيمياها مى‏رسد / چشم بد را چشم نيكو مى‏كند
entries.english: Nay, from Your eye come alchemies: they turn the evil eye into the good eye.

entries.farsi: چشم شه بر چشم باز دل زده‏ست / ‏چشم بازش سخت با همت شده‏ست
entries.english: The King’s eye has smitten the eye of the falcon-heart, and its falcon-eye has become mightily aspiring,

entries.farsi: تا ز بس همت كه يابيد از نظر / مى‏نگيرد باز شه جز شير نر
entries.english: So that, because of the great aspiration which it has gained from the look, the royal falcon will catch nothing but the fierce lion.

entries.farsi: شير چه كآن شاه باز معنوى / ‏هم شكار تست و هم صيدش توى
entries.english: What of the lion? The spiritual royal falcon is Your quarry and at the same time You art its prey.

entries.farsi: باز دل را كه پى تو مى‏پريد / از عطاى بى‏حدت چشمى رسيد
entries.english: The call uttered by the falcon-soul in the meadow of devotion is cries of “I love not them that set.”

entries.farsi: شد صفير باز جان در مرج دين / ‏نعره‏هاى ال أُحِبُّ اآلفلين
entries.english: From Your infinite bounty there came an eye to the falcon-soul that was flying for Your sake.

entries.farsi: يافت بينى بوى و گوش از تو سماع / ‏هر حسى را قسمتى آمد مشاع
entries.english: From You its nose gained smell, and its ear the hearing: to each sense was allotted a portion distributed.

entries.farsi: هر حسى را چون دهى ره سوى غيب / ‏نبود آن حس را فتور مرگ و شيب
entries.english: Since You give to each sense the means of access to the Unseen, that sense is not subject to the frailty of death and hoary old age.

entries.farsi: مالك الملكى به حس چيزى دهى / تا كه بر حسها كند آن حس شهى
entries.english: You are the Lord of the kingdom: You give to the sense something, so that that sense exercises sovereignty over the senses.

sections.title_fa: حكايت شب دزدان كه سلطان محمود شب در ميان ايشان افتاد كه من يكى‏ام از شما و بر احوال ‏ايشان مطلع شدن الى آخره
sections.title_en: Story of the night-thieves with whom Sultan Mahmud fell in during the night, saying,
“I am one of you”; and how he became acquainted with their affairs, etc.

entries.farsi: شب چو شه محمود بر مى‏گشت فرد / با گروهى قوم دزدان باز خورد
entries.english: While King Mahmud was roaming about alone at night he encountered a band of thieves.

entries.farsi: آن يكى گفت اى گروه مكر كيش / ‏تا بگويد هر يكى فرهنگ خويش
