entries.english: Thereupon they said to him, “Who are you, O honest man?” “I am one of you,” replied the King.

entries.farsi: پس بگفتندش كيى اى بو الوفا / گفت شه من هم يكى‏ام از شما
entries.english: One said, “O company practised in cunning, let each of us declare his talent;

entries.farsi: آن يكى گفت اى گروه فن فروش / ‏هست خاصيت مرا اندر دو گوش
entries.english: Let him tell his comrades in the night-talk what skill he possesses in his nature.”

entries.farsi: تا بگويد با حريفان در سمر / كاو چه دارد در جبلت از هنر
entries.english: One said, “O you fellows who are exhibiting cleverness, my specialty lies in my two ears.

entries.farsi: كه بدانم سگ چه مى‏گويد به بانگ / ‏قوم گفتندش ز دينارى دو دانگ
entries.english: That I know what a dog is saying when it barks.” The party replied, “Two dángs of a dinar.”

entries.farsi: آن دگر گفت اى گروه زر پرست / ‏جمله خاصيت مرا چشم اندر است
entries.english: Another said, “O company of gold-worshippers, my specialty lies wholly in my eyes.

entries.farsi: هر كه را شب بينم اندر قيروان / ‏روز بشناسم من او را بى‏گمان
entries.english: If I see any one in the world by night, I know him by day without doubt.”

entries.farsi: گفت يك خاصيتم در بازو است / ‏كه زنم من نقبها با زور دست
entries.english: Another said, “My specialty lies in my arm: I make tunnels by strength of hand.”

entries.farsi: گفت يك خاصيتم در بينى است / كار من در خاكها بو بينى است
entries.english: Another said, “My specialty lies in my nose: my business is to detect the smell in different earths.

entries.farsi: سر الناس معادن داد دست / ‏كه رسول آن را پى چه گفته است
entries.english: The secret of ‘men are mines’ has yielded itself, so that why the Prophet has said it.

entries.farsi: من ز خاك تن بدانم كاندر آن / ‏چند نقد است و چه دارد او ز كان
entries.english: From the earth of the body I know how much good ore is therein and what mine it holds.

entries.farsi: در يكى كان زر بى‏اندازه درج / ‏و آن دگر دخلش بود كمتر ز خرج
entries.english: In one mine is contained gold immeasurable, while another revenue from it is less than the expenditure.

entries.farsi: بو كنم دانم ز هر پيراهنى / ‏گر بود يوسف و گر آهرمنى
entries.english: Like Majnun, I smell the soil and detect the soil of Layla without mistake.

entries.farsi: همچو مجنون بو كنم من خاك را / خاك ليلى را بيابم بى‏خطا
entries.english: I smell and know from every shirt whether it is a Joseph or an Ahriman.

entries.farsi: همچو احمد كه برد بوى از يمن / ‏ز آن نصيبى يافت اين بينى من
entries.english: Like Ahmad, who catches scent from Yemen, this nose of mine has gained some portion of that ,

entries.farsi: كه كدامين خاك همسايه‏ى زر است / ‏يا كدامين خاك صفر و ابتر است
entries.english: So that I can smell which soil is a neighbour of gold, or which soil is empty and poor.”

entries.farsi: گفت يك نك خاصيت در پنجه‏ام / ‏كه كمندى افكنم طول علم
entries.english: Another said, “Look here, my specialty lies in my fist: I can throw a lasso to the height of a mountain,

entries.farsi: همچو احمد كه كمند انداخت جانش / ‏تا كمندش برد سوى آسمانش
entries.english: Like Ahmad, whose spirit threw a lasso so that his lasso bore him to Heaven,

entries.farsi: گفت حقش اى كمند انداز بيت / ‏آن ز من دان ما رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رميت
entries.english: And God said to him, ‘O thrower of the lasso at the House, deem that to be from Me: you did not throw when you threw.’”

entries.farsi: پس بپرسيدند ز آن شه كاى سند / مر ترا خاصيت اندر چه بود
entries.english: Then they asked the King, saying, “O man of authority, in what may your special talent consist?”

entries.farsi: گفت در ريشم بود خاصيتم / ‏كه رهانم مجرمان را از نقم
entries.english: He replied, “My specialty lies in my beard: I can save criminals from punishment.

entries.farsi: مجرمان را چون به جالدان دهند / چون بجنبد ريش من ايشان رهند
entries.english: When criminals are handed over to the executioners, as soon as my beard moves they are saved.

entries.farsi: چون بجنبانم به رحمت ريش را / طى كنند آن قتل و آن تشويش را
entries.english: When I move my beard in mercy, they put an end to the killing and the trouble.”

entries.farsi: قوم گفتندش كه قطب ما توى / ‏كه خالص روز محنتمان شوى
entries.english: The company said to him, “You are our qutb, for you will be the deliverance on the day of tribulation.”

entries.farsi: بعد از آن جمله بهم بيرون شدند / سوى قصر آن شه ميمون شدند
entries.english: [Afterwards they all set out together and went towards the palace of the fortunate King.]

entries.farsi: چون سگى بانگى بزد از سوى راست / ‏گفت مى‏گويد كه سلطان با شماست
entries.english: When a dog barked on the right, said, “It says ‘The Sultan is with you.’”

entries.farsi: خاك بو كرد آن دگر از ريوه‏اى / گفت اين هست از وثاق بيوه‏اى
entries.english: Another smelt the ground from a hill and said, “This belongs to the house of a widow.”

entries.farsi: پس كمند انداخت استاد كمند / تا شدند آن سوى ديوار بلند
entries.english: Then the skilful master of the lasso threw his lasso, so that they got over the lofty wall.

entries.farsi: جاى ديگر خاك را چون بوى كرد / گفت خاك مخزن شاهى است فرد
entries.english: When he smelt the earth in this other place, he said, “it is the earth of the treasury of a peerless king.”

entries.farsi: نقب زن زد نقب و در مخزن رسيد / هر يكى از مخزن اسبابى كشيد
entries.english: The tunneller made a tunnel and reached the treasury: every one carried off some goods from the treasury.

entries.farsi: بس زر و زربفت و گوهرهاى زفت / ‏قوم بردند و نهان كردند تفت
entries.english: The band took away much gold and gold-embroidered cloth and big pearls and quickly concealed them.

entries.farsi: شه معين ديد منزلگاهشان / ‏حليه و نام و پناه و راهشان
entries.english: The King saw distinctly their lodging-place and their personal appearance and names and refuge and way.

entries.farsi: خويش را دزديد از ايشان باز گشت / ‏روز در ديوان بگفت آن سر گذشت
entries.english: He stole away from them and returned, and day related his adventure in council.

entries.farsi: پس روان گشتند سرهنگان مست / ‏تا كه دزدان را گرفتند و ببست
entries.english: Thereupon furious officers rushed away to arrest and pinion the thieves.

entries.farsi: دست بسته سوى ديوان آمدند / وز نهيب جان خود لرزان شدند
entries.english: They came handcuffed into the council-chamber, and they were trembling in fear for their lives.

entries.farsi: چون كه استادند پيش تخت شاه / ‏يار شبشان بود آن شاه چو ماه
entries.english: When they stood before the King’s throne, that moon-like King was their night’s companion.

entries.farsi: آن كه چشمش شب به هرك انداختى / ‏روز ديدى بى‏شكش بشناختى
entries.english: He who could without hesitation recognise by day any one on whom he had cast his eye by night

entries.farsi: شاه را بر تخت ديد و گفت اين / ‏بود با ما دوش شب گرد و قرين
entries.english: Saw the King on the throne and said, “This man was going about with us last night and was our comrade.

entries.farsi: آن كه چندين خاصيت در ريش اوست / ‏اين گرفت ما هم از تفتيش اوست
entries.english: He who has such a great talent in his beard— our arrest is the result of his enquiry.”

entries.farsi: رف شه بود چشمش الجرم / ‏بر گشاد از معرفت لب با حشم
entries.english: His eye was a knower of the King: consequently he opened his lips of knowledge to his followers.

entries.farsi: گفت وَ هُوَ مَعَكُمْ اين شاه بود / فعل ما مى‏ديد و سرمان مى‏شنود
entries.english: He said, “This King was and He is with you: he was seeing our actions and hearing our secret.

entries.farsi: چشم من ره برد شب شه را شناخت / ‏جمله شب با روى ماهش عشق باخت
entries.english: My eye made its way, recognised the King by night, and all night long played the game of love with his moon-like face.

entries.farsi: امت خود را بخواهم من از او / كاو نگرداند ز عارف هيچ رو
entries.english: I will beg forgiveness for my people from him, for he never averts his face from the knower.

entries.farsi: چشم عارف دان امان هر دو كون / ‏كه بدو يابيد هر بهرام عون
entries.english: Deem the eye of the knower to be the salvation of the two worlds, whereby every Bahram obtained help.

entries.farsi: ز آن محمد شافع هر داغ بود / كه ز جز حق چشم او ما زاغ بود
entries.english: Mohammed was the intercessor for every brand because his eye did not swerve for anything except God.

entries.farsi: در شب دنيا كه محجوب است شيد / ناظر حق بود و زو بودش اميد
entries.english: In the night of this world, where the sun is veiled, he was beholding God, and his hope was in Him.

entries.farsi: از أَ لَمْ نَشْ رَحْ دو چشمش سرمه يافت / ‏ديد آن چه جبرئيل آن بر نتافت
entries.english: His eyes received eye salve from “Did not We expand your breast?” He saw that which Gabriel could not endure.

entries.farsi: مر يتيمى را كه سرمه‏ى حق كشد / گردد او در يتيم با رشد
entries.english: The orphan to whom God applies eye salve becomes the orphan pearl endowed with guidance.

entries.farsi: نور او بر درها غالب شود / آن چنان مطلوب را طالب شود
entries.english: Its light overpowers pearls; it desires such an object of desire.

entries.farsi: در نظر بودش مقامات العباد / الجرم نامش خدا شاهد نهاد
entries.english: The stations of God’s servants were visible to him: consequently God named him ‘The Witness.’

entries.farsi: آلت شاهد زبان و چشم تيز / كه ز شب خيزش ندارد سر گريز
entries.english: The weapons of the Witness are a trenchant tongue and a keen eye, whose nightly vigil no secret can elude.

entries.farsi: قاضيان را در حكومت اين فن است / ‏شاهد ايشان را دو چشم روشن است
entries.english: Though a thousand pretenders may raise their heads, the Judge turns his ear towards the Witness.

entries.farsi: گر هزاران مدعى سر بر زند / گوش قاضى جانب شاهد كند
entries.english: This is the practice of judges in dealing justice: to them the witness is two clear eyes.

entries.farsi: گفت شاهد ز آن به جاى ديده است / ‏كاو به ديده‏ى بى‏غرض سر ديده است
entries.english: The words of the Witness are equivalent to the eye because he has seen the secret with a disinterested eye.

entries.farsi: مدعى ديده‏ست اما با غرض / ‏پرده باشد ديده‏ى دل را غرض
entries.english: The pretender has seen it, but with self-interest: self-interest is a veil upon the eye of the heart.

entries.farsi: حق همى‏خواهد كه تو زاهد شوى / ‏تا غرض بگذارى و شاهد شوى
entries.english: God desires that you should become an ascetic (zahid) in order that you may abandon self-interest and become a Witness (shahid);

entries.farsi: كاين غرضها پرده‏ى ديده بود / بر نظر چون پرده پيچيده بود
entries.english: For these motives of self-interest are veils upon the eye: they enfold the sight, like a veil.

entries.farsi: پس نبيند جمله را با طم و رم / ‏حبك الاشياء يعمى و يصم
entries.english: Therefore he does not see the whole in various aspects: your love of things makes you blind and deaf.

entries.farsi: در دلش خورشيد چون نورى نشاند / پيشش اختر را مقاديرى نماند
entries.english: Since the Sun caused a light to dwell in his heart, the stars no longer had any values for him.

entries.farsi: پس بديد او بى‏حجاب اسرار را / سير روح مومن و كفار را
entries.english: Therefore he beheld the mysteries without veil: the journey of the spirit of the true believers and the infidels.

entries.farsi: در زمين حق را و در چرخ سمى / ‏نيست پنهان‏تر ز روح آدمى
entries.english: God has not in the earth or in the lofty heaven anything more occult than the spirit of Man.

entries.farsi: باز كرد از رطب و يابس حق نورد / روح را مِنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّي مهر كرد
entries.english: God has unfolded moist or dry, He has sealed the spirit: of the amr of my Lord.’

entries.farsi: پس چو ديد آن روح را چشم عزيز / پس بر او پنهان نماند هيچ چيز
entries.english: Therefore, since the august eye beheld that spirit, nothing remains hidden from him.

entries.farsi: شاهد مطلق بود در هر نزاع / ‏بشكند گفتش خمار هر صداع
entries.english: He is the absolute witness in every dispute: his word crushes the crop sickness of every headache.

entries.farsi: نام حق عدل است و شاهد آن اوست / ‏شاهد عدل است زين رو چشم دوست
entries.english: God is named ‘the Just,’ and the Witness belongs to Him: for this reason the just Witness is the eye of the Beloved.

entries.farsi: منظر حق دل بود در دو سرا / كه نظر در شاهد آيد شاه را
entries.english: The object of God’s regard in both worlds is the heart, for the king’s gaze is fixed upon the favourite.

entries.farsi: عشق حق و سر شاهد بازى‏اش / ‏بود مايه‏ى جمله پرده سازى‏اش
entries.english: God’s love and the mystery of His dallying with His favourite were the origin of all His veil-making.

entries.farsi: پس از آن لوالك گفت اندر لقا / در شب معراج شاهدباز ما
entries.english: On that account, then, in meeting on the night of the Ascension our fond of dalliance said, ‘But for.’

entries.farsi: اين قضا بر نيك و بد حاكم بود / بر قضا شاهد نه حاكم مى‏شود
entries.english: This Destiny rules good and evil: does not the Witness become the ruler of Destiny?

entries.farsi: شد اسير آن قضا مير قضا / ‏شاد باش اى چشم تيز مرتضى
entries.english: The bondsman of Destiny became the Commander of Destiny: hail to you, O keen-sighted one who art pleasing!

entries.farsi: عارف از معروف بس درخواست كرد / كاى رقيب ما تو اندر گرم و سرد
entries.english: The knower made many a petition to the Known, saying, ‘O You who watches over us in heat and cold,

entries.farsi: اى مشير ما تو اندر خير و شر / از اشارتهات دل‏مان بى‏خبر
entries.english: O You who gives us intimations in weal and woe, our hearts are unaware of Your intimations,

entries.farsi: اى يرانا ال نراه روز و شب / ‏چشم بند ما شده ديد سبب
entries.english: O who daily and nightly sees us and whom we see not, regarding the secondary cause has muffled our eyes.

entries.farsi: چشم من از چشمها بگزيده شد / تا كه در شب آفتابم ديده شد
entries.english: My eye has been chosen above eyes, so that the Sun was beheld by me in the night.

entries.farsi: لطف معروف تو بود آن اى بهى / ‏پس كمال البر فى اتمامه
entries.english: That was Your well-known grace, O Beauteous One; and, ‘The perfection of kindness consists in making it complete.’

entries.farsi: يا رب اتمم نورنا فى الساهرة / ‏و انجنا من مفضحات قاهره
entries.english: O Lord, make our light complete in the plain of Resurrection and deliver us from shameful and overwhelming indignities!

entries.farsi: يار شب را روز مهجورى مده / ‏جان قربت ديده را دورى مده
entries.english: Do not let Your night-companion be banished in the daytime, do not inflict farness on the soul that has experienced nearness.

entries.farsi: بعد تو مرگ است با درد و نكال / ‏خاصه بعدى كه بود بعد الوصال
entries.english: Absence from You is a grievous and tormenting death, especially the absence that comes after enjoyment of Your favour.

entries.farsi: آن كه ديده‏ستت مكن ناديده‏اش / ‏آب زن بر سبزه‏ى باليده‏اش
entries.english: Do not put him that has seen You in the position of one that has not seen: sprinkle water on his verdure that has sprung up.

entries.farsi: من نكردم الابالى در روش / ‏تو مكن هم الابالى در خلش
entries.english: I have not acted recklessly while faring: do not You either act recklessly in pricking.

entries.farsi: هين مران از روى خود او را بعيد / آن كه او يك بار آن روى تو ديد
entries.english: Oh, do not drive far from Your face him who once beheld Your face!

entries.farsi: ديد روى جز تو شد غل گلو / ‏كل شى‏ء ما سوى اللهَّ باطل
entries.english: To behold the face of anyone but You is an iron collar for the throat: everything except God is vain.

entries.farsi: باطلند و مى‏نمايندم رشد / ز انكه باطل باطالن را مى‏كشد
entries.english: They are vain, but they show me the right way because vanity attracts the vain.

entries.farsi: ذره ذره كاندر اين ارض و سماست / ‏جنس خود را هر يكى چون كهرباست
entries.english: Each one of the atoms on atoms which exist in this earth and heaven is like amber for its congener.

entries.farsi: معده نان را مى‏كشد تا مستقر / مى‏كشد مر آب را تف جگر
entries.english: The belly attracts bread to its resting-place; the heat of the liver attracts water.

entries.farsi: ز انكه حس چشم آمد رنگ كش / ‏مغز و بينى مى‏كشد بوهاى خوش
entries.english: The eye is an attractor of beautiful persons from these quarters of the town; the brain is seeking scents from the rose-garden,

entries.farsi: چشم جذاب بتان زين كويها / مغز جويان از گلستان بويها
entries.english: Because the sense peculiar to the eye is an attractor of colour, while the brain and nose attract sweet perfumes.

entries.farsi: ين كششها اى خداى راز دان / ‏تو به جذب لطف خودمان ده امان
entries.english: O Lord who knows the secret, do You preserve us from these attractions by the attraction of Your grace!

entries.farsi: غالبى بر جاذبان اى مشترى / ‏شايد ار درماندگان را واخرى
entries.english: You, O Purchaser, are dominant over attractors: it would be fitting if You redeem the helpless.”

entries.farsi: رو به شه آورد چون تشنه به ابر / آن كه بود اندر شب قدر آن بدر
entries.english: He turned his face to the King as a thirsty man to a cloud— he who on the Night of Power was the Full-moon’s own.

entries.farsi: چون لسان و جان او بود آن او / آن او با او بود گستاخ گو
entries.english: Since his tongue and his spirit were His, he who is His may converse with Him boldly.

entries.farsi: وقت آن شد اى شه مكتوم سير / كز كرم ريشى بجنبانى به خير
entries.english: He said, “We have been bound like the spirit in its prison of clay: You are the Sun of the spirit on the Day of Judgement.

entries.farsi: گفت ما گشتيم چون جان بند طين / ‏آفتاب جان تويى در يوم دين
entries.english: O King whose course is concealed, the time is come for You graciously to make a movement with Your beard in clemency.

entries.farsi: هر يكى خاصيت خود را نمود / آن هنرها جمله بد بختى فزود
entries.english: Each one has displayed his specialty: all those talents have increased ill-fortune.

entries.farsi: آن هنرها گردن ما را ببست / ‏ز آن مناصب سر نگون‏ساريم و پست
entries.english: Those talents have bound our necks; by those high attainments we are headlong and low.

entries.farsi: آن هنر فى جيدنا حبل مسد / روز مردن نيست ز آن فن‏ها مدد
entries.english: Talent is a cord of palm-fibre on our neck: there is no help from those accomplishments on the day of death.”

entries.farsi: جز همان خاصيت آن خوش حواس / ‏كه به شب بد چشم او سلطان شناس
entries.english: Save only the specialty of that man endowed with goodly perceptions whose eye was recognising the Sultan in the night.

entries.farsi: آن هنرها جمله غول راه بود / غير چشمى كو ز شه آگاه بود
entries.english: All those talents were ghouls on the road, except the eye which was aware of the King.

entries.farsi: شاه را شرم از وى آمد روز بار / كه به شب بر روى شه بودش نظار
entries.english: On the day of audience the King was ashamed of him whose gaze was on the King’s face at night.

entries.farsi: خاصيت در گوش هم نيكو بود / كاو به بانگ سگ ز شير آگه شود
entries.english: And the dog that is acquainted with the loving King— even him you must entitle “the Dog of the Cave.”

entries.farsi: و آن سگ آگاه از شاه وداد / خود سگ كهفش لقب بايد نهاد
entries.english: Excellent, too, is the specialty in the ear; for he by the bark of a dog is made aware of the Lion.

entries.farsi: سگ چو بيدار است شب چون پاسبان / ‏بى‏خبر نبود ز شب خيز شهان
entries.english: When the dog is awake during the night, like a watchman, he is not ignorant of the nightly vigil of the kings.

entries.farsi: هين ز بد نامان نبايد ننگ داشت / ‏هوش بر اسرارشان بايد گماشت
entries.english: Listen, you must not disdain them that have a bad name: you must set your mind on their inward parts.

entries.farsi: هر كه او يك بار خود بد نام شد / خود نبايد نام جست و خام شد
entries.english: Whoever has once got a bad name must not seek a name and become half-baked.

entries.farsi: اى بسا زر كه سيه تابش كنند / تا شود ايمن ز تاراج و گزند
entries.english: Oh, many a gold is made black polished iron in order that it may be saved from pillage and calamity.

sections.title_fa: قصه آن كه گاو بحرى گوهر كاويان از قعر دريا بر آورد شب بر ساحل دريا نهد در درخش و تاب آن مى‏چرد، بازرگان از كمين برون آيد چون گاو از گوهر دورتر رفته باشد بازرگان به لجم و گل تيره ‏گوهر را بپوشاند و بر درخت گريزد الى آخر القصه و التقريب

sections.title_en: Story of the sea-cow: how it brings up the royal pearl from the depths of the ocean and at night lays it on the seashore and feeds in the resplendence and lustre thereof; and how the trader comes forth from his hiding-place and, when the cow has gone some distance away from the pearl, covers the pearl with loam and black clay and runs off and climbs a tree;
and so on to the end of the story and exposition.

entries.farsi: گاو آبى گوهر از بحر آورد / بنهد اندر مرج و گردش مى‏چرد
entries.english: The water-cow fetches a pearl out of the sea, lays it on the meadow, and grazes around it.

entries.farsi: در شعاع نور گوهر گاو آب / ‏مى‏چرد از سنبل و سوسن شتاب
entries.english: In the radiance of the light of the pearl the water-cow feeds hurriedly on hyacinths and lilies.

entries.farsi: ز آن فگنده‏ى گاو آبى عنبر است / ‏كه غذايش نرگس و نيلوفر است
entries.english: The excrement of the water-cow is ambergris because its food is narcissus and water lily.

entries.farsi: هر كه باشد قوت او نور جلال / ‏چون نزايد از لبش سحر حالل
entries.english: Anyone whose food is the Light of Majesty, how should not lawful magic spring from his lips?

entries.farsi: هر كه چون زنبور وحى استش تفل / ‏چون نباشد خانه‏ى او پر عسل
entries.english: Anyone who, like the bee, has been given inspiration as a prize, how should not his house be full of honey?

entries.farsi: مى‏چرد در نور گوهر آن بقر / ناگهان گردد ز گوهر دورتر
entries.english: The cow grazes in the light of the pearl; suddenly it moves some distance away from the pearl.

entries.farsi: تاجرى بر در نهد لجم سياه / ‏تا شود تاريك مرج و سبزه گاه
entries.english: A trader puts black loam on the pearl, so that the meadow and verdant ground becomes dark.

entries.farsi: پس گريزد مرد تاجر بر درخت / ‏گاو جويان مرد را با شاخ سخت
entries.english: Then the trader takes refuge on a tree, while the cow seeks the man with its hard horn.

entries.farsi: بيست بار آن گاو تازد گرد مرج / ‏تا كند آن خصم را در شاخ درج
entries.english: Twenty times the cow runs about the meadow, in order to impale its enemy on its horn.

entries.farsi: چون از او نوميد گردد گاو نر / آيد آن جا كه نهاده بد گهر
entries.english: When the fierce cow despairs of him, it comes to the place where the pearl was laid

entries.farsi: لجم بيند فوق در شاهوار / پس ز طين بگريزد او ابليس‏وار
entries.english: And sees the loam over the royal pearl; then it runs away from the clay, like Iblís.

entries.farsi: كآن بليس از متن طين كور و كر است / ‏گاو كى داند كه در گل گوهر است
entries.english: Iblís is blind and deaf to the gist of the clay, how should the cow know that the pearl is in the clay?

entries.farsi: اهْبِطُ وا افكند جان را در حضيض / ‏از نمازش كرد محروم اين محيض
entries.english: (The Divine command) fall cast the spirit into abasement: this menstruation excluded it from prayer.

entries.farsi: اى رفيقان زين مقيل و ز آن مقال / ‏اتقوا ان الهوى حيض الرجال
entries.english: O comrades, beware of this resting-place and of that talk: truly, sensuality is the menstruation of men.

entries.farsi: اهْبِطُ وا افكند جان را در بدن / ‏تا به گل پنهان بود در عدن
entries.english: (The Divine command) fall cast the spirit into the body, that the pearl of Aden might be hidden in clay.

entries.farsi: تاجرش داند و ليكن گاو نى / ‏اهل دل دانند و هر گل كاو نى
entries.english: The trader knows it, but the cow does not: the spiritual know, but not any clay digger.

entries.farsi: هر گلى كاندر دل او گوهرى است / ‏گوهرش غماز طين ديگرى است
entries.english: Every piece of clay in the heart of which there is a pearl— its pearl can tell the secrets of clay;

entries.farsi: و آن گلى كز رش حق نورى نيافت / ‏صحبت گلهاى پر در بر نتافت
entries.english: While the clay that has not been illumined by God’s sprinkling cannot bear the companionship of the pieces of clay that are filled with pearls.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد موش ما / هست بر لبهاى جو بر گوش ما
entries.english: This topic is endless; our mouse on the bank of the river is on our ear.

sections.title_fa: رجوع كردن به قصه‏ى طلب كردن آن موش آن چغز را در لب جو و كشيدن سر رشته تا چغز را در آب خبر شود از طلب او
sections.title_en: Return to the Story of the mouse seeking the frog on the river-bank and pulling the string
in order that the frog in the water might become aware of his seeking him.

entries.farsi: آن سرشته‏ى عشق رشته مى‏كشد / بر اميد وصل چغز با رشد
entries.english: That moulded of love is pulling the string in hope of being united with the righteous frog.

entries.farsi: مى‏تند بر رشته‏ى دل دم به دم / ‏كه سر رشته به دست آورده‏ام
entries.english: He is perpetually harping on the heart-string, saying, “I have got the end of the string in my paw.

entries.farsi: همچو تارى شد دل و جان در شهود / تا سر رشته به من رويى نمود
entries.english: My heart and soul have become as a thread in contemplation, ever since the end of the string showed itself to me.”

entries.farsi: خود غراب البين آمد ناگهان / ‏در شكار موش و بردش ز آن مكان
entries.english: But suddenly the raven of separation came to chase the mouse and carried it off from that spot.

entries.farsi: چون بر آمد بر هوا موش از غراب / ‏منسحب شد چغز نيز از قعر آب
entries.english: When the mouse was taken up into the air by the raven, the frog too was dragged from the bottom of the water.

entries.farsi: موش در منقار زاغ و چغز هم / ‏در هوا آويخته پا در رتم
entries.english: The mouse in the raven’s beak, and the frog likewise suspended in the air, its foot in the string.

entries.farsi: خلق مى‏گفتند زاغ از مكر و كيد / چغز آبى را چگونه كرد صيد
entries.english: The people were saying, “How could the raven make the water-frog its prey by craft and cun­ ning? How could it go into the water, and how could it carry him off? When was the water-frog the raven’s prey?”

entries.farsi: چغز گفتا اين سزاى آن كسى / ‏كاو چو بى‏آبان شود جفت خسى
entries.english: “This,” said the frog “is the fit punishment for that one who, like persons devoid of honour, consorts with a rascal.”

entries.farsi: چون شد اندر آب و چونش در ربود / چغز آبى كى شكار زاغ بود
entries.english: Oh, alas, alas for the sorrow caused by a base friend! O sirs, seek ye a good companion.

entries.farsi: اى فغان از يار ناجنس اى فغان / ‏همنشين نيك جوييد اى مهان
entries.english: Reason complains bitterly of the vicious carnal soul: as an ugly nose on a beautiful face.

entries.farsi: عقل را افغان ز نفس پر عيوب / ‏همچو بينى بدى بر روى خوب
entries.english: Reason was saying to him, “it is certain that congeniality is spiritual in origin and is not from water and clay.”

entries.farsi: عقل مى‏گفتش كه جنسيت يقين / ‏از ره معنى است نى از آب و طين
entries.english: Take heed, do not become a worshipper of form and do not say this. Do not seek the secret of congeniality in the form.

entries.farsi: هين مشو صورت پرست و اين مگو / سر جنسيت به صورت در مجو
entries.english: Form resembles the mineral and the stone: an inorganic thing has no knowledge of congeniality.

entries.farsi: صورت آمد چون جماد و چون حجر / نيست جامد را ز جنسيت خبر
entries.english: The spirit is like an ant, and the body like a grain of wheat which it carries to and fro continually.

entries.farsi: جان چو مور و تن چو دانه‏ى گندمى / ‏مى‏كشاند سو به سويش هر دمى
entries.english: The ant knows that the grains of which it has taken charge will be changed and become homogeneous with it.

entries.farsi: مور داند كآن حبوب مرتهن / ‏مستحيل و جنس من خواهد شدن
entries.english: One ant picks up barley on the road; another ant picks up a grain of wheat and runs away.

entries.farsi: آن يكى مورى گرفت از راه جو / مور ديگر گندمى بگرفت و دو
entries.english: The barley does not hurry to the wheat, but the ant comes to the ant; yes.

entries.farsi: جو سوى گندم نمى‏تازد ولى / ‏مور سوى مور مى‏آيد بلى
entries.english: The going of the barley to the wheat is consequential: the ant, mark you, returns to its congener.

entries.farsi: رفتن جو سوى گندم تابع است / ‏مور را بين كه به جنسش راجع است
entries.english: Do not say, “Why did the wheat go to the barley?” Fix your eye on the holder, not on that which he holds in pawn.

entries.farsi: تو مگو گندم چرا شد سوى جو / چشم را بر خصم نه نى بر گرو
entries.english: A black ant on a black felt cloth: the ant is hidden; the grain is visible on its way,

entries.farsi: عقل گويد چشم را نيكو نگر / دانه هرگز كى رود بى‏دانه بر
entries.english: Reason says, “Look well to your eye: when does a grain ever go along without a grain-bearer?”

entries.farsi: زين سبب آمد سوى اصحاب كلب / ‏هست صورتها حبوب و مور قلب
entries.english: On this account the dog came to the Companions: the forms are the grains, while the heart is the ant.

entries.farsi: اين قفس پيدا و آن فرخش نهان / ‏بى‏قفس كش كى قفس باشد روان
entries.english: Hence Jesus goes to the holy ones of Heaven: the cages were diverse, the young birds were of the same kind.

entries.farsi: ز آن شود عيسى سوى پاكان چرخ / ‏بد قفسها مختلف يك جنس فرخ
entries.english: This cage is visible, but the young bird in it is hidden: how should the cage is moving without a cage-carrier?

entries.farsi: اى خنك چشمى كه عقل استش امير / عاقبت بين باشد و حبر و قرير
entries.english: Oh, blessed is the eye that is ruled by reason that discerns the end and is wise and cool.

entries.farsi: فرق زشت و نغز از عقل آوريد / نى ز چشمى كز سيه گفت و سپيد
entries.english: Get the distinction between evil and good from reason, not from the eye that tells of black and white.

entries.farsi: آفت مرغ است چشم كام بين / ‏مخلص مرغ است عقل دام بين
entries.english: The eye is beguiled by the verdure on dunghills, reason says, “Put it to my touchstone.”

entries.farsi: چشم غره شد به خضراى دمن / ‏عقل گويد بر محك ماش زن
entries.english: The eye that sees its desire is the bird’s bane; reason, which sees the trap, is the bird’s means of deliverance.

entries.farsi: دام ديگر بد كه عقلش درنيافت / ‏وحى غايب بين بدين سو ز آن شتافت
entries.english: There was another trap which reason did not perceive; hence the inspiration which beholds the unseen sped in this direction.

entries.farsi: جنس و ناجنس از خرد دانى شناخت / ‏سوى صورتها نشايد زود تاخت
entries.english: By reason you can recognise congener and non-congener: you ought not to run at once to forms.

entries.farsi: بر كشيدش فوق اين نيلى حصار / مرغ گردونى چو چغزش زاغ‏وار
entries.english: My being your congener is not in respect of form: Jesus, in the form of man, was homogeneous with the angels

entries.farsi: نيست جنسيت به صورت لى و لك / ‏عيسى آمد در بشر جنس ملك
entries.english: The celestial Bird carried him up above this dark-blue fortress as the raven the frog.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى عبد الغوث و ربودن پريان او را و سالها ميان پريان ساكن شدن او و بعد از سالها آمدن او ‏به شهر و فرزندان خويش و باز ناشكيفتن او از آن پريان به حكم جنسيت معنى و هم دلى او با ايشان
sections.title_en: Story of ‘Abdu ’l-Ghawth and his being carried off by the peris and staying among them for years,
and how after years he returned to his town and his children, but could not endure to be parted from the peris, because he was really their congener and spiritually one with them.

entries.farsi: بود عبد الغوث هم جنس پرى / ‏چون پرى نه سال در پنهان پرى
entries.english: ‘Abdu ’l-Ghawth was a congener of the peri: for nine years he was flying about invisibly, like a peri.

entries.farsi: شد زنش را نسل از شوى دگر / و آن يتيمانش ز مرگش در سمر
entries.english: His wife had offspring by another husband, and his orphans used to talk of his death,

entries.farsi: كه مر او را گرگ زد يا ره زنى / ‏يا فتاد اندر چهى يا مكمنى
entries.english: Saying, “A wolf or a brigand attacked him, or he fell into a pit or ambush.”

entries.farsi: جمله فرزندانش در اشغال مست / ‏خود نگفتندى كه بابايى بده‏ست
entries.english: All his children were passionately absorbed in occupations: they never said that they had a father.

entries.farsi: بعد نه سال آمد او هم عاريه / ‏گشت پيدا باز شد متواريه
entries.english: After nine years he came temporarily: he appeared and disappeared again.

entries.farsi: يك مهى مهمان فرزندان خويش / ‏بود و ز آن پس كس نديدش رنگ بيش
entries.english: He was the guest of his children for one month, and after that nobody saw any more of him.

entries.farsi: برد هم جنسى پريانش چنان / ‏كه ربايد روح را زخم سنان
entries.english: Homogeneity with the peris carried him off; just as a spear thrust ravishes the spirit.

entries.farsi: چون بهشتى جنس جنت آمده‏ست / ‏هم ز جنسيت شود يزدان پرست
entries.english: Since one who is destined for Paradise is homogeneous with Paradise, on account of homogeneity he also becomes a worshipper of God.

entries.farsi: نه نبى فرمود جود و محمده / ‏شاخ جنت دان به دنيا آمده
entries.english: Has not the Prophet said, “Know that liberality and virtue are branches of Paradise come into this world”?

entries.farsi: مهرها را جمله جنس مهر خوان / ‏قهرها را جمله جنس قهر دان
entries.english: Declare all loves to be homogeneous with Love; deem all wraths to be homogeneous with Wrath.

entries.farsi: الابالى الابالى آورد / ز انكه جنس هم بوند اندر خرد
entries.english: The reckless man gets a reckless man, because they are congenial in respect of their understanding.

entries.farsi: بود جنسيت در ادريس از نجوم / ‏هشت سال او با زحل بد در قدوم
entries.english: The congeniality in Idrís was from the stars: for eight years he was coming along with Saturn.

entries.farsi: بعد غيبت چون كه آورد او قدوم / ‏در زمين مى‏گفت او درس نجوم
entries.english: He was his companion in the East and in the West; his partner in conversation and familiar with his characteristics.

entries.farsi: در مشارق در مغارب يار او / هم حديث و محرم آثار او
entries.english: When after his absence he arrived, on the earth he was always giving lessons in astronomy.

entries.farsi: پيش او استارگان خوش صف زده / ‏اختران در درس او حاضر شده
entries.english: The stars gladly ranged themselves in ranks before him: the stars attended his lectures,

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه خلق آواز نجوم / مى‏شنيدند از خصوص و از عموم
entries.english: So that the people, nobles and commons alike, would hear the voices of the stars.

entries.farsi: جذب جنسيت كشيده تا زمين / ‏اختران را پيش او كرده مبين
entries.english: The attraction exerted by spiritual affinity drew the stars down to the earth and caused them to speak plainly before him.

entries.farsi: هر يكى نام خود و احوال خود / باز گفته پيش او شرح رصد
entries.english: Each one declared its name and its circumstances and expounded to him astronomical observation.

entries.farsi: آن نظر كه كرد حق در وى نهان / ‏چون نهد در تو تو گردى جنس آن
entries.english: What is homogeneity? A species of insight whereby people gain admission into one another.

entries.farsi: چيست جنسيت يكى نوع نظر / كه بدان يابند ره در همدگر
entries.english: When God endows you with the same insight which He has hidden in him (another person), you become his congener.

entries.farsi: هر طرف چه مى‏كشد تن را نظر / بى‏خبر را كى كشاند با خبر
entries.english: What draws a body in any direction? Insight. How should the conscious attract the unconscious?

entries.farsi: چون كه اندر مرد خوى زن نهد / او مخنث گردد و گان مى‏دهد
entries.english: When He implants in a man the nature of a woman, he becomes a catamite and has sex with men.

entries.farsi: چون نهد در زن خدا خوى نرى / ‏طالب زن گردد آن زن سعترى
entries.english: When God implants in a woman the masculine nature, she becomes a lesbian.

entries.farsi: چون نهد در تو صفات جبرئيل / ‏همچو فرخى بر هوا جويى سبيل
entries.english: When He implants in you the qualities of Gabriel, you will seek the way up to the air, like a young bird,

entries.farsi: منتظر بنهاده ديده در هوا / از زمين بيگانه عاشق بر سما
entries.english: Gazing expectantly, your eye fixed upon the air, estranged from the earth and enamoured of heaven.

entries.farsi: چون نهد در تو صفتهاى خرى / ‏صد پرت گر هست بر آخور پرى
entries.english: When He implants in you the asinine qualities, if you have a hundred wings you will fly to the stable.

entries.farsi: از پى صورت نيامد موش خوار / از خبيثى شد زبون موش خوار
entries.english: The mouse is not despised for its form: it becomes a helpless victim of the kite because of its villainous character.

entries.farsi: طعمه جوى و خائن و ظلمت پرست / ‏از پنير و فستق و دوشاب مست
entries.english: It is a food-seeker and a traitor and a lover of darkness, besotted with cheese and pistachio nuts and syrup.

entries.farsi: باز اشهب را چو باشد خوى موش / ‏ننگ موشان باشد و عار وحوش
entries.english: When the white falcon has the nature of a mouse, it is an object of contempt to the mice and a disgrace to the wild animals.

entries.farsi: خوى آن هاروت و ماروت اى پسر / چون بگشت و دادشان خوى بشر
entries.english: O son, when the nature of Harut and Marut was changed and He bestowed on them the nature of man,

entries.farsi: در فتادند از لَنَحْ نُ الصافون / ‏در چه بابل ببسته سر نگون
entries.english: They fell from truly; we are they that stand in rows into the pit at Babylon shackled head-foremost.

entries.farsi: لوح محفوظ از نظرشان دور شد / لوح ايشان ساحر و مسحور شد
entries.english: The Guarded Tablet was removed from their sight: sorcerer and ensorcelled became their tablet.

entries.farsi: پر همان و سر همان هيكل همان / ‏موسيى بر عرش و فرعونى مهان
entries.english: The same arms, the same head, the same figure— a Moses is celestial, while a Pharaoh is contemptible.

entries.farsi: خاك گور از مرد هم يابد شرف / ‏تا نهد بر گور او دل روى و كف
entries.english: Be always in quest of the nature and consort with him whose nature is good: observe how rose-oil has received the nature of the rose.

entries.farsi: در پى خو باش و با خوش خو نشين / ‏خو پذيرى روغن گل را ببين
entries.english: The earth of the grave is ennobled by the man, so that the heart lays his face and hands on his grave.

entries.farsi: خاك از همسايگى جسم پاك / ‏چون مشرف آمد و اقبال‏ناك
entries.english: Since the earth is ennobled and made fortunate by the neighbourhood of the pure body,

entries.farsi: پس تو هم الجار ثم الدار گو / گر دلى دارى برو دل دار جو
entries.english: Then, say, “first the neighbour, then the house”: if you have a heart, go, seek a sweetheart.

entries.farsi: خاك او هم سيرت جان مى‏شود / سرمه‏ى چشم عزيزان مى‏شود
entries.english: His dust is endued with the character of his soul: it becomes an eye salve for the eyes of those who are dear.

entries.farsi: اى بسا در گور خفته خاك‏وار / به ز صد احيا به نفع و انتشار
entries.english: Oh, many a one sleeping like dust in the grave is superior in usefulness and open-handedness to a hundred living.

entries.farsi: سايه برده او و خاكش سايه‏مند / صد هزاران زنده در سايه‏ى وى‏اند
entries.english: He has taken away his shadow, but his dust is overshadowing: hundreds of thousands of the living are in his shadow.

sections.title_fa: داستان آن مرد كه وظيفه‏اى داشت از محتسب تبريز و وامها كرده بود بر اميد آن وظيفه و او را خبر نه از وفات او، حاصل از هيچ زنده‏اى وام او گزارده نشد الا از محتسب متوفى گزارده شد چنان كه گفته‏اند
sections.title_en: Story of the man who had an allowance from the Police Inspector of Tabriz and had incurred debts

in expectation of that allowance, since he was unaware of his death. The gist is that his debts were paid, not by any living person, but by the deceased Inspector, as has been said, “He that died and found peace is not dead: the dead one is the man dead among the living.”

entries.farsi: ليس من مات فاستراح بميت / انما الميت ميت الاحياء
entries.english: A certain dervish, who was in debt, came from the outlying provinces to Tabriz.

entries.farsi: آن يكى درويش ز اطراف ديار / جانب تبريز آمد وامدار
entries.english: His debts amounted to nine thousand pieces of gold. It happened that in Tabriz was Badru’din ‘Umar.

entries.farsi: نه هزارش وام بد از زر مگر / بود در تبريز بدر الدين عمر
entries.english: He was the Police Inspector, at heart he was an ocean: every hair’s tip of him was a dwelling-place of Hatím.

entries.farsi: محتسب بد او به دل بحر آمده / ‏هر سر مويش يكى حاتم‏كده
entries.english: Hatím, had he been, would have become a beggar to him and laid his head and made himself the dust of his feet.

entries.farsi: حاتم ار بودى گداى او شدى / ‏سر نهادى خاك پاى او شدى
entries.english: If he had given an ocean of limpid water to a thirsty man, such was his generosity that he would be ashamed of that gift;

entries.farsi: گر بدادى تشنه را بحرى زالل / ‏در كرم شرمنده بودى ز آن نوال
entries.english: And if he had made a mote a place of sunrise, that would to his lofty aspiration to be an unworthy action.

entries.farsi: ور بكردى ذره‏اى را مشرقى / ‏بودى آن در همتش نالايقى
entries.english: That poor stranger came in hope of him, for to poor strangers he was always a kinsman and relative.

entries.farsi: بر اميد او بيامد آن غريب / ‏كاو غريبان را بدى خويش و نسيب
entries.english: That poor stranger was familiar with his door and had paid innumerable debts from his bounty.

entries.farsi: با درش بود آن غريب آموخته / ‏وام بى‏حد از عطايش توخته
entries.english: In reliance upon that generous he ran into debt, for the man was confident of his donations.

entries.farsi: هم به پشت آن كريم او وام كرد / كه به بخششهاش واثق بود مرد
entries.english: He had been made reckless by him and eager to incur debts in hope of that munificent sea.

entries.farsi: الابالى گشته زو و وام جو / بر اميد قلزم اكرام خو
entries.english: His creditors looked sour, while he was laughing happily, like the rose, on account of that garden of generous souls.

entries.farsi: وام داران رو ترش او شاد كام / ‏همچو گل خندان از آن روض الكرام
entries.english: When his back is warmed by the Sun of the Arabs, what does he care for the moustache of Bu Lahab?

entries.farsi: گرم شد پشتش ز خورشيد عرب / ‏چه غم استش از سبال بو لهب
entries.english: When he has a covenant and alliance with the rain-cloud, how should he grudge water to the water-carriers?

entries.farsi: ساحران واقف از دست خدا / كى نهند اين دست و پا را دست و پا
entries.english: How should the magicians who were acquainted with God’s Hand bestow hands and feet upon these hands and feet?

entries.farsi: چون كه دارد عهد و پيوند سحاب / ‏كى دريغ آيد ز سقايانش آب
entries.english: The fox that is backed by those lions will break the skulls of the leopards with his fist.

sections.title_fa: ‏بشكند كله‏ى پلنگان را به مشت روبهى كه هست ز آن شيرانش پشت آمدن جعفر به گرفتن قلعه اى به تنهايى و مشورت كردن ملك آن قلعه در دفع او و گفتن آن وزير ملك را كه زنهار تسليم كن و از جهل تهور مكن كه اين مرد مويد است و از حق جمعيت عظيم دارد در
‏جان خويش الى آخره
sections.title_en: How Jafar, may God be well-pleased with him, advanced alone to capture a fortress, and how the king of the fortress consulted as to the means of repelling him, and how the vizier said to the king, “Beware! Surrender and do not be so foolhardy as to hurl yourself upon him;
for this man is aided and possesses in his soul a great collectedness from God,” etc.

entries.farsi: چون كه جعفر رفت سوى قلعه‏اى / ‏قلعه پيش كام خشكش جرعه‏اى
entries.english: When Jafar advanced against a certain fortress, the fortress to his dry palate a single gulp.

entries.farsi: يك سواره تاخت تا قلعه به كر / تا در قلعه ببستند از حذر
entries.english: Riding alone, he charged up to the fortress, so that they locked the fortress-gate in dread.

entries.farsi: زهره نه كس را كه پيش آيد به جنگ / ‏اهل كشتى را چه زهره با نهنگ
entries.english: No one dared to meet him in battle: what stomach has the ship’s crew with a leviathan?

entries.farsi: روى آورد آن ملك سوى وزير / كه چه چاره‏ست اندرين وقت اى مشير
entries.english: The king turned to his vizier, saying, “What is to be done in this crisis, Counsellor?”

entries.farsi: گفت آن كه ترك گويى كبر و فن / ‏پيش او آيى به شمشير و كفن
entries.english: He replied, “that you should bid farewell to pride and cunning, and come to him with sword and shroud.”

entries.farsi: گفت آخر نه يكى مردى است فرد / گفت منگر خوار در فردى مرد
entries.english: “Why,” said the king, “is not he a single man alone?” He replied, “Do not look with contempt on the man’s loneliness.

entries.farsi: چشم بگشا قلعه را بنگر نكو / همچو سيماب است لرزان پيش او
entries.english: Open your eye: look well at the fortress: it is trembling before him like quicksilver.

entries.farsi: شسته در زين آن چنان محكم پى است / ‏گوييا شرقى و غربى با وى است
entries.english: He sits on the saddle; his nerve is just as unshaken as if an East and West were accompanying him.

entries.farsi: چند كس همچون فدايى تاختند / خويشتن را پيش او انداختند
entries.english: Several men rushed forward, like Fida’is, and flung themselves into combat with him.

entries.farsi: هر يكى را او به گرزى مى‏فگند / سر نگون‏سار اندر اقدام سمند
entries.english: He felled each of them with a blow of his mace headlong at the feet of his steed.

entries.farsi: داده بودش صنع حق جمعيتى / ‏كه همى‏زد يك تنه بر امتى
entries.english: God’s action had bestowed on him such collectedness that he was attacking a people single-handed.

entries.farsi: چشم من چون ديد روى آن قباد / كثرت اعداد از چشمم فتاد
entries.english: When mine eye beheld the face of that emperor, plurality vanished from my sight.”

entries.farsi: اختران بسيار و خورشيد ار يكى است / ‏پيش او بنياد ايشان مندكى است
entries.english: The stars are many; though the sun is one, on his appearance their foundation is demolished.

entries.farsi: گر هزاران موش پيش آرند سر / گربه را نه ترس باشد نه حذر
entries.english: If a thousand mice put forth their heads, the cat feels no fear or apprehension of danger.

entries.farsi: كى به پيش آيند موشان اى فالن / ‏نيست جمعيت درون جانشان
entries.english: How should mice advance, O such-and-such? They have no collectedness in their souls.

entries.farsi: هست جمعيت به صورتها فشار / جمع معنى خواه هين از كردگار
entries.english: The collectedness in outward forms is a vain thing: listen, beg from the Creator collectedness of spirit.

entries.farsi: نيست جمعيت ز بسيارى جسم / ‏جسم را بر باد قايم دان چو اسم
entries.english: Collectedness is not the result of bodily multitude: know that body, like name, is built on air.

entries.farsi: در دل موش ار بدى جمعيتى / ‏جمع گشتى چند موش از حميتى
entries.english: If there were any collectedness in the heart of the mouse, a number of mice would be collected by a feeling of indignation,

entries.farsi: آن يكى چشمش بكندى از ضراب / ‏و آن دگر گوشش دريدى هم بناب
entries.english: And, rushing up like assassins, they would throw themselves on a cat without any respite.

entries.farsi: بر زدندى چون فدايى حمله‏اى / ‏خويش را بر گربه‏ى بى‏مهله‏اى
entries.english: One would tear out her eyes in conflict, while another would rip her ears with its teeth,

entries.farsi: و آن دگر سوراخ كردى پهلواش / ‏از جماعت گم شدى بيرون شواش
entries.english: And another make a hole in her side: there would be no way of escape for her from the united party.

entries.farsi: ليك جمعيت ندارد جان موش / ‏بجهد از جانش به بانگ گربه هوش
entries.english: But the soul of the mouse has no collectedness: at the cry of a cat its wits fly out of its soul.

entries.farsi: خشك گردد موش ز آن گربه‏ى عيار / گر بود اعداد موشان صد هزار
entries.english: The mouse is paralysed by the wily cat, if the numbers of the mice amount to a hundred thousand.

entries.farsi: از رمه‏ى انبه چه غم قصاب را / انبهى هش چه بندد خواب را
entries.english: What cares the butcher for the numerous flocks? How can abundance of consciousness prevent slumber?

entries.farsi: مالِكَ الْمُلْكِ است جمعيت دهد / شير را تا بر گله‏ى گوران جهد
entries.english: He is the Lord of the kingdom: He gives collectedness to the lion, so that he springs on the herd of wild asses.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران گور ده شاخ و دلير / چون عدم باشند پيش صول شير
entries.english: A hundred thousand savage and courageous wild asses are as naught before the onset of the lion.

entries.farsi: مالِكَ الْمُلْكِ است بدهد ملك حسن / ‏يوسفى را تا بود چون ماء مزن
entries.english: He is the Lord of the kingdom: He gives to a Joseph the kingdom of Beauty, so that he is as the water of white clouds.

entries.farsi: در رخى بنهد شعاع اخترى / ‏كه شود شاهى غالم دخترى
entries.english: He bestows upon one face the radiance of a star, so that a king becomes the slave of a girl.

entries.farsi: بنهد اندر روى ديگر نور خود / كه ببيند نيم شب هر نيك و بد
entries.english: He bestows upon another face His own Light, so that at midnight it sees everything good and evil.

entries.farsi: يوسف و موسى ز حق بردند نور / در رخ و رخسار و در ذات الصدور
entries.english: Joseph and Moses fetched light from God into their cheeks and countenances and into their inmost bosoms.

entries.farsi: روى موسى بارقى انگيخته / ‏پيش رو او توبره آويخته
entries.english: The face of Moses shot forth a flashing beam: he hung a veil in front of his face.

entries.farsi: نور رويش آن چنان بردى بصر / كه زمرد از دو ديده‏ى مار كر
entries.english: The splendour of his face would have dazzled eyes as the emerald the eyes of the deaf adder.

entries.farsi: او ز حق درخواسته تا توبره / ‏گردد آن نور قوى را ساتره
entries.english: He besought God that the veil might become a covering for that powerful Light.

entries.farsi: توبره گفت از گليمت ساز هين / ‏كآن لباس عارفى آمد امين
entries.english: He said, “Listen, make a veil of your felt raiment, for the garment of gnosis can be trusted,

entries.farsi: كآن كسا از نور صبرى يافته‏ست / ‏نور جان در تار و پودش تافته‏ست
entries.english: Because that robe has become inured to the Light: the Light of the Spirit shines through its warp and woof.

entries.farsi: جز چنين خرقه نخواهد شد صوان / ‏نور ما را بر نتابد غير آن
entries.english: Nothing will be a repository except a mantle like this: nothing else can endure Our Light.

entries.farsi: كوه قاف ار پيش آيد بهر سد / همچو كوه طور نورش بر درد
entries.english: If Mt Qáf should come forward as a barrier, the Light would rend it asunder like Mt Sinai.”

entries.farsi: از كمال قدرت ابدان رجال / ‏يافت اندر نور بى‏چون احتمال
entries.english: Through the omnipotence the bodies of men have gained ability to support the unconditioned Light.

entries.farsi: آن چه طورش بر نتابد ذره‏اى / ‏قدرتش جا سازد از قاروره‏اى
entries.english: His power makes a glass vessel the dwelling-place of that Light of which Sinai cannot bear a mote.

entries.farsi: گشت مشكات و زجاجى جاى نور / كه همى‏درد ز نور آن قاف و طور
entries.english: A lamp-niche and a lamp-glass have become the dwelling-place of the Light by which Mt Qáf and Mt Sinai are torn to pieces.

entries.farsi: جسمشان مشكات دان دلشان زجاج / ‏تافته بر عرش و افلاک اين سراج
entries.english: Know that their bodies are the lamp-niche and their hearts the glass: this lamp illumines the empyrean and the heavens.

entries.farsi: نورشان حيران اين نور آمده / ‏چون ستاره زين ضحى فانى شده
entries.english: Their light is dazzled by this Light and vanishes like the stars in this radiance of morning.

entries.farsi: در دل مومن بگنجيدم چو ضيف / ‏بى‏ز چون و بى‏چگونه بى‏ز كيف
entries.english: Hence the Seal of the prophets has related (the saying) of the everlasting and eternal Lord—

entries.farsi: زين حكايت كرد آن ختم رسل / ‏از مليك ال يزال و لم يزل
entries.english: “I am not contained in the heavens or in the void or in the exalted intelligences and souls;

entries.farsi: كه نگنجيدم در افلاک و خال / در عقول و در نفوس با عال
entries.english: I am contained, as a guest, in the true believer’s heart, without qualification or definition or description,

entries.farsi: تا به داللى آن دل فوق و تحت / ‏يابد از من پادشاهيها و بخت
entries.english: To the end that by the mediation of that heart above and below may win from Me sovereignties and fortune.

entries.farsi: بى‏چنين آيينه از خوبى من / ‏بر نتابد نه زمين و نه زمن
entries.english: Without such a mirror neither Earth nor Time could bear the vision of My beauty.

entries.farsi: بر دو كون اسب ترحم تاختم / ‏بس عريض آيينه‏اى بر ساختم
entries.english: I caused the steed of mercy to gallop over the two worlds: I fashioned a very spacious mirror.

entries.farsi: هر دمى زين آينه پنجاه عرس / بشنو آيينه ولى شرحش مپرس
entries.english: From this mirror at every moment fifty wedding-feasts: hearken to the mirror, but do not ask to describe it.”

entries.farsi: حاصل اين كز لبس خويشش پرده ساخت / ‏كه نفوذ آن قمر را مى‏شناخت
entries.english: The gist is this, that Moses made a veil of his raiment, since he knew the penetrating nature of that Moon.

entries.farsi: گر بدى پرده ز غير لبس او / پاره گشتى گر بدى كوه دو تو
entries.english: Had the veil been of anything except his raiment, it would have been torn to shreds, if it had been a solid mountain.

entries.farsi: ز آهنين ديوارها نافذ شدى / ‏توبره با نور حق چه فن زدى
entries.english: It would penetrate through iron walls: what contrivance could the veil employ against the Light of God?

entries.farsi: گشته بود آن توبره صاحب تفى / ‏بود وقت شور خرقه‏ى عارفى
entries.english: That veil had become glowing: it was the mantle of a gnostic in the moment of ecstasy.

entries.farsi: ز آن شود آتش رهين سوخته / ‏كاوست با آتش ز پيش آموخته
entries.english: The fire is deposited in the tinder because it is already familiar with the fire.

entries.farsi: و ز هوا و عشق آن نور رشاد / خود صفورا هر دو ديده باد داد
entries.english: And in sooth Safura, from desire and love for that Light of true guidance, sacrificed both her eyes.

entries.farsi: اوال بر بست يك چشم و بديد / نور روى او و آن چشمش پريد
entries.english: At first she closed one eye and beheld the light of his face; and that eye was lost.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن صبرش نماند و آن دگر / بر گشاد و كرد خرج آن قمر
entries.english: Afterwards she could no longer restrain herself and she opened the other and spent it on that Moon.

entries.farsi: همچنان مرد مجاهد نان دهد / چون بر او زد نور طاعت جان دهد
entries.english: Even so the warrior gives away his bread; when the light of devotion strikes on him, he gives away his life.

entries.farsi: پس زنى گفتش ز چشم عبهرى / ‏كه ز دستت رفت حسرت مى‏خورى
entries.english: Then a woman said to her, “Are you grieving for the jonquil-like eye that you have lost?”

entries.farsi: روزن چشمم ز مه ويران شده‏ست / ‏ليك مه چون گنج در ويران نشست
entries.english: “I am grieving,” she replied, “would that I had a hundred thousand eyes to lavish!

entries.farsi: گفت حسرت مى‏خورم كه صد هزار / ديده بودى تا همى‏كردم نثار
entries.english: The window, mine eye, has been ruined by the Moon; but the Moon is seated like the treasure in the ruin.

entries.farsi: كى گذارد گنج كاين ويرانه‏ام / ‏ياد آرد از رواق و خانه‏ام
entries.english: How should the treasure let this ruin of mine have memory of my porch and house?”

entries.farsi: نور روى يوسفى وقت عبور / مى‏فتادى در شباك هر قصور
entries.english: The light of Joseph’s face, when he was passing by, used to fall on the latticed windows of every villa,

entries.farsi: پس بگفتندى درون خانه در / يوسف است اين سو به سيران و گذر
entries.english: And the people within the house would say, “Joseph is taking a walk in this quarter and passing by”;

entries.farsi: ز انكه بر ديوار ديدندى شعاع / ‏فهم كردندى پس اصحاب بقاع
entries.english: For they would see the radiance on the wall, and then the landlords would understand.

entries.farsi: خانه‏اى را كش دريچه‏ست آن طرف / ‏دارد از سيران آن يوسف شرف
entries.english: The house that has its window in that direction is ennobled by that Joseph’s walking for recreation.

entries.farsi: عشق‏ورزى آن دريچه‏كردن است / ‏كز جمال دوست سينه روشن است
entries.english: Listen, open a window towards Joseph and begin to delight yourself by looking at him through the aperture.

entries.farsi: هين دريچه سوى يوسف باز كن / ‏وز شكافش فرجه‏اى آغاز كن
entries.english: The business of love is to make that window, for the breast is illumined by the beauty of the Beloved.

entries.farsi: پس هماره روى معشوقه نگر / اين به دست تست بشنو اى پدر
entries.english: Therefore gaze incessantly on the face of the Beloved! This is in your power. Hearken, O father!

entries.farsi: راه كن در اندرونها خويش را / دور كن ادراك غير انديش را
entries.english: Make a way for yourself into the innermost parts: banish the perception that is concerned with other.

entries.farsi: كيميا دارى دواى پوست كن / ‏دشمنان را زين صناعت دوست كن
entries.english: You possess an elixir: treat your skin, and by means of this art make your enemies your friends.

entries.farsi: چون شدى زيبا بدان زيبا رسى / ‏كه رهاند روح را از بى‏كسى
entries.english: When you have become beauteous you will attain unto the Beauteous One who delivers the spirit from friendlessness.

entries.farsi: پرورش مر باغ جانها را نمش / ‏زنده كرده مرده‏ى غم را دمش
entries.english: His moisture is nourishment for the garden of spirits; His breath revives him that has died of anguish.

entries.farsi: نه همه ملك جهان دون دهد / صد هزاران ملك گوناگون دهد
entries.english: He does not bestow the entire kingdom of the base world; He bestows a hundred thousand kingdoms of diverse kinds.

entries.farsi: بر سر ملك جمالش داد حق / ‏ملكت تعبير بى‏درس و سبق
entries.english: God gave him, in addition to the kingdom of beauty, the kingdom of interpretation without his having studied and taken lessons.

entries.farsi: ملكت حسنش سوى زندان كشيد / ملكت علمش سوى كيوان كشيد
entries.english: The kingdom of beauty led him to prison; the kingdom of knowledge led him to Saturn.

entries.farsi: شه غالم او شد از علم و هنر / ملك علم از ملك حسن استوده‏تر
entries.english: Because of his knowledge and skill the King became his slave: the kingdom of knowledge is more praiseworthy than the kingdom of beauty.

sections.title_fa: رجوع كردن به حكايت آن شخص وام كرده و آمدن او به اميد عنايت آن محتسب سوى تبريز
sections.title_en: Return to the Story of the man who incurred debts and his coming to Tabriz in hope of the favour of the Inspector of Police.

entries.farsi: آن غريب ممتحن از بيم وام / ‏در ره آمد سوى آن دار السلام
entries.english: The poor stranger, afflicted with fear on account of his debts, set out on the way to that Abode of Peace.

entries.farsi: شد سوى تبريز و كوى گلستان / ‏خفته اوميدش فراز گل ستان
entries.english: He went to Tabriz and the rose-garden district: his hope was reclining on roses.

entries.farsi: زد ز دار الملك تبريز سنى / ‏بر اميدش روشنى بر روشنى
entries.english: From the glorious imperial city of Tabriz darted light upon light on his hope.

entries.farsi: جانش خندان شد از آن روضه‏ى رجال / ‏از نسيم يوسف و مصر وصال
entries.english: His spirit was laughing for that orchard of men and the fragrant breeze from Joseph and the Egypt of union.

entries.farsi: گفت يا حادى انخ لى ناقتى / ‏جاء اسعادى و طارت فاقتى
entries.english: He cried, “O cameleer, let my camel kneel for me: my help is come and my need is flown.

entries.farsi: ابركى يا تاقتى طاب الامور / ان تبريزا مناجات الصدور
entries.english: Kneel down, O my camel! All goes well: truly, Tabriz is the place where princes alight.

entries.farsi: اسرحى يا ناقتى حول الرياض / ان تبريزا لنا نعم المفاض
entries.english: Graze, O my camel, round the meadows: truly Tabriz is for us the most excellent source of bountifulness.

entries.farsi: ساربانا بار بگشا ز اشتران / ‏شهر تبريز است و كوى گلستان
entries.english: O camel-driver, unload the camels: it is the city of Tabriz and the district of the rose-garden.

entries.farsi: فر فردوسى است اين پاليز را / شعشعه‏ى عرشى است اين تبريز را
entries.english: This garden has the splendour of Paradise: this Tabriz has the brilliance of Heaven.

entries.farsi: هر زمانى فوح روح انگيز جان / ‏از فراز عرش بر تبريزيان
entries.english: At every moment of time joy-enkindling odours diffused by the Spirit from above the empyrean upon the inhabitants of Tabriz.”

entries.farsi: چون وثاق محتسب جست آن غريب / ‏خلق گفتندش كه بگذشت آن حبيب
entries.english: When the poor stranger sought the Inspector’s house, the people told him that the loved one had passed away.

entries.farsi: او پرير از دار دنيا نقل كرد / مرد و زن از واقعه‏ى او روى زرد
entries.english: “The day before yesterday,” they said, “he removed from this world: man and woman is pale for the calamity that has overtaken him.

entries.farsi: رفت آن طاوس عرشى سوى عرش / ‏چون رسيد از هاتفانش بوى عرش
entries.english: That celestial peacock went to Heaven, when the scent of Heaven reached him from invisible messengers.

entries.farsi: راند او كشتى از اين ساحل پرير / گشته بود آن خواجه زين غم خانه سير
entries.english: Although his shadow was the refuge of people, the Sun rolled it up very quickly.

entries.farsi: سايه‏اش گر چه پناه خلق بود / در نورديد آفتابش زود زود
entries.english: He pushed off his boat from this beach the day before yesterday: the Khwaja had become sated with this house of sorrow.”

entries.farsi: نعره‏اى زد مرد و بى‏هوش اوفتاد / گوييا او نيز در پى جان بداد
entries.english: The man shrieked and fell senseless: you would say that he too had given up the ghost on the heels.

entries.farsi: پس گالب و آب بر رويش زدند / همرهان بر حالتش گريان شدند
entries.english: Then they threw julep and water on his face: his fellow-travellers wept and bewailed his plight.

entries.farsi: تا به شب بى‏خويش بود و بعد از آن / ‏نيم مرده باز گشت از غيب جان
entries.english: He remained unconscious till nightfall, and then his soul returned, half-dead, from the Unseen.

sections.title_fa: با خبر شدن آن غريب از وفات آن محتسب و استغفار او از اعتماد بر مخلوق و تعويل بر عطاى َمخلوق و ياد نعمتهاى حق كردنش و انابت بحق از جرم خود، ثُمَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِرَبِّهِمْ يَعْدِلُون
sections.title_en: How the poor stranger was informed of the Inspector’s death and begged God to pardon him for having relied upon a created being and having rested his hopes upon the bounty of a created being;
and how he remembered the blessings he had received from God, and turned to God and repented of his sin: “then those who disbelieve equal.”

entries.farsi: چون به هوش آمد بگفت اى كردگار / مجرمم بودم به خلق اوميدوار
entries.english: When he came to his senses, he said, “O Maker, I am a sinner: I was setting my hopes on creatures.

entries.farsi: گر چه خواجه بس سخاوت كرده بود / هيچ آن كفو عطاى تو نبود
entries.english: Though the Khwaja had shown great generosity that was never a match for Your bounty.

entries.farsi: او كله بخشيد و تو سر پر خرد / او قبا بخشيد و تو بالا و قد
entries.english: He gave the cap, but You the head filled with intelligence; he gave the coat, but You the tall figure and stature.

entries.farsi: او زرم داد و تو دست زر شمار / او ستورم داد و تو عقل سوار
entries.english: He gave me gold, but You the hand that counts gold; he gave me the beast for riding, but You the mind that rides it.

entries.farsi: خواجه شمعم داد و تو چشم قرير / خواجه نقلم داد و تو طعمه پذير
entries.english: The Khwaja gave me the candle, but You the cool eye; the Khwaja gave me the dessert, but You the food-receiving.

entries.farsi: او وظيفه داد و تو عمر و حيات / ‏وعده‏اش زر وعده‏ى تو طيبات
entries.english: He gave me the stipend, but You life and animate existence; his promise was gold, but Your promise the pure things.

entries.farsi: او وثاقم داد و تو چرخ و زمين / ‏در وثاقت او و صد چون او سمين
entries.english: He gave me a house, but You the sky and the earth: in Your house he and a hundred like him fat.

entries.farsi: زر از آن تست زر او نافريد / نان از آن تست نانش از تو رسيد
entries.english: Gold is Yours: he did not create gold. Bread is Yours: bread came to him from You.

entries.farsi: آن سخا و رحم هم تو دادى‏اش / ‏كز سخاوت مى‏فزودى شادى‏اش
entries.english: You also gave him generosity and pity, and his joy was increased by that generosity.

entries.farsi: ما كجا بوديم كآن ديان دين / ‏عقل مى‏كاريد اندر آب و طين
entries.english: I made him my qibla: I let the original qibla-Maker fall.”

entries.farsi: من مر او را قبله‏ى خود ساختم / ‏قبله ساز اصل را انداختم
entries.english: Where were we when the Judge of Judgement was sowing reason in the water and clay?—

entries.farsi: چون همى‏كرد از عدم گردون پديد / وين بساط خاك را مى‏گستريد
entries.english: Since He was producing the sky from non-existence and spreading this carpet of earth,

entries.farsi: اى بسا بنيادها پنهان و فاش / ‏مضمر اين سقف كرد و اين فراش
entries.english: And making lamps of the stars and of the natural properties a lock together with the keys.

entries.farsi: ز اختران مى‏ساخت او مصباحها / و ز طبايع قفل با مفتاحها
entries.english: Oh, how many structures hidden and plain to see has He enclosed in this roof and this carpet!

entries.farsi: آدم اسطرالب اوصاف علوست / ‏وصف آدم مظهر آيات اوست
entries.english: Adam is the astrolabe of the attributes of Sublimity: the nature of Adam is the theatre for His revelations.

entries.farsi: هر چه در وى مى‏نمايد عكس اوست / ‏همچو عكس ماه اندر آب جوست
entries.english: Whatever appears in him is the reflection of Him, just as the moon is reflected in the water of the river.

entries.farsi: بر سطرالبش نقوش عنكبوت / ‏بهر اوصاف ازل دارد ثبوت
entries.english: The figures on the “spider” on his astrolabe are there for the sake of the Eternal Attributes,

entries.farsi: تا ز چرخ غيب و ز خورشيد روح / ‏عنكبوتش درس گويد از شروح
entries.english: In order that its “spider” may give lessons in exposition of the sky of the Unseen and the sun of the Spirit.

entries.farsi: عنكبوت و اين سطرالب رشاد / بى‏منجم در كف عام اوفتاد
entries.english: Without an astronomer, the “spider” and this astrolabe which guides aright fall into the hands of the vulgar.

entries.farsi: انبيا را داد حق تنجيم اين / ‏غيب را چشمى ببايد غيب بين
entries.english: God bestowed this astronomy on the prophets: for the Unseen an eye that observes the Unseen is necessary.

entries.farsi: از برون دان آن چه در چاهت نمود / ور نه آن شيرى كه در چه شد فرود
entries.english: These generations fell into the well of the present world: everyone saw in the well his own reflection.

entries.farsi: در چه دنيا فتادند اين قرون / ‏عكس خود را ديد هر يك چه درون
entries.english: Know that what appears to you in the well is from outside; else you are the lion that plunged into the well.

entries.farsi: برد خرگوشيش از ره كاى فالن / ‏در تگ چاه است آن شير ژيان
entries.english: A hare led him astray, saying, “O such-and-such, this furious lion is at the bottom of the well.

entries.farsi: در رو اندر چاه كين از وى بكش / ‏چون از او غالبترى سر بركنش
entries.english: Go into the well and wreak vengeance on him: tear off his head, since you art mightier than he.”

entries.farsi: آن مقلد سخره‏ى خرگوش شد / از خيال خويشتن پر جوش شد
entries.english: That follower of authority was subjugated by the hare: he was filled with boiling by his own fancy.

entries.farsi: او نگفت اين نقش داد آب نيست / ‏اين بجز تقليب آن قالب نيست
entries.english: He did not say, “This image is not produced by the water: this is not by anything except the changing activity of the Changer.”

entries.farsi: تو هم از دشمن چو كينى مى‏كشى / ‏اى زبون شش غلط در هر ششى
entries.english: So when you, O slave to the six, wreak vengeance on your enemy you are in error concerning all the six.

entries.farsi: آن عداوت اندر او عكس حق است / ‏كز صفات قهر آن جا مشتق است
entries.english: That enmity in him is reflected from God, for it is derived from the attributes of Wrath there;

entries.farsi: و آن گنه در وى ز جنس جرم تست / ‏بايد آن خو را ز طبع خويش شست
entries.english: And that sin in him is homogeneous with your sin: you must wash that disposition out of your own nature.

entries.farsi: خلق زشتت اندر او رويت نمود / كه ترا او صفحه‏ى آيينه بود
entries.english: Your evil character showed itself to you in him because he was the surface of a mirror to you.

entries.farsi: مى‏زند بر آب استاره‏ى سنى / ‏خاك تو بر عكس اختر مى‏زنى
entries.english: When you have seen your ugliness in the mirror, O Hasan, do not strike at the mirror!

entries.farsi: چون كه قبح خويش ديدى اى حسن / ‏اندر آيينه بر آيينه مزن
entries.english: A lofty star is reflected in the water, and you cast earth on the star’s reflection,

entries.farsi: كاين ستاره‏ى نحس در آب آمده‏ست / ‏تا كند او سعد ما را زير دست
entries.english: Saying, “This unlucky star has come into the water to overthrow my good luck.”

entries.farsi: خاك استيال بريزى بر سرش / ‏چون كه پندارى ز شبهه اخترش
entries.english: You pour upon it the earth of subjection, because on account of the resemblance you deem it to be the star.

entries.farsi: عكس پنهان گشت و اندر غيب راند / تو گمان بردى كه آن اختر نماند
entries.english: The reflection becomes hidden and disappears, you think that the star is no more;

entries.farsi: آن ستاره‏ى نحس هست اندر سما / هم بدان سو بايدش كردن دوا
entries.english: The unlucky star is in the sky: it is in that quarter it must be cured;

entries.farsi: بلكه بايد دل سوى بى‏سوى بست / ‏نحس اين سو عكس نحس بى‏سو است
entries.english: Nay, you must fix your heart on that which is illimitable; the ill-luck in this quarter is the reflection of the ill-luck in the realm where no quarters exist.

entries.farsi: داد داد حق شناس و بخششش / ‏عكس آن داد است اندر پنج و شش
entries.english: Know that gifts are the gift of God and are His bounty: it is the reflection of those gifts that appears in the five and the six.

entries.farsi: گر بود داد خسان افزون ز ريگ / ‏تو بميرى و آن بماند مرده ريگ
entries.english: Though the gifts of the base be more than the sands, you will die and they will be left behind as an inheritance.

entries.farsi: عكس آخر چند پايد در نظر / اصل بينى پيشه كن اى كژ نگر
entries.english: After all, how long does a reflection remain in view? Make a practice of contemplating the origin, O you who look awry.

entries.farsi: حق چو بخشش كرد بر اهل نياز / با عطا بخشيدشان عمر دراز
entries.english: When God bestows bounty on those who supplicate Him in their need, together with His gift He bestows on them a long life.

entries.farsi: خالدين شد نعمت و منعم عليه / ‏محيى الموتاست فاجتازوا إليه
entries.english: The benefit and the beneficiary are made enduring forever. It is He that brings the dead to life, so repair unto Him.

entries.farsi: داد حق با تو در آميزد چو جان / ‏آن چنان كه آن تو باشى و تو آن
entries.english: The gifts of God are mingled with you like the spirit, in such wise that you are they and they are you.

entries.farsi: گر نماند اشتهاى نان و آب / ‏بدهدت بى‏اين دو قوت مستطاب
entries.english: If you have no more appetite for bread and water, He will give you goodly nourishment without these two.

entries.farsi: فربهى گر رفت حق در الغرى / ‏فربهى پنهانت بخشد آن سرى
entries.english: If your fatness is gone, God will give you from yonder fatness concealed in leanness.

entries.farsi: جان چه باشد كه تو سازى زو سند / حق به عشق خويش زنده‏ت مى‏كند
entries.english: Since He gives the peri nourishment from scent and gives every angel nourishment from spirit,

entries.farsi: زو حيات عشق خواه و جان مخواه / ‏تو از او آن رزق خواه و نان مخواه
entries.english: What is the soul that you should make a support of it? God will make you living by His love.

entries.farsi: چون پرى را قوت از بو مى‏دهد / هر ملك را قوت جان او مى‏دهد
entries.english: Ask of Him the life of love and do not ask for the soul: ask of Him that provision and do not ask for bread.

entries.farsi: خلق را چون آب دان صاف و زالل / اندر آن تابان صفات ذو الجلال
entries.english: Know that created beings is like pure and limpid water in which the attributes of the Almighty are shining.

entries.farsi: علمشان و عدلشان و لطفشان / ‏چون ستاره‏ى چرخ در آب روان
entries.english: Their knowledge and their justice and their clemency are like a star of heaven in running water.

entries.farsi: پادشاهان مظهر شاهى حق / ‏فاضالن مرآت آگاهى حق
entries.english: Kings are the theatre for the manifestation of God’s kingship; the learned are the mirrors for God’s wisdom.

entries.farsi: قرنها بگذشت و اين قرن نوى است / ‏ماه آن ماه است آب آن آب نيست
entries.english: Generations have passed away, and this is a new generation: the moon is the same moon, the water is not the same water.

entries.farsi: عدل آن عدل است و فضل آن فضل هم / ‏ليك مستبدل شد آن قرن و امم
entries.english: The justice is the same justice, and the learning is the same learning too; but those generations and peoples have been changed.

entries.farsi: قرنها بر قرنها رفت اى همام / ‏وين معانى برقرار و بر دوام
entries.english: Generations on generations have gone, O sire, but these Ideas are permanent and everlasting.

entries.farsi: آب مبدل شد در اين جو چند بار / عكس ماه و عكس اختر برقرار
entries.english: The water in this channel has been changed many times: the reflection of the moon and of the stars remains unaltered.

entries.farsi: اين صفتها چون نجوم معنوى است / ‏دان كه بر چرخ معانى مستوى است
entries.english: Therefore its foundation is not in the running water; nay, but in the regions of the breadth of Heaven.

entries.farsi: پس بنايش نيست بر آب روان / ‏بلكه بر اقطار عرض آسمان
entries.english: These attributes are like Ideal stars: know that they are established in the sphere of the Ideas.

entries.farsi: خوب رويان آينه‏ى خوبى او / عشق ايشان عكس مطلوبى او
entries.english: The beauteous are the mirror of His beauty: love for them is the reflection of the desire of which He is the object.

entries.farsi: جمله تصويرات عكس آب جوست / ‏چون بمالى چشم خود خود جمله اوست
entries.english: This cheek and mole goes to the Source thereof: how should a phantom continue in the water forever?

entries.farsi: هم به اصل خود رود اين خد و خال / ‏دايما در آب كى ماند خيال
entries.english: The whole sum of pictured forms is a reflection in the water of the river: when you rub your eye, all of them are really He.

entries.farsi: باز عقلش گفت بگذار اين حول / ‏خل دوشاب است و دوشاب است خل
entries.english: Again, his reason said, “Abandon this seeing double: vinegar is grape-syrup and grape-syrup is vinegar.

entries.farsi: خواجه را چون غير گفتى از قصور / شرم دار اى احول از شاه غيور
entries.english: Since, from defect, you have called the Khwaja ‘other’, be ashamed before the jealous King, O man of double sight.

entries.farsi: خواجه را كه در گذشته‏ست از اثير / جنس اين موشان تاريكى مگير
entries.english: Do not suppose the Khwaja, who has passed beyond the ether, to be homogeneous with these mice of darkness.

entries.farsi: خواجه را جان بين مبين جسم گران / ‏مغز بين او را مبينش استخوان
entries.english: Regard the Khwaja as spirit; do not regard him as gross body: regard him as marrow, do not regard him as bone.

entries.farsi: خواجه را از چشم ابليس لعين / ‏منگر و نسبت مكن او را به طين
entries.english: Do not look at the Khwaja with the eye of Iblís the accursed, and do not relate him to clay.

entries.farsi: همره خورشيد را شب‏پر مخوان / ‏آن كه او مسجود شد ساجد مدان
entries.english: Do not call the fellow-traveller of the Sun ‘a bat’: do not call him who was worshipped a worshipper.

entries.farsi: عكسها را ماند اين و عكس نيست / ‏در مثال عكس حق بنمودنى است
entries.english: This resembles the reflections; but it is not a reflection, it is the appearance of God in the likeness of a reflection.

entries.farsi: آفتابى ديد او جامد نماند / روغن گل روغن كنجد نماند
entries.english: He beheld a Sun and remained frozen no more: the oil of roses was no longer oil of sesame.

entries.farsi: چون مبدل گشته‏اند ابدال حق / ‏نيستند از خلق بر گردان ورق
entries.english: Since the Abdal of God have been transmuted, they are not among created beings: turn over a leaf!

entries.farsi: قبله‏ى وحدانيت دو چون بود / خاك مسجود ماليك چون شود
entries.english: How should the qibla, namely, the Unity, be two? How should earth be worshipped by the angels?

entries.farsi: آن چه در جو ديد كى باشد خيال / ‏چون كه شد از ديدنش پر صد جوال
entries.english: When a man sees the reflection of apples in this river, and the sight of them fills his skirt with apples,

entries.farsi: چون در اين جو ديد عكس سيب مرد / دامنش را ديد آن پر سيب كرد
entries.english: How should that which he saw in the river be a phantom, when a hundred sacks have been filled by his vision?

entries.farsi: تن مبين و آن مكن كآن بكم و صم / ‏كَذَّبُوا بِالْحَقِّ لَمَّا جاءهم
entries.english: Do not regard the body, and do not act like those dumb and deaf men disbelieved in the Truth when it came to them.

entries.farsi: ما رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رَمَيْتَ احمد بده‏ست / ‏ديدن او ديدن خالق شده‏ست
entries.english: The Khwaja is You did not throw when you threw: to see him is to see the Creator.

entries.farsi: خدمت او خدمت حق كردن است / ‏روز ديدن ديدن اين روزن است
entries.english: To serve him is to serve God: to see this window is to see the Daylight;

entries.farsi: خاصه اين روزن درخشان از خود است / ‏نى وديعه‏ى آفتاب و فرقد است
entries.english: Especially this window is resplendent of itself: nothing is deposited by the sun and the Farqad.

entries.farsi: هم از آن خورشيد زد بر روزنى / ‏ليك از راه و سوى معهود نى
entries.english: From that Sun, too, strike upon a window, but not in the ordinary way and direction.

entries.farsi: در ميان شمس و اين روزن رهى / ‏هست روزنها نشد زو آگهى
entries.english: Between the Sun and this window there is a way; the windows are not acquainted with it,

entries.farsi: تا اگر ابرى بر آيد چرخ پوش / ‏اندر اين روزن بود نورش به جوش
entries.english: So that, if a cloud arise and cover the sky, in this window its light will be coruscating.

entries.farsi: غير راه اين هوا و شش جهت / ‏در ميان روزن و خور مألفت
entries.english: There is familiarity between the window and the Sun, otherwise than the way of this atmosphere and the six directions.

entries.farsi: مدحت و تسبيح او تسبيح حق / ‏ميوه مى‏رويد ز عين اين طبق
entries.english: To praise and glorify the Perfect Man is to glorify God: the fruit is growing out of the essential nature of this tray.

entries.farsi: سيب رويد زين سبد خوش لخت لخت / ‏عيب نبود گر نهى نامش درخت
entries.english: Apples grow from this basket in fine variety: it is no harm if you bestow on it the name ‘tree.’

entries.farsi: آن چه رويد از درخت بارور / زين سبد رويد همان نوع از ثمر
entries.english: Call this basket ‘the Apple-tree,’ for between the two there is a hidden way.

entries.farsi: اين سبد را تو درخت سيب خوان / ‏كه ميان هر دو راه آمد نهان
entries.english: That which grows from the fruit-bearing Tree— the same kind of fruit grows from this basket.

entries.farsi: پس سبد را تو درخت بخت بين / ‏زير سايه‏ى اين سبد خوش مى‏نشين
entries.english: Therefore regard the basket as the Tree of Fortune and sit happily under the shade of this basket.

entries.farsi: نان چو اطالق آورد اى مهربان / ‏نان چرا مى‏گويى‏اش محموده خوان
entries.english: When bread produces looseness, why call it bread, O kindly man? Call it a purgative.

entries.farsi: خاك ره چون چشم روشن كرد و جان / ‏خاك او را سرمه بين و سرمه دان
entries.english: When the dust on the road illumines the eye and the spirit, regard its dust as eye salve and know that it is eye salve.

entries.farsi: چون ز روى اين زمين تابد شروق / ‏من چرا بالا كنم رو در عيوق
entries.english: When the sunrise shines forth from the face of this earth, why should I lift up my face to Ayyuq?

entries.farsi: شد فنا هستش مخوان اى چشم شوخ / ‏در چنين جو خشك كى ماند كلوخ
entries.english: He is non-existent: do not call him existent, O bold-eyed man! How should the sod remain dry in a River like this?

entries.farsi: پيش اين خورشيد كى تابد هالل / ‏با چنان رستم چه باشد زور زال
entries.english: How should the new-moon shine in the presence of this Sun? What is the strength of a decrepit old woman against such a Rustam?

entries.farsi: دو مگو و دو مدان و دو مخوان / ‏بنده را در خواجه‏ى خود محو دان
entries.english: The Agent is seeking and prevailing , to the end that He may utterly destroy existences.

entries.farsi: طالب است و غالب است آن كردگار / تا ز هستيها بر آرد او دمار
entries.english: Do not say ‘two,’ do not know ‘two,’ and do not call ‘two’: deem the slave to be effaced in his master.

entries.farsi: خواجه هم در نور خواجه آفرين / ‏فانى است و مرده و مات و دفين
entries.english: The Khwaja likewise is negated and dead and checkmated and buried in the Khwaja’s Creator.

entries.farsi: چون جدا بينى ز حق اين خواجه را / گم كنى هم متن و هم ديباجه را
entries.english: When you regard this Khwaja as separate from God, you lose both the text and the preface.

entries.farsi: چشم و دل را هين گذاره كن ز طين / ‏اين يكى قبله‏ست دو قبله مبين
entries.english: Listen; let your eye and your heart pass beyond the clay! This is One Qibla: do not see two qiblas.

entries.farsi: چون دو ديدى ماندى از هر دو طرف / ‏آتشى در خف فتاد و رفت خف
entries.english: When you see two you remain deprived of both sides: a flame falls on the touchwood, and the touchwood is gone.”
