sections.title_fa: مثل دو بين همچو آن غريب شهر كاش عمر نام كه از يك دكانش به سبب اين به آن دكان ديگر حواله كرد، و او فهم نكرد كه همه دكان يكى است در اين معنى كه به عمر نان نفروشند هم اينجا تدارك كنم من غلط كردم نامم عمر نيست چون بدين دكان توبه و تدارك كنم نان يابم از همه دكانهاى اين شهر، و اگر بى‏تدارك همچنين عمر نام باشم از اين دكان در گذرم محرومم و احولم و اين دكانها
‏را از هم جدا دانسته‏ام
sections.title_en: Parable of the man who sees double. like the stranger in the town of Kash, whose name was ‘Umar. Because of this they passed him on from one shop to another. He did not perceive that all the shops were one in this respect that they would not sell bread to Umar;, “Here I will repair my error,

‘I made a mistake: my name is not Umar.’ When I recant and repair my error in this shop, I shall get bread from all the shops in the town; but if, without repairing my error, I still keep the name ‘Umar and depart from this shop, I am deprived and seeing double, for I have deemed these shops to be separate from each other.”

entries.farsi: گر عمر نامى تو اندر شهر كاش / ‏كس بنفروشد به صد دانگت لواش
entries.english: If your name is ‘Umar, nobody in the town of Kash will sell you a roll of bread for a hundred dángs.

entries.farsi: چون به يك دكان بگفتى عمرم / ‏اين عمر را نان فروشيد از كرم
entries.english: When you say at one shop, “I am ‘Umar: kindly sell bread to this ‘Umar,”

entries.farsi: او بگويد رو بدان ديگر دكان / ‏ز آن يكى نان به كزين پنجاه نان
entries.english: He will say, “Go to that other shop: one loaf from that is better than fifty from this.”

entries.farsi: پس زدى اشراق آن نااحولى / ‏بر دل كاشى شدى عمر على
entries.english: If he had not been seeing double, he would have replied, “There is no other shop”;

entries.farsi: گر نبودى احول او اندر نظر / او بگفتى نيست دكانى دگر
entries.english: And then the illumination produced by not seeing double would have shot upon the heart of him of Kash, and ‘Umar would have become ‘Alí.

entries.farsi: اين ازينجا گويد آن خباز را / اين عمر را نان فروش اى نانبا
entries.english: This says, from this place to that baker, “O baker, sell bread to this ‘Umar”;

entries.farsi: چون شنيد او هم عمر نان در كشيد / پس فرستادت به دكان بعيد
entries.english: And he too, on hearing ‘Umar, withholds bread and sends to a shop some way off,

entries.farsi: كاين عمر را نان ده اى انباز من / راز يعنى فهم كن ز آواز من
entries.english: Saying, “Give bread to this ‘Umar, O my partner,” that is, “apprehend the secret from my voice.”

entries.farsi: او همت ز آن سو حواله مى‏كند / هين عمر آمد كه تا بر نان زند
entries.english: He also will pass you on from there, “Listen, ‘Umar is come to get some bread.”

entries.farsi: چون به يك دكان عمر بودى برو / در همه كاشان ز نان محروم شو
entries.english: When you have been ‘Umar in one shop, go and do not expect to obtain bread in all Kashan.

entries.farsi: ور به يك دكان على گفتى بگير / نان از اينجا بى‏حواله و بى‏ز حير
entries.english: But if you have said in one shop, “Alí,” obtain bread from this place without being passed on and without trouble.

entries.farsi: حول دو بين چو بى‏بر شد ز نوش / ‏احول ده بينى اى مادر فروش
entries.english: Since the squinter who sees two is deprived of the enjoyment of delicious food, you are seeing ten, O you who would sell your mother!

entries.farsi: اندر اين كاشان خاك از احولى / ‏چون عمر مى‏گرد چون نبوى على
entries.english: Because of seeing double, wander like Umar in this Kashan of earth, since you are not Alí.

entries.farsi: هست احول را در اين ويرانه دير / گوشه گوشه نقل نو اى ثم خير
entries.english: In this ruined monastery the man who sees double is removing from one nook to another, O, “The good is there.”

entries.farsi: ور دو چشم حق شناس آمد ترا / دوست پر بين عرصه‏ى هر دو سرا
entries.english: But if you get two eyes that can recognise God, see the expanse of both worlds full of the Beloved,

entries.farsi: اندر اين جو غنچه ديدى يا شجر / همچو هر جو تو خيالش ظن مبر
entries.english: You escape from being transferred from place to place in this Kashan filled with fear and hope.

entries.farsi: وارهيدى از حواله‏ى جا به جا / اندر اين كاشان پر خوف و رجا
entries.english: If you have seen buds or trees in this River, do not suppose that they are a phantom like any river;

entries.farsi: كه ترا از عين اين عكس نقوش / ‏حق حقيقت گردد و ميوه فروش
entries.english: For by means of the very reflection of these images God is made real to you and sells the fruit.

entries.farsi: پس به معنى باغ باشد اين نه آب / ‏پس مشو عريان چو بلقيس از حباب
entries.english: By means of this Water the eye is freed from seeing double: it sees the reflection, and the basket is filled.

entries.farsi: چشم از اين آب از حول حر مى‏شود / عكس مى‏بيند سبد پر مى‏شود
entries.english: Therefore this is really an orchard, not water: do not, then, like Bilqís, strip yourself from the waves.

entries.farsi: بار گوناگونست بر پشت خران / ‏هين به يك چوب اين خران را تو مران
entries.english: Diverse loads are upon the backs of asses: do not drive these asses with one stick.

entries.farsi: بر يكى خر بار لعل و گوهر است / بر يكى خر بار سنگ و مرمر است
entries.english: One ass is laden with rubies and pearls, another with stones and marble.

entries.farsi: بر همه جوها تو اين حكمت مران / ‏اندر اين جو ماه بين عكسش مخوان
entries.english: Do not apply this principle to all rivers; in this River behold the Moon, and do not call it a reflection.

entries.farsi: آب خضر است اين نه آب دام و دد / هر چه اندر وى نمايد حق بود
entries.english: This is the Water of Khizr, not the water drunk by herbivorous animals and beasts of prey: everything that appears in it is Real.

entries.farsi: زين تگ جو ماه گويد من مهم / ‏من نه عكسم هم حديث و همرهم
entries.english: From the bottom of this River the Moon cries, “I am the Moon, I am not a reflection: I am conversing and travelling with.

entries.farsi: اندر اين جو آن چه بر بالاست هست / ‏خواه بالا خواه در وى دار دست
entries.english: That which is above is in this River: take possession of it either above or in that as you please.

entries.farsi: از دگر جوها مگير اين جوى را / ماه دان اين پرتو مه روى را
entries.english: Do not assume this River to be of other rivers: know that this ray of the moon-faced is the Moon.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد آن غريب / ‏بس گريست از درد خواجه شد كئيب
entries.english: This topic is endless. The poor stranger wept exceedingly: he was heart-broken by grief for the Khwaja.

sections.title_fa: توزيع كردن پاى مرد در جمله‏ى شهر تبريز و جمع شدن اندك چيز و رفتن آن غريب به تربت ‏محتسب به زيارت و اين قصه را بر سر گور او گفتن به طريق توجه الى آخره
sections.title_en: How the bailiff sought subscriptions in all parts of the city of Tabriz, and how a small amount
was collected, and how the poor stranger went to visit the Inspector’s tomb and related this tale on his grave by the method of concentrating the mind on prayer, etc.

entries.farsi: واقعه‏ى آن وام او مشهور شد / پاى مرد از درد او رنجور شد
entries.english: The calamity of his debts became notorious, and the bailiff was distressed by his grief.

entries.farsi: از پى توزيع گرد شهر گشت / ‏از طمع مى‏گفت هر جا سر گذشت
entries.english: He went round the city to collect subscriptions and everywhere, in hope, he told all that had happened;

entries.farsi: هيچ نآورد از ره كديه‏به دست / ‏غير صد دينار آن كديه پرست
entries.english: That devoted beggar obtained by means of begging no more than a hundred dinars.

entries.farsi: پاى مرد آمد به دو دستش گرفت / ‏شد به گور آن كريم بس شگفت
entries.english: The bailiff came to him and took his hand and went to the grave of that very wonderful generous man.

entries.farsi: گفت چون توفيق يابد بنده‏اى / ‏كه كند مهمانى فرخنده‏اى
entries.english: He said, “When a servant gains the Divine favour so that he entertains a fortunate man,

entries.farsi: شكر او شكر خدا باشد يقين / ‏چون به احسان كرد توفيقش قرين
entries.english: And gives up his own wealth for his sake and sacrifices his own dignity for the sake of dignity on him,

entries.farsi: مال خود ايثار راه او كند / جاه خود ايثار جاه او كند
entries.english: Gratitude to him is certainly gratitude to God, since the Divine favour caused him to show beneficence.

entries.farsi: ترك شكرش ترك شكر حق بود / حق او ال شك به حق ملحق بود
entries.english: To be ungrateful to him is to be ungrateful to God: beyond doubt his right is consequent on God.

entries.farsi: شكر مى‏كن مر خدا را در نعم / ‏نيز مى‏كن شكر و ذكر خواجه هم
entries.english: Always give thanks to God for His bounties, and always give thanks and praise to the Khwaja too.

entries.farsi: رحمت مادر اگر چه از خداست / ‏خدمت او هم فريضه ست و سزاست
entries.english: Though a mother’s tenderness is from God, it is a sacred duty and a worthy task to serve her.

entries.farsi: در قيامت بنده را گويد خدا / هين چه كردى آن چه من دادم ترا
entries.english: For this reason God has said, ‘bless him (the Prophet),’ for Mohammed was one to whom were transferred.

entries.farsi: زين سبب فرمود حق صَ لُّوا عليه / ‏كه محمد بود محتال إليه
entries.english: At the Resurrection God will say to His servant, ‘Listen, what have you done with that which I bestowed on you?’

entries.farsi: گويد اى رب شكر تو كردم به جان / ‏چون ز تو بود اصل آن روزى و نان
entries.english: He will reply, ‘O Lord, I gave thanks to You with my soul, since the source of my daily provision and bread was in You.’

entries.farsi: گويدش حق نه نكردى شكر من / ‏چون نكردى شكر آن اكرام فن
entries.english: God will say to him, ‘Nay, you did not give thanks to Me, inasmuch as you did not give thanks to him who made a practice of generosity.

entries.farsi: بر كريمى كرده‏اى ظلم و ستم / ‏نه ز دست او رسيدت نعمتم
entries.english: You have done wrong and injustice to a generous man: did not My bounty come to you by his hand?’”

entries.farsi: چون به گور آن ولى نعمت رسيد / گشت گريان زار و آمد در نشيد
entries.english: When he arrived at the tomb of his benefactor, he began to weep bitterly and broke into loud lamentation.

entries.farsi: گفت اى پشت و پناه هر نبيل / ‏مرتجى و غوث ابناء السبيل
entries.english: He said, “O you who were the support and refuge of every noble man and the hope and helper of wayfarers,

entries.farsi: اى غم ارزاق ما بر خاطرت / ‏اى چو رزق عام احسان و برت
entries.english: O you on whose heart the care for our means of livelihood, O you whose beneficence and charity were the universal provision of sustenance,

entries.farsi: اى فقيران را عشيره و والدين / ‏در خراج و خرج و در ايفاء دين
entries.english: O you who were kinsfolk and parents to the poor in their taxes and expenses and in discharging their debts,

entries.farsi: اى چو بحر از بهر نزديكان گهر / داده و تحفه سوى دوران مطر
entries.english: O you who like the sea, gave pearls for those near and rain as a gift to those afar,

entries.farsi: پشت ما گرم از تو بود اى آفتاب / ‏رونق هر قصر و گنج هر خراب
entries.english: Our backs were warmed by you, O sun the splendour in every palace and the treasure in every ruin.

entries.farsi: اى در ابرويت نديده‏كس گره / ‏اى چو ميكائيل راد و رزق ده
entries.english: O you in whose eyebrow none ever saw knots, O you generous and bountiful as Michael,

entries.farsi: اى دلت پيوسته با درياى غيب / ‏اى به قاف مكرمت عنقاى غيب
entries.english: O you whose heart was connected with the Sea of the Unseen, O you, who were the invisible Anqa on the Qáf of munificence,

entries.farsi: ياد نآورده كه از مالم چه رفت / ‏سقف سمت همتت هرگز نكفت
entries.english: Who never took thought how much of your wealth had gone, and the roof of the azimuth of your magnanimity was never cloven,

entries.farsi: اى من و صد همچو من در ماه و سال / ‏مر ترا چون نسل تو گشته عيال
entries.english: O you to whom in month and year I and a hundred like me had become a family like your own children,

entries.farsi: نقد ما و جنس ما و رخت ما / نام ما و فخر ما و بخت ما
entries.english: You were our ready money and our movables and our furniture, our fame and our glory and our fortune.

entries.farsi: تو نمردى ناز و بخت ما بمرد / عيش ما و رزق مستوفى بمرد
entries.english: You are not dead; our luxury and fortune are dead, our happy life is dead and the sustenance that was provided in full measure.

entries.farsi: واحد كالالف در رزم و كرم / ‏صد چو حاتم گاه ايثار نعم
entries.english: A single person like a thousand in warfare and in generosity; as a hundred Hatims in the hour of lavishing bounties unselfishly.

entries.farsi: حاتم ار مرده بمرده مى‏دهد / گر دكانهاى شمرده مى‏دهد
entries.english: If Hatím bestows dead on the dead, he bestows a certain number of walnuts.

entries.farsi: تو حياتى مى‏دهى در هر نفس / ‏كز نفيسى مى‏نگنجد در نفس
entries.english: You at every moment art bestowing a life that, because of its preciousness, cannot be contained in breath.

entries.farsi: تو حياتى مى‏دهى بس پايدار / نقد زر بى‏كساد و بى‏شمار
entries.english: You are bestowing a life exceedingly enduring, real gold coin exempt from depreciation and beyond count.

entries.farsi: وارثى نابوده يك خوى ترا / اى فلك سجده كنان كوى ترا
entries.english: There exists no heir to one disposition of yours, O you to whose abode Heaven is bowing in worship.

entries.farsi: خلق را از گرگ غم لطفت شبان / ‏چون كليم اللهَّ شبان مهربان
entries.english: Your grace is the shepherd of all who have been created, from the wolf of pain—a loving shepherd like God’s Kalím.”

entries.farsi: در پى او تا به شب در جستجو / و آن رمه غايب شده از چشم او
entries.english: One sheep fled from God’s Kalím: the feet of Moses were blistered and his shoes dropped off.

entries.farsi: گوسفندى از كليم اللهَّ گريخت / ‏پاى موسى آبله شد نعل ريخت
entries.english: He continued searching after it till nightfall, and the flock had vanished from his sight.

entries.farsi: كف همى‏ماليد بر پشت و سرش / ‏مى‏نواخت از مهر همچون مادرش
entries.english: The sheep was enfeebled and exhausted by fatigue: then God’s Kalím shook the dust off it,

entries.farsi: گوسفند از ماندگى شد سست و ماند / پس كليم اهلل گرد از وى فشاند
entries.english: And stroked its back and head with his hand, fondling it lovingly like a mother.

entries.farsi: نيم ذره طيرگى و خشم نى / غير مهر و رحم و آب چشم نى
entries.english: Not half a mite of irritation and anger, nothing but love and pity and tears!

entries.farsi: گفت گيرم بر منت رحمى نبود / طبع تو بر خود چرا استم نمود
entries.english: He said, “I grant you had no pity on me, why did your nature show cruelty to itself?”

entries.farsi: با ماليك گفت يزدان آن زمان / ‏كه نبوت را همى‏زيبد فالن
entries.english: At that moment God said to the angels, “So-and-so is suitable for prophethood.”

entries.farsi: مصطفى فرمود خود كه هر نبى / ‏كرد چوپانيش برنا يا صبى
entries.english: Mustafá himself has said that every prophet herded sheep as a young man or boy,

entries.farsi: بى‏شبانى كردن و آن امتحان / ‏حق ندادش پيشوايى جهان
entries.english: Without his having been a shepherd and that trial, God did not bestow on him the leadership of the world.

entries.farsi: گفت سائل هم تو نيز اى پهلوان / ‏گفت من هم بوده‏ام دهرى شبان
entries.english: A questioner said, “Even you, O man of might?” “I too,” he replied, “was a shepherd for a long while.”

entries.farsi: تا شود پيدا وقار و صبرشان / ‏كردشان پيش از نبوت حق شبان
entries.english: In order that their calmness and fortitude should be displayed, God made them shepherds before prophethood.

entries.farsi: هر اميرى كاو شبانى بشر / آن چنان آرد كه باشد موتمر
entries.english: Every prince who performs the task of shepherding mankind in such wise that he obeys the Commandments,

entries.farsi: حلم موسى‏وار اندر رعى خود / او بجاى آرد به تدبير و خرد
entries.english: In tending them with foresight and understanding shows forbearance like that of Moses,

entries.farsi: الجرم حقش دهد چوپانيى / ‏بر فراز چرخ مه روحانيى
entries.english: Inevitably God will bestow on him a spiritual shepherd’s office above the sphere of the moon,

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه انبيا را زين رعا / بر كشيد و داد رعى اصفيا
entries.english: Even as He raised the prophets from this herding and gave them the task of tending the righteous.

entries.farsi: خواجه بارى تو در اين چوپانى‏ات / ‏كردى آن چه كور گردد شانى‏ات
entries.english: “You, in short, O Khwaja, have performed in your shepherding that which causes him that hates you to become blind.

entries.farsi: دانم آن جا در مكافات ايزدت / ‏سرورى جاودانه بخشدت
entries.english: I know that God will give you yonder an everlasting sovereignty in compensation.

entries.farsi: بر اميد كف چون درياى تو / بر وظيفه دادن و ايفاى تو
entries.english: In hope of your hand as the ocean and upon your giving a stipend and discharging (my obligations) in full,

entries.farsi: وام كردم نه هزار از زر گزاف / ‏تو كجايى تا شود اين درد صاف
entries.english: I recklessly incurred debts nine thousand pieces of gold: where are you, that these dregs may become clear?

entries.farsi: تو كجايى تا كه خندان چون چمن / ‏گويى‏ام بستان و ده چندان ز من
entries.english: Where are you, that laughing like the garden you may say, ‘Receive that and ten times as much from me’?

entries.farsi: تو كجايى تا مرا خندان كنى / ‏لطف و احسان چون خداوندان كنى
entries.english: Where are you that you may make me laughing and show favour and beneficence as lords?

entries.farsi: تو كجايى تا برى در مخزنم / ‏تا كنى از وام و فاقه ايمنم
entries.english: Where are you, that you may take me into your treasury and make me secure from debt and poverty?—

entries.farsi: من همى‏گويم بس و تو مفضلم / ‏گفته كاين هم گير از بهر دلم
entries.english: I am saying continually, ‘Enough!’ and you, my bounteous friend, replying, ‘Accept this too for my heart’s sake.’

entries.farsi: حاش هلل تو برونى زين جهان / ‏هم به وقت زندگى هم اين زمان
entries.english: How can a world be contained under the clay? How should a Heaven be contained in the earth?

entries.farsi: چون همى‏گنجد جهانى زير طين / ‏چون بگنجد آسمانى در زمين
entries.english: God forbid! You are beyond this world both in your lifetime and at the present hour.

entries.farsi: در هواى غيب مرغى مى‏پرد / سايه‏ى او بر زمين مى‏گسترد
entries.english: A bird is flying in the atmosphere of the Unseen: its shadow falls on a piece of earth.

entries.farsi: جسم سايه‏ى سايه‏ى سايه‏ى دل است / ‏جسم كى اندر خور پايه‏ى دل است
entries.english: The body is the shadow of the shadow of the shadow of the heart: how is the body worthy of the rank of the heart?

entries.farsi: مرد خفته روح او چون آفتاب / ‏در فلك تابان و تن در جامه خواب
entries.english: A man lies asleep: his spirit is shining in Heaven, like the sun, while his body is in bed.

entries.farsi: جان نهان اندر خال همچون سجاف / ‏تن تقلب مى‏كند زير لحاف
entries.english: His spirit is hidden in the Void, like the fringe: his body is turning to and fro beneath the coverlet.

entries.farsi: روح چون من امر ربى مختفى است / ‏هر مثالى كه بگويم منتفى است
entries.english: Since the spirit, being from the command of my Lord, is invisible, every similitude that I may utter is denying.

entries.farsi: اى عجب كو لعل شكر بار تو / و آن جوابات خوش و اسرار تو
entries.english: Oh, where, I wonder, is your sugar-shedding ruby lip and those sweet replies and mysteries of yours?

entries.farsi: اى عجب كو آن عقيق قند خا / آن كليد قفل مشكلهاى ما
entries.english: Oh, where, I wonder, is that candy-chewing cornelian lip, the key to the lock of our perplexities?

entries.farsi: اى عجب كو آن دم چون ذو الفقار / آن كه كردى عقلها را بى‏قرار
entries.english: Oh, where, I wonder, is that breath as Dhu ’l-faqár, that used to make our understandings distraught?

entries.farsi: كو همانجا كه صفات رحمت است / ‏قدرت است و نزهت است و فطنت است
entries.english: How long, like a ringdove seeking her nest, ‘where (ku) and where and where and where and where and where?’

entries.farsi: چند همچون فاخته‏ى كاشانه جو / كو و كو و كو و كو و كو و كو
entries.english: Where? In the place where are the Attributes of Mercy, and Power and Transcendence, and Intelligence.

entries.farsi: كو همانجا كه دل و انديشه‏اش / ‏دايم آن جا بد چو شير و بيشه‏اش
entries.english: Where? In the same place where his heart and thought always dwelt, like the lion in his jungle.

entries.farsi: كو همانجا كه اميد مرد و زن / ‏مى‏رود در وقت اندوه و حزن
entries.english: Where? In that place whither the hope of man and woman turns in the hour of anguish and sorrow.

entries.farsi: كو همانجا كه به وقت علتى / ‏چشم پرد بر اميد صحتى
entries.english: Where? In the place to which in time of illness the eye takes wing in hope of health—

entries.farsi: آن طرف كه بهر دفع زشتيى / ‏باد جويى بهر كشت و كشتيى
entries.english: In that quarter where, in order to avert a calamity, you seek wind for the corn or a ship;

entries.farsi: آن طرف كه دل اشارت مى‏كند / چون زبان يا هو عبارت مى‏كند
entries.english: In that quarter which is signified by the heart when the tongue utters the expression ‘Yá Hú.’

entries.farsi: او مع اللهَّ است بى‏كوكو همى / ‏كاش جوالهانه ما كو گفتمى
entries.english: He is always with God beyond ‘where? Where?’ (Ku, ku). Would that like weavers I might have said ma ku!

entries.farsi: عقل ما كو تا ببيند غرب و شرق / ‏روحها را مى‏زند صد گونه برق
entries.english: Where is our reason, that it should perceive the spiritual West and East flashing forth a hundred kinds of splendour?

entries.farsi: جزر و مدش بد به بحرى در زبد / منتهى شد جزر و باقى ماند مد
entries.english: His ebb and flow was caused by a foaming Sea: the ebb has ceased and the flow remains.

entries.farsi: نه هزارم وام و من بى‏دست رس / ‏هست صد دينار از اين توزيع و بس
entries.english: I am nine thousand dinars in debt and have no resources: there are a hundred dinars, from this subscription.

entries.farsi: حق كشيدت ماندم در كش مكش / ‏مى‏روم نوميد اى خاك تو خوش
entries.english: God has withdrawn you and I am left in agony: I am going in despair, O you whose dust is sweet!

entries.farsi: همتى مى‏دار در پر حسرتت / ‏اى همايون روى و دست و همتت
entries.english: Keep in your mind a prayer for your grief-stricken, O you whose face and hands and prayers are auspicious.

entries.farsi: آمدم بر چشمه و اصل عيون / ‏يافتم در وى به جاى آب خون
entries.english: I come to the spring and the source of fountains: I find in it instead of water blood.

entries.farsi: چرخ آن چرخ است آن مهتاب نيست / ‏جوى آن جوى است آب آن آب نيست
entries.english: The sky is the same sky, it is not the same moonlight: the river is the same river, the water is not the same water.

entries.farsi: تو شدى سوى خدا اى محترم / ‏پس به سوى حق روم من نيز هم
entries.english: There are benefactors, where is that one who was found to be good? There are stars, where is that sun?

entries.farsi: محسنان هستند كو آن مستطاب / ‏اختران هستند كو آن آفتاب
entries.english: You have gone unto God, O venerated man: I too, therefore, will go unto God.”

entries.farsi: مجمع و پاى علم ماوى القرون / ‏هست حق كل لدينا محضرون
entries.english: God is the assembly-place where the generations are mustered under His banner: all are brought before Us.

entries.farsi: دم به دم در صفحه‏ى انديشه‏شان / ‏ثبت و محوى مى‏كند آن بى‏نشان
entries.english: The pictures, whether unconscious or conscious, are present in the hand of the Painter.

entries.farsi: نقشها گر بى‏خبر گر با خبر / در كف نقاش باشد محتضر
entries.english: Moment by moment that traceless One is setting down on the page of their thought and obliterating it.

entries.farsi: خشم مى‏آرد رضا را مى‏برد / بخل مى‏آرد سخا را مى‏برد
entries.english: He is putting anger and taking acquiescence away: He is putting stinginess and taking generosity away.

entries.farsi: نيم لحظه مدركاتم شام و غدو / هيچ خالى نيست زين اثبات و محو
entries.english: Never for half a wink at eve or morn are my ideas exempt from this imprinting and obliterating.

entries.farsi: كوزه‏گر با كوزه باشد كارساز / كوزه از خود كى شود پهن و دراز
entries.english: The potter works at the pot to fashion it: how should the pot become broad and long of itself?

entries.farsi: چوب در دست دروگر معتكف / ‏ور نه چون گردد بريده و موتلف
entries.english: The wood is kept constantly in the carpenter’s hand: else how should it be hewn and put into right shape?

entries.farsi: جامه اندر دست خياطى بود / ور نه از خود چون بدوزد يا درد
entries.english: The garment is in the hands of a tailor: else how should it sew and cut of itself?

entries.farsi: مشك با سقا بود اى منتهى / ‏ور نه از خود چون شود پر يا تهى
entries.english: The water-skin is with the water-carrier, O adept: else how should it become full or empty by itself?

entries.farsi: هر دمى پر مى‏شوى تى مى‏شوى / ‏پس بدان كه در كف صنع ويى
entries.english: You are being filled and emptied at every moment: know, then, that you are in the hand of His working.

entries.farsi: چشم بند از چشم روزى كى رود / صنع از صانع چه سان شيدا شود
entries.english: On the Day when the eye-bandage falls from the eye, how madly will the work be enamoured of the Worker!

entries.farsi: چشم دارى تو به چشم خود نگر / منگر از چشم سفيهى بى‏خبر
entries.english: If you have an eye, look with your own eye: do not look through the eye of an ignorant fool.

entries.farsi: گوش دارى تو به گوش خود شنو / گوش گوالن را چرا باشى گرو
entries.english: If you have an ear, hearken with your own ear: why be dependent on the ears of blockheads?

entries.farsi: بى‏ز تقليدى نظر را پيشه كن / ‏هم براى عقل خود انديشه كن
entries.english: Make a practice of seeing without blindly following any authority: think in accordance with the view of your own reason.

sections.title_fa: ديدن خوارزمشاه در سيران در موكب خود اسبى بس نادر و تعلق دل شاه به حسن و چستى آن اسب و سرد كردن عماد الملك آن اسب را در دل شاه و گزيدن شاه گفت او را بر ديد خويش چنان كه حكيم سنايى در الهى نامه فرمود ‏يوسفى يابى از گزى كرباس چون زبان حسد شود نخاس
از داللى برادران يوسف حسودانه در دل مشتريان آن چندان حسن پوشيده شد و زشت نمودن گرفت َكه وَ كانُوا فِيهِ مِنَ الزَّاهِدِين
sections.title_en: How the Khwárizmsháh, may God have mercy upon him, while riding for pleasure, saw an exceedingly fine horse in his cavalcade; and how the king’s heart fell in love with the beauty

and elegance of the horse; and how the ‘Imádu ’l-Mulk caused the horse to appear undesirable to the king; and how the king preferred his word to his own sight, as the Hakim, may God have mercy upon him, has said in the Iláhí-náma: “When the tongue of envy turns slave-dealer, you may get a Joseph for an ell of linen.” Owing to the envious feelings of Joseph’s brethren
when they acted as brokers, such a great beauty was veiled from the heart of the buyers and he began to seem ugly, for “they were setting little value on him.”

entries.farsi: بود اميرى را يكى اسبى گزين / ‏در گله‏ى سلطان نبودش يك قرين
entries.english: A certain Amír had a fine horse: there was no equal to it in the Sultan’s troop.

entries.farsi: او سواره گشت در موكب بگاه / ‏ناگهان ديد اسب را خوارزمشاه
entries.english: Early he rode out in the royal cavalcade: suddenly the Khwárizmsháh observed the horse.

entries.farsi: چشم شه را فر و رنگ او ربود / تا به رجعت چشم شه با اسب بود
entries.english: Its beauty and colour enraptured the king’s eye: till his return the king’s eye was following the horse.

entries.farsi: بر هر آن عضوش كه افكندى نظر / هر يكش خوشتر نمودى ز آن دگر
entries.english: On whichever limb he let his gaze fall, each seemed to him more pleasing than the other.

entries.farsi: غير چستى و گشى و روحنت / ‏حق بر او افكنده بد نادر صفت
entries.english: Besides elegance and beauty and spiritedness, God had bestowed on it exquisite qualities.

entries.farsi: پس تجسس كرد عقل پادشاه / ‏كاين چه باشد كه زند بر عقل راه
entries.english: Then the king’s mind sought to discover what it could be that waylaid his reason,

entries.farsi: چشم من پر است و سير است و غنى / ‏از دو صد خورشيد دارد روشنى
entries.english: Saying, “My eye is full and satisfied and wanting naught: it is illumined by two hundred suns.

entries.farsi: اى رخ شاهان بر من بى‏ذقى / ‏نيم اسبم در ربايد بى‏حقى
entries.english: Oh, the rook of kings is a pawn in my sight; a knight enraptures me without any justification.

entries.farsi: جادويى كردست جادو آفرين / ‏جذبه باشد آن نه خاصيات اين
entries.english: The Creator of witchery has bewitched me: it is an attraction, not the peculiar virtues of this.”

entries.farsi: فاتحه خواند و بسى الحول كرد / فاتحه‏ش در سينه مى‏افزود درد
entries.english: He recited the Fatiha and uttered many a lá hawl; the Fatiha increased the passion in his breast,

entries.farsi: ز انكه او را فاتحه خود مى‏كشيد / گر نمايد غير هم تمويه اوست
entries.english: Because the Fatiha itself was drawing him on: the Fatiha is unique in drawing on and averting.

entries.farsi: ‏ور رود غير از نظر تنبيه اوست / پس يقين گشتش كه جذبه ز آن سرى است
entries.english: If another appears, it is His illusion; and if another vanishes from sight, it is His awakening.

entries.farsi: ‏كار حق هر لحظه نادر آورى است / اسب سنگين گاو سنگين ز ابتال
entries.english: Then it became certain to him that the attraction was from Yonder: the action of God is producing marvels at every moment.

entries.farsi: مى‏شود مسجود از مكر خدا / پيش كافر نيست بت را ثانيى
entries.english: Because of the probation a stone horse a stone cow becomes, through God’s deception, an object of worship.

entries.farsi: ‏نيست بت را فر و نه روحانيى / چيست آن جاذب نهان اندر نهان
entries.english: In the eyes of the infidel the idol has no second, the idol has neither glory nor spirituality.

entries.farsi: ‏در جهان تابيده از ديگر جهان / عقل محجوب است و جان هم زين كمين
entries.english: What is the attracting power, hidden in the hiddenmost, that shines forth in this world from the other world?

entries.farsi: ‏من نمى‏بينم تو مى‏تانى ببين / چون كه خوارزمشه ز سيران باز گشت
entries.english: The intellect is barred, and the spirit also, from this ambush; I cannot see it: see it you can!

entries.farsi: ‏با خواص ملك خود هم راز گشت / پس به سرهنگان بفرمود آن زمان
entries.english: When the Khwárizmsháh returned from his ride, he conferred with the nobles of his kingdom.

entries.farsi: ‏تا بيارند اسب را ز آن خاندان / همچو آتش در رسيدند آن گروه
entries.english: Then he immediately ordered the officers to fetch the horse from that household.

entries.farsi: ‏همچو پشمى گشت امير همچو كوه / جانش از درد و غبين تا لب رسيد
entries.english: As fire, the party arrived: the Amír who was like a mountain became as a piece of wool

entries.farsi: جز عماد الملك زنهارى نديد / كه عماد الملك بد پاى علم
entries.english: He almost expired from the anguish and fraud: he saw no protection except the ‘Imádu ’l-Mulk;

entries.farsi: ‏بهر هر مظلوم و هر مقتول غم / محترم‏تر خود نبد زو سرورى
entries.english: For the ‘Imádu ’l-Mulk was the foot of the banner to which every victim of injustice and every one stricken by distress would flock for refuge.

entries.farsi: ‏پيش سلطان بود چون پيغمبرى / بى‏طمع بود و اصيل و پارسا
entries.english: In truth there was no chief more revered than he: in the eyes of the Sultan he was like a prophet.

entries.farsi: رايض و شب خيز و حاتم در سخا / بس همايون راى و با تدبير و راد
entries.english: He was not ambitious, strong-minded, devout, and ascetic, one who kept vigils and was Hatím in generosity;

entries.farsi: آزموده راى او در هر مراد / هم به بذل جان سخى و هم به مال
entries.english: Very felicitous in judgement, endowed with foresight, and sage: his judgement had been proved in everything that he sought to attain.

entries.farsi: ‏طالب خورشيد غيب او چون هالل / در اميرى او غريب و محتبس
entries.english: Generous both in self-sacrifice and in sacrificing wealth: always seeking the Sun of the invisible world, like the new-moon.

entries.farsi: ‏در صفات فقر و خلت ملتبس / بوده هر محتاج را همچون پدر
entries.english: In his princedom he felt strange and embarrassed: he was clad in the attributes of poverty and love.

entries.farsi: پيش سلطان شافع و دفع ضرر / مر بدان را ستر چون حلم خدا
entries.english: He was like a father to everyone in need: before the Sultan he was an intercessor and the means of averting harm.

entries.farsi: خلق او بر عكس خلقان و جدا / بارها مى‏شد به سوى كوه فرد
entries.english: To the wicked he was a covering, like the clemency of God: his nature was opposite to created beings and apart.

entries.farsi: شاه با صد البه او را منع كرد / هر دم از صد جرم را شافع شدى
entries.english: Many a time he would have gone alone to the mountains, the Sultan prevented him by a hundred humble entreaties.

entries.farsi: سر برهنه كرد و بر خاك اوفتاد / كه حرم با هر چه دارم گو بگير
entries.english: If at every moment he had interceded for a hundred sins, the Sultan’s eye would have been abashed before him.

entries.farsi: ‏چشم سلطان را از او شرم آمدى / رفت او پيش عماد الملك راد
entries.english: He went to the noble ‘Imádu ’l-Mulk: he bared his head and fell on the ground,

entries.farsi: تا بگيرد حاصلم را هر مغير / اين يكى اسب است جانم رهن اوست
entries.english: Saying “Let him take my harem together with all that I possess! Let any raider seize my revenue!

entries.farsi: ‏گر برد مردم يقين اى خير دوست / گر برد اين اسب را از دست من
entries.english: There is this one horse—my soul is devoted to it: if he takes it, I will surely die O lover of good.

entries.farsi: ‏بر سرم مال اى مسيحا زود دست / از زن و زر و عقارم صبر هست
entries.english: If he takes this horse out of my hands, I know for certain that I shall not live.

entries.farsi: ‏من يقين دانم نخواهم زيستن / چون خدا پيوستگيى داده است
entries.english: Since God has bestowed a connection, stroke my head at once with your hand, O Messiah!

entries.farsi: ‏امتحان كن امتحان گفت و قدم / آن عماد الملك گريان چشم مال
entries.english: I can bear the loss of my women and gold and estates: this is not pretence nor is it an imposture.

entries.farsi: ‏اين تكلف نيست نى تزويرى است / اندر اين گر مى‏ندارى باورم
entries.english: If you do not believe me in this, try me; try me in word and deed!”

entries.farsi: ‏پيش سلطان در دويد آشفته حال / لب ببست و پيش سلطان ايستاد
entries.english: Weeping and wiping his eyes, the ‘Imádu ’l-Mulk ran, with agitated mien, into the presence of the Sultan.

entries.farsi: رازگويان با خدا رب العباد / ايستاده راز سلطان مى‏شنيد
entries.english: He closed his lips and stood before the Sultan, communing with God the Lord of His slaves.

entries.farsi: و اندرون انديشه‏اش اين مى‏تنيد / كاى خدا گر آن جوان كژ رفت راه
entries.english: He stood and listened to the Sultan’s intimate talk, while inwardly his thought was weaving this—

entries.farsi: ‏كه نشايد ساختن جز تو پناه / تو از آن خود بكن از وى مگير
entries.english: “O God, if that young man has gone the wrong way, for it is not fitting to make anyone except You a refuge,

entries.farsi: گر چه او خواهد خالص از هر اسير / ز انكه محتاجند اين خلقان همه
entries.english: Act in Your own fashion and be not offended with him although he beseech any prisoner to deliver him,

entries.farsi: ‏از گدايى گير تا سلطان همه / با حضور آفتاب با كمال
entries.english: Because all these creatures are in need: take all from a beggar to the Sultan.”

entries.farsi: ‏رهنمايى جستن از شمع و ذبال / با حضور آفتاب خوش مساغ
entries.english: To seek guidance from candle and wick when the perfect Sun is present,

entries.farsi: ‏روشنايى جستن از شمع و چراغ / بى‏گمان ترك ادب باشد ز ما
entries.english: To seek light from candle and lamp when the smoothly-rolling Sun is present,

entries.farsi: كفر نعمت باشد و فعل هوا / ليك اغلب موشها در افتكار
entries.english: Doubtless it is irreverence on our part; it is ingratitude and an act of self-will,

entries.farsi: همچو خفاشند ظلمت دوستدار / در شب ار خفاش كرمى مى‏خورد
entries.english: But most minds in thinking are lovers of darkness, like the bat.

entries.farsi: كرم را خورشيد جان مى‏پرورد / در شب ار خفاش از كرمى است مست
entries.english: If the bat eats a worm during the night, the Sun fosters the life of the worm.

entries.farsi: دشمن خود را نواله مى‏دهد / ليك شهبازى كه او خفاش نيست
entries.english: If the bat is intoxicated with a worm during the night, by the Sun the worm has been caused to move.

entries.farsi: ‏كرم از خورشيد جنبنده شده است / آفتابى كه ضيا زو مى‏زهد
entries.english: The Sun from where radiance gushes forth is giving food to his enemy.

entries.farsi: ‏چشم بازش راست بين و روشنى است / گر به شب جويد چو خفاش او نمو
entries.english: But the royal falcon which is not a bat and whose falcon-eyes are seeing truly and are clear,

entries.farsi: در ادب خورشيد مالد گوش او / گويدش گيرم كه آن خفاش لد
entries.english: If it, like the bat, seeks increase during the night, the Sun will rub its ear in correction,

entries.farsi: علتى دارد ترا بارى چه شد / مالشت بدهم به زجر از اكتئاب
entries.english: And will say to it, “I grant that the perverse bat has an infirmity, anyhow what is the matter with you?

entries.farsi: ‏تا نتابى سر دگر از آفتاب
entries.english: I will chastise you severely with affliction, in order that you may not again turn your head away from the Sun.”

sections.title_fa: مواخذه‏ى يوسف صديق عليه السلام به حبس بضع سنين به سبب يارى خواستن از غير حق و گفتن اذْكُرْنِي عِ نْدَ رَبِّكَ ، مع تقريره
sections.title_en: How Joseph the Siddíq—the blessings of God be upon him!—was punished with imprisonment
“for several years” because of his seeking help from another than God and saying, “Mention me in your lord’s presence,” together with the exposition thereof.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه يوسف از زندانيى / ‏با نيازى خاضعى سعدانيى
entries.english: That is like Joseph’s of a prisoner, a needy abject groundling.

entries.farsi: خواست يارى گفت چون بيرون روى / ‏پيش شه گردد امورت مستوى
entries.english: He besought him for help and said, “When you come out, your affairs will prosper with the king.

entries.farsi: ياد من كن پيش تخت آن عزيز / تا مرا هم واخرد زين حبس نيز
entries.english: Make mention of me before the throne of that mighty prince, that he may redeem me also from this prison.”

entries.farsi: كى دهد زندانيى در اقتناص / ‏مرد زندانى ديگر را خالص
entries.english: How should a prisoner in captivity give release to another imprisoned man?

entries.farsi: اهل دنيا جملگان زندانى‏اند / انتظار مرگ دار فانى‏اند
entries.english: All the people of this world are prisoners in expectation of death in the abode that is passing away;

entries.farsi: جز مگر نادر يكى فردانيى / ‏تن به زندان جان او كيوانيى
entries.english: Except, to be sure, in the rare case of one who is single, one whose body is in the prison and his spirit like Saturn.

entries.farsi: پس جزاى آن كه ديد او را معين / ‏ماند يوسف حبس در بضع سنين
entries.english: Therefore, in retribution for having regarded him as a helper, Joseph was left in prison for several years.

entries.farsi: ياد يوسف ديو از عقلش سترد / و ز دلش ديو آن سخن از ياد برد
entries.english: The Devil erased from his mind the recollection of Joseph and removed from his memory those words.

entries.farsi: زين گنه كآمد از آن نيكو خصال / ‏ماند در زندان ز داور چند سال
entries.english: In consequence of the sin which proceeded from that man of goodly qualities, he was left in prison for several years by the Judge,

entries.farsi: كه چه تقصير آمد از خورشيد داد / تا تو چون خفاش افتى در سواد
entries.english: Who said, “What failure was shown by the Sun of justice that you should fall, like a bat, into the blackness?

entries.farsi: هين چه تقصير آمد از بحر و سحاب / ‏تا تو يارى خواهى از ريگ و سراب
entries.english: Listen, what failure was shown by the sea and the cloud that you should seek help from the sand and the mirage?

entries.farsi: عام اگر خفاش طبعند و مجاز / يوسفا دارى تو آخر چشم باز
entries.english: If the ordinary are bats by nature and unreal, you, at least, O Joseph, have the eye of the falcon.

entries.farsi: گر خفاشى رفت در كور و كبود / باز سلطان ديده را بارى چه بود
entries.english: If a bat went into the blind and blue, after all what ailed the falcon that had seen the Sultan?”

entries.farsi: پس ادب كردش بدين جرم اوستاد / كه مساز از چوب پوسيده عماد
entries.english: Therefore the Master punished him for this sin, saying, “Do not make your prop of rotten wood”;

entries.farsi: آن چنانش انس و مستى داد حق / ‏كه نه زندان ماند پيشش نه غسق
entries.english: But He caused Joseph to be engrossed with Him, to the end that his heart should not be pained by that imprisonment.

entries.farsi: ليك يوسف را به خود مشغول كرد / تا نيايد در دلش ز آن حبس درد
entries.english: God gave him such intimate joy and rapture that neither the prison nor the murk remained to him.

entries.farsi: نيست زندانى وحش‏تر از رحم / ‏ناخوش و تاريك و پر خون و وخم
entries.english: There is no prison more frightful than the womb— noisome and dark and full of blood and unhealthy;

entries.farsi: چون گشادت حق دريچه سوى خويش / ‏در رحم هر دم فزايد تنت بيش
entries.english: When God has opened for you a window in His direction, your body in the womb grows more every moment,

entries.farsi: اندر آن زندان ز ذوق بى‏قياس / ‏خوش شكفت از غرس جسم تو حواس
entries.english: And in that prison, from the immeasurable delight, the senses blossom happily from the plant, your body.

entries.farsi: ز آن رحم بيرون شدن بر تو درشت / ‏مى‏گريزى از زهارش سوى پشت
entries.english: It is grievous to you to go forth from the womb: you are fleeing from her pubes towards her back.

entries.farsi: راه لذت از درون دان نه از برون / ‏ابلهى دان جستن قصر و حصون
entries.english: Know that the way of pleasure is from within, not from without: know that it is folly to seek palaces and castles.

entries.farsi: آن يكى در كنج مسجد مست و شاد / و آن دگر در باغ ترش و بى‏مراد
entries.english: One man is enraptured and delighted in the nook of a mosque, while another is morose and disappointed in a garden.

entries.farsi: قصر چيزى نيست ويران كن بدن / ‏گنج در ويرانى است اى مير من
entries.english: The palace is nothing: ruin your body! The treasure lays in the ruin, O my prince.

entries.farsi: اين نمى‏بينى كه در بزم شراب / ‏مست آن گه خوش شود كاو شد خراب
entries.english: Don’t you see that at the wine-feast the drunkard becomes happy when he becomes ruined?

entries.farsi: گر چه پر نقش است خانه بركنش / ‏گنج جو و ز گنج آبادان كنش
entries.english: Although the house is full of pictures, demolish it: seek the treasure, and with the treasure put it into good repair.

entries.farsi: خانه‏اى پر نقش تصوير و خيال / ‏وين صور چون پرده بر گنج وصال
entries.english: It is a house filled with pictures of imagination and fancy, and these forms are as a veil over the treasure of union.

entries.farsi: پرتو گنج است و تابشهاى زر / كه در اين سينه همى‏جوشد صور
entries.english: It is the radiance of the Treasure and the splendours of the gold that cause the forms to surge up in this breast.

entries.farsi: هم ز لطف و عكس آب با شرف / ‏پرده شد بر روى آب اجزاى كف
entries.english: It is from the purity and translucence of the noble Water that the particles of foam have veiled the face of the Water.

entries.farsi: هم ز لطف و جوش جان با ثمن / پرده‏اى بر روى جان شد شخص تن
entries.english: It is from the purity and agitation of the precious Spirit that the bodily figure has veiled the face of the Spirit.

entries.farsi: پس مثل بشنو كه در افواه خاست / ‏كآنچه بر ماست اى برادر هم ز ماست
entries.english: Listen, then, to the adage that issued from the mouths— “this which is upon us, O brother, is from us.”

entries.farsi: زين حجاب اين تشنگان كف پرست / ‏ز آب صافى اوفتاده دور دست
entries.english: Because of this veil, these thirsty ones who are fond of the foam have got out of reach of the pure Water.

entries.farsi: آفتابا با چو تو قبله و امام / ‏شب پرستى و خفاشى مى‏كنيم
entries.english: “O Sun, notwithstanding a qibla and Imam like You, we worship the night and behave in the manner of bats.

entries.farsi: سوى خود كن اين خفاشان را مطار / زين خفاشيشان بخر اى مستجار
entries.english: Make these bats to fly towards You and redeem them from this bat-like disposition, O You whose protection is implored!

entries.farsi: اين جوان زين جرم ضال است و مغير / كه به من آمد ولى او را مگير
entries.english: This youth, by this sin, has gone astray and trespassed, for he came to me; but do not chastise him.”

entries.farsi: در عماد الملك اين انديشه‏ها / گشته جوشان چون اسد در بيشه‏ها
entries.english: In the ‘Imádu ’l-Mulk these thoughts were raging like a lion through the jungles.

entries.farsi: ايستاده پيش سلطان ظاهرش / ‏در رياض غيب جان طايرش
entries.english: His exterior stood before the Sultan, his soaring spirit was in the meadows of the Unseen.

entries.farsi: چون ماليك او به اقليم أَ لست / ‏هر دمى مى‏شد به شرب تازه مست
entries.english: Like the angels, he was instantly being intoxicated with fresh draughts in the realm of Alast;

entries.farsi: اندرون سور و برون چون پر غمى / ‏در تن همچون لحد خوش عالمى
entries.english: Inwardly a wedding-feast, but outwardly like a man filled with sorrow; a delectable world in a tomb-like body.

entries.farsi: او در اين حيرت بد و در انتظار / تا چه پيدا آيد از غيب و سرار
entries.english: He was in this bewilderment and waiting to see what would appear from the things occult and mysterious,

entries.farsi: اسب را اندر كشيدند آن زمان / ‏پيش خوارزمشاه سرهنگان كشان
entries.english: At that time the officers brought the horse along into the presence of the Khwárizmsháh.

entries.farsi: الحق اندر زير اين چرخ كبود / آن چنان كره به قد و تك نبود
entries.english: Truly beneath this azure sky there was no colt like that in figure and in fleetness.

entries.farsi: مى‏ربودى رنگ او هر ديده را / مرحب آن از برق و مه زاييده را
entries.english: Its colour dazzled every eye: “Hail to the steed born of the lightning and the moon!”

entries.farsi: همچو مه همچون عطارد تيز رو / گوييا صرصر علف بودش نه جو
entries.english: It moved as swiftly as the moon and Mercury: you might say that its fodder was the sarsar wind, not barley.

entries.farsi: ماه عرصه‏ى آسمان را در شبى / ‏مى‏برد اندر مسير و مذهبى
entries.english: The moon traverses the expanse of heaven in one night during a single journey and course.

entries.farsi: چون به يك شب مه بريد ابراج را / از چه منكر مى‏شوى معراج را
entries.english: Since the moon traversed the signs of the zodiac in one night, why will you disbelieve the Ascension?

entries.farsi: صد چو ماه است آن عجب در يتيم / ‏كه به يك ايماى او شد مه دو نيم
entries.english: That wondrous orphan Pearl is as a hundred moons, for at a nod from him the moon became two halves.

entries.farsi: آن عجب كاو در شكاف مه نمود / هم به قدر ضعف حس خلق بود
entries.english: The marvel which he displayed in splitting the moon was only according to the measure of the weakness of the perception possessed by the people.

entries.farsi: تو برون رو هم ز افلاک و دوار / و آن گهان نظاره كن آن كار و بار
entries.english: The work and business of the prophets and messengers is beyond the skies and the stars.

entries.farsi: كار و بار انبيا و مرسلون / ‏هست از افلاک و اخترها برون
entries.english: Also go beyond the skies and the revolving, and then contemplate that work and business.

entries.farsi: در ميان بيضه‏اى چون فرخها / نشنوى تسبيح مرغان هوا
entries.english: While you are inside the egg, like chicks, you cannot hear the glorification of God by the birds of the air.

entries.farsi: معجزات اينجا نخواهد شرح گشت / ‏ز اسب و خوارمشاه گو و سر گذشت
entries.english: The miracles will not be set forth here: tell of the horse and the Khwárizmsháh and what happened.

entries.farsi: آفتاب لطف حق بر هر چه تافت / ‏از سگ و از اسب فر كهف يافت
entries.english: Whatever the sun of God’s grace shines upon, whether it be dog or horse, gains the glory of the Cave;

entries.farsi: تاب لطفش را تو يكسان هم مدان / ‏سنگ را و لعل را داد او نشان
entries.english: Yet deem not the radiance of His grace to be uniform: it has given a sign to the pebble and the ruby.

entries.farsi: لعل را ز آن هست گنج مقتبس / ‏سنگ را گرمى و تابانى و بس
entries.english: From that the ruby has a borrowed treasure; the pebble has only heat and brightness.

entries.farsi: آن كه بر ديوار افتد آفتاب / ‏آن چنان نبود كز آب و اضطراب
entries.english: The sun falling on a wall is not the same as from water and quivering movement.

entries.farsi: كاى اخى بس خوب اسبى نيست اين / ‏از بهشت است اين مگر نه از زمين
entries.english: After the peerless king had been astounded by it for a moment, he turned his face to the ‘Imádu ’l-Mulk,

entries.farsi: چون دمى حيران شد از وى شاه فرد / روى خود سوى عماد الملك كرد
entries.english: Saying, “O vizier, is not this an exceedingly beautiful horse? Surely it belongs to Paradise, not to the earth.”

entries.farsi: پس عماد الملك گفتش اى خديو / چون فرشته گردد از ميل تو ديو
entries.english: Thereupon the ‘Imádu ’l-Mulk said to him, “O emperor, a demon is made angelic by your inclination.

entries.farsi: در نظر آنچ آورى گرديد نيك / ‏بس گش و رعناست اين مركب و ليك
entries.english: That on which you look appears good. This steed is very handsome and graceful, and yet

entries.farsi: هست ناقص آن سر اندر پيكرش / ‏چون سر گاو است گويى آن سرش
entries.english: The head is a blemish in its form: you might say that its head is like the head of an ox.”

entries.farsi: در دل خوارمشه اين دم كار كرد / اسب را در منظر شه خوار كرد
entries.english: These words worked on the heart of the Khwárizmsháh and caused the horse to be cheap in the king’s sight.

entries.farsi: چون غرض دالله گشت و واصفى / ‏از سه گز كرباس يابى يوسفى
entries.english: When prejudice becomes a go-between and describer, you may get a Joseph for three ells of linen.

entries.farsi: چون كه هنگام فراق جان شود / ديو دالل در ايمان شود
entries.english: When the hour arrives for the spirit’s parting, the Devil becomes a broker of the pearl of Faith,

entries.farsi: پس فرو شد ابله ايمان را شتاب / ‏اندر آن تنگى به يك ابريق آب
entries.english: And then in that sore distress the fool hastily sells his faith for a jug of water;

entries.farsi: و آن خيالى باشد و ابريق نى / ‏قصد آن دالل جز تخريق نى
entries.english: But it is a phantom and not a jug: the aim of the broker is naught but trickery.

entries.farsi: اين زمان كه تو صحيح و فربهى / ‏صدق را بهر خيالى مى‏دهى
entries.english: At this time, when you are healthy and fat, you are giving up the Truth for a phantom.

entries.farsi: مى‏فروشى هر زمانى در كان / ‏همچو طفلى مى‏ستانى گردكان
entries.english: You are constantly selling the pearls of the mine and taking walnuts, like a child;

entries.farsi: در خيالت صورتى جوشيده‏اى / ‏همچو جوزى وقت دق پوسيده‏اى
entries.english: Therefore it is no wonder if you act in this way in the sickness of your day of doom.

entries.farsi: پس در آن رنجورى روز اجل / ‏نيست نادر گر بود اينت عمل
entries.english: You have concocted an idea in your fancy: when you are rattled like a walnut, you are rotten.

entries.farsi: هست از آغاز چون بدر آن خيال / ‏ليك آخر مى‏شود همچون هالل
entries.english: In the beginning that phantom resembles the full-moon, but in the end it will become like the new-moon.

entries.farsi: گر تو اول بنگرى چون آخرش / ‏فارغ آيى از فريب فاترش
entries.english: If you regard its first as being like its last, you will be quit of its feeble deception.

entries.farsi: جوز پوسيده‏ست دنيا اى امين / ‏امتحانش كم كن از دورش ببين
entries.english: This world is a rotten walnut: O man of trust, do not make trial of it, behold it from afar.

entries.farsi: شاه ديد آن اسب را با چشم حال / ‏و آن عماد الملك با چشم مال
entries.english: The king viewed the horse with regard to the present, while the ‘Imádu ’l- Mulk with regard to the future.

entries.farsi: چشم شه دو گز همى‏ديد از لغز / چشم آن پايان نگر پنجاه گز
entries.english: The king’s eye, because of distortion, saw two ells; the eye of him who regarded the end saw fifty ells.

entries.farsi: آن چه سرمه‏ست آن كه يزدان مى‏كشد / كز پس صد پرده بيند جان رشد
entries.english: What an eye salve is that which God applies, so that the spirit discerns the truth behind a hundred curtains!

entries.farsi: چشم مهتر چون به آخر بود جفت / ‏پس بدان ديده جهان را جيفه گفت
entries.english: Since the Chief ’s eye was ever fixed on the end, by reason of that eye he called the world a carcass.

entries.farsi: زين يكى ذمش كه بشنود او و حسب / ‏پس فسرد اندر دل شه مهر اسب
entries.english: On hearing only this single blame from him, the love in the king’s heart for the horse became chilled.

entries.farsi: چشم خود بگذاشت و چشم او گزيد / هوش خود بگذاشت و قول او شنيد
entries.english: He abandoned his own eye and preferred his eye: he abandoned his own intelligence and listened to his words.

entries.farsi: اين بهانه بود و آن ديان فرد / از نياز آن در دل شه سرد كرد
entries.english: This was the pretext, and at entreaty the unique Judge caused it to be cold in the king’s heart.

entries.farsi: در ببست از حسن او پيش بصر / آن سخن بد در ميان چون بانگ در
entries.english: He shut the door on its beauty to the eye: those words intervened like the sound of the door.

entries.farsi: پاك بنايى كه بر سازد حصون / ‏در جهان غيب از گفت و فسون
entries.english: He made that cryptic saying a veil over the king’s eye, a veil through which the moon appears to be black.

entries.farsi: پرده كرد آن نكته را بر چشم شه / ‏كه از آن پرده نمايد مه سيه
entries.english: Pure is the Builder who in the unseen world constructs castles of speech and beguiling talk.

entries.farsi: بانگ در دان گفت را از قصر راز / تا كه بانگ واشده‏ست اين يا فراز
entries.english: Know that speech is the sound of the door from the palace of mystery: consider whether it is the sound of opening or shutting.

entries.farsi: بانگ در محسوس و در از حس برون / ‏تبصرون اين بانگ و در ال تبصرون
entries.english: The sound of the door is perceptible, but the door is beyond perception: you see this sound, but the door you see not.

entries.farsi: چنگ حكمت چون كه خوش آواز شد / تا چه در از روض جنت باز شد
entries.english: When the harp of wisdom breaks into melody, what door of the Garden of Paradise has been opened.

entries.farsi: بانگ گفت بد چو در وا مى‏شود / از سقر تا خود چه در وامى شود
entries.english: When the sound of evil speech becomes loud, what door of Hell is being opened.

entries.farsi: بانگ در بشنو چو دورى از درش / ‏اى خنك او را كه واشد منظرش
entries.english: Since you are far from its door, listen to the sound of the door: oh, blest is he whose eye has been opened.

entries.farsi: چون تو مى‏بينى كه نيكى مى‏كنى / ‏بر حيات و راحتى بر مى‏زنى
entries.english: When you are aware of doing a good action, you obtain a life and joy;

entries.farsi: چون كه تقصير و فسادى مى‏رود / آن حيات و ذوق پنهان مى‏شود
entries.english: And when a fault and evil deed issues, that life and rapture disappears.

entries.farsi: ديد خود مگذار از ديد خسان / ‏كه به مردارت كشند اين كركسان
entries.english: Do not abandon your own eye from regard for the vile, for these vultures will lead you to the carcass.

entries.farsi: چشم چون نرگس فرو بندى كه چى / ‏هين عصايم كش كه كورم اى اچى
entries.english: You close your narcissus-like eye, saying, “What? Hey, sir, take my stick, for I am blind”;

entries.farsi: و آن عصا كش كه گزيدى در سفر / خود ببينى باشد از تو كورتر
entries.english: But if you would only look, the guide whom you have chosen for the journey is blinder than you.

entries.farsi: دست كورانه بحبل اللهَّ زن / ‏جز بر امر و نهى يزدانى متن
entries.english: Grasp in a blind man’s fashion the rope of Allah: do not cling to anything but the Divine commandments and prohibitions.

entries.farsi: چيست حبل اللهَّ رها كردن هوا / كاين هوا شد صرصرى مر عاد را
entries.english: What is the rope of Allah? To renounce self-will, for this self-will was a roaring wind to Ad

entries.farsi: خلق در زندان نشسته از هواست / ‏مرغ را پرها ببسته از هواست
entries.english: It is from self-will that folk are sitting in jail; it is from self-will that the bird’s wings are tied.

entries.farsi: ماهى اندر تابه‏ى گرم از هواست / ‏رفته از مستوريان شرم از هواست
entries.english: It is from self-will that the fish is in a hot pan; it is from self-will that shame is gone from the modest.

entries.farsi: خشم شحنه شعله‏ى نار از هواست / ‏چار ميخ و هيبت دار از هواست
entries.english: The anger of the police magistrate is a fiery spark from self-will; crucifixion and the awfulness of the gallows are of self-will.

entries.farsi: شحنه‏ى اجسام ديدى بر زمين / ‏شحنه‏ى احكام جان را هم ببين
entries.english: You have seen the magistrate of bodies on the earth: see also the magistrate who executes judgments against the soul.

entries.farsi: روح را در غيب خود اشكنجه‏هاست / ‏ليك تا نجهى شكنجه در خفاست
entries.english: Truly tortures are inflicted on the soul in the world invisible, but until you escape the torture is concealed.

entries.farsi: آن كه در چه زاد و در آب سياه / ‏او چه داند لطف دشت و رنج چاه
entries.english: When you are freed you will behold the torture and perdition, because contrary is made manifest by contrary.

entries.farsi: چون رهيدى بينى اشكنجه و دمار / ز انكه ضد از ضد گردد آشكار
entries.english: He that was born in the well and the black water, how should he know the pleasantness of the open country and the pain of the well?

entries.farsi: چون رها كردى هوا از بيم حق / ‏در رسد سغراق از تسنيم حق
entries.english: When, from fear of God, you have relinquished self-will, the goblet from God’s Tasním will arrive.

entries.farsi: ال تطرق فى هواك سل سبيل / ‏من جناب اللهَّ نحو السلسبيل
entries.english: Do not in your self-will make a way: ask of God’s Majesty the way to Salsabíl.

entries.farsi: ال تكن طوع الهوى مثل الحشيش / ان ظل العرش اولى من عريش
entries.english: Be not submissive to self-will like hay: in truth the shade of the Divine Throne is better than the summer-house.

entries.farsi: با دل خود شه نفرمود اين قدر / شير را مفريب زين راس البقر
entries.english: The Sultan said, “Take the horse back and with all speed redeem me from this wrong.”

entries.farsi: گفت سلطان اسب را واپس بريد / زودتر زين مظلمه بازم خريد
entries.english: The King did not say in his heart, “Do not deceive the lion so greatly by means of the head of an ox.

entries.farsi: پاى گاو اندر ميان آرى ز داو / رو ندوزد حق بر اسبى شاخ گاو
entries.english: You drag in the ox in order to cheat: go, God does not stick the horns of an ox upon a horse.”

entries.farsi: بس مناسب صنعت است اين شهره زاو / كى نهد بر جسم اسب او عضو گاو
entries.english: This renowned Master-builder observes great congruity in His workmanship: how should He attach to a horse’s body part of an ox?

entries.farsi: در ميان قصرها تخريجها / از سوى اين سوى آن صهريج‏ها
entries.english: The Master-builder has made bodies congruously: He has constructed moving palaces,

entries.farsi: ز او ابدان را مناسب ساخته / ‏قصرهاى منتقل پرداخته
entries.english: Balconies in them and cisterns from this to that;

entries.farsi: و ز درونشان عالمى بى‏منتها / در ميان خرگهى چندين فضا
entries.english: And within them an infinite world: this entire expanse in a single tent.

entries.farsi: گه چو كابوسى نمايد ماه را / گه نمايد روضه قعر چاه را
entries.english: Now He causes the moon to seem like an incubus, now He causes the bottom of a well to have the semblance of a garden.

entries.farsi: قبض و بسط چشم دل از ذو الجلال / ‏دم به دم چون مى‏كند سحر حالل
entries.english: Inasmuch as the closing and opening of the eye of the heart by the Almighty is continually working lawful magic,

entries.farsi: زين سبب درخواست حق از مصطفى / زشت را هم زشت و حق را حق نما
entries.english: For this reason Mustafá entreated God, saying, “Let the false appear as false and the true as true,

entries.farsi: تا به آخر چون بگردانى ورق / ‏از پشيمانى نيفتم در قلق
entries.english: So that at last, when You turn the leaf, I may not by sorrow fall into agitation.”

entries.farsi: مكر كه كرد آن عماد الملك فرد / مالك الملكش بدان ارشاد كرد
entries.english: It was the Lord of the Kingdom guided the peerless ‘Imádu ’l- Mulk to the deception which he practised.

entries.farsi: مكر حق سرچشمه‏ى اين مكرهاست / ‏قلب بين اصبعين كبرياست
entries.english: God’s deception is the fountainhead of these deceptions: the heart is between the two fingers of the Majesty.

entries.farsi: آن كه سازد در دلت مكر و قياس / ‏آتشى داند زدن اندر پالس
entries.english: He who creates deception and analogy in your heart can set the sackcloth on fire.

sections.title_fa: رجوع كردن به قصه‏ى آن پاى مرد و آن غريب وام دار و باز گشتن ايشان از سر گور خواجه و ‏خواب ديدن پاى مرد خواجه را الى آخره
sections.title_en: Return to the Story of the bailiff and the poor debtor: how they turned back from the Khwaja’s grave, and how the bailiff saw the Khwaja in a dream, etc.

entries.farsi: بى‏نهايت آمد اين خوش سر گذشت / ‏چون غريب از گور خواجه باز گشت
entries.english: This goodly episode is endless. When the poor stranger turned back from the Khwaja’s grave,

entries.farsi: لوتش آورد و حكايتهاش گفت / ‏كز اميد اندر دلش صد گل شكفت
entries.english: The bailiff took him to his house and handed over to him the purse of a hundred dinars.

entries.farsi: پاى مردش سوى خانه‏ى خويش برد / مهر صد دينار را با او سپرد
entries.english: He fetched delicacies for him and told him stories, so that from the hope a hundred roses blossomed in his heart.

entries.farsi: آن چه بعد العسر يسر او ديده بود / با غريب از قصه‏ى آن لب گشود
entries.english: He opened his lips to relate the ease which he had experienced after difficulty.

entries.farsi: نيم شب بگذشت و افسانه كنان / ‏خوابشان انداخت تا مرعاى جان
entries.english: Midnight passed, and narrating: sleep transported them to the meadow where the spirit feeds.

entries.farsi: ديد پا مرد آن همايون خواجه را / اندر آن شب خواب بر صدر سرا
entries.english: On that night the bailiff dreamed that he saw the blessed Khwaja on the high-seat in the palace.

entries.farsi: خواجه گفت اى پاى مرد با نمك / ‏آن چه گفتى من شنيدم يك به يك
entries.english: The Khwaja said, “O excellent bailiff, I have heard what you said, point by point,

entries.farsi: ليك پاسخ دادنم فرمان نبود / بى‏اشارت لب نيارستم گشود
entries.english: But I was not commanded to answer, and I dare not open my lips without being directed.

entries.farsi: ما چو واقف گشته‏ايم از چون و چند / مهر بر لبهاى ما بنهاده‏اند
entries.english: Now that we have become acquainted with the conditions and degrees, a seal has been laid upon our lips,

entries.farsi: تا نگردد رازهاى غيب فاش / ‏تا نگردد منهدم عيش و معاش
entries.english: Lest the mysteries of the Unseen should be divulged and the life and livelihood be destroyed,

entries.farsi: تا ندرد پرده‏ى غفلت تمام / ‏تا نماند ديگ محنت نيم خام
entries.english: And lest the veil of forgetfulness should be entirely rent and the pot of tribulation be left half-raw.

entries.farsi: ما همه گوشيم كر شد نقش گوش / ‏ما همه نطقيم ليكن لب خموش
entries.english: We are all ear, the form of the ear has become deaf: we are all speech, but our lips are silent.

entries.farsi: هر چه ما داديم ديديم اين زمان / ‏اين جهان پرده‏ست و عين است آن جهان
entries.english: We now see everything that we gave: this world is the veil, and that world is the vision.

entries.farsi: روز كشتن روز پنهان كردن است / ‏تخم در خاكى پريشان كردن است
entries.english: The day of sowing is the day of concealment and scattering seed in a piece of earth.

entries.farsi: وقت بدرودن گه منجل زدن / ‏روز پاداش آمد و پيدا شدن
entries.english: The season of reaping and the time of plying the sickle is the day of recompense and manifestation.

sections.title_fa: گفتن خواجه در خواب به آن پاى مرد وجوه وام آن دوست را كه آمده بود و نشان دادن جاى دفن آن سيم و پيغام كردن به وارثان كه البته آن را بسيار نبينند و هيچ باز نگيرد و اگر چه او هيچ از آن قبول نكند يا بعضى را قبول نكند هم آن جا بگذارند تا هر انكه خواهد بر گيرد كه من با خدا نذرها ‏كردم كه از آن سيم به من و متعلقان من حبه اى باز نگردد الى آخره

sections.title_en: How the Khwaja disclosed to the bailiff in his dream the means of paying the debts incurred by the friend who had come; and how he indicated the spot where the money was buried, and sent a message to his heirs that on no account should they regard that as too much or withhold anything, and that though he were to refuse the whole or a part of it they must let it remain in the place, in order that anyone who wished might take it away;
‘for,’ said he, ‘I have made vows to God that not one mite of that money shall come back again to me and those connected with me,’ etc.

entries.farsi: بشنو اكنون داد مهمان جديد / من همى‏ديدم كه او خواهد رسيد
entries.english: Now hear the bounty for my new guest. I foresaw that he would arrive,

entries.farsi: كه وفاى وام او هستند و بيش / ‏تا كه ضيفم را نگردد سينه ريش
entries.english: And I had heard the news of his debt; I packed up two or three jewels for him,

entries.farsi: من شنوده بودم از وامش خبر / بسته بهر او دو سه پاره گهر
entries.english: Which are the full payment of his debt, and more: in order that the heart of my guest should not be wounded.

entries.farsi: وام دارد از ذهب او نه هزار / وام را از بعض اين گو برگزار
entries.english: He owes nine thousand of gold: let him discharge his debt with some of these.

entries.farsi: خواستم تا آن به دست خود دهم / ‏در فالن دفتر نبشته است اين قسم
entries.english: There will be a great many of them left over: let him expend and include me too in a benediction.

entries.farsi: فضله ماند زين بسى گو خرج كن / ‏در دعايى گو مرا هم درج كن
entries.english: I wished to give them with my own hand: these assignments are written in such-and-such a note-book.

entries.farsi: خود اجل مهلت ندادم تا كه من / ‏خفيه بسپارم بدو در عدن
entries.english: Death, however, did not allow me time to hand over to him secretly the pearls of Aden.

entries.farsi: لعل و ياقوت است بهر وام او / در خنورى و نبشته نام او
entries.english: Rubies and corundums for his debt are in a certain vessel on which his name is written.

entries.farsi: در فالن طاقيش مدفون كرده‏ام / ‏من غم آن يار پيشين خورده‏ام
entries.english: I have buried it in a certain vault: I have shown solicitude for my ancient friend.

entries.farsi: قيمت آن را نداند جز ملوك / ‏فاجتهد بالبيع ان ال يخدعوك
entries.english: None but kings can know the value of that: take care, then, that they do not cheat you in the sale.

entries.farsi: در بيوع آن كن تو از خوف غرار / كه رسول آموخت سه روز اختيار
entries.english: In sales, for fear of being swindled, behave in the same manner as the Prophet, who taught three days’ option.

entries.farsi: از كساد آن مترس و در ميفت / ‏كه رواج آن نخواهد هيچ خفت
entries.english: Do not be afraid of it depreciating and do not fall, since the demand for it will never decline.

entries.farsi: وارثانم را سالم من بگو / وين وصيت را بگو هم مو به مو
entries.english: Give my heirs a greeting from me and rehearse to them this injunction, point by point,

entries.farsi: تا ز بسيارى آن زر نشكهند / بى‏گرانى پيش آن مهمان نهند
entries.english: In order that they may not be deterred by the largeness of the gold, but may deliver it to that guest without reluctance.

entries.farsi: ور بگويد او نخواهم اين فره / ‏گو بگير و هر كه را خواهى بده
entries.english: And if he says that he does not want so much, bid him take it and bestow it on whom he will.

entries.farsi: ز آن چه دادم باز ستانم نفير / سوى پستان باز نايد هيچ شير
entries.english: I will not take back a speck of what I have given: the milk never comes back to the teat.

entries.farsi: هر كه آن جا بگذرد زر مى‏برد / نيست هديه‏ى مخلصان را مسترد
entries.english: According to the Prophet’s saying, he who reclaims a gift will have become like a dog devouring his vomit.

entries.farsi: ور ببندد در نبايد آن زرش / ‏تا بريزند آن عطا را بر درش
entries.english: And if he shut the door and has no need of the gold, let them pour the bounty at his door,

entries.farsi: گشته باشد همچو سگ قى را اكول / ‏مسترد نحله بر قول رسول
entries.english: Everyone who passes may carry gold away: the gifts of the sincere are never taken back.

entries.farsi: بهر او بنهاده‏ام آن از دو سال / ‏كرده‏ام من نذرها با ذو الجلال
entries.english: I laid it in store for him two years ago and vowed to the Almighty.

entries.farsi: ور روا دارند چيزى ز آن ستد / بيست چندان خود زيانشان اوفتد
entries.english: And if they deem it permissible to take aught, truly twentyfold loss will befall them.

entries.farsi: گر روانم را پژوالنند زود / صد در محنت بر ايشان بر گشود
entries.english: If they vex my spirit, a hundred doors of tribulation will at once be opened for them.

entries.farsi: از خدا اوميد دارم من لبق / ‏كه رساند حق را در مستحق
entries.english: I have good hope of God that He will cause the due to reach the person who has the right to it.”

entries.farsi: دو قضيه‏ى ديگر او را شرح داد / لب به ذكر آن نخواهم بر گشاد
entries.english: He unfolded two other matters to him, I will not open my lips in mention of them,

entries.farsi: تا بماند دو قضيه سر و راز / هم نگردد مثنوى چندين دراز
entries.english: In order that two matters may remain secret and mysterious, and also that the Masnavi may not become so very long.

entries.farsi: بر جهيد از خواب انگشتك زنان / ‏گه غزل گويان و گه نوحه كنان
entries.english: He sprang up from sleep, snapping his fingers, now singing love-songs and now making lament.

entries.farsi: گفت مهمان در چه سوداهاستى / ‏پاى مردا مست و خوش بر خاستى
entries.english: The guest said, “In what mad fits are you? O bailiff, you have risen intoxicated and merry.

entries.farsi: تا چه ديدى خواب دوش اى بو العال / كه نمى‏گنجى تو در شهر و فال
entries.english: I wonder what you dreamed last night, O exalted one, that you cannot be contained in city or desert.

entries.farsi: خواب ديده پيل تو هندوستان / ‏كه رميده‏ستى ز حلقه‏ى دوستان
entries.english: Your elephant has dreamed of Hindustan, for you have fled from the circle of your friends.”

entries.farsi: گفت سوداناك خوابى ديده‏ام / ‏در دل خود آفتابى ديده‏ام
entries.english: He replied, “I have dreamed a mad dream: I have beheld a sun in my heart.

entries.farsi: خواب ديدم خواجه‏ى بيدار را / آن سپرده جان پى ديدار را
entries.english: In my dream I saw the wakeful Khwaja, who gave up his life for vision.

entries.farsi: خواب ديدم خواجه‏ى معطى المنى / ‏واحد كالالف ان امر عنى
entries.english: In my dream I saw the Khwaja, the giver of things desired, one man like a thousand if any affair happened.”

entries.farsi: مست و بى‏خود اين چنين بر مى‏شمرد / تا كه مستى عقل و هوشش را ببرد
entries.english: Drunk and beside himself, he continued to recount in this fashion till intoxication bereft him of reason and consciousness.

entries.farsi: در ميان خانه افتاد او دراز / خلق انبه گرد او آمد فراز
entries.english: He fell at full length in the middle of the room: a crowd of people gathered round him.

entries.farsi: با خود آمد گفت اى بحر خوشى / ‏اى نهاده هوشها در بى‏هشى
entries.english: When he came to himself, he said, “O Sea of bliss, O You who has stored forms of consciousness in unconsciousness,

entries.farsi: خواب در بنهاده اى بيداريى / ‏بسته‏اى در بى‏دلى دل داريى
entries.english: You have stored wakefulness in sleep; You have fastened a dominion over the heart to the state of one who has lost his heart.

entries.farsi: توانگرى پنهان كنى در ذل فقر / طوق دولت بسته اندر غل فقر
entries.english: You conceal riches in the lowliness of poverty; You fasten the necklace of wealth to the iron collar of poverty.”

entries.farsi: روضه اندر آتش نمرود درج / ‏دخلها رويان شده از بذل و خرج
entries.english: Contrary is secretly enclosed in contrary: fire is enclosed in boiling water.

entries.farsi: ضد اندر ضد پنهان مندرج / ‏آتش اندر آب سوزان مندرج
entries.english: A garden is enclosed in Nimrod’s fire: revenues grow from giving and spending;

entries.farsi: تا بگفته مصطفى شاه نجاح / ‏السماح يا اولى النعمى رباح
entries.english: So that Mustafá, the King of prosperity, has said, “O possessors of wealth, munificence are a gainful trade.”

entries.farsi: جوشش و افزونى زر در زكات / ‏عصمت از فحشا و منكر در صالت
entries.english: Riches were never diminished by alms-giving: in truth, acts of charity are an excellent means of attaching to one’s self.

entries.farsi: ما نقص مال من الصدقات قط / انما الخيرات نعم المرتبط
entries.english: In the poor-tax is the overflow and increase of gold: in the ritual prayer is preservation from lewdness and iniquity.

entries.farsi: آن زكاتت كيسه‏ات را پاسبان / ‏و آن صالتت هم ز گرگانت شبان
entries.english: The poor-tax is the keeper of your purse; the ritual prayer is the shepherd who saves you from the wolves.

entries.farsi: ميوه‏ى شيرين نهان در شاخ و برگ / ‏زندگى جاودان در زير مرگ
entries.english: The sweet fruit is hidden in boughs and leaves: the everlasting life is under death.

entries.farsi: زبل گشته قوت خاك از شيوه‏اى / ‏ز آن غذا زاده زمين را ميوه‏اى
entries.english: Dung, by a certain manner, becomes nutriment for the earth, and by means of that food a fruit is born to the earth.

entries.farsi: در عدم پنهان شده موجوديى / ‏در سرشت ساجدى مسجوديى
entries.english: An existence is concealed in non-existence, adorability in the nature of adoration.

entries.farsi: آهن و سنگ از برونش مظلمى / ‏اندرون نورى و شمع عالمى
entries.english: The steel and flint are dark externally, inwardly a light and a world-illuminating candle.

entries.farsi: درج در خوفى هزاران ايمنى / ‏در سواد چشم چندان روشنى
entries.english: In a single fear are enclosed a thousand securities; in the black of the eye ever so many brilliancies.

entries.farsi: اندرون گاو تن شه زاده‏اى / ‏گنج در ويرانه‏اى بنهاده‏اى
entries.english: Within the cow-like body there is a prince, a treasure deposited in a ruin,

entries.farsi: تا خرى پيرى گريزد ز آن نفيس / ‏گاو بيند شاه نى يعنى بليس
entries.english: To the end that an old ass, Iblís to wit, may flee from that precious and may see the cow and not the king.

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن پادشاه و وصيت كردن او سه پسر خويش را كه در اين سفر در ممالك من فالن جا چنين ترتيب نهيد و فالن جا چنين نواب نصب كنيد اما اللهَّ اللهَّ به فالن قلعه مرويد و گرد آن مگرديد
sections.title_en: Story of the King who enjoined his three sons, saying, “In this journey through my empire

establish certain arrangements in such-and-such a place and appoint certain viceroys in such-and-such a place, but for God’s sake, for God’s sake, do not go to such-and-such a fortress and do not roam around it.”

entries.farsi: بود شاهى شاه را بد سه پسر / هر سه صاحب فطنت و صاحب نظر
entries.english: There was a King, and the King had three sons: all three endowed with sagacity and discernment.

entries.farsi: هر يكى از ديگرى استوده‏تر / در سخا و در وغا و كر و فر
entries.english: Each one more praise worthy than another in generosity and in battle and in exercising royal sway.

entries.farsi: پيش شه شه زادگان استاده جمع / ‏قرة العينان شه همچون سه شمع
entries.english: The princes, the delight of the King’s eye, stood together, like three candles, before the King,

entries.farsi: از ره پنهان ز عينين پسر / مى‏كشيد آبى نخيل آن پدر
entries.english: And the father’s palm-tree was drawing water by a hidden channel from the two fountains of the son.

entries.farsi: تا ز فرزند آب اين چشمه شتاب / ‏مى‏رود سوى رياض مام و باب
entries.english: So long as the water of this fountain is running swiftly from the son towards the gardens of his mother and father,

entries.farsi: تازه مى‏باشد رياض والدين / ‏گشته جارى عينشان زين هر دو عين
entries.english: His parents’ gardens will always be fresh: their fountain is made to flow by both these fountains.

entries.farsi: چون شود چشمه ز بيمارى عليل / ‏خشك گردد برگ و شاخ آن نخيل
entries.english: When from sickness the fountain fails, the leaves and boughs of the palm-tree become withered.

entries.farsi: خشكى نخلش همى‏گويد پديد / كه ز فرزند آن شجر نم مى‏كشيد
entries.english: The withering of his palm-tree tells plainly that the tree was drawing moisture from the son.

entries.farsi: اى بسا كاريز پنهان همچنين / ‏متصل با جانتان يا غافلين
entries.english: How many a hidden conduit is connected in like fashion with your souls, O you heedless ones!

entries.farsi: اى كشيده ز اسمان و از زمين / ‏مايه‏ها تا گشته جسم تو سمين
entries.english: O you who have drawn stocks from heaven and earth, so that your body has grown fat,

entries.farsi: جز نفخت كان ز وهاب آمده‏ست / ‏روح را باش آن دگرها بى‏هده‏ست
entries.english: This is a loan: you need not stuff so much, for you must pay back what you have taken—

entries.farsi: عاريه‏ست اين كم همى بايد فشارد / كانچه بگرفتى همى بايد گزارد
entries.english: Except “I breathed,” for that has come from the Munificent. Cleave to the spirit! The other things are vain.

entries.farsi: بى‏هده نسبت به جان مى‏گويمش / ‏نى به نسبت با صنيع محكمش
entries.english: I call them vain in relation to the spirit, not in relation to His consummate making.

sections.title_fa: بيان استمداد عارف از سرچشمه‏ى حيات ابدى و مستغنى شدن او از استمداد و اجتذاب از چشمه‏هاى آبهاى بى‏وفا كه عالمه ذلك التجافى عن دار الغرور كه آدمى چون بر مددهاى آن چشمه‏ها اعتماد كند در طلب چشمه‏ى باقى دايم سست شود كز عاريه‏ها ترا درى نگشايد كارى ز درون جان تو مى‏بايد به ز آن جويى كه آن ز بيرون آيد يك چشمه‏ى آب از درون خانه

sections.title_en: Explaining that the gnostic seeks replenishment from the Fountainhead of everlasting life and that he is relieved of any need to seek replenishment and draw from the fountains of inconstant water; and the sign thereof is his holding aloof from the abode of delusion; for when a man relies on the replenishments drawn from those fountains, he slackens in his search

for the Fountain everlasting and permanent. “A work done from within your soul is necessary, for no door will be opened to you by things given on loan. A water-spring inside the house is better than an aqueduct that comes from outside.”

entries.farsi: تو ز صد ينبوع شربت مى‏كشى / ‏هر چه ز آن صد كم شود كاهد خوشى
entries.english: How goodly is the Conduit which is the source of things! It makes you independent of these conduits.

entries.farsi: حبذا كاريز اصل چيزها / فارغت آرد از اين كاريزها
entries.english: You are quaffing drink from a hundred fountains: whenever any of those hundred yields less, your pleasure is diminished;

entries.farsi: چون بجوشد از درون چشمه‏ى سنى / ‏ز استراق چشمه‏ها گردى غنى
entries.english: When the sublime Fountain gushes from within, no longer need you steal from the fountains.

entries.farsi: قرة العينت چو ز آب و گل بود / راتبه‏ى اين قره درد دل بود
entries.english: Since your eye is rejoiced by water and earth, heart’s sorrow is the payment for this joy.

entries.farsi: قلعه را چون آب آيد از برون / ‏در زمان امن باشد بر فزون
entries.english: When water comes to a fortress from outside, it is more than enough in times of peace;

entries.farsi: چون كه دشمن گرد آن حلقه كند / تا كه اندر خونشان غرقه كند
entries.english: When the enemy forms a ring round that, in order that he may drown them in blood,

entries.farsi: آب بيرون را ببرند آن سپاه / ‏تا نباشد قلعه را ز آنها پناه
entries.english: The troops cut off the outside water, that the fortress may have no refuge from them.

entries.farsi: آن زمان يك چاه شورى از درون / ‏به ز صد جيحون شيرين از برون
entries.english: At that time a briny well inside is better than a hundred sweet rivers outside.

entries.farsi: قاطع الاسباب و لشكرهاى مرگ / ‏همچو دى آيد به قطع شاخ و برگ
entries.english: The Cutter of cords and the armies of Death come, like December, to cut the boughs and leaves,

entries.farsi: در جهان نبود مددشان از بهار / جز مگر در جان بهار روى يار
entries.english: There is no succour for them in the world from Spring, except perchance the Spring of the Beloved’s face in the soul.

entries.farsi: ز آن لقب شد خاك را دار الغرور / كاو كشد پا را سپس يوم العبور
entries.english: The Earth is entitled “the Abode of delusion” because she draws back her foot on the day of passage.

entries.farsi: پيش از آن بر راست و بر چپ مى‏دويد / كه بچينم درد تو چيزى نچيد
entries.english: Before that she was running right and left, saying, “I will take away your sorrow”; but she never took anything away.

entries.farsi: او بگفتى مر ترا وقت غمان / ‏دور از تو رنج و ده كه در ميان
entries.english: In the hour of anxieties she would say to you, “May pain be far from you, and ten mountains between!”

entries.farsi: چون سپاه رنج آمد بست دم / ‏خود نمى‏گويد ترا من ديده‏ام
entries.english: When the army of Pain arrives, she holds her breath: she will not even say, “I have seen you.”

entries.farsi: حق پى شيطان بدين سان زد مثل / ‏كه ترا در رزم آرد با حيل
entries.english: God made a parable concerning the Devil on this wise: “He leads you into battle by his cunning tricks,

entries.farsi: كه ترا يارى دهم من با توام / ‏در خطرها پيش تو من مى‏دوم
entries.english: Saying, ‘I will give you help, I am beside you, I will run before you in the perils;

entries.farsi: اسپرت باشم گه تير خدنگ / ‏مخلص تو باشم اندر وقت تنگ
entries.english: I will be your shield amidst the arrows of khadang wood; I will be your refuge in the hour of distress;

entries.farsi: جان فداى تو كنم در انتعاش / ‏رستمى شيرى هال مردانه باش
