entries.farsi: پست گفتندى به صد خوف و حذر / راز را غير خدا محرم نبود
entries.english: They were telling each other their secrets allusively in low tones with a hundred fears and precautions.

entries.farsi: آن را جز آسمان هم دم نبود / اصطالحاتى ميان همدگر
entries.english: None but God was the confidant of their secret; their sighs were breathed to Heaven alone.

entries.farsi: داشتندى بهر ايراد خبر / زين لسان الطير عام آموختند
entries.english: They were using certain mystical terms among themselves in order to convey information.

entries.farsi: طمطراق و سرورى اندوختند / صورت آواز مرغ است آن كالم
entries.english: The ordinary have learned this birds’ language and have acquired prestige and authority.

entries.farsi: ‏غافل است از حال مرغان مرد خام / كو سليمانى كه داند لحن طير
entries.english: That terminology is the image of the bird’s voice: the uninitiated man is ignorant of the state of the birds.

entries.farsi: ديو گر چه ملك گيرد هست غير / ديو بر شبه سليمان كرد ايست
entries.english: Where is the Solomon who knows the birds’ song? The demon, though he seizes the kingdom, is an alien.

entries.farsi: ‏علم مكرش هست و علمناش نيست / چون سليمان از خدا بشاش بود
entries.english: The demon in the likeness of Solomon stood: he knows how to deceive, but he does not possess we have been taught.

entries.farsi: منطق الطيرى ز علمناش بود / تو از آن مرغ هوايى فهم كن
entries.english: Inasmuch as Solomon was rejoiced exceedingly by God, he had a birds’ language from we have been taught.

entries.farsi: ‏كه نديده‏ستى طيور من لدن / جاى سيمرغان بود آن سوى قاف
entries.english: Understand that you are a bird of the air because you have not beheld the esoteric birds.

entries.farsi: ‏هر خيالى را نباشد دست باف / جز خيالى را كه ديد آن اتفاق
entries.english: The home of the Simurghs is beyond Qáf: it is not a hand-loom to any imagination,

entries.farsi: ‏آن گهش بعد العيان افتد فراق / نه فراق قطع بهر مصلحت
entries.english: But only to the imagination that beholds it by chance and then, after the vision, is parted—

entries.farsi: آفتاب از برف يك دم در كشد / بهر جان خويش جو از ايشان صالح
entries.english: Not a parting that involves severance, for a wise purpose; for that high estate is secure from every parting.

entries.farsi: ‏كايمن است از هر فراق آن منقبت / بهر استبقاى آن روحى جسد
entries.english: In order to preserve the spiritual body the Sun for a time withdraws from the snow.

entries.farsi: ‏هين مدزدد از حرف ايشان اصطالح / آن زليخا از سپندان تا به عود
entries.english: Seek good for your soul from them: beware, do not steal mystical expressions from their language.

entries.farsi: محرمان را سر آن معلوم كرد / چون بگفتى موم ز آتش نرم شد
entries.english: Zalikha had applied to Joseph the name of everything, from rue-seed to aloes wood.

entries.farsi: نام جمله‏ى چيز يوسف كرده بود / نام او در نامها مكتوم كرد
entries.english: She concealed his name in names and made the inner meaning thereof known to confidants.

entries.farsi: اين بدى كان يار با ما گرم شد / ور بگفتى مه بر آمد بنگريد
entries.english: When she said, “The wax is softened by the fire,” this meant, “My beloved is very fond of me.”

entries.farsi: ور بگفتى سبز شد آن شاخ بيد / ور بگفتى برگها خوش مى‏طپند
entries.english: And if she said, “Look, the moon is risen”; or if she said, “The willow-bough is green”;

entries.farsi: ور بگفتى خوش همى‏سوزد سپند / ور بگفتى گل به بلبل راز گفت
entries.english: Or if she said, “The leaves are quivering mightily”; or if she said, “The rue-seed is burning merrily”;

entries.farsi: ‏ور بگفتى شه سر شهناز گفت / ور بگفتى چه همايون است بخت
entries.english: Or if she said, “The rose has told her secret to the nightingale”; or if she said, “The king has disclosed his passion for Shahnáz”;

entries.farsi: ‏ور بگفتى كه بر افشانيد رخت / ور بگفتى كه سقا آورد آب
entries.english: Or if she said, “How auspicious is Fortune!” or if she said, “Give the furniture a good dusting”;

entries.farsi: ‏ور بگفتى كه بر آمد آفتاب / ور بگفتى دوش ديگى پخته‏اند
entries.english: Or if she said, “The water-carrier has brought the water”; or if she said, “The sun is risen”;

entries.farsi: يا حوائج از پزش يك لخته‏اند / ور بگفتى هست نانها بى‏نمك
entries.english: Or if she said, “Last night they cooked a pot full of food” or “The vegetables are cooked to perfection”;

entries.farsi: ‏ور بگفتى عكس مى‏گردد فلك / ور بگفتى كه به درد آمد سرم
entries.english: Or if she said, “The loaves have no salt”; or if she said, “The heavenly sphere is going round in the contrary direction”;

entries.farsi: ‏ور بگفتى درد سر شد خوشترم / گر ستودى اعتناق او بدى
entries.english: Or if she said, “My head aches”; or if she said, “My headache is better”—

entries.farsi: ‏ور نكوهيدى فراق او بدى / صد هزاران نام گر بر هم زدى
entries.english: If she praised, it was his caresses; and if she blamed, it was separation from him.

entries.farsi: ‏قصد او و خواه او يوسف بدى / گرسنه بودى چو گفتى نام او
entries.english: If she piled up a hundred thousand names, her meaning and intention was always Joseph.

entries.farsi: مى‏شدى او سير و مست جام او / تشنگيش از نام او ساكن شدى
entries.english: Were she hungry, as soon as she spoke his name she would be filled and intoxicated by his cup.

entries.farsi: درد او در حال گشتى سودمند / وقت سرما بودى او را پوستين
entries.english: Her thirst would be quenched by his name: the name of Joseph was a sherbet to her soul;

entries.farsi: ‏نام يوسف شربت باطن شدى / ور بدى درديش ز آن نام بلند
entries.english: And if she were in pain, her pain would immediately be turned into profit by that exalted name.

entries.farsi: ‏اين كند در عشق نام دوست اين / عام مى‏خوانند هر دم نام پاك
entries.english: In cold weather it was a fur to her. This, the Beloved’s name can do in love.

entries.farsi: ‏اين عمل نكند چو نبود عشقناك / آن چه عيسى كرده بود از نام هو
entries.english: The ordinary are always pronouncing the Holy Name, it does not do this work since they are not endowed with love.

entries.farsi: مى‏شدى پيدا و را از نام او / چون كه با حق متصل گرديد جان
entries.english: That which Jesus had wrought by the Name of Hu was manifested to her through the name of him.

entries.farsi: ‏ذكر آن اين است و ذكر اينست آن / خالى از خود بود و پر از عشق دوست
entries.english: When the soul has been united with God, to speak of that is this, and to speak of this is that.

entries.farsi: ‏پس ز كوزه آن تالبد كه در اوست / خنده بوى زعفران وصل داد
entries.english: She was empty of self and filled with love for her friend, and, “A pot drips what is in it.”

entries.farsi: گريه بوهاى پياز آن بعاد / هر يكى را هست در دل صد مراد
entries.english: The scent of the saffron of union produces laughter; the smell of the onion of absence tears.

entries.farsi: اين نباشد مذهب عشق و وداد / يار آمد عشق را روز آفتاب
entries.english: Everyone has in his heart a hundred objects of desire; this is not the way of love and fondness.

entries.farsi: ‏آفتاب آن روى را همچون نقاب / آن كه نشناسد نقاب از روى يار
entries.english: Love’s sun in the day-time is the Beloved: the sun is as a veil over that Face.

entries.farsi: عابد الشمس است دست از وى بدار / روز او و روزى عاشق هم او
entries.english: He that does not know the veil from the Face of the Beloved is a sun-worshipper: keep your hand off from him.

entries.farsi: دل همو دل سوزى عاشق هم او / ماهيان را نقد شد از عين آب
entries.english: He is both the lover’s day and daily bread; He is both the lover’s heart and heart-burning.

entries.farsi: ‏نان و آب و جامه و دارو و خواب / همچو طفل است او ز پستان شير گير
entries.english: Fish receive directly from the Essence of the Water their bread and water and clothes and drugs and sleep.

entries.farsi: او نداند در دو عالم غير شير / طفل داند هم نداند شير را
entries.english: He is like a child getting milk from the breast: he knows nothing in the two worlds except the milk.

entries.farsi: راه نبود اين طرف تدبير را / گيج كرد اين گرد نامه روح را
entries.english: The child knows the milk and yet he does not know it: consideration has no means of entrance here.

entries.farsi: تا بيابد فاتح و مفتوح را / گيج نبود در روش بلك اندر او
entries.english: This circular made the spirit crazy to find the Opener and that which is opened.

entries.farsi: حاملش دريا بود نه سيل و جو / چون بيابد او كه يابد گم شود
entries.english: It is not crazy in going; nay, it is the Sea within it that bears it along, not a torrent or a river.

entries.farsi: همچو سيلى غرقه‏ى قلزم شود / دانه گم شد آنگهى او تين بود
entries.english: How should it find? He that finds becomes lost: like a torrent he is absorbed in the Ocean.

entries.farsi: تا نمردى زر ندادم اين بود / آن بزرگين گفت اى اخوان من
entries.english: The seed is lost: then does it become a fig-tree. This is “I did not give the money till you died.”

sections.title_fa: بعد مكث ايشان متوارى در بالد چين در شهر تخت گاه و بعد دراز شدن صبر، بى‏صبر شدن آن ،بزرگين كه من رفتم الوداع خود را بر شاه عرضه كنم ،اما قدمى تنيلنى مقصودى او القى راسى كفوادى ثمه يا سر بنهم همچو دل از دست آن جا يا پاى رساندم به مقصود و مراد ،و نصيحت برادران او را سود ناداشتن اضلها اللهَّ كيف ترشدها يا عاذل العاشقين دع فئه ‏الى آخره

sections.title_en: How, after they had stayed in hiding and tarried patiently for a long while in the capital of China, where the Emperor was enthroned, the eldest lost patience and said, “Farewell! I will go and present myself to the King. Either my feet will bring me to the object of my quest, or I will lose my head there as my heart”— “Either my feet will bring me to the object of my quest and desire, or I will give away

my head there as my heart”— and how the good advice of his brothers was of no avail. “O you that chide those in love, let them alone! How should you direct a band which God has led astray?” And so forth.

entries.farsi: مر مرا اين صبر در آتش نشاند / طاقت من زين صبورى طاق شد
entries.english: The eldest said, “O my brother, from waiting this soul of mine is on the verge.

entries.farsi: ‏ز انتظار آمد به لب اين جان من / الابالى گشته‏ام صبرم نماند
entries.english: I have become reckless, I can endure no more: this endurance has set me on fire.

entries.farsi: واقعه‏ى من عبرت عشاق شد / من ز جان سير آمدم اندر فراق
entries.english: My strength is exhausted by this fortitude: my plight is a warning to lovers.

entries.farsi: سر ببر تا عشق سر بخشد مرا / دين من از عشق زنده بودن است
entries.english: I am weary of my life in separation: it is hypocrisy to be alive in separation.

entries.farsi: ‏زنده بودن در فراق آمد نفاق / چند درد فرقتش بكشد مرا
entries.english: How long will the anguish of separation from her be killing me? Cut off my head, in order that Love may give me a head.

entries.farsi: ‏زندگى زين جان و سر ننگ من است / تيغ هست از جان عاشق گرد روب
entries.english: My religion is, to be alive by Love: life from this soul and head is a disgrace to me.

entries.farsi: ‏ز انكه سيف افتاد محاء الذنوب / چون غبار تن بشد ماهم بتافت
entries.english: The sword sweeps the dust away from the lover’s soul, because the sword is a wiper-out of sins.

entries.farsi: ‏ماه جان من هواى صاف يافت / عمرها بر طبل عشقت اى صنم
entries.english: When the bodily dust is gone, my moon shines: my spirit’s moon finds a clear sky.

entries.farsi: ‏ان فى موتى حياتى مى‏زنم / دعوى مرغ آبيى كردست جان
entries.english: For ages, O adored one, I have been beating the drum of love for you ‘Lo, my life depends on my dying.’

entries.farsi: ‏كى ز طوفان بال دارد فغان / بط را ز اشكستن كشتى چه غم
entries.english: My spirit has boasted that it is a water-bird: how should it lament the flood of tribulation?

entries.farsi: ‏كشتى‏اش بر آب بس باشد قدم / زنده زين دعوى بود جان و تنم
entries.english: What cares the duck for shipwreck? Her feet in the water are ship enough.

entries.farsi: ‏من از اين دعوى چگونه تن زنم / خواب مى‏بينم ولى در خواب نه
entries.english: My soul and body are alive by this boast: how should I refrain from making this boast?

entries.farsi: ‏مدعى هستم ولى كذاب نه / گر مرا صد بار تو گردن زنى
entries.english: I am dreaming but I am not asleep; I am a boaster but I am not a liar.

entries.farsi: ‏همچو شمعم بر فروزم روشنى / آتش ار خرمن بگيرد پيش و پس
entries.english: Though you behead me a hundred times, I am like a candle: I will burn brightly.

entries.farsi: ‏شب روان را خرمن آن ماه بس / كرده يوسف را نهان و مختبى
entries.english: Though the stack catches fire in front and behind, the stack of that Moon is enough for travellers in the night.

entries.farsi: ‏حيلت اخوان ز يعقوب نبى / خفيه كردندش به حيلت سازيى
entries.english: Joseph was hidden and concealed from Jacob the prophet by the trickery of his brethren.

entries.farsi: ‏كرد آخر پيرهن غمازيى / آن دو گفتندش نصيحت در سمر
entries.english: They put him out of sight by an artifice; at last his shirt gave information.”

entries.farsi: كه مكن ز اخطار خود را بى‏خبر / هين منه بر ريشهاى ما نمك
entries.english: The two admonished him in converse, saying, “Do not ignore the dangers.

entries.farsi: ‏هين مخور اين زهر بر جلدى و شك / جز به تدبير يكى شيخى خبير
entries.english: Listen, do not put salt on our wounds! Beware; do not drink this poison rashly and in doubt.

entries.farsi: چون روى چون نبودت قلبى بصير / واى آن مرغى كه ناروييده پر
entries.english: How can you go without being counselled by a wise Shaykh, since you have not a discerning heart?

entries.farsi: بر پرد در اوج و افتد در خطر / عقل باشد مرد را بال و پرى
entries.english: Woe to the unfledged bird that flies up to the zenith and falls into peril!”

entries.farsi: ‏چون ندارد عقل عقل رهبرى / يا مظفر يا مظفر جوى باش
entries.english: Intelligence is wings and feathers to a man: when he lacks intelligence, the intelligence of a guide.

entries.farsi: ‏يا نظرور يا نظرور جوى باش / بى‏ز مفتاح خرد اين قرع باب
entries.english: Either be victorious or in search of a victor: either have insight or be in search of one endowed with insight.

entries.farsi: و ز جراحتهاى هم رنگ دوا / مار استاده‏ست بر سينه چو مرگ
entries.english: Without the key, namely, intelligence, this knocking at the door is prompted by self-will, not by right motives.

entries.farsi: ‏از هوا باشد نه از روى صواب / عالمى در دام مى‏بين از هوا
entries.english: See a whole world ensnared by self-will and by wounds that look like remedies.

entries.farsi: ‏در دهانش بهر صيد اشگرف برگ / در حشايش چون حشيشى او به پاست
entries.english: The snake, as death, stands on its breast, with a big leaf in its mouth in order to catch its prey.

entries.farsi: ‏مرغ پندارد كه او شاخ گياست / چون نشيند بهر خور بر روى برگ
entries.english: It stands erect, like an herb, amidst the herbage, the bird thinks it is the stalk of a plant.

entries.farsi: ‏در فتد اندر دهان مار و مرگ / كرده تمساحى دهان خويش باز
entries.english: When it settles on the leaf for the purpose of eating, it falls into the mouth of the snake and death.

entries.farsi: گرد دندانهاش كرمان دراز / از بقيه‏ى خور كه در دندانش ماند
entries.english: A crocodile opens its mouth: its teeth are surrounded by long worms.

entries.farsi: كرمها روييد و بر دندان نشاند / مرغكان بينند كرم و قوت را
entries.english: The worms were produced by the residue of food left in its teeth; and it gave them lodging there.

entries.farsi: مرج پندارند آن تابوت را / چون دهان پر شد ز مرغ او ناگهان
entries.english: The little birds see the worms and the food and imagine that coffin to be a meadow.

entries.farsi: ‏در كشدشان و فرو بندد دهان / اين جهان پر ز نقل و پر ز نان
entries.english: When its mouth is filled with birds, it suddenly swallows them and closes its mouth.

entries.farsi: ‏چون دهان باز آن تمساح دان / بهر كرم و طعمه‏اى روزى تراش
entries.english: Know that this world full of dessert and bread is like the open mouth of the crocodile.

entries.farsi: ‏از فن تمساح دهر ايمن مباش / روبه افتد پهن اندر زير خاك
entries.english: O you who scrape together the means of livelihood, for worms and morsels do not feel secure from the artfulness of the crocodile, Time.

entries.farsi: ‏بر سر خاكش حبوب مكرناك / تا بيايد زاغ غافل سوى آن
entries.english: A fox falls flat under his earth: above his earth are deceptive grains,

entries.farsi: ‏پاى او گيرد به مكر آن مكر دان / صد هزاران مكر در حيوان چو هست
entries.english: In order that the heedless crow may approach them and the crafty one cunningly seize her by the leg.

entries.farsi: ‏چون بود مكر بشر كاو مهتر است / مصحفى در كف چو زين العابدين
entries.english: Since there are a hundred thousand cunning tricks in animals, how must be the cunning of Man who is superior!

entries.farsi: ‏خنجرى پر قهر اندر آستين / گويدت خندان كه اى موالى من
entries.english: In his hand a copy of the Holy Book as Zaynu ’l- Abidin; in his sleeve a vengeful dagger.

entries.farsi: ‏در دل او بابلى پر سحر و فن / زهر قاتل صورتش شهد است و شير
entries.english: He addresses you smilingly—“O my lord,” in his heart there is a Babylon of sorcery and guileful spells.

entries.farsi: هين مرو بى‏صحبت پير خبير / جمله لذات هوا مكر است و زرق
entries.english: Deadly poison, in appearance he is honey and milk. Beware, do not go save in company with a wise preceptor.

entries.farsi: ‏سور تاريكى است گرد نور برق / برق نور كوته و كذب و مجاز
entries.english: All selfish pleasures are a deceit and fraud: round the lightning-flash is a wall of darkness.

entries.farsi: گرد او ظلمات و راه تو دراز / نه به نورش نامه تانى خواندن
entries.english: The lightning is a brief gleam, false and fleeting, surrounded by darkness; and your way is long.

entries.farsi: ‏نه به منزل اسب دانى راندن / ليك جرم آن كه باشى رهن برق
entries.english: By its light you can neither read a letter nor ride to your destination.

entries.farsi: ‏از تو رو اندر كشد انوار شرق / مى‏كشاند مكر برقت بى‏دليل
entries.english: But, as a penalty for your being enthralled by the lightning, the beams of sunrise withdraw themselves from you.

entries.farsi: ‏در مفازه‏ى مظلمى شب ميل ميل / بر كه افتى گاه و در جوى اوفتى
entries.english: Mile after mile through the night the lightning’s deception leads you on, without a guide, in a dark wilderness.

entries.farsi: ‏گه بدين سو گه بدان سوى اوفتى / خود نبينى تو دليل اى جاه جو
entries.english: Now you fall on a mountain, now into a river; now you wander in this direction, now in that.

entries.farsi: ور ببينى رو بگردانى از او / كه سفر كردم در اين ره شصت ميل
entries.english: O seeker of worldly estate, you will never find the guide; and if you find him, you will avert your face from him,

entries.farsi: ‏مر مرا گمراه گويد اين دليل / گر نهم من گوش سوى اين شگفت
entries.english: Saying, “I have travelled sixty miles on this road, and this guide tells me I have lost my way.

entries.farsi: ‏ز امر او را هم ز سر بايد گرفت / من در اين ره عمر خود كردم گرو
entries.english: If I give ear to this marvel, I must begin my journey again under his authority.

entries.farsi: هر چه بادا باد اى خواجه برو / راه كردى ليك در ظن چو برق
entries.english: I have devoted my life to this journey: come what may. Begone, O Khwaja!”

entries.farsi: ‏عشر آن ره كن پى وحى چو شرق / ظن ال يغنى من الحق خوانده‏اى
entries.english: You have journeyed, but in opinion as lightning: make a tenth part of that journey for the sake of inspiration as the sunrise.

entries.farsi: يا تو آن كشتى بر اين كشتى ببند / گويد او چون ترك گيرم گير و دار
entries.english: You have read, Opinion cannot serve instead of truth, and by a lightning-flash like that you have been blinded to a rising sun. Listen, come into our boat, O wretched man, or tie that boat to this boat.”

entries.farsi: ‏و ز چنان برقى ز شرقى مانده‏اى / هى در آ در كشتى ما اى نژند
entries.english: He replies, “How should I abandon power and dominion? How should I follow you blindly?”

entries.farsi: چون روم من در طفيلت كوروار / كور با رهبر به از تنها يقين
entries.english: A blind man is certainly better off with a guide than alone: in the former case there is one ignominy, while in the latter there are a hundred.

entries.farsi: ‏ز ان يكى ننگ است و صد ننگ است از اين / مى‏گريزى از پشه در كژدمى
entries.english: You are fleeing from a gnat to a scorpion; you are fleeing from a dewdrop into an ocean.

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏گريزى در يمى تو از نمى / مى‏گريزى از جفاهاى پدر
entries.english: You are fleeing from your father’s unkindness into the midst of scoundrels and mischief and trouble.

entries.farsi: در ميان لوطيان و شور و شر / مى‏گريزى همچو يوسف ز اندهى
entries.english: Like Joseph, you are fleeing from one sorrow to fall into a well through “let us frolic and play.”

entries.farsi: ‏تا ز نرتع نلعب افتى در چهى / در چه افتى زين تفرج همچو او
entries.english: Because of this pastime you fall into a well, like him; but where is the favour to help you?

entries.farsi: مر ترا ليك آن عنايت يار كو / گر نبودى آن به دستورى پدر
entries.english: Had it not been by his father’s leave, he would never have emerged from the well till the Resurrection;

entries.farsi: بر نياوردى ز چه تا حشر سر / آن پدر بهر دل او اذن داد
entries.english: In order to please him his father gave the permission and said, “Since this is your desire, may good come!”

entries.farsi: گفت چون اين است ميلت خير باد / هر ضريرى كز مسيحى سر كشد
entries.english: Any blind man who turns away in scorn from a Messiah will be left, like the Jews, without guidance;

entries.farsi: او جهودانه بماند از رشد / قابل ضو بود اگر چه كور بود
entries.english: Though he was blind, he was capable of receiving light; from showing this aversion he becomes blind and blue.

entries.farsi: شد از اين اعراض او كور و كبود / گويدش عيسى بزن در من دو دست
entries.english: Jesus says to him, “O blind man, cling to me with both hands: I have a precious eye salve.

entries.farsi: ‏اى عمى كحل عزيزى با من است / از من ار كورى بيابى روشنى
entries.english: If you are blind, you will obtain light from me and lay hold of the Joseph’s shirt of the spirit.”

entries.farsi: ‏اندر آن اقبال و منهاج ره است / كار و بارى كه ندارد پا و سر
entries.english: The fortune and highway lies in the business that comes to you after utter defeat.

entries.farsi: ترك كن هى پير خر اى پير خر / غير پير استاد و سر لشكر مباد
entries.english: Give up the business that has no foot or head: listen, old donkey, get for yourself a Pir!

entries.farsi: پير گردون نى ولى پير رشاد / در زمان چون پير را شد زير دست
entries.english: May none but the Pir be master and captain!— not the Pir of the rolling sky, but the Pir of right guidance.

entries.farsi: ‏روشنايى ديد آن ظلمت پرست / شرط تسليم است نه كار دراز
entries.english: The devotee of darkness sees the light immediately as soon as he becomes subject to the Pir.

entries.farsi: سود نبود در ضاللت ترك تاز / من نجويم زين سپس راه اثير
entries.english: What is required is self-surrender, not long toil: it is useless to rush about in error.

entries.farsi: پير جويم پير جويم پير پير / پير باشد نردبان آسمان
entries.english: Henceforth I will not seek the way to the Ether: I will seek the Pir, I will seek the Pir, the Pir, the Pir!

entries.farsi: ‏تير پران از كه گردد از كمان / نه ز ابراهيم نمرود گران
entries.english: The Pir is the ladder to Heaven: by whom is the arrow made to fly? By the bow.

entries.farsi: ‏كرد با كركس سفر بر آسمان / از هوا شد سوى بالا او بسى
entries.english: Was it not Abraham that caused the gross Nimrod to journey to heaven by means of the vulture?

entries.farsi: ‏ليك بر گردون نپرد كركسى / گفتش ابراهيم اى مرد سفر
entries.english: By self-will, he often went upward; but no vulture can fly to heaven.

entries.farsi: كركست من باشم اينت خوبتر / چون ز من سازى ببالا نردبان
entries.english: Abraham said to him, “O traveller, I will be your vulture: this is more seemly for you.

entries.farsi: ‏بى‏پريدن بر روى بر آسمان / آن چنان كه مى‏رود تا غرب و شرق
entries.english: When you make of me a ladder to go aloft, you will ascend to heaven without flying”—

entries.farsi: ‏بى‏ز زاد و راحله دل همچو برق / آن چنان كه مى‏رود شب ز اغتراب
entries.english: As the heart, without provisions or riding-camel, travels as lightning to west and east;

entries.farsi: ‏حس مردم شهرها در وقت خواب / آن چنان كه عارف از راه نهان
entries.english: As man’s consciousness, wandering abroad while he is asleep travels during the night to cities;

entries.farsi: ‏خوش نشسته مى‏رود در صد جهان / گر نداده‏ستش چنين رفتار دست
entries.english: As the gnostic, sitting quietly travels by a hidden track through a hundred worlds.

entries.farsi: ‏اين خبرها ز آن واليت از كى است / اين خبرها وين روايات محق
entries.english: If he has not been endowed with power to travel like this, from whom are these reports concerning that country?

entries.farsi: ‏صد هزاران پير بر وى متفق / يك خالفى نى ميان اين عيون
entries.english: Hundreds of thousands of Pirs are agreed upon these reports and these veracious narratives.

entries.farsi: ‏آن چنان كه هست در علم ظنون / آن تحرى آمد اندر ليل تار
entries.english: Amongst these sources there is no dispute, such as there is in knowledge based on opinions.

entries.farsi: وين حضور كعبه و وسط نهار / خيز اى نمرود پر جوى از كسان
entries.english: That is searching in the dark night, while this is the presence of the Ka‘ba and midday.

entries.farsi: ‏نردبانى نايدت زين كركسان / عقل جزوى كركس آمد اى مقل
entries.english: Arise, O Nimrod, and seek wings from personages: you will not get any ladder from these vultures.

entries.farsi: ‏پر او با جيفه خوارى متصل / عقل ابدالان چو پر جبرئيل
entries.english: The vulture is the particular reason, O poor one: its wings are connected with the eating of carrion;

entries.farsi: ‏فارغ از مردارم و كركس نى‏ام / ترك كركس كن كه من باشم كست
entries.english: The reason of the Abdal is like the wings of Gabriel: it soars, mile by mile, up to the shade of the lote-tree.

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏پرد تا ظل سدره ميل ميل / باز سلطانم گشم نيكو پى‏ام
entries.english: “I am a royal falcon, I am fair and auspicious, I have nothing to do with carrion: I am not a vulture.

entries.farsi: بايد استا پيشه را و كسب را / خويشتن رسوا مكن در شهر چين
entries.english: Abandon the vulture, for I will be your helper: a wing of mine is better for you than a hundred vultures.”

entries.farsi: ‏يك پر من بهتر از صد كركست / چند بر عميا دوانى اسب را
entries.english: How long will you gallop blindly? For a trade and business one needs a master.

entries.farsi: ‏عاقلى جو خويش از وى در مچين / آن چه گويد آن فالطون زمان
entries.english: Do not disgrace yourself in the capital of China: seek a sage and do not separate yourself from him.

entries.farsi: ‏هين هوا بگذار و رو بر وفق آن / جمله مى‏گويند اندر چين به جد
entries.english: Listen, whatever the Plato of the age bids you do, give up your self-will and act in accordance with that.

entries.farsi: بهر شاه خويشتن كه لَمْ يلد / شاه ما خود هيچ فرزندى نزاد
entries.english: All in China are saying in zeal for their King, “He begets not.

entries.farsi: بلكه سوى خويش زن را ره نداد / هر كه از شاهان از اين نوعش بگفت
entries.english: Never in truth has our King begotten a child; nay, he has not allowed a woman to approach him.”

entries.farsi: ‏گردنش با تيغ بران كرد جفت / شاه گويد چون كه گفتى اين مقال
entries.english: When any king says of him something of this sort, he weds his neck to the cutting scimitar.

entries.farsi: ‏يا بكن ثابت كه دارم من عيال / مر مرا دختر اگر ثابت كنى
entries.english: The King says, “Since you have spoken these words, either proves that I have a wife and family—

entries.farsi: بر كشم از صوفى جان دلق تو / سر نخواهى برد هيچ از تيغ تو
entries.english: And if you prove that I have a daughter, you are safe from my keen sword—

entries.farsi: ‏يافتى از تيغ تيزم ايمنى / ور نه بى‏شك من ببرم حلق تو
entries.english: Or else without any doubt I will cut your throat: I will tear the mantle off the Sufi, your spirit.

entries.farsi: اى بگفته الف كذب آميغ تو / بنگر اى از جهل گفته ناحقى
entries.english: You will never save your head from the sword, O you that have spoken vain and lying words!

entries.farsi: پر ز سرهاى بريده زين غلو / جمله اندر كار اين دعوى شدند
entries.english: O you that have foolishly spoken an untruth, behold a moat full of severed heads!—

entries.farsi: ‏پر ز سرهاى بريده خندقى / خندقى از قعر خندق تا گلو
entries.english: A moat filled from its bottom to its mouth with heads severed on account of this enormity.

entries.farsi: گردن خود را بدين دعوى زدند / هان ببين اين را به چشم اعتبار
entries.english: All have been sacrificed to this assertion: they have beheaded themselves with this assertion.

entries.farsi: اين چنين دعوى مينديش و ميار / تلخ خواهى كرد بر ما عمر ما
entries.english: Beware! Regard this with a heedful eye: do not conceive or utter such an assertion!”

entries.farsi: كى بر اين مى‏دارد اى دادر ترا / گر رود صد سال آنك آگاه نيست
entries.english: “You will make our lives bitter to us: who is inducing you to this, O brother?

entries.farsi: ‏بر عما آن از حساب راه نيست / بى‏سالحى در مرو در معركه
entries.english: If one who is ignorant should journey a hundred years in blindness that is not reckoned as a journey.

entries.farsi: ‏همچو بى‏باكان مرو در تهلكه / اين همه گفتند و گفت آن ناصبور
entries.english: Do not go into battle unarmed, do not go recklessly into destruction.”

entries.farsi: كه مرا زين گفته‏ها آيد نفور / سينه پر آتش مرا چون منقل است
entries.english: They said all this, but the impatient replied, “These words inspire me with repugnance.

entries.farsi: ‏كشت كامل گشت وقت منجل است / صدر را صبرى بد اكنون آن نماند
entries.english: My bosom is full of fire, like a brazier: the crop is ripe; it is time for the sickle.

entries.farsi: بر مقام صبر عشق آتش نشاند / صبر من مرد آن شبى كه عشق زاد
entries.english: There was fortitude in my breast, now it is no more: Love has set fire to the dwelling-place of fortitude.

entries.farsi: در گذشت او حاضران را عمر باد / اى محدث از خطاب و از خطوب
entries.english: My fortitude died on the night when Love was born: it has passed away— long live those who are present!

entries.farsi: ‏ز آن گذشتم آهن سردى مكوب / سر نگونم هى رها كن پاى من
entries.english: O you that tell of rebuke and punishments, I have passed beyond that: do not beat a piece of cold iron!

entries.farsi: ‏فهم كو در جمله‏ى اجزاى من / اشترم من تا توانم مى‏كشم
entries.english: I am headlong: hey, let go my feet! Where in all my limbs is understanding?

entries.farsi: ‏چون فتادم زار با كشتن خوشم / پر سر مقطوع اگر صد خندق است
entries.english: I am a camel: I carry as long as I can, when I fall down exhausted, I am glad to be killed.

entries.farsi: ‏پيش درد من مزاح مطلق است / من نخواهم زد دگر از خوف و بيم
entries.english: If there are a hundred moats full of severed heads, it is an absolute pleasantry in comparison with my anguish.

entries.farsi: ‏اين چنين طبل هوا زير گليم / من علم اكنون به صحرا مى‏زنم
entries.english: Nevermore in fear and dread will I beat such a drum of passion under a blanket.

entries.farsi: ‏يا سر اندازى و يا روى صنم / حلق كان نبود سزاى آن شراب
entries.english: Now I will plant my banner in the open plain: either to lose my head or the face of my adored one!

entries.farsi: ‏آن بريده به به شمشير و ضراب / ديده كان نبود ز وصلش در فره
entries.english: The throat that is not worthy of that wine— it is best it should be cut by blows of the sword;

entries.farsi: بركنش كه نبود آن بر سر نكو / اندر آن دستى كه نبود آن نصاب
entries.english: The eye that is not in abundance by union with her— such an eye is best white and blind;

entries.farsi: ‏آن چنان ديده سپيد و كور به / گوش كان نبود سزاى راز او
entries.english: The ear that is not worthy of her secret— tear it off, for it is no good on the head;

entries.farsi: ‏آن شكسته به به ساطور قصاب / آن چنان پايى كه از رفتار او
entries.english: The hand in which there is not the amount— it is best that it should be chopped off by the butcher’s knife;

entries.farsi: جان نپيوندد به نرگس‏زار او / آن چنان پا در حديد اوليتر است
entries.english: The foot by whose faring the spirit is not led into her narcissus-plot—

entries.farsi: ‏كانچنان پا عاقبت درد سر است
entries.english: Such a foot is best in iron, for such a foot is ultimately headache.

sections.title_fa: بيان مجاهد كه دست از مجاهده باز ندارد اگر چه داند بسطت عطاى حق را كه آن مقصود از طرف ديگر و به سبب نوع عمل ديگر بدو رساند كه در وهم او نبوده باشد و همه وهم و اميد در اين طريق معين بسته باشد، حلقه‏ى همين در مى‏زند بو كه حق تعالى آن روزى را از در ديگر بدو رساند كه او آن تدبير نكرده باشد، وَ يَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ ال يَحْ تَسِ بُ ، العبد يدبر و اللهَّ يقدر، و بود كه بنده را وهم بندگى
بود كه مرا از غير اين در برساند اگر چه من حلقه‏ى اين در مى‏زنم، حق تعالى او را هم از اين در روزى رساند، فى الجمله اين همه درهاى يك سراى است، مع تقريره
sections.title_en: Setting forth the earnest seeker who does not refrain from exerting himself to the utmost, although he knows that the amplitude of God’s bounty may cause the object of his desire to reach him

from a different quarter and by means of work of a different kind which he has never imagined; but since all his thoughts and hopes are fixed on this particular method, he continues to knock at this same door, maybe God most High will cause his appointed portion to reach him through some other door which he has not foreseen, ‘and will provide for him from a quarter on which he does not reckon’—‘Man proposes but God disposes.’ And, a slave may conceive, as beseems a slave,

that although he keeps knocking at this door he will be supplied from another door; and God most High may cause his portion to reach him through this very door. In short, all these are the doors of one Palace. And the exposition thereof.

entries.farsi: يا درين ره آيدم اين كام من / ‏يا چو باز آيم ز ره سوى وطن
entries.english: Either this desire of mine will be fulfilled on this journey or when I return home from the journey.

entries.farsi: بو كه موقوف است كامم بر سفر / چون سفر كردم بيابم در حضر
entries.english: It may be that my desire depends on going abroad and that after I have gone abroad I shall attain at home.

entries.farsi: يار را چندين بجويم جد و چست / ‏كه بدانم كه نمى‏بايست جست
entries.english: I will seek the Beloved with all my might and energy until I know whether I need not have sought.

entries.farsi: آن معيت كى رود در گوش من / ‏تا نگردم گرد دوران زمن
entries.english: How should His being with me enter my ear unless I wander round the world?

entries.farsi: حق معيت گفت و دل را مهر كرد / تا كه عكس آيد به گوش دل نه طرد
entries.english: How should I apprehend the mystery of His being with me except after long journeys?”

entries.farsi: كى كنم من از معيت فهم راز / جز كه از بعد سفرهاى دراز
entries.english: God has said that He is with us, but He has sealed the heart in order that it may enter the heart’s ear contrariwise, not directly.

entries.farsi: چون سفرها كرد و داد راه داد / بعد از آن مهر از دل او بر گشاد
entries.english: When he has made journeys and performed the duties of the Way, after that the seal is removed from his heart.

entries.farsi: چون خطائين آن حساب با صفا / بعد از آن گويد اگر دانستمى
entries.english: As “the two errors,” the excellent calculation becomes clear to him after two mistakes.

entries.farsi: نايد آن دانش به تيزى فكر / آن چنان كه وجه وام شيخ بود
entries.english: After that, he says, “If I had known this being with God, how should I have searched for Him?

entries.farsi: ‏اين معيت را كى او را جستمى / دانش آن بود موقوف سفر
entries.english: The knowledge thereof depended on journeying: that knowledge is not to be gained by keenness of thought.”

entries.farsi: بسته و موقوف گريه‏ى آن وجود / كودك حلواييى بگريست زار
entries.english: It is just as the payment of the Shaykh’s debts was contingent and dependent on the weeping of that creature.

entries.farsi: توخته شد وام آن شيخ كبار / گفته شد آن داستان معنوى
entries.english: The confectioner’s boy wept bitterly, the debts of the venerable Shaykh were discharged.

entries.farsi: ‏پيش از اين اندر خالل مثنوى / در دلت خوف افكند از موضعى
entries.english: That spiritual tale has already been related in the course of the Masnavi.

entries.farsi: و آن مرادت از كسى ديگر دهد / اى طمع در بسته در يك جاى سخت
entries.english: He puts in your heart the fear of a certain position, in order that no other may be an object of hope to you.

entries.farsi: ‏تا نباشد غير آنت مطمعى / در طمع خود فايده‏ى ديگر نهد
entries.english: To your hope He attaches another advantage; but He grants you your wish from someone else.

entries.farsi: ‏كايدم ميوه از آن عالى درخت / آن طمع ز آن جا نخواهد شد وفا
entries.english: O you who have fixed your hopes firmly on one quarter, saying, “The fruit will come to me from that lofty tree,”

entries.farsi: بل ز جاى ديگر آيد آن عطا / آن طمع را پس چرا در تو نهاد
entries.english: Your hope will not be fulfilled from there; nay, the bounty will come from another place.

entries.farsi: كه مرادم از كجا خواهد رسيد / تا بدانى عجز خويش و جهل خويش
entries.english: Why, then, did He implant in you that hope, since He would not give you the thing from that quarter?

entries.farsi: چون نخواهد ز آن طرف آن چيز داد / از براى حكمتى و صنعتى
entries.english: For a wise purpose and contrivance; and also in order that your heart may be in a state of bewilderment.

entries.farsi: ‏نيز تا باشد دلت در حيرتى / تا دلت حيران بود اى مستفيد
entries.english: That your heart may be bewildered, O learner, from where the object of your desire will come.

entries.farsi: ‏تا شود ايقان تو در غيب بيش / هم دلت حيران بود در منتجع
entries.english: That you may know your weakness and your ignorance and that consequently your faith in the Unseen may be increased;

entries.farsi: ‏كه چه روياند مصرف زين طمع / طمع دارى روزيى در درزيى
entries.english: And, moreover, that your heart may be perplexed concerning the source whence the benefit will arrive, and what the Disposer will produce from this hope.

entries.farsi: كه ز وهمت بود آن مكسب بعيد / پس طمع در درزيى بهر چه بود
entries.english: You hope a means of livelihood in tailoring, so that by working as a tailor you may earn money all your life;

entries.farsi: ‏تا ز خياطى برى زر تا زيى / رزق تو در زرگرى آرد پديد
entries.english: He causes your daily bread to come to you in the goldsmith’s craft— a means of gain that was far from your imagination.

entries.farsi: چون نخواست آن رزق ز آن جانب گشود / بهر نادر حكمتى در علم حق
entries.english: Why, then, were your hopes set on tailoring, when He did not intend to let your daily bread reach you from that side?

entries.farsi: ‏كه نبشت آن حكم را در ما سبق / نيز تا حيران بود انديشه‏ات
entries.english: By reason of a marvellous providence in the knowledge of God— an edict which He wrote in the past;

entries.farsi: ‏تا كه حيرانى بود كل پيشه‏ات / يا وصال يار زين سعيم رسد
entries.english: And also to the end that your thoughts should be bewildered, so that bewilderment should be your whole occupation.

entries.farsi: يا ز راهى خارج از سعى جسد / من نگويم زين طريق آيد مراد
entries.english: “My union with the Beloved will be achieved either by this effort or by some means outside of bodily effort.

entries.farsi: مى‏تپم تا از كجا خواهد گشاد / سر بريده مرغ هر سو مى‏فتد
entries.english: I do not assert that my object will be gained in this way: I am palpitating to ascertain from what quarter it will appear.

entries.farsi: تا كدامين سو رهد جان از جسد / يا مراد من بر آيد زين خروج
entries.english: The decapitated bird tumbles in every direction to see in what direction its spirit may escape from its body.

entries.farsi: ‏يا ز برجى ديگر از ذاتِ البروج
entries.english: My desire will be attained either by this going forth or through some other gateway by Heaven.”

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن شخص كه خواب ديد كه آن چه مى‏طلبى از يسار به مصر وفا شود آن جا گنجى است در فالن محله در فالن خانه چون به مصر آمد كسى گفت من خواب ديده‏ام كه گنجى است به بغداد در فالن محله در فالن خانه نام محله و خانه‏ى اين شخص بگفت آن شخص فهم كرد كه آن گنج در مصر گفتن جهت آن بود كه مرا يقين كنند كه در غير خانه‏ى خود نمى‏بايد جستن و ليكن اين گنج يقين و
محقق جز در مصر حاصل نشود
sections.title_en: Story of the person who dreamed that his hopes of opulence would be fulfilled in Cairo and that there was a treasure in a certain house in a certain quarter of that city. When he came to Cairo, someone said to him, “I have dreamed of a treasure in such and such a quarter and such and such

a house in Baghdad”; and he named the quarter and house in which this person lived. The latter perceived, however, that the information concerning the treasure in Cairo had been given to him in order to make him realize that, he must not seek anywhere but in his own house, this treasure would really and truly be gained only in Cairo.

entries.farsi: بود يك ميراثى مال و عقار / جمله را خورد و بماند او عور و زار
entries.english: There was a man who inherited money and estates: he squandered all and was left destitute and miserable.

entries.farsi: مال ميراثى ندارد خود وفا / چون به ناكام از گذشته شد جدا
entries.english: Inherited wealth indeed does not remain constant, since it was parted against its will from the deceased one.

entries.farsi: او نداند قدر هم كآسان بيافت / ‏كاو به كد و رنج و كسبش كم شتافت
entries.english: Just because he got it easily, he does not know its value; for he never made haste to work and toil and earn it.

entries.farsi: قدر جان ز آن مى‏ندانى اى فالن / ‏كه بدادت حق به بخشش رايگان
entries.english: O such-and-such, you know not the value of your soul because God bountifully gave it to you for nothing.

entries.farsi: نقد رفت و كاله رفت و خانه‏ها / ماند چون جغدان در آن ويرانه‏ها
entries.english: His ready money went and his furniture and houses went: he was left (alone) like owls in the deserts.

entries.farsi: چون تهى شد ياد حق آغاز كرد / يا رب و يا رب اجرنى ساز كرد
entries.english: He cried, “O Lord, You gave provision: the provision is gone: either give some provision or send death.”

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب برگ دادى رفت برگ / ‏يا بده برگى و يا بفرست مرگ
entries.english: When he became empty, he began to call unto God: he started the tune of “O Lord!” and “O Lord, protect me!”

entries.farsi: چون پيمبر گفت مومن مزهر است / ‏در زمان خاليى ناله‏گر است
entries.english: Since the Prophet has said that the true believer is a lute, makes music at the time when it is empty—

entries.farsi: چون شود پر مطربش بنهد ز دست / ‏پر مشو كاسيب دست او خوش است
entries.english: As soon as it is filled, the minstrel lays it down— do not become full, for sweet is the touch of His hand.

entries.farsi: تى شو و خوش باش بين اصبعين / ‏كز مى ال اين سر مست است اين
entries.english: Become empty and stay happily between two fingers, for “where” is intoxicated with the wine of “nowhere.”

entries.farsi: رفت طغيان آب از چشمش گشاد / آب چشمش زرع دين را آب داد
entries.english: Stubbornness departed and released the water from his eye: his tears watered the crops of devotion.

sections.title_fa: ‏سبب تاخير اجابت دعاى مومن
sections.title_en: The reason why the answer to the true believer’s prayer is delayed.

entries.farsi: اى بسا مخلص كه نالد در دعا / تا رود دود خلوصش بر سما
entries.english: Oh, how many a sincere moans in prayer, so that the smoke of his sincerity ascends to Heaven,

entries.farsi: تا رود بالاى اين سقف برين / ‏بوى مجمر از انين المذنبين
entries.english: And from the lamentation of the sinful the perfume of the censer floats up beyond this lofty roof!

entries.farsi: پس ماليك با خدا نالند زار / كاى مجيب هر دعا وى مستجار
entries.english: Then the angels beseech God piteously, saying, “O You who answer every prayer and O You whose protection is invoked,

entries.farsi: بنده‏ى مومن تضرع مى‏كند / او نمى‏داند بجز تو مستند
entries.english: A faithful slave is making humble entreaty: he knows none but You on whom to rely.

entries.farsi: تو عطا بيگانگان را مى‏دهى / ‏از تو دارد آرزو هر مشتهى
entries.english: You bestow Your bounty on strangers: every ardent wisher gains his desire from You.”

entries.farsi: حق بفرمايد كه نز خوارى اوست / ‏عين تاخير عطا يارى اوست
entries.english: God says, “It is not that he is despicable; the very deferment of the bounty is helping him.

entries.farsi: حاجت آوردش ز غفلت سوى من / ‏آن كشيدش موكشان در كوى من
entries.english: Need caused him to turn towards Me from his forgetfulness: it dragged him by the hair into My presence.

entries.farsi: گر بر آرم حاجتش او وا رود / هم در آن بازيچه مستغرق شود
entries.english: If I satisfy his need, he will go back and become absorbed in that idle play.

entries.farsi: گر چه مى‏نالد به جان يا مستجار / دل شكسته سينه خسته گو بزار
entries.english: Although he is crying with his soul, ‘O You whose protection is invoked,’ let him moan with broken heart and wounded breast!

entries.farsi: خوش همى‏آيد مرا آواز او / و آن خدايا گفتن و آن راز او
entries.english: His voice pleases me and his cries of ‘O Lord’ and his secret,

entries.farsi: طوطيان و بلبالن را از پسند / از خوش آوازى قفس در مى‏كنند
entries.english: And how in supplication and pleading he would gladly beguile Me with every sort.”

entries.farsi: و آنكه اندر البه و در ماجرا / مى‏فريباند به هر نوعى مرا
entries.english: Parrots and nightingales are put into cages because they give pleasure by their sweet song;

entries.farsi: زاغ را و جغد را اندر قفص / ‏كى كنند اين خود نيامد در قصص
entries.english: How should crows and owls be caged? This has never been recorded in story.

entries.farsi: پيش شاهدباز چون آيد دو تن / ‏آن يكى كمپير و ديگر خوش ذقن
entries.english: When two persons, one of them a decrepit old man and the other a beardless, come to an admirer of handsome boys,

entries.farsi: هر دو نان خواهند او زوتر فطير / آرد و كمپير را گويد كه گير
entries.english: And both ask for bread, he will at once fetch the unleavened bread and bid the old man take it;

entries.farsi: و آن دگر را كه خوش استش قد و خد / كى دهد نان بل به تاخير افكند
entries.english: But how should he give bread to the other, by whose figure and cheeks he is pleased? Nay, he will delay him

entries.farsi: گويدش بنشين زمانى بى‏گزند / كه به خانه نان تازه مى‏پزند
entries.english: And say to him, “Sit down a while, it will do no harm; for the new bread is baking in the house”;

entries.farsi: چون رسد آن نان گرمش بعد كد / گويدش بنشين كه حلوا مى‏رسد
entries.english: And when, after the work, the hot bread is brought to him, he will say to him, “Sit down, for halwá is coming.”

entries.farsi: هم بدين فن دار دارش مى‏كند / وز ره پنهان شكارش مى‏كند
entries.english: In this same fashion he is always detaining him and seeking covertly to make him his prey,

entries.farsi: كه مرا كارى است با تو يك زمان / ‏منتظر مى‏باش اى خوب جهان
entries.english: Saying, “I have some business to do with you: wait a moment, O beauty of the world!”

entries.farsi: بى‏مرادى مومنان از نيك و بد / تو يقين مى‏دان كه بهر اين بود
entries.english: Know for sure that this is the reason why the true believers suffer disappointment in good or evil.

sections.title_fa: رجوع كردن به قصه‏ى آن شخص كه به او گنج نشان دادند به مصر و بيان تضرع او از درويشى به ‏حضرت حق
sections.title_en: Returning to the Story of the person who was given a clue to the treasure at Cairo,
and setting forth his supplication to God on account of his poverty.

entries.farsi: مرد ميراثى چو خورد و شد فقير / آمد اندر يا رب و گريه و نفير
entries.english: When the man who received the inheritance had squandered it and become a pauper, he began to cry “O Lord!” and weep and lament.

entries.farsi: خود كه كوبد اين در رحمت نثار / كه نيابد در اجابت صد بهار
entries.english: Truly, who shall knock at this Door, from which mercy is showered, without gaining in response a hundred springs?

entries.farsi: خواب ديد او هاتفى گفت او شنيد / كه غناى تو به مصر آيد پديد
entries.english: He dreamed that he heard a Voice from heaven saying, “Your fortune will be found in Cairo;

entries.farsi: رو به مصر آن جا شود كار تو راست / ‏كرد كديه‏ت را قبول او مرتجاست
entries.english: Go to Cairo: there your affair will be set right. He has accepted your humble petition: He is the Object of hope.

entries.farsi: بى‏درنگى هين ز بغداد اى نژند / رو به سوى مصر و منبت‏گاه قند
entries.english: In such-and-such a spot is a great treasure: you must go to Cairo in quest of it.

entries.farsi: در فالن موضع يكى گنجى است زفت / ‏در پى آن بايدت تا مصر رفت
entries.english: Listen, O wretched man, go without any delay from Baghdad to Cairo and the home of sugar-candy.”

entries.farsi: چون ز بغداد آمد او تا سوى مصر / گرم شد پشتش چو ديد او روى مصر
entries.english: When he departed from Baghdad to Cairo, at the sight of Cairo his courage was restored,

entries.farsi: بر اميد وعده‏ى هاتف كه گنج / ‏يابد اندر مصر بهر دفع رنج
entries.english: Hoping for the promise given by the heavenly Voice that he would find in Cairo the treasure to remove his trouble—

entries.farsi: در فالن كوى و فالن موضع دفين / ‏هست گنجى سخت نادر بس گزين
entries.english: “In such and such a quarter and such and such a spot there is a buried treasure exceedingly rare and very choice.”

entries.farsi: ليك نفقه‏ش بيش و كم چيزى نماند / خواست دقى بر عوام الناس راند
entries.english: But of money for expenses, great or small, he had nothing left; and he was about to go and beg from the common folk,

entries.farsi: ليك شرم و همتش دامن گرفت / ‏خويش را در صبر افشردن گرفت
entries.english: But shame and honour held him back; he began to plant himself firmly on fortitude.

entries.farsi: باز نفسش از مجاعت بر طپيد / ز انتجاع و خواستن چاره نديد
entries.english: However, his soul fluttered on account of hunger: he saw no means of escape from foraging and begging.

entries.farsi: گفت شب بيرون روم من نرم نرم / ‏تا ز ظلمت نايدم در كديه شرم
entries.english: “At nightfall,” he said, “I will slip out very quietly, in order that I may beg in the dark without feeling ashamed.

entries.farsi: اندر اين انديشه بيرون شد به كوى / ‏و اندر اين فكرت همى‏شد سو به سوى
entries.english: At night I will chant and bawl like a night-mendicant, that half a dáng may come to me from the roofs.”

entries.farsi: همچو شبكوكى كنم شب ذكر و بانگ / ‏تا رسد از بامهايم نيم دانگ
entries.english: Thus meditating, he went out into the street, and with these thoughts he wandered to and fro.

entries.farsi: يك زمان مانع همى‏شد شرم و جاه / ‏يك زمانى جوع مى‏گفتش بخواه
entries.english: At one moment shame and dignity prevented him, at another moment hunger said to him, “Beg!”

entries.farsi: پاى پيش و پاى پس تا ثلث شب / ‏كه بخواهم يا بخسبم خشك لب
entries.english: Till a third part of the night was gone, one foot forward and one foot backward, “Shall I beg or shall I lie down to sleep with my lips dry?”

sections.title_fa: رسيدن آن شخص به مصر و شب بيرون آمدن به كوى از بهر شبكوكى و گدايى و گرفتن عسس او ٌرا و مراد او حاصل شدن از عسس بعد از خوردن زخم بسيار، وَ عَ سى‏ أَنْ تَكْرَهُوا شَ يْئاً وَ هُوَ خَيْر لَكُمْ، و قوله تعالى سَ يَجْ عَلُ اللهَُّ بَعْدَ عُسْ رٍ يُسْ راً، و قوله تعالى إِنَّ مَعَ الْعُسْ رِ يُسْ راً، و قوله عليه السلام اشتدى أزمة تنفرجى، و جميع القرآن و الكتب المنزلة فى تقرير هذا

sections.title_en: How that person arrived at Cairo and at night came out into the street to play the mendicant and beg, and how he was arrested by the night-patrol and after having been soundly beaten succeeded through him in gaining his object. “And it may be that ye loathe a thing though it is better for you”; and as God most High has said, “God will surely vouchsafe after hardship ease”;

and as God most High has said, “Lo, with hardship goes ease”; and as he, on whom be peace, has said, “O year of drought, become severe, and then you will pass away.” And the whole of the Qur’an and all the Revealed Books confirm this.

entries.farsi: ناگهانى خود عسس او را گرفت / ‏مشت و چوبش زد ز صفرا ناشكفت
entries.english: Suddenly the night-patrol seized him and, unable to restrain his anger, beat him with fist and cudgel.

entries.farsi: اتفاقا اندر آن شبهاى تار / ديده بد مردم ز شب دزدان ضرار
entries.english: As it happened, the people had suffered losses in those dark nights from night-thieves.

entries.farsi: بود شبهاى مخوف و منتحس / ‏پس به جد مى‏جست دزدان را عسس
entries.english: They were nights of alarm and disaster, and the police were searching for the thieves with all their might,

entries.farsi: تا خليفه گفت كه ببريد دست / ‏هر كه شب گردد و گر خويش من است
entries.english: That the Khalífa said, “Cut off the hand of anyone who roams about by night, even if he is a kinsman of mine.”

entries.farsi: بر عسس كرده ملك تهديد و بيم / ‏كه چرا باشيد بر دزدان رحيم
entries.english: The king had terrified the police with threats, saying, “Why are you merciful to the thieves?

entries.farsi: رحم بر دزدان و هر منحوس دست / ‏بر ضعيفان ضربت و بى‏رحمى است
entries.english: For what reason do you believe their blarney or why do you accept gold from them?”

entries.farsi: عشوه‏شان را از چه رو باور كنيد / يا چرا زيشان قبول زر كنيد
entries.english: To show mercy to thieves and any sinister-handed person is to inflict blows and have no mercy on the weak.

entries.farsi: هين ز رنج خاص مسكل ز انتقام / ‏رنج او كم بين ببين تو رنج عام
entries.english: Beware; from sympathy with a particular do not let him go unpunished: do not consider his sufferings, consider the sufferings of the public.

entries.farsi: اصبع ملدوغ بر در دفع شر / در تعدى و هالك تن نگر
entries.english: Amputate the snake-bitten finger to prevent mischief: keep in view the infection and destruction of the body.

entries.farsi: اتفاقا اندر آن ايام دزد / گشته بود انبوه پخته و خام دزد
entries.english: In those days, as it happened, the thieves, both expert and unskilled, had become numerous.

entries.farsi: در چنين وقتش بديد و سخت زد / چوبها و زخمهاى بى‏عدد
entries.english: He saw him at such a time and gave him a sound drubbing and blows without number.

entries.farsi: نعره و فرياد ز آن درويش خاست / ‏كه مزن تا من بگويم حال راست
entries.english: Shrieks and cries for mercy arose from the poor wretch: “Don’t strike! Let me tell the truth about it all!”

entries.farsi: گفت اينك دادمت مهلت بگو / تا به شب چون آمدى بيرون به كو
entries.english: He replied, “Look now, I will give you time: speak, that I may learn how you came out into the streets by night.

entries.farsi: تو نه‏اى زينجا، غريب و منكرى / ‏راستى گو تا به چه مكر اندرى
entries.english: You do not belong to this place; you are a stranger and unknown: tell me truly what you are plotting.

entries.farsi: اهل ديوان بر عسس طعنه زدند / كه چرا دزدان كنون انبه شدند
entries.english: The government officials have attacked the police, asking why there are now such a great number of thieves.

entries.farsi: انبهى از تست و از امثال تست / ‏وا نما ياران زشتت را نخست
entries.english: It is owing to you and the likes of you that they are so numerous: first disclose your wicked associates;

entries.farsi: ور نه كين جمله را از تو كشم / ‏تا شود ايمن زر هر محتشم
entries.english: Otherwise I will exact from you the vengeance incurred by all of them, in order that every respectable person’s money may be safe.”

entries.farsi: گفت او از بعد سوگندان پر / كه نيم من خانه سوز و كيسه بر
entries.english: After taking many oaths he replied, “I am not a housebreaker or cutpurse.

entries.farsi: من نه مرد دزدى و بى‏دادى‏ام / ‏من غريب مصرم و بغدادى‏ام
entries.english: I am no thief and criminal: I am a stranger in Cairo, I belong to Baghdad.”

sections.title_fa: ‏بيان اين خبر كه الكذب ريبه و الصدق طمانينه
sections.title_en: Explaining the Tradition, “Falsehood causes suspicion, while veracity inspires confidence.”

entries.farsi: قصه‏ى آن خواب و گنج زر بگفت / ‏پس ز صدق او دل آن كس شكفت
entries.english: He related the story of his dream and the treasure of gold, and from his veracity the man’s heart expanded.

entries.farsi: بوى صدقش آمد از سوگند او / سوز او پيدا شد و اسپند او
entries.english: From his oaths he scented the truth: in him the combustion and the rue-seed were evident.

entries.farsi: دل بيارامد به گفتار صواب / ‏آن چنان كه تشنه آرامد به آب
entries.english: The heart is comforted by true words, just as a thirsty man is comforted by water—

entries.farsi: جز دل محجوب كاو را علتى است / ‏از نبى‏اش تا غبى تمييز نيست
entries.english: Except the heart of one who is veiled and suffers from a malady, he cannot distinguish between a prophet and a dolt;

entries.farsi: مه شكافد و آن دل محجوب نى / ‏ز انكه مردود است او محبوب نى
entries.english: Or else, the message that is from the place were to descend upon the moon, it would be split asunder.

entries.farsi: ور نه آن پيغام كز موضع بود / بر زند بر مه شكافيده شود
entries.english: The moon would be split, but not the heart of him who is veiled; for he is rejected, he is not beloved.

entries.farsi: يك سخن از دوزخ آيد سوى لب / ‏يك سخن از شهر جان در كوى لب
entries.english: The night-patrol’s eye became a fountain with wetting tears, not from the dry words, nay, but from the fragrance in the heart.

entries.farsi: چشمه شد چشم عسس ز اشك مبل / ‏نى ز گفت خشك بل از بوى دل
entries.english: One word comes to the lips from Hell, one word into the region of the lips from the Spiritual City.

entries.farsi: بحر جان افزا و بحر پر حرج / ‏در ميان هر دو بحر اين لب مرج
entries.english: There is the spirit-increasing sea and the distressful sea: these lips are where the two seas meet.

entries.farsi: كاله‏ى معيوب قلب كيسه بر / كاله‏ى پر سود مستشرف چو در
entries.english: Like a great mart between towns: goods come there from all directions:

entries.farsi: چون يپنلو در ميان شهرها / از نواحى آيد آن جا بهرها
entries.english: Damaged, spurious, and swindling commodities lucrative commodities highly esteemed, like pearls.

entries.farsi: زين يپنلو هر كه بازرگان‏تر است / ‏بر سره و بر قلبها ديده‏ور است
entries.english: The shrewdest traders in this mart inspect the genuine and spurious wares.

entries.farsi: شد يپنلو مر و را دار الرباح / ‏و آن دگر را از عمى دار الجناح
entries.english: To him the mart is a place of gain, while to others in their blindness it is a place of loss.

entries.farsi: بر يكى قند است و بر ديگر چو زهر / بر يكى لطف است و بر ديگر چو قهر
entries.english: Every particle of the world, one by one, is a fetter for the fool and a means of deliverance for the wise.

entries.farsi: هر يكى ز اجزاى عالم يك به يك / ‏بر غبى بند است و بر استاد فك
entries.english: It is candy for one and as poison for another: it is mercy for one and as wrath for another.

entries.farsi: هر جمادى با نبى افسانه‏گو / كعبه با حاجى گواه و نطق خو
entries.english: Every inanimate thing tells a tale to the Prophet: the Ka‘ba testifies to the pilgrim and is eloquent.

entries.farsi: بر مصلى مسجد آمد هم گواه / ‏كاو همى‏آمد به من از دور راه
entries.english: The mosque, too, bears witness to him who performs the ritual prayer, saying, “He came a long way to me.”

entries.farsi: با خليل آتش گل و ريحان و ورد / باز بر نمروديان مرگ است و درد
entries.english: The fire is flowers and sweet basils and roses to Khalíl; to those like Nimrod, on the contrary, it is death and anguish.

entries.farsi: بارها خوردى تو نان دفع ذبول / ‏اين همان نان است چون نبوى ملول
entries.english: We have said this many a time, O Hasan: I will never be weary of setting it forth.

entries.farsi: بارها گفتيم اين را اى حسن / ‏مى‏نگردم از بيانش سير من
entries.english: Many a time have you eaten bread to prevent getting thin: it is the same bread: why are not you surfeited?

entries.farsi: در تو جوعى مى‏رسد نو ز اعتدال / ‏كه همى‏سوزد از او تخمه و مالل
entries.english: In normal health, a new hunger comes to you, by which indigestion and satiety are consumed.

entries.farsi: هر كه را درد مجاعت نقد شد / نو شدن با جزو جزوش عقد شد
entries.english: When one actually feels the pangs of hunger, refreshment is associated with every part.

entries.farsi: لذت از جوع است نه از نقل نو / با مجاعت از شكر به نان جو
entries.english: The pleasure is from hunger, not from new dessert: hunger makes barley-bread more delicious than sugar.

entries.farsi: پس ز بى‏جوعى است و ز تخمه‏ى تمام / ‏آن ماللت نه ز تكرار كالم
entries.english: That weariness, then, is caused by lack of hunger and complete indigestion, not by repetition of the discourse.

entries.farsi: چون ز دكان و مكاس و قيل و قال / ‏در فريب مردمت نايد مالل
entries.english: How is it that you are not weary of your shop and of haggling and disputing in order to cheat people?

entries.farsi: چون ز غيبت و اكل لحم مردمان / ‏شصت سالت سيريى نامد از آن
entries.english: How is it that you have not been surfeited by speaking ill of men in their absence and backbiting them for sixty years?

entries.farsi: عشوه‏ها در صيد شله‏ى كفته تو / بى‏ملولى بارها خوش گفته تو
entries.english: Time after time, without wearying, you have gaily spoken false words of flattery in pursuit of a vile woman;

entries.farsi: بار آخر گويى‏اش سوزان و چست / ‏گرم‏تر صد بار از بار نخست
entries.english: And the last time you utter them with fire and energy, a hundred times more ardently than the first time.

entries.farsi: درد داروى كهن را نو كند / درد هر شاخ ملولى خو كند
entries.english: Passion makes the old medicine new; passion lops every bough of weariness.

entries.farsi: كيمياى نو كننده دردهاست / ‏كو ملولى آن طرف كه درد خاست
entries.english: Passion is the elixir that makes new: how weariness where passion has arisen?

entries.farsi: هين مزن تو از ملولى آه سرد / درد جو و درد جو و درد درد
entries.english: Oh, do not sigh heavily from weariness: seek passion, seek passion, passion, passion!

entries.farsi: آب شورى نيست درمان عطش / ‏وقت خوردن گر نمايد سرد و خوش
entries.english: Vain remedies beguile passion: they are brigands and those who extort money in the form of tolls.

entries.farsi: خادع دردند درمانهاى ژاژ / ره زنند و زرستانان رسم باژ
entries.english: Briny water is no remedy for thirst: if it seems cold and delicious at the moment of drinking,

entries.farsi: ليك خادع گشت و مانع شد ز جست / ‏ز آب شيرينى كز او صد سبزه رست
entries.english: Yet it beguiles and prevents from seeking the sweet water by which a hundred plants are made to grow.

entries.farsi: همچنين هر زر قلبى مانع است / ‏از شناس زر خوش هر جا كه هست
entries.english: Likewise every piece of spurious gold prevents from recognising the good gold wherever it is.

entries.farsi: پا و پرت را به تزويرى بريد / كه مراد تو منم گير اى مريد
entries.english: It cuts off your feet and your wings by imposture, saying, “I am what you seek: take me, O seeker.”

entries.farsi: گفت دردت چينم او خود درد بود / مات بود ار چه به ظاهر برد بود
entries.english: It says, “I will remove your passion,” in truth it is dregs: it is checkmate though it is victory in appearance.

entries.farsi: رو ز درمان دروغين مى‏گريز / تا شود دردت مصيب و مشك بيز
entries.english: Go; always flee from the false remedy, in order that your passion may be successful and rich in perfume.

entries.farsi: گفت نه دزدى تو و نه فاسقى / ‏مرد نيكى ليك گول و احمقى
entries.english: He said, “You are not a thief and you are not a reprobate: you are a good man, but you are foolish and silly.

entries.farsi: بر خيال و خواب چندين ره كنى / ‏نيست عقلت را تسويى روشنى
entries.english: You make such a long journey, on a phantasy and dream: your intelligence has not the least spark of brightness.

entries.farsi: بارها من خواب ديدم مستمر / كه به بغداد است گنجى مستتر
entries.english: I have dreamed many times, continuously, that there is a concealed treasure at Baghdad,

entries.farsi: در فالن سوى و فالن كويى دفين / ‏بود آن خود نام كوى اين حزين
entries.english: Buried in such-and-such a quarter and such-and-such a street” — the name, in fact, was that of the street where this sorrowful man lived.

entries.farsi: ديده‏ام خود بارها اين خواب من / ‏كه به بغداد است گنجى در وطن
entries.english: “It is in so-and-so’s house: go and seek it!”— the enemy named the house and mentioned his name.

entries.farsi: هست در خانه‏ى فالنى رو بجو / نام خانه و نام او گفت آن عدو
entries.english: “I myself have often dreamed that there is a treasure in the dwelling-place at Baghdad.

entries.farsi: خواب احمق اليق عقل وى است / ‏همچو او بى‏قيمت است و الشى است
entries.english: I never left my home on account of this phantasy, you in consequence of a single dream come without thinking of the fatigue.

entries.farsi: هيچ من از جا نرفتم زين خيال / ‏تو به يك خوابى بيايى بى‏مالل
entries.english: The dreams of a fool are suitable to his intelligence: like it, they are worthless and good-for-nothing.

entries.farsi: خواب زن كمتر ز خواب مرد دان / ‏از پى نقصان عقل و ضعف جان
entries.english: Know that a woman’s dreams are inferior to those of a man because of her deficiency of intelligence and weakness of soul.

entries.farsi: خواب ناقص عقل و گول آيد كساد / پس ز بى‏عقلى چه باشد خواب باد
entries.english: The dreams of one deficient in intelligence and foolish are of little value: what, then, must be the dreams produced by lack of intelligence? Wind!”

entries.farsi: گفت با خود گنج در خانه‏ى من است / ‏پس مرا آن جا چه فقر و شيون است
entries.english: He said to himself, “The treasure is in my house: then why am I poverty-stricken and lamenting there?

entries.farsi: بر سر گنج از گدايى مرده‏ام / ‏ز انكه اندر غفلت و در پرده‏ام
entries.english: Living over the treasure, I have died of beggary because I am heedless and blind.”

entries.farsi: زين بشارت مست شد دردش نماند / صد هزار الحمد بى‏لب او بخواند
entries.english: At this good news he was intoxicated: his sorrow vanished, and without lips he chanted a hundred thousand praises to God.

entries.farsi: گفت بد موقوف اين لت لوت من / ‏آب حيوان بود در حانوت من
entries.english: He said, “My food depended on these blows: the Water of life was in my shop.

entries.farsi: رو كه بر لوت شگرفى بر زدم / ‏كورى آن وهم كه مفلس بدم
entries.english: Begone, for I have met with a great piece of fortune, to confound the idea that I was destitute.

entries.farsi: خواه احمق دان مرا خواهى فرو / آن من شد هر چه مى‏خواهى بگو
entries.english: Deem me foolish or contemptible as you please: it is mine, say what you like.

entries.farsi: من مراد خويش ديدم بى‏گمان / ‏هر چه خواهى گو مرا اى بد دهان
entries.english: Beyond doubt I have seen my wish: call me anything you please, O foul-mouthed one!

entries.farsi: تو مرا پر درد گو اى محتشم / ‏پيش تو پر درد و پيش خود خوشم
entries.english: Call me sorrowful, O respected sir: in your view I am sorrowful, but in my view I am happy.

entries.farsi: واى اگر بر عكس بودى اين مطار / پيش تو گلزار و پيش خويش زار
entries.english: Alas, if the case had been reversed a rose-garden in your view and miserable in my own!”

sections.title_en: Parable.

entries.farsi: گفت او گر مى‏نداند عامى‏ام / ‏خويش را من نيك مى‏دانم كى‏ام
entries.english: One day a base fellow said to a dervish, “You are unknown to anyone here.”

entries.farsi: گفت با درويش روزى يك خسى / ‏كه ترا اينجا نمى‏داند كسى
entries.english: He replied, “If the ordinary do not know me, I know very well who I am.

entries.farsi: واى اگر بر عكس بودى درد و ريش / ‏او بدى بيناى من من كور خويش
entries.english: Alas, if the pain and sore had been reversed and he had seen me, while I was blind to myself!”

entries.farsi: احمقم گير احمقم من نيك بخت / ‏بخت بهتر از لجاج و روى سخت
entries.english: “Suppose I am a fool, I am a lucky fool: luck is better than perversity and a hard face.

entries.farsi: اين سخن بر وفق ظنت مى‏جهد / ور نه بختم داد عقلم هم دهد
entries.english: These words express your opinion; for my luck at the same time endows me with all that belongs to intelligence.”

sections.title_fa: باز گشتن آن شخص شادمان و مراد يافته و خداى را شكر گويان و سجده كنان و حيران در غرايب اشارات حق و ظهور تاويالت آن در وجهى كه هيچ عقلى و فهمى بدان جا نرسد
sections.title_en: How that person returned rejoicing and successful and giving thanks to God and prostrating himself and amazed at the wondrous indications vouchsafed by God and the coming to light
of the interpretations thereof in a way that no mind and understanding can conceive.

entries.farsi: باز گشت از مصر تا بغداد او / ساجد و راكع ثناگر شكر گو
entries.english: He returned from Cairo to Baghdad, prostrating himself and bowing and giving praise and thanks.

entries.farsi: جمله ره حيران و مست او زين عجب / ‏ز انعكاس روزى و راه طلب
entries.english: All the way he was bewildered and intoxicated by this marvel, by the complete change as regards his daily bread and the method of seeking,

entries.farsi: كز كجا اوميدوارم كرده بود / و ز كجا افشاند بر من سيم و سود
entries.english: Saying, “Whence did He make me hopeful and whence did He shower money and profit upon me!

entries.farsi: اين چه حكمت بود كه قبله‏ى مراد / كردم از خانه برون گمراه و شاد
entries.english: What wisdom was this, that the Object of desire caused me to go forth from my home gladly on a fool’s errand,

entries.farsi: باز آن عين ضاللت را به جود / حق وسيلت كرد اندر رشد و سود
entries.english: So that I was hastening to lose the way and at every moment was being farther removed from that which I sought—

entries.farsi: تا شتابان در ضاللت مى‏شدم / ‏هر دم از مطلب جداتر مى‏بدم
entries.english: And then God in His munificence made that very aberration the means of reaching the right road and gaining wealth!”

entries.farsi: گمرهى را منهج ايمان كند / كژروى را محصد احسان كند
entries.english: He makes losing the way an avenue to faith; He makes going wrong a field for the harvest of righteousness,

entries.farsi: اندرون زهر ترياق آن حفى / كرد تا گويند ذو اللطف الخفى
entries.english: To the end that no righteous man may be without fear, and that no traitor may be without hope.

entries.farsi: تا نباشد هيچ محسن بى‏وجا / تا نباشد هيچ خاين بى‏رجا
entries.english: The Gracious One has put an antidote in the poison in order that they may say He is the Lord of hidden grace.

entries.farsi: نيست مخفى در نماز آن مكرمت / ‏در گنه خلعت نهد آن مغفرت
entries.english: That bounty is not mysterious in piety; Forgiveness bestows a robe of honour in sin.

entries.farsi: منكران را قصد اذالل ثقات / ‏ذل شده عز و ظهور معجزات
entries.english: The unbelievers sought to abase those who were worthy of trust: abasement became exaltation and miracles being displayed.

entries.farsi: قصدشان ز انكار ذل دين بده / ‏عين ذل عز رسوالن آمده
entries.english: In their unbelief they attempted to abase the religion: that very abasement was turned to glory for the prophets.

entries.farsi: گر نه انكار آمدى از هر بدى / ‏معجزه و برهان چرا نازل شدى
entries.english: Unless every wicked man had shown unbelief, why should evidentiary miracles have appeared?

entries.farsi: خصم منكر تا نشد مصداق خواه / ‏كى كند قاضى تقاضاى گواه
entries.english: How should a judge require evidence until his disbelieving opponent has demanded proof of his veracity?

entries.farsi: معجزه همچون گواه آمد زكى / ‏بهر صدق مدعى در بى‏شكى
entries.english: The miracle is like an honest witness to the indubitable veracity of the claimant.

entries.farsi: طعن چون مى‏آمد از هر ناشناخت / ‏معجزه مى‏داد حق و مى‏نواخت
entries.english: Since they were being attacked by every ignoramus, God bestowed on them the gift of miracles and showed them favour.

entries.farsi: ساحران آورده حاضر نيك و بد / تا كه جرح معجزه‏ى موسى كند
entries.english: The plots of Pharaoh were three-hundredfold: all became his abasement and subjugation.

entries.farsi: مكر آن فرعون سيصد تو بده / ‏جمله ذل او و قمع او شده
entries.english: He brought magicians, good and bad, into his presence in order that he might invalidate the miracles of Moses,

entries.farsi: تا عصا را باطل و رسوا كند / اعتبارش را ز دلها بر كند
entries.english: That he might nullify the rod and put it to shame and remove from hearts the respect for it.

entries.farsi: عين آن مكر آيت موسى شود / اعتبار آن عصا بالا رود
entries.english: Those very plots only serve to manifest the veracity of Moses: the prestige of his rod goes up.

entries.farsi: لشكر آرد او پگه تا حول نيل / ‏تا زند بر موسى و قومش سبيل
entries.english: He leads his army betimes to the neighbourhood of the Nile in order to waylay Moses and his people;

entries.farsi: ايمنى امت موسى شود / او به تحت الارض و هامون در رود
entries.english: it only serves to ensure the safety of the followers of Moses, he goes under the earth and the plain.

entries.farsi: گر به مصر اندر بدى او نامدى / ‏وهم از سبطى كجا زايل شدى
entries.english: If he had stayed in Egypt, he would not have marched: how, would the Israelites have been relieved of dread?

entries.farsi: آمد و در سبط افكند او گداز / كه بدان كه امن در خوف است راز
entries.english: He marched and caused the Israelites to be consumed; know that safety is concealed in danger.

entries.farsi: آن بود لطف خفى كاو را صمد / نار بنمايد خود آن نورى بود
entries.english: The hidden grace consists in this is that the Lord shows unto him a fire, but it is really a light.

entries.farsi: نيست مخفى مزد دادن در تقى / ساحران را اجر بين بعد از خطا
entries.english: There is nothing mysterious in rewarding piety, look at the reward bestowed on the magicians after their sin!

entries.farsi: نيست مخفى وصل اندر پرورش / ‏ساحران را وصل داد او در برش
entries.english: There is nothing mysterious in the favour shown while cherishing, He bestowed His favour on the magicians in the amputation.

entries.farsi: نيست مخفى سير با پاى روا / ساحران را سير بين در قطع پا
entries.english: There is nothing mysterious in journeying with feet that move, but look at the journey of the magicians when their feet had been cut off!

entries.farsi: عارفان ز آنند دايم آمنون / ‏كه گذر كردند از درياى خون
entries.english: The knowers of God are safe for ever because they have passed through a sea of blood.

entries.farsi: امن ديدى گشته در خوفى خفى / ‏خوف بين هم در اميدى اى صفى
entries.english: Safety appeared to them from the very midst of terror; consequently they are always in a state of increase.

entries.farsi: امنشان از عين خوف آمد پديد / الجرم باشند هر دم در مزيد
entries.english: You have seen that safety is concealed in a fear: O excellent man, observe also that fear is in hope.

entries.farsi: آن امير از مكر بر عيسى تند / عيسى اندر خانه رو پنهان كند
entries.english: A certain Amír cunningly shadows Jesus: Jesus hides himself in the house.

entries.farsi: اندر آيد تا شود او تاجدار / خود ز شبه عيسى آيد تاج دار
entries.english: He enters in order that he may wear the crown: because of his likeness to Jesus he himself becomes the crown of the gibbet.

entries.farsi: هى مياويزيد من عيسى نى‏ام / ‏من اميرم بر جهودان خوش پى‏ام
entries.english: “Oh, do not hang me: I am not Jesus, I am the Amír, and I am well-disposed to the Jews.”

entries.farsi: زوترش بر دار آويزيد كاو / عيسى است از دست ما تخليط جو
entries.english: “Hang him on the gibbet,” “with all speed, for he is Jesus: seeking to escape from our hands by personating another.”

entries.farsi: چند لشكر مى‏رود تا بر خورد / برگ او فى گردد و بر سر خورد
entries.english: How often does an army march to enjoy the fruits: its equipment becomes spoil, and it is overthrown?

entries.farsi: چند بازرگان رود بر بوى سود / عيد پندارد بسوزد همچو عود
entries.english: How often does a merchant go in hope of gain: he thinks it will be a feast (‘id), but he is consumed like aloes-wood (‘ud)?

entries.farsi: چند در عالم بود بر عكس اين / ‏زهر پندارد بود آن انگبين
entries.english: How often in the world does it happen contrariwise to this: one fancies poison when it is honey?

entries.farsi: بس سپه بنهاده دل بر مرگ خويش / ‏روشنيها و ظفر آيد به پيش
entries.english: Often, soldiers have made up their minds to die, splendour and victory appear.

entries.farsi: ابرهه با پيل بهر ذل بيت / ‏آمده تا افكند حى را چو ميت
entries.english: Abraha came with the elephant to dishonour the House, that he might throw down the living as though dead,

entries.farsi: تا حريم كعبه را ويران كند / جمله را ز آن جاى سر گردان كند
entries.english: And destroy the holy Ka‘ba and cause all to wander forth from that place,

entries.farsi: تا همه زوار گرد او تنند / كعبه‏ى او را همه قبله كنند
entries.english: In order that all the pilgrims might gather round him and might all turn in worship to his Ka‘ba,

entries.farsi: و ز عرب كينه كشد اندر گزند / كه چرا در كعبه‏ام آتش زنند
entries.english: And that he might take vengeance on the Arabs for the injury, for “why,” said he, “should they set my Ka‘ba on fire?”

entries.farsi: عين سعيش عزت كعبه شده / ‏موجب اعزاز آن بيت آمده
entries.english: His efforts only turned to glory for the Ka‘ba: they caused the House to be glorified.

entries.farsi: او و كعبه‏ى او شده مخسوف‏تر / از چى است اين از عنايات قدر
entries.english: The glory of the Meccans had been one: it became a hundred: their glory was now extending to the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: مكيان را عز يكى بد صد شده / ‏تا قيامت عزشان ممتد شده
entries.english: He and his Ka‘ba were eclipsed more. Whence is this? From the favours of the Decree.

entries.farsi: از جهاز ابرهه‏ى همچون دده / ‏آن فقيران عرب توانگر شده
entries.english: Those poor Arabs were enriched by the equipment and baggage of Abraha, like a wild beast.

entries.farsi: او گمان برده كه لشكر مى‏كشيد / بهر اهل بيت او زر مى‏كشيد
entries.english: He thought that he was bringing an army: he was bringing gold for the defenders of the House.

entries.farsi: اندر اين فسخ عزايم وين همم / ‏در تماشا بود در ره هر قدم
entries.english: He was every step of the way, in contemplating this annulment of fixed purposes and ambitions.

entries.farsi: خانه آمد گنج را او باز يافت / كارش از لطف خدايى ساز يافت
entries.english: He came home, he discovered the treasure: by Divine grace his fortune was restored.

sections.title_fa: مكرر كردن برادران پند دادن بزرگين را و تاب ناآوردن او پند را و در رميدن او از ايشان و شيدا و بى‏خود رفتن و خود را در بارگاه پادشاه انداختن بى‏دستورى خواستن ليك از فرط عشق نه از ‏گستاخى و ال ابالى، الى آخره

sections.title_en: How the brothers repeated their advice to eldest, and how he was unable to endure it and ran away from them and went off, frenzied and beside himself, and rushed into the King’s audience-chamber without asking permission; but from excess of passionate love, not from disrespect and recklessness, etc.

entries.farsi: آن دو گفتندش كه اندر جان ما / هست پاسخها چو نجم اندر سما
entries.english: The two said to him, “In our souls are answers, like stars in the sky.

entries.farsi: گر نگوييم آن نيايد راست نرد / ور بگوييم آن دلت آيد به درد
entries.english: Unless we speak, the game will not come out right; and if we speak, your heart will be grieved.

entries.farsi: همچو چغزيم اندر آب از گفت الم / ‏و ز خموشى اختناق است و سقم
entries.english: We are like frogs in the water: it is painful to speak, while the result of silence is suffocation and illness.

entries.farsi: گر نگوييم آشتى را نور نيست / ‏ور بگوييم آن سخن دستور نيست
entries.english: If we speak not, friendship has no light; and if we speak, it is without leave.”

entries.farsi: در زمان بر جست كاى خويشان وداع / ‏انما الدنيا و ما فيها متاع
entries.english: Straightway he sprang up, crying, “Farewell, O kinsmen: truly this world and all therein is but a passing enjoyment,”

entries.farsi: پس برون جست او چو تيرى از كمان / ‏كه مجال گفت كم بود آن زمان
entries.english: And darted away like an arrow from the bow, so that there was no opportunity to speak at that time.

entries.farsi: اندر آمد مست پيش شاه چين / ‏زود مستانه ببوسيد او زمين
entries.english: He came intoxicated into the presence of the King of China and at once kissed the earth frenziedly.

entries.farsi: شاه را مكشوف يك يك حالشان / ‏اول و آخر غم و زلزالشان
entries.english: To the King their feelings, their passion and agitation, were open in every detail from first to last.

entries.farsi: ميش مشغول است در مرعاى خويش / ‏ليك چوپان واقف است از حال ميش
entries.english: The sheep are busy in their pasture, but the shepherd knows all about the sheep.

entries.farsi: كلكم راع بداند از رمه / ‏كى علف خوار است و كى در ملحمه
entries.english: “Each of you is a shepherd,” knows which of the flock is feeding and which is in combat.

entries.farsi: گر چه در صورت از آن صف دور بود / ليك چون دف در ميان سور بود
entries.english: Although apparently he was far from those ranks, yet he was like the tambourine at a wedding-feast.

entries.farsi: واقف از سوز و لهيب آن وفود / مصلحت آن بد كه خشك آورده بود
entries.english: Well acquainted with the burning and flaming of those who came to his court, in his wisdom he had ignored them and kept silence.

entries.farsi: در ميان جانشان بود آن سمى / ‏ليك قاصد كرده خود را اعجمى
entries.english: That exalted was in the midst of their souls, but he had purposely feigned to be unfamiliar.

entries.farsi: صورت آتش بود پايان ديگ / ‏معنى آتش بود در جان ديگ
entries.english: The form of the fire is beneath the kettle; the spirit of the fire is in the soul of the kettle.

entries.farsi: صورتش بيرون و معنيش اندرون / ‏معنى معشوق جان در رگ چو خون
