entries.english: Its form is outside and its spirit inside: the spirit of the soul’s Beloved is like blood in the veins.

entries.farsi: شاه زاده پيش شه زانو زده / ‏ده معرف شارح حالش شده
entries.english: The prince knelt before the King, ten announcers gave a description of his state.

entries.farsi: گر چه شه عارف بد از كل پيش پيش / ‏ليك مى‏كردى معرف كار خويش
entries.english: Although the King knew it all long ago, yet the announcer was performing the duties of his office.

entries.farsi: در درون يك ذره نور عارفى / ‏به بود از صد معرف اى صفى
entries.english: O sincere man, a single atom of the light of knowledge within is better than a hundred announcers.

entries.farsi: گوش را رهن معرف داشتن / ‏آيت محجوبى است و حزر و ظن
entries.english: To confine one’s attention to the announcer is a mark of being debarred and of conjecture and opinion.

entries.farsi: آن كه او را چشم دل شد ديدبان / ‏ديد خواهد چشم او عين العيان
entries.english: He whose scout is his inward eye— his eye will behold with the very acme of clairvoyance.

entries.farsi: با تواتر نيست قانع جان او / بل ز چشم دل رسد ايقان او
entries.english: His soul is not content with traditional authority; nay, his feeling of certainty comes from the inward eye.

entries.farsi: پس معرف پيش شاه منتجب / ‏در بيان حال او بگشود لب
entries.english: Then the announcer opened his lips to describe his plight in the presence of the elect King.

entries.farsi: گفت شاها صيد احسان تو است / ‏پادشاهى كن كه بى‏بيرون شو است
entries.english: He said, “O King, he is fallen a prey to your beneficence: show kingly favour, for he has no means of escape.

entries.farsi: دست در فتراك اين دولت زده‏ست / ‏بر سر سر مست او بر مال دست
entries.english: He has clutched the saddle-strap of this empire: stroke his distraught head with your hand!”

entries.farsi: گفت شه هر منصبى و ملكتى / ‏كالتماسش هست يابد اين فتى
entries.english: The King replied, “This youth will obtain every high dignity and sovereignty that he seeks.

entries.farsi: بيست چندان ملك كاو شد ز آن برى / ‏بخشمش اينجا و ما خود بر سرى
entries.english: I will bestow on him here twenty times as many kingdoms as he has relinquished, and myself into the bargain.”

entries.farsi: گفت تا شاهيت در وى عشق كاشت / ‏جز هواى تو هوايى كى گذاشت
entries.english: He said, “Since your royal majesty sowed in him the seed of love, how could it leave any passion except passion for you?

entries.farsi: بندگى تش چنان در خورد شد / كه شهى اندر دل او سرد شد
entries.english: It is so agreeable to him to be your slave that kingship has become cold comfort to his heart.

entries.farsi: شاهى و شه زادگى در باخته است / ‏از پى تو در غريبى ساخته است
entries.english: He has gambled away kingship and princedom: for your sake he has put up with living in exile.

entries.farsi: صوفى است انداخت خرقه‏ى وجد در / كى رود او بر سر خرقه دگر
entries.english: He is a Sufi: he has flung away his mantle in ecstasy: how should he turn again to his mantle?

entries.farsi: ميل سوى خرقه‏اى داده و ندم / ‏آن چنان باشد كه من مغبون شدم
entries.english: To hanker for the given away mantle and repent is as much as to say, ‘I have been swindled:

entries.farsi: باز ده آن خرقه اين سو اى قرين / ‏كه نمى‏ارزيد آن يعنى بدين
entries.english: Put the mantle back here, O comrade, for that was not worth it, that is, this.’

entries.farsi: دور از عاشق كه اين فكر آيدش / ‏ور بيايد خاك بر سر بايدش
entries.english: Far be it from a lover that such a thought should occur to him; and if it does, dust ought to be on his head.

entries.farsi: خاصه خرقه‏ى ملك دنيا كابترست / ‏پنج دانگ مستى‏اش درد سر است
entries.english: Love is worth a hundred mantles like that of the body, which contains a life and sensation and reason;

entries.farsi: عشق ارزد صد چو خرقه‏ى كالبد / كه حياتى دارد و حس و خرد
entries.english: Especially the mantle of worldly dominion, which is cut short: a pennyworth of intoxication with it is headache.

entries.farsi: عامل عشق است معزولش مكن / ‏جز به عشق خويش مشغولش مكن
entries.english: Worldly dominion is lawful to those who indulge the body: we are devoted to the everlasting kingdom of Love.

entries.farsi: ملك دنيا تن پرستان را حالل / ‏ما غالم ملك عشق بى‏زوال
entries.english: He is Love’s agent: do not deprive him of his employment, do not let him be employed in aught but loving you.

entries.farsi: منصبى كانم ز رويت محجب است / ‏عين معزولى است و نامش منصب است
entries.english: The office that veils me from your face is the very essence of unemployment, though it is called ‘office.’

entries.farsi: موجب تاخير اينجا آمدن / ‏فقد استعداد بود و ضعف فن
entries.english: The cause of delay in coming hither was lack of capability and defect of skill.”

entries.farsi: بى‏ز استعداد در كانى روى / ‏بر يكى حبه نگردى محتوى
entries.english: You go into a mine without capability; you will not gain possession of a single grain,

entries.farsi: همچو عنينى كه بكرى را خرد / گر چه سيمين‏بر بود كى بر خورد
entries.english: Like an impotent man who buys a virgin for a slave: even if she is silver-breasted, how can he enjoy?

entries.farsi: چون چراغى بى‏ز زيت و بى‏فتيل / ‏نه كثير استش ز شمع و نه قليل
entries.english: Like a lamp without oil or wick that gets neither much nor little from the taper.

entries.farsi: در گلستان اندر آيد اخشمى / ‏كى شود مغزش ز ريحان خرمى
entries.english: One who cannot smell enter a garden, how should his brain be delighted by the fragrant herbs?—

entries.farsi: همچو خوبى دلبرى مهمان غر / بانگ چنگ و بربطى در پيش كر
entries.english: Like a beautiful and heart-attracting woman who is the guest of an impotent man; like the sound of a harp or lute in the ears of the deaf;

entries.farsi: همچو مرغ خاك كايد در بحار / ز آن چه يابد جز هالك و جز خسار
entries.english: Like the land-bird that falls into great waters: what should it find there but death and perdition?

entries.farsi: همچو بى‏گندم شده در آسيا / جز سپيدى ريش و مو نبود عطا
entries.english: Like one who, having no wheat, goes to a mill: nothing will be given to him except the whitening of his beard and hair.

entries.farsi: آسياى چرخ بر بى‏گندمان / ‏مو سپيدى بخشد و ضعف ميان
entries.english: The celestial mill bestows on those who have no wheat whiteness of hair and weakness in the loins;

entries.farsi: ليك با با گندمان اين آسيا / ملك بخش آمد دهد كار و كيا
entries.english: But on those who bring wheat with them this mill bestows empire and gives them sovereign power.

entries.farsi: اول استعداد جنت بايدت / ‏تا ز جنت زندگانى زايدت
entries.english: You must first be qualified for Paradise in order that from Paradise the life may be born to you.

entries.farsi: طفل نو را از شراب و از كباب / ‏چه حالوت و ز قصور و از قباب
entries.english: What pleasure has the new-born child in wine and roast-meat and palaces and domes?

entries.farsi: بهر استعداد تا اكنون نشست / ‏شوق از حد رفت و آن نامد به دست
entries.english: These parables have no limit: do not seek words: go and acquire capability!

entries.farsi: حد ندارد اين مثل كم جو سخن / ‏تو برو تحصيل استعداد كن
entries.english: “He tarried until now for the sake of capability, ere it was acquired his longing burst bounds.”

entries.farsi: گفت استعداد هم از شه رسد / بى‏ز جان كى مستعد گردد جسد
entries.english: He said, “Capability too is imparted by the King: how should the body be made capable without the soul?”

entries.farsi: لطفهاى شه غمش را در نوشت / ‏شد كه صيد شه كند او صيد گشت
entries.english: The favours of the King did away with his anguish: he had gone to hunt the King: he became the King’s prey.

entries.farsi: هر كه جوياى اميرى شد يقين / ‏پيش از آن او در اسيرى شد رهين
entries.english: “Whoever goes in chase of a quarry like you does not catch his quarry till he is himself caught.”

entries.farsi: هر كه در اشكار چون تو صيد شد / صيد را ناكرده قيد او قيد شد
entries.english: It is certain that every seeker of princedom is thrown into captivity before it.

entries.farsi: عكس مى‏دان نقش ديباچه‏ى جهان / ‏نام هر بنده‏ى جهان خواجه‏ى جهان
entries.english: Know that what is depicted on this mundane frontispiece is preposterous: every slave to the world is named “lord of the world.”

entries.farsi: اى تن كژ فكرت معكوس رو / صد هزار آزاد را كرده گرو
entries.english: O wrong-thinking perversely-acting body, you that have enthralled a hundred thousand freemen,

entries.farsi: مدتى بگذار اين حيلت پزى / ‏چند دم پيش از اجل آزاد زى
entries.english: Abandon this guileful plotting for a time: live free a few moments ere you die;

entries.farsi: ور در آزاديت چون خر راه نيست / ‏همچو دول‏ات سير جز در چاه نيست
entries.english: For if, like the ass, you have no way of attaining to freedom, your movement, like that of the bucket can only be into the well.

entries.farsi: مدتى رو ترك جان من بگو / رو حريف ديگرى جز من بجو
entries.english: Go; take leave of my spirit for awhile: go, seek another companion instead of me.

entries.farsi: نوبت من شد مرا آزاد كن / ‏ديگرى را غير من داماد كن
entries.english: My turn is finished: set me free, espouse another, someone else.

entries.farsi: اى تن صد كاره ترك من بگو / عمر من بردى كسى ديگر بجو
entries.english: O body with your hundred concerns bid me farewell: you have taken my life: seek another.

sections.title_fa: مفتون شدن قاضى بر زن جوحى و در صندوق ماندن و نايب قاضى صندوق را خريدن، باز سال دوم آمدن زن جوحى بر اميد بازى پارينه و گفتن قاضى كه مرا آزاد كن و كسى ديگر را بجوى الى آخر ‏القصه

sections.title_en: How a cadi was infatuated with the wife of Juhi and remained in a chest, and how the cadi’s deputy purchased the chest; and how next year Juhi’s wife came again, hoping to play the same trick last year, the cadi said, “Set me free and seek someone else”; and so on to the end of the story.

entries.farsi: جوحى هر سالى ز درويشى به فن / ‏رو به زن كردى كه اى دل خواه زن
entries.english: Every year, on account of poverty, Juhi would artfully turn to his wife and say, “O sweetheart,

entries.farsi: قوس ابرو تير غمزه دام كيد / بهر چه دادت خدا از بهر صيد
entries.english: Since you have the weapons, go; catch some game in order that we may get milk from your prey.

entries.farsi: چون سالحت هست رو صيدى بگير / تا بدوشانيم از صيد تو شير
entries.english: Why has God given you the bow of your eyebrow, the arrow of your amorous glance, and the snare of your craftiness? For hunting.

entries.farsi: رو پى مرغى شگرفى دام نه / ‏دانه بنما ليك در خوردش مده
entries.english: Go; lay the snare for a big bird: show the bait, but do not let him eat it.

entries.farsi: كام بنما و كن او را تلخ كام / ‏كى خورد دانه چو شد در حبس دام
entries.english: Show him his wish, but disappoint him: how can he eat the bait when he is imprisoned in the snare?”

entries.farsi: شد زن او نزد قاضى در گله / ‏كه مرا افغان ز شوى ده دله
entries.english: His wife went to the cadi to complain, saying, “I appeal for help against my faithless husband.”

entries.farsi: قصه كوته كن كه قاضى شد شكار / از مقال و از جمال آن نگار
entries.english: To cut the tale short, the cadi fell a prey to the words and beauty of the fair woman.

entries.farsi: گفت اندر محكمه است اين غلغله / ‏من نتانم فهم كردن اين گله
entries.english: He said, “There is such a noise in the court of justice I cannot understand this complaint;

entries.farsi: گر به خلوت آيى اى سرو سهى / ‏از ستمكارى شو شرحم دهى
entries.english: If you will come to my private house, O cypress-slender one, and describe to me the injurious behaviour of your husband”—

entries.farsi: گفت خانه‏ى تو ز هر نيك و بدى / ‏باشد از بهر گله آمد شدى
entries.english: “In your house,” she replied, “there will be a coming and going of every sort of people, good and bad, for the purpose of making complaints.”

entries.farsi: خانه‏ى سر جمله پر سودا بود / صدر پر وسواس و پر غوغا بود
entries.english: If the house of the head be wholly filled with a mad passion, the breast will be full of anxiety and commotion.

entries.farsi: باقى اعضا ز فكر آسوده‏اند / و آن صدور از صادران فرسوده‏اند
entries.english: The rest of the members are undisturbed by thinking, while those breasts are consumed by thoughts that return.

entries.farsi: در خزان و باد خوف حق گريز / آن شقايقهاى پارين را بريز
entries.english: Take refuge in the autumn gale of fear of God: let last year’s flowers be shed;

entries.farsi: اين شقايق منع نو اشكوفه‏هاست / ‏كه درخت دل براى آن نماست
entries.english: These flowers prevent the new buds, and it is for the sake of their growth that the tree of the heart exists.

entries.farsi: خويش را در خواب كن زين افتكار / سر ز زير خواب در يقظت بر آر
entries.english: Put yourself to sleep from this thinking: lift up your head from sleep into wakefulness.

entries.farsi: همچو آن اصحاب كهف اى خواجه زود / رو به ايقاظا كه تحسبهم رقود
entries.english: Like the Men of the Cave, pass quickly, O Khwaja, into awake, though you would deem them asleep.

entries.farsi: گفت قاضى اى صنم معمول چيست / ‏گفت خانه‏ى اين كنيزك بس تهى است
entries.english: “O adorable one,” said the cadi, “what can be contrived?” She answered, “This handmaid’s house is quite empty.

entries.farsi: خصم در ده رفت و حارس نيز نيست / ‏بهر خلوت سخت نيكو مسكنى است
entries.english: The enemy has gone into the country, and the caretaker is not there either: it is a very good place for meeting in private.

entries.farsi: امشب ار امكان بود آن جا بيا / كار شب بى‏سمعه است و بى‏ريا
entries.english: Come there to-night if possible: what one does by night is without making hear of it or see it;

entries.farsi: جمله جاسوسان ز خمر خواب مست / ‏زنگى شب جمله را گردن زده‏ست
entries.english: All the spies are intoxicated with the wine of sleep: all have been beheaded by the Negro, Night.”

entries.farsi: خواند بر قاضى فسون‏هاى عجب / ‏آن شكر لب و آن گهانى از چه لب
entries.english: The sugar-lipped chanted wondrous spells over the cadi— and then with what lips!

entries.farsi: چند با آدم بليس افسانه كرد / چون حوا گفتش بخور آن گاه خورد
entries.english: How often did Iblís chatter with Adam!— but when Eve told him to eat, then did he eat.

entries.farsi: اولين خون در جهان ظلم و داد / از كف قابيل بهر زن فتاد
entries.english: The first blood in this world of iniquity and justice was shed by Cain for the sake of a woman.

entries.farsi: نوح چون بر تابه بريان ساختى / ‏واهله بر تابه سنگ انداختى
entries.english: Whenever Noah was frying meat in the frying-pan, Wahila would throw stones at the frying-pan,

entries.farsi: مكر زن بر كار او چيره شدى / ‏آب صاف وعظ او تيره شدى
entries.english: And his wife’s plotting would defeat his work; the clear water of his exhortation would become turbid;

entries.farsi: قوم را پيغام كردى از نهان / ‏كه نگه داريد دين زين گمرهان
entries.english: She used to send secret messages to the folk, saying, “Preserve your religion from these erring men!”

sections.title_fa: ‏رفتن قاضى به خانه‏ى زن جوحى و حلقه زدن جوحى به خشم بر در و گريختن قاضى در صندوق الى آخره
sections.title_en: How the cadi went to the house of Juhi’s wife, and how Juhi knocked angrily at the door, and how the cadi took refuge in a chest, etc.

entries.farsi: مكر زن پايان ندارد رفت شب / ‏قاضى زيرك سوى زن بهر دب
entries.english: The guile of woman is infinite. The sagacious cadi went at night to the wife for the sake of crawling .

entries.farsi: زن دو شمع و نقل مجلس راست كرد / گفت ما مستيم بى‏اين آب خورد
entries.english: The wife set two candles and the dessert for his entertainment. “Without this drink,” said he: “I am intoxicated.”

entries.farsi: اندر آن دم جوحى آمد در بزد / جست قاضى مهربى تا در خزد
entries.english: At that moment Juhi came and knocked at the door: the cadi looked for a place into which he could slink for refuge.

entries.farsi: غير صندوقى نديد او خلوتى / ‏رفت در صندوق از خوف آن فتى
entries.english: He saw no hiding-place but a chest: in his fright the man went into the chest.

entries.farsi: اندر آمد جوحى و گفت اى حريف / ‏اى وبالم در ربيع و در خريف
entries.english: Juhi came in and said, “O spouse, O you who are my plague in spring and autumn,

entries.farsi: من چه دارم كه فدايت نيست آن / ‏كه ز من فرياد دارى هر زمان
entries.english: What do I possess that is not sacrificed to you: that you are always crying out at me?

entries.farsi: بر لب خشكم گشادستى زبان / ‏گاه مفلس خوانيم گه قلتبان
entries.english: You have let loose your tongue at my dry crusts: now you call me ‘pauper,’ now ‘cuckold.’

entries.farsi: اين دو علت گر بود اى جان مرا / آن يكى از تست و ديگر از خدا
entries.english: If, my dear, I suffer from these two maladies, one comes from you and the other from God.

entries.farsi: من چه دارم غير آن صندوق كان / ‏هست مايه‏ى تهمت و پايه‏ى گمان
entries.english: What do I possess but that chest, which is a source of suspicion and a ground for surmise?

entries.farsi: خلق پندارند زر دارم درون / ‏داد واگيرند از من زين ظنون
entries.english: People think I keep gold in it, and because of these opinions charity is withheld from me.

entries.farsi: صورت صندوق بس زيباست ليك / ‏از عروض و سيم و زر خالى است نيك
entries.english: The appearance of the chest is very pleasing, but it is quite empty of goods and silver and gold.

entries.farsi: چون تن زراق خوب و با وقار / اندر آن سله نيابى غير مار
entries.english: Like the hypocrite, handsome and dignified; in the basket you will find nothing except a snake.

entries.farsi: من برم صندوق را فردا به كو / پس بسوزم در ميان چار سو
entries.english: To-morrow I will take the chest into the street and burn it in the midst of the market at the cross-ways,

entries.farsi: تا ببيند مومن و گبر و جهود / كه در اين صندوق جز لعنت نبود
entries.english: That true believer and Zoroastrian and Jew may see there was nothing in this chest but cursing.”

entries.farsi: گفت زن هى در گذر اى مرد از اين / ‏خورد سوگند ان كه نكنم جز چنين
entries.english: “O husband,” cried the woman, “come now, and give up this idea!” He swore several times that he would do just as he had said.

entries.farsi: از پگه حمال آورد او چو باد / زود آن صندوق بر پشتش نهاد
entries.english: Early he like the wind, fetched a porter, and immediately put the chest on his back.

entries.farsi: اندر آن صندوق قاضى از نكال / ‏بانگ مى‏زد كاى حمال و اى حمال
entries.english: The cadi inside the chest shouted in an agony, “O porter! O porter!”

entries.farsi: كرد آن حمال راست و چپ نظر / كز چه سود در مى‏رسد بانگ و خبر
entries.english: The porter looked to the right and the left to see from what direction the shouts and warnings were coming.

entries.farsi: هاتف است اين داعى من اى عجب / ‏يا پرى‏ام مى‏كند پنهان طلب
entries.english: “I wonder,” Said he, “is it a hatif, this voice which is calling me, or is it a peri summoning me mysteriously?”

entries.farsi: چون پياپى گشت آن آواز و بيش / ‏گفت هاتف نيست باز آمد به خويش
entries.english: When the shouts followed one another in succession and increased, he said, “’It is not a hatif,” and recovered himself.

entries.farsi: عاقبت دانست كان بانگ و فغان / ‏بد ز صندوق و كسى در وى نهان
entries.english: At last he perceived that the shouts and cries for help came from the chest and that somebody was concealed in it.

entries.farsi: عاشقى كاو در غم معشوق رفت / ‏گر چه بيرون است در صندوق رفت
entries.english: The lover who has fallen passionately in love with an object of affection has gone into the chest, though he is outside.

entries.farsi: عمر در صندوق برد از اندهان / ‏جز كه صندوقى نبيند از جهان
entries.english: He has spent his life in the chest on account of cares: he can see nothing of the world except a chest.

entries.farsi: آن سرى كه نيست فوق آسمان / ‏از هوس او را در آن صندوق دان
entries.english: The head that is not above the sky— know that it is in that chest by its vain desires.

entries.farsi: چون ز صندوق بدن بيرون رود / او ز گورى سوى گورى مى‏شود
entries.english: When he goes forth from the chest of the body, he will go from one tomb to another tomb.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد قاضى‏اش / ‏گفت اى حمال و اى صندوق كش
entries.english: This topic is endless. The cadi said to him, “O porter, O carrier of the chest,

entries.farsi: از من آگه كن درون محكمه / ‏نايبم را زودتر با اين همه
entries.english: Give news of me to my deputy at the court of justice and acquaint him with all this as quickly as possible,

entries.farsi: تا خرد اين را به زر زين بى‏خرد / همچنين بسته به خانه‏ى ما برد
entries.english: In order that he may buy this with gold from this witless fellow and take it fastened, just as it is, to my house.”

entries.farsi: اى خدا بگمار قومى روحمند / تا ز صندوق بدنمان واخرند
entries.english: O Lord, appoint a spiritually endowed company to redeem us from the chest of the body!

entries.farsi: خلق را از بند صندوق فسون / ‏كى خرد جز انبيا و مرسلون
entries.english: Who but the prophets and apostles can redeem the people from confinement in the chest of guile?

entries.farsi: از هزاران يك كسى خوش منظر است / ‏كه بداند كاو به صندوق اندر است
entries.english: Among thousands there is one person of comely aspect, who knows that he is inside the chest.

entries.farsi: او جهان را ديده باشد پيش از آن / ‏تا بدان ضد اين ضدش گردد عيان
entries.english: He must formerly have beheld the world, so that by means of that contrary this contrary should be made evident to him.

entries.farsi: زين سبب كه علم ضاله‏ى مومن است / ‏عارف ضاله‏ى خود است و موقن است
entries.english: Because “knowledge is the true believer’s lost camel,” he recognises his own lost camel and feels certain.

entries.farsi: آن كه هرگز روز نيكو خود نديد / او در اين ادبار كى خواهد طپيد
entries.english: He that has never seen good fortune, how will he be perturbed in this calamity?

entries.farsi: يا به طفلى در اسيرى اوفتاد / يا خود از اول ز مادر بنده زاد
entries.english: Either he fell into captivity in childhood, or was born a slave at first from his mother’s womb.

entries.farsi: ذوق آزادى نديده جان او / هست صندوق صور ميدان او
entries.english: His soul has never known the delight of freedom: the chest of forms is his arena.

entries.farsi: دايما محبوس عقلش در صور / از قفس اندر قفس دارد گذر
entries.english: His mind is forever imprisoned in forms: he passes from cage into cage.

entries.farsi: منفذش نه از قفس سوى عال / در قفسها مى‏رود از جا به جا
entries.english: He has no means of passing beyond the cage aloft: he goes to and fro into cages.

entries.farsi: در نبى ان استطعتم فانفذوا / اين سخن با جن و انس آمد ز هو
entries.english: In the Qur’an, “If you have the power, pass beyond”: these words came from Him to the Jinn and mankind.

entries.farsi: گفت منفذ نيست از گردونتان / ‏جز به سلطان و به وحى آسمان
entries.english: He said, “There is no way for you to pass beyond the sky save by authority and by inspiration from Heaven.”

entries.farsi: گر ز صندوقى به صندوقى رود / او سمايى نيست صندوقى بود
entries.english: If he goes from chest to chest, he is not of Heaven, he is of the chest.

entries.farsi: فرجه‏ى صندوق نو نو مسكر است / ‏درنيابد كاو به صندوق اندر است
entries.english: The pleasure of changing his chest stupefies him anew: he does not perceive that he is inside the chest.

entries.farsi: گر نشد غره بدين صندوقها / همچو قاضى جويد اطالق و رها
entries.english: If he is not deluded by these chests, he seeks release and deliverance, like the cadi.

entries.farsi: آن كه داند اين نشانش آن شناس / ‏كاو نباشد بى‏فغان و بى‏هراس
entries.english: Know that the mark of one who apprehends this is his crying for help and being in terror.

entries.farsi: همچو قاضى باشد او در ارتعاد / كى بر آيد يك دمى از جانش شاد
entries.english: Like the cadi, he will be quaking: how should a breath of joy rise from his soul?

sections.title_fa: ‏آمدن نايب قاضى ميان بازار و خريدارى كردن صندوق را از جوحى الى آخره
sections.title_en: The arrival of the cadi’s deputy in the bazaar and his purchase of the chest from Juhi, etc.

entries.farsi: نايب آمد گفت صندوقت به چند / گفت نه صد بيشتر زر مى‏دهند
entries.english: The deputy arrived and asked, “How much for your chest?” “They are offering nine hundred pieces of gold and more,” said he,

entries.farsi: من نمى‏آيم فروتر از هزار / گر خريدارى گشا كيسه بيار
entries.english: “I will not come lower than a thousand: if you intend to buy, open your purse and produce.”

entries.farsi: گفت شرمى دار اى كوته نمد / قيمت صندوق خود پيدا بود
entries.english: He replied, “Have some shame, you in the short felt frock! The value of the chest is self-evident.”

entries.farsi: گفت بى‏رويت شرى خود فاسدى است / ‏بيع ما زير گليم اين راست نيست
entries.english: He said, “To buy without seeing is an iniquity: our bargain is in the dark: this is not right.

entries.farsi: بر گشايم گر نمى‏ارزد مخر / تا نباشد بر تو حيفى اى پدر
entries.english: I will open: if it is not worth, don’t buy, lest you be defrauded, O father!”

entries.farsi: گفت اى ستار بر مگشاى راز / سر ببسته مى‏خرم با من بساز
entries.english: He said, “O Veiler, do not reveal the secret!”, “I will buy it with the lid on: come to terms with me.

entries.farsi: ستر كن تا بر تو ستارى كنند / تا نبينى ايمنى بر كس مخند
entries.english: Veil in order that veiling may be vouchsafed to you: do not deride any one till you see security.

entries.farsi: آن چه بر تو خواه آن باشد پسند / بر دگر كس آن كن از رنج و گزند
entries.english: Many like you have been left in this chest and have landed themselves in tribulation.

entries.farsi: بس در اين صندوق چون تو مانده‏اند / خويش را اندر بال بنشانده‏اند
entries.english: Inflict upon another the pain and injury that you would wish and approve for yourself,

entries.farsi: ز انكه بر مرصاد حق و اندر كمين / ‏مى‏دهد پاداش پيش از يوم دين
entries.english: For God is lying in wait and in ambush, ready to give retribution before the Day of Judgement.

entries.farsi: آن عظيم العرش عرش او محيط / تخت دادش بر همه جانها بسيط
entries.english: All-encompassing is the Throne of Him who is enthroned in grandeur: over all souls is spread the Throne of His justice.

entries.farsi: گوشه‏ى عرشش به تو پيوسته است / ‏هين مجنبان جز به دين و داد دست
entries.english: A corner of His throne is touching you: beware, do not move a hand to act impiously or unjustly.

entries.farsi: تو مراقب باش بر احوال خويش / ‏نوش بين در داد و بعد از ظلم نيش
entries.english: Keep a careful watch over your own behaviour: observe that the honey is in justice and that after injustice the sting comes.”

entries.farsi: گفت آرى اين چه كردم استم است / ‏ليك هم مى‏دان كه بادى اظلم است
entries.english: He said, “Yes, what I did is wrong, but at the same time know that the aggressor is the more unjust.”

entries.farsi: گفت نايب يك به يك ما بادييم / ‏با سواد وجه اندر شادييم
entries.english: The deputy replied, “We are aggressors, every one of us, but notwithstanding our blackness of face we are happy,

entries.farsi: ماجرا بسيار شد در من يزيد / داد صد دينار و آن از وى خريد
entries.english: Like the Negro who is happy and pleased, he does not see his face, others see it.”

entries.farsi: همچو زنگى كاو بود شادان و خوش / ‏او نبيند غير او بيند رخش
entries.english: The altercation in bidding was prolonged: he paid a hundred dinars and bought it from him.

entries.farsi: هر دمى صندوقيى اى بد پسند / هاتفان و غيبيانت مى‏خرند
entries.english: O you that find wickedness agreeable, you are always in the chest: the hatifs and those who belong to the Unseen are redeeming you.

sections.title_fa: در تفسير اين خبر كه مصطفى صلوات اللهَّ عليه فرمود من كنت مواله فعلى مواله تا منافقان طعنه زدند كه بس نبودش كه ما مطيعى و چاكرى نموديم او را چاكرى كودكى خلم آلودمان هم مى‏فرمايد ‏الى آخره

sections.title_en: Expounding the Tradition that Mustafá said, the blessings of God be upon him: “When I am the protector of any one, ‘Alí too is his protector,” so that the Hypocrites asked sarcastically, “Was not he satisfied with the obedience and service rendered by us to himself that he bids us render the same service to a snivelling child?” etc.

entries.farsi: زين سبب پيغمبر با اجتهاد / نام خود و آن على موال نهاد
entries.english: For this reason the Prophet, who laboured with the utmost zeal, applied the name “protector” to himself and to Alí.

entries.farsi: گفت هر كاو را منم موال و دوست / كيست موال آن كه آزادت كند
entries.english: He said, “My cousin Alí is the protector and friend of everyone who is under my protection.”

entries.farsi: بند رقيت ز پايت بر كند / چون به آزادى نبوت هادى است
entries.english: Who is the “protector”? He that sets you free and removes the fetters of servitude from your feet.

entries.farsi: ‏مومنان را ز انبيا آزادى است / اى گروه مومنان شادى كنيد
entries.english: Since prophethood is the guide to freedom, freedom is bestowed on true believers by the prophets.

entries.farsi: همچو سرو و سوسن آزادى كنيد / ليك مى‏گوييد هر دم شكر آب
entries.english: Rejoice O community of true believers: show yourselves to be “free” as the cypress and the lily;

entries.farsi: ‏بى‏زبان چون گلستان خوش خضاب / بى‏زبان گويند سرو و سبزه‏زار
entries.english: But do you, like the gay-coloured garden, at every moment give unspoken thanks to the Water.

entries.farsi: شكر آب و شكر عدل نو بهار / حله‏ها پوشيده و دامن كشان
entries.english: The cypresses and the green orchard mutely thank the water and show gratitude for the justice of spring:

entries.farsi: جسمشان چون درج پر در ثمار / مريمان بى‏شوى آبست از مسيح
entries.english: Clad in robes and trailing their skirts, drunken and dancing and jubilant and scattering perfume;

entries.farsi: ‏مست و رقاص و خوش و عنبر فشان / جزو جزو آبستن از شاه بهار
entries.english: Every part impregnated by royal spring, their bodies as caskets filled with pearly fruit;

entries.farsi: ‏خامشان بى‏الف و گفتارى فصيح / ماه ما بى‏نطق خوش بر تافته‏ست
entries.english: Like Maries, having no husband, yet big with a Messiah; silent ones, wordless and devoid of articulate expression,

entries.farsi: ‏هر زبان نطق از فر ما يافته‏ست / نطق عيسى از فر مريم بود
entries.english: “Our Moon has shone brightly without speech: every tongue has derived its speech from our beauty.”

entries.farsi: نطق آدم پرتو آن دم بود / تا زيادت گردد از شكر اى ثقات
entries.english: The speech of Jesus is from the beauty of Mary; the speech of Adam is a ray of the Breath.

entries.farsi: ‏پس نبات ديگر است اندر نبات / عكس آن اينجاست ذل من قنع
entries.english: In order that from thanksgiving, O men of trust, increase may accrue; then other plants are amidst the herbage.

entries.farsi: ‏اندر اين طور است عز من طمع / در جوال نفس خود چندين مرو
entries.english: Here the reverse is, he that is content shall be abased; in this case, he that covets shall be exalted.

entries.farsi: از خريداران خود غافل مشو
entries.english: Do not go so much into the sack of your fleshly soul; do not be forgetful of your purchasers.

sections.title_fa: ،باز آمدن زن جوحى به محكمه‏ى قاضى سال دوم بر اميد وظيفه‏ى پارسال و شناختن قاضى او را ‏الى اتمامه
sections.title_en: How next year Juhi’s wife returned to the court of the cadi, hoping for the same contribution as last year, and how the cadi recognised her, and so on to the end of the story.

entries.farsi: بعد سالى باز جوحى از محن / ‏رو به زن كرد و بگفت اى چست زن
entries.english: After a year Juhi, in consequence of the afflictions, turned to his wife and said, “O clever wife,

entries.farsi: آن وظيفه‏ى پار را تجديد كن / ‏پيش قاضى از گله‏ى من گو سخن
entries.english: Renew last year’s contribution: complain of me to the cadi.”

entries.farsi: زن بر قاضى در آمد با زنان / ‏مر زنى را كرد آن زن ترجمان
entries.english: The wife came before the cadi with women: she made a certain woman her interpreter,

entries.farsi: نا بنشناسد ز گفتن قاضى‏اش / ‏ياد نايد از بالى ماضى‏اش
entries.english: Lest the cadi should recognise her by her speech and remember his past misfortune.

entries.farsi: هست فتنه غمزه‏ى غماز زن / ‏ليك آن صد تو شود ز آواز زن
entries.english: The coquettish glances of a woman are fascinating, but that is increased a hundredfold by her voice.

entries.farsi: چون نمى‏تانست آوازى فراشت / ‏غمزه‏ى تنهاى زن سودى نداشت
entries.english: Since she durst not raise a sound, the wife’s ogling looks alone were of no avail.

entries.farsi: گفت قاضى رو تو خصمت را بيار / تا دهم كار ترا با او قرار
entries.english: “Go,” said the cadi, “and fetch the defendant, that I may settle your quarrel with him.”

entries.farsi: جوحى آمد قاضيش نشناخت زود / كاو به وقت لقيه در صندوق بود
entries.english: Juhi arrived; the cadi did not recognise him at once, for at meeting he was in the chest.

entries.farsi: زو شنيده بود آواز از برون / ‏در شرى و بيع و در نقص و فزون
entries.english: He had heard his voice outside, during the buying and selling and chaffering.

entries.farsi: گفت نفقه‏ى زن چرا ندهى تمام / ‏گفت از جان شرع را هستم غالم
entries.english: He said, “Why won’t you give your wife all the money she needs for expenses?” He replied, “I am devoted with soul to the religious law,

entries.farsi: ليك اگر ميرم ندارم من كفن / ‏مفلس اين لعبم و شش پنجزن
entries.english: But if I die I do not possess the shroud: I am bankrupt in this game, I have gambled everything away.”

entries.farsi: زين سخن قاضى مگر بشناختش / ‏ياد آورد آن دغل و آن باختش
entries.english: From these words the cadi, as it happened, recognised him and called to mind his roguery and the trick he had played.

entries.farsi: از شش و از پنج عارف گشت فرد / محترز گشته است زين شش پنج نرد
entries.english: “You played that game with me,” he said: “last year you put me out of action.

entries.farsi: نوبت من رفت امسال آن قمار / با دگر كس باز دست از من بدار
entries.english: My turn is past: this year try that gamble on someone else and keep your hands off me!”

entries.farsi: گفت آن شش پنج با من باختى / ‏پار اندر شش درم انداختى
entries.english: The knower of God has been isolated from the six and the five: he has become on his guard against the sixes and fives of the backgammon.

entries.farsi: رست او از پنج حس و شش جهت / ‏از وراى آن همه كرد آگهت
entries.english: He has escaped from the five senses and the six directions: he has made you acquainted with beyond all that.

entries.farsi: شد اشاراتش اشارات ازل / ‏جاوز الاوهام طرا و اعتزل
entries.english: His intimations are the intimations of Eternity: he has transcended all conceptions and withdrawn himself apart.

entries.farsi: زين چه شش گوشه گر نبود برون / ‏چون بر آرد يوسفى را از درون
entries.english: Unless he is outside of this hexagonal well, how should he bring up a Joseph from the inside?

entries.farsi: واردى بالاى چرخ بى‏ستن / ‏جسم او چون دلو در چه چاره كن
entries.english: He is one who goes to draw water above the pillarless firmament, his body, like a bucket, is in the well, helping.

entries.farsi: يوسفان چنگال در دلوش زده / ‏رسته از چاه و شه مصرى شده
entries.english: The Josephs cling to his bucket, escape from the well, and become kings of Egypt.

entries.farsi: دلوهاى ديگر از چه آب جو / دلو او فارغ ز آب اصحاب جو
entries.english: The other buckets seek water from the well: his bucket has no concern with the water, it seeks friends.

entries.farsi: دلوها غواص آب از بهر قوت / ‏دلو او قوت و حيات جان حوت
entries.english: The buckets plunge into the water for food: his bucket is the food and life of the soul of the fish.

entries.farsi: دلو چه و حبل چه و چرخ چى / ‏اين مثالى بس ركيك است اى اچى
entries.english: The buckets are attached to the lofty wheel: his bucket is in two Almighty fingers.

entries.farsi: دلوها وابسته‏ى چرخ بلند / دلو او در اصبعين زورمند
entries.english: What bucket and what cord and what wheel? This is a very weak comparison, O pasha.

entries.farsi: از كجا آرم مثالى بى‏شكست / ‏كفو آن نه آيد و نه آمده‏ست
entries.english: Where shall I get a comparison that is without frailty? One to match him will not come, and never has come.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران مرد پنهان در يكى / صد كمان و تير درج ناوكى
entries.english: A hundred thousand men concealed in a single man, a hundred bows and arrows enclosed in a single blowpipe;

entries.farsi: ما رميت إذ رميتى فتنه‏اى / ‏صد هزاران خرمن اندر حفنه‏اى
entries.english: You did not throw when you threw, a temptation, a hundred thousand stacks in a handful.

entries.farsi: آفتابى در يكى ذره نهان / ناگهان آن ذره بگشايد دهان
entries.english: A sun hidden in a mote: suddenly that mote opens its mouth.

entries.farsi: ذره ذره گردد افلاک و زمين / ‏پيش آن خورشيد چون جست از كمين
entries.english: The heavens and the earth crumble to atoms before that Sun when he springs forth from ambush.

entries.farsi: اين چنين جانى چه در خورد تنست / ‏هين بشو اى تن از اين جان هر دو دست
entries.english: How is a spirit like this meet for the body? Listen, O body, wash your hands of this spirit!

entries.farsi: اى تن گشته وثاق جان بس است / چند تاند بحر در مشكى نشست
entries.english: O body that has become the spirit’s dwelling-place, it is enough: how long can the Sea abide in a water-skin?

entries.farsi: اى هزاران جبرئيل اندر بشر / اى مسيحان نهان در جوف خر
entries.english: O you who are a thousand Gabriels in man, O you, who are Messiahs inside the ass,

entries.farsi: اى هزاران كعبه پنهان در كنيس / اى غلط انداز عفريت و بليس
entries.english: O you who are a thousand Ka‘bas concealed in a church, O you who causes ifrít and devil to fall into error,

entries.farsi: سجده‏گاه المكانى در مكان / مر بليسان را ز تو ويران دكان
entries.english: You are the spaceless Object of worship in space: the devils have their shop destroyed by you,

entries.farsi: كه چرا من خدمت اين طين كنم / ‏صورتى را من لقب چون دين كنم
entries.english: “How should I pay homage to this clay? How should I bestow on a form a title signifying obedience?”

entries.farsi: نيست صورت چشم را نيكو بمال / ‏تا ببينى شعشعه‏ى نور جلال
entries.english: He is not the form: rub your eye well, that you may behold the radiance of the light of glory!

sections.title_fa: ‏باز آمدن به شرح قصه‏ى شاه زاده و مالزمت او در حضرت شاه
sections.title_en: Resuming the explanation of the Story of the prince and his constant attendance

entries.farsi: شاه زاده پيش شه حيران اين / ‏هفت گردون ديده در يك مشت طين
entries.english: at the court of the King.

entries.farsi: آمده در خاطرش كاين بس خفى است / ‏اين همه معنى است پس صورت ز چيست
entries.english: The prince in the presence of the King was bewildered by this: he beheld the Seven Heavens in a handful of clay.

entries.farsi: هيچ ممكن نى به بحثى لب گشود / ليك جان با جان دمى خامش نبود
entries.english: Nowise was it possible to open his lips in discussion, but never for a moment did soul cease to converse with soul.

entries.farsi: صورتى از صورتت بيزار كن / ‏خفته‏اى هر خفته را بيدار كن
entries.english: It came into his mind that It was exceedingly mysterious— “all this is reality: whence, then, comes the form?”

entries.farsi: آن كالمت مى‏رهاند از كالم / ‏و آن سقامت مى‏جهاند از سقام
entries.english: A form that frees you from form, a sleeper that awakens everyone who is asleep.

entries.farsi: پس سقام عشق جان صحت است / ‏رنجهايش حسرت هر راحت است
entries.english: The words deliver from words and the sickness lets you escape from the sickness.

entries.farsi: اى تن اكنون دست خود زين جان بشو / ور نمى‏شويى جز اين جانى بجو
entries.english: Therefore the sickness of love is the soul of health: its pains are the envy of every pleasure.

entries.farsi: حاصل آن شه نيك او را مى‏نواخت / ‏او از آن خورشيد چون مه مى‏گداخت
entries.english: O body, wash your hands of this soul now, or if you will not wash, seek another soul than this!

entries.farsi: آن گداز عاشقان باشد نمو / همچو مه اندر گدازش تازه رو
entries.english: In short, the King cherished him fondly, and in that Sun he was melting away like the moon.

entries.farsi: جمله رنجوران دوا دارند اميد / نالد اين رنجور كم افزون كنيد
entries.english: The melting away of lovers is growth: like the moon, he has a fresh face while he is melting away.

entries.farsi: خوشتر از اين سم نديدم شربتى / ‏زين مرض خوشتر نباشد صحتى
entries.english: All the sick hope to be cured, but this sick one sobs, crying, “Increase my sickness!

entries.farsi: زين گنه بهتر نباشد طاعتى / ‏سالها نسبت بدين دم ساعتى
entries.english: I have found no drink sweeter than this poison: no state of health can be sweeter than this disease.

entries.farsi: مدتى بد پيش اين شه زين نسق / ‏دل كباب و جان نهاده بر طبق
entries.english: No act of piety can be better than this sin: years in comparison with this moment are an hour.”

entries.farsi: گفت شه از هر كسى يك سر بريد / من ز شه هر لحظه قربانم جديد
entries.english: In this fashion he remained with this King for a long while, his heart kabab and his soul laid on the tray.

entries.farsi: من فقيرم از زر از سر محتشم / ‏صد هزاران سر خلف دارد سرم
entries.english: He said, “The King beheads every one once, I am sacrificed anew by the King at every instant.

entries.farsi: با دو پا در عشق نتوان تاختن / ‏با يكى سر عشق نتوان باختن
entries.english: I am poor in gold, but rich in heads: my head has a hundred heads to take its place.
No one can run in Love with two feet: no one can play Love with one head;

entries.farsi: هر كسى را خود دو پا و يك سر است / ‏با هزاران پا و سر تن نادر است
entries.english: Yet everyone has two feet and one head: the body with thousands of feet and heads is a rarity.”

entries.farsi: زين سبب هنگامه‏ها شد كل هدر / هست اين هنگامه هر دم گرمتر
entries.english: On this account all combats are in vain, this combat grows hotter every moment.

entries.farsi: معدن گرمى است اندر المكان / ‏هفت دوزخ از شرارش يك دخان
entries.english: The source of its heat lies beyond the realm of space: the seven Hells are a smoke from the sparks of its fire.

sections.title_en: Setting forth how Hell will say, when the Bridge Sirat is over it, “O believer, pass more quickly across the Sirat! Quick, make haste, lest the greatness of your light put out my fire,”, “Pass, O believer, for lo, your light has extinguished my fire.”

entries.farsi: ز آتش عاشق از اين رو اى صفى / ‏مى‏شود دوزخ ضعيف و منطفى
entries.english: For this reason, O sincere man, Hell is enfeebled and extinguished by the fire of Love.

entries.farsi: كفر كه كبريت دوزخ اوست بس / ‏بين كه مى‏پخساند او را اين نفس
entries.english: It says to him, “Pass speedily, O respected one, or else my fire will be destroyed by your flames.”

entries.farsi: گويدش بگذر سبك اى محتشم / ‏ور نه ز آتشهاى تو مرد آتشم
entries.english: Behold how this breath dissolves infidelity, which alone is the brimstone of Hell!

entries.farsi: زود كبريتت بدين سودا سپار / تا نه دوزخ بر تو تازد نه شرار
entries.english: Quickly entrust your brimstone to this passion, in order that neither Hell nor its sparks may assail you.

entries.farsi: گويدش جنت گذر كن همچو باد / ور نه گردد هر چه من دارم كساد
entries.english: Paradise says to him, “Pass like the wind, or else all that I possess will become unsalable;

entries.farsi: رفت عمرش چاره را فرصت نيافت / ‏صبر بس سوزان بد و جان بر نتافت
entries.english: For you are the owner of the stack, I am a gleaner: I am an idol, you are the provinces of China.”

entries.farsi: كه تو صاحب خرمنى من خوشه‏چين / من بتى‏ام تو واليتهاى چين
entries.english: Both Hell and Paradise are trembling in fear of him: neither the one nor the other feels safe from him.

entries.farsi: هست لرزان زو جحيم و هم جنان / ‏نه مر اين را نه مر آن را زو امان
entries.english: His life sped away and he found no opportunity to cure: the waiting consumed him exceedingly and his soul could not endure it.

entries.farsi: مدتى دندان كنان اين مى‏كشيد / نارسيده عمر او آخر رسيد
entries.english: For a long time, gnashing his teeth, he suffered this: ere he attained, his life reached its end.

entries.farsi: صورت معشوق زو شد در نهفت / ‏رفت و شد با معنى معشوق جفت
entries.english: The form of the Beloved vanished from him: he died and was united with the reality of the Beloved.

entries.farsi: گفت لبسش گر ز شعر و ششتر است / ‏اعتناق بى‏حجابش خوشتر است
entries.english: He said, “Though his raiment was of silk and Shushtar cloth, his unscreened embrace is sweeter.

entries.farsi: من شدم عريان ز تن او از خيال / ‏مى‏خرامم در نهايات الوصال
entries.english: I am denuded of my body, and he of phantasy: I am advancing triumphantly in the consummation of union.”

entries.farsi: اين مباحث تا بدين جا گفتنى است / ‏هر چه آيد زين سپس بنهفتنى است
entries.english: These topics may be discussed up to this point; all that comes after this must be kept hid;

entries.farsi: ور بگويى ور بكوشى صد هزار / هست بيگار و نگردد آشكار
entries.english: And if you would tell it and make a hundred thousand efforts, It is fruitless labour, for it will never become clear.

entries.farsi: تا به دريا سير اسب و زين بود / بعد از اينت مركب چوبين بود
entries.english: As far as the sea, it is a journey on horseback: after this you have a wooden horse.

entries.farsi: مركب چوبين به خشكى ابتر است / ‏خاص آن درياييان را رهبر است
entries.english: The wooden horse is no good on the dry land: it carries exclusively those who voyage on the sea.

entries.farsi: اين خموشى مركب چوبين بود / بحريان را خامشى تلقين بود
entries.english: The wooden horse is this silence: silence gives instruction to the sea-folk.

entries.farsi: هر خموشى كه ملولت مى‏كند / نعره‏هاى عشق آن سو مى‏زند
entries.english: Every silent one who wearies you is uttering shrieks of love Yonder.

entries.farsi: تو همى‏گويى عجب خامش چراست / ‏او همى‏گويد عجب گوشش كجاست
entries.english: You say, “I wonder why he is silent”; he says, “How strange! Where is his ear?

entries.farsi: من ز نعره كر شدم او بى‏خبر / تيز گوشان زين سمر هستند كر
entries.english: I am deafened by the shrieks, he is unaware.” The sharp-eared are deaf to this converse.

entries.farsi: آن يكى در خواب نعره مى‏زند / صد هزاران بحث و تلقين مى‏كند
entries.english: Someone cries aloud in his dream and gives a hundred thousand discussions and communications,

entries.farsi: اين نشسته پهلوى او بى‏خبر / خفته خود آن است و كر ز آن شور و شر
entries.english: This other sitting beside him is unaware: it is really he who is asleep and deaf to that turmoil and tumult.

entries.farsi: و آن كسى كش مركب چوبين شكست / ‏غرقه شد در آب او خود ماهى است
entries.english: And he whose wooden horse is shattered and sunk in the water, he in sooth is the fish.

entries.farsi: نه خموش است و نه گويا نادرى است / ‏حال او را در عبارت نام نيست
entries.english: He is neither silent nor speaking: he is a marvel: there is no name to describe his state.

entries.farsi: نيست زين دو هر دو هست آن بو العجب / ‏شرح اين گفتن برون است از ادب
entries.english: He does not belong to these two, that prodigy is both: to explain this would transgress the limits of due reverence.

entries.farsi: اين مثال آمد ركيك و بى‏ورود / ليك در محسوس از اين بهتر نبود
entries.english: This comparison is poor and unsuccessful, but in the sensible there was none better than this.

sections.title_fa: متوفى شدن بزرگين از شه زادگان و آمدن برادر ميانين به جنازه‏ى برادر كه آن كوچكين صاحب فراش بود از رنجورى و نواختن پادشاه ميانين را تا او هم لنگ احسان شد، ماند پيش پادشاه صد ‏هزار غنايم غيبى و عينى بدو رسيد از دولت و نظر آن شاه، مع تقرير بعضه

sections.title_en: The death of the eldest prince, and how the middle brother came to his funeral—for the youngest was confined to his bed by illness; and how the King treated the middle brother with great affection, so that he too was crippled by his kindness; he remained with the King, and a hundred thousand spoils, from the unseen and visible worlds, were conferred upon him by the fortune and favour of the King;
with an exposition of some part thereof.

entries.farsi: شاه ديدش گفت قاصد اين كى است / ‏كه از آن بحر است و اين هم ماهى است
entries.english: The youngest was ill, and the middle one came alone to the funeral of the eldest.

entries.farsi: كوچكين رنجور بود و آن وسط / بر جنازه‏ى آن بزرگ آمد فقط
entries.english: The King espied him, he said with a purpose, “Who is this?—for he is of that sea, and he too is a fish.”

entries.farsi: شه نوازيدش كه هستى يادگار / كرد او را هم بدين پرسش شكار
entries.english: Then the announcer said, “He is a son of the same father: this brother is younger than that brother.”

entries.farsi: پس معرف گفت پور آن پدر / اين برادر ز آن برادر خردتر
entries.english: The King greeted him affectionately, saying, “You are a keepsake”; and by this enquiry made him too his prey.

entries.farsi: از نواز شاه آن زار حنيذ / در تن خود غير جان جانى بديد
entries.english: In consequence of the kindness shown by the King, that wretched man, roasted, found in his body a soul other than the soul.

entries.farsi: در دل خود ديد عالى غلغله / ‏كه نيابد صوفى آن در صد چله
entries.english: He felt within his heart a sublime emotion which the Sufi does not experience during a hundred chilas.

entries.farsi: عرصه و ديوار و كوه سنگ بافت / ‏پيش او چون نار خندان مى‏شكافت
entries.english: Court-yard and wall and mountain woven of stone seemed to split open before him like a laughing pomegranate.

entries.farsi: ذره ذره پيش او همچون قباب / ‏دم به دم مى‏كرد صد گون فتح باب
entries.english: One by one, the atoms were momently opening their doors to him, like tents, in a hundred diverse ways.

entries.farsi: باب گه روزن شدى گاهى شعاع / ‏خاك گه گندم شدى و گاه صاع
entries.english: The door would become now the window, now the sunbeams; the earth would become now the wheat, now the bushel.

entries.farsi: در نظرها چرخ بس كهنه و قديد / پيش چشمش هر دمى خلق جديد
entries.english: In eyes the heavens are very old and threadbare; in his eye it was a new creation at every moment.

entries.farsi: روح زيبا چون كه وا رست از جسد / از قضا بى‏شك چنين چشمش رسد
entries.english: When the beauteous spirit is delivered from the body, no doubt an eye like this will be conferred upon it by destiny.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران غيب پيشش شد پديد / آن چه چشم محرمان بيند بديد
entries.english: A hundred thousand mysteries were revealed to him: he beheld that which the eyes of the initiated behold.

entries.farsi: آن چه او اندر كتب برخوانده بود / چشم را در صورت آن بر گشود
entries.english: He opened eye on the form of that which he had read in books.

entries.farsi: از غبار مركب آن شاه نر / يافت او كحل عزيزى در بصر
entries.english: From the dust of the mighty King’s horse he obtained a precious eye salve for his eyesight.

entries.farsi: بر چنين گلزار دامن مى‏كشيد / جزو جزوش نعره زن هَلْ مِنْ مزيد
entries.english: In such a garden of flowers he was trailing his skirt, while every part of him was crying, “Is there any more?”

entries.farsi: گلشنى كز بقل رويد يك دم است / ‏گلشنى كز عقل رويد خرم است
entries.english: The flowers that grow from plants are a moment; the flowers that grow from Reason are fresh.

entries.farsi: گلشنى كز گل دمد گردد تباه / ‏گلشنى كز دل دمد وا فرحتاه
entries.english: The flowers that bloom from earth become faded; the flowers that bloom from the heart—oh, what a joy!

entries.farsi: علمهاى با مزه‏ى دانسته‏مان / ‏ز آن گلستان يك دو سه گلدسته دان
entries.english: Know that the delightful sciences known to us are two or three bunches of flowers from that Garden.

entries.farsi: ز آن زبون اين دو سه گل دسته‏ايم / ‏كه در گلزار بر خود بسته‏ايم
entries.english: We are devoted to these two or three bunches of flowers because we have shut the Garden-door on ourselves.

entries.farsi: آن چنان مفتاحها هر دم به نان / ‏مى‏فتد اى جان دريغا از بنان
entries.english: Alas, O on account of bread such keys are always dropping from your fingers!

entries.farsi: باز استسقات چون شد موج زن / ‏ملك شهرى بايدت پر نان و زن
entries.english: And if for a moment you are relieved from preoccupation with bread, you dangle about the chador and your passion for women;

entries.farsi: ور دمى هم فارغ آرندت ز نان / ‏گرد چادر گردى و عشق زنان
entries.english: And then, when your dropsy breaks into billows, you must have under your sway a city full of bread and women.

entries.farsi: مار بودى اژدها گشتى مگر / يك سرت بود اين زمانى هفت سر
entries.english: You were a snake: indeed you have become a dragon. You had one head: now you have seven heads.

entries.farsi: اژدهاى هفت سر دوزخ بود / حرص تو دانه‏ست و دوزخ فخ بود
entries.english: Hell is a seven-headed dragon: your greed is the bait and Hell the snare.

entries.farsi: دام را بدران بسوزان دانه را / باز كن درهاى نو اين خانه را
entries.english: Pull the snare to pieces, burn the bait, open new doors in this tenement!

entries.farsi: چون تو عاشق نيستى اى نر گدا / همچو كوهى بى‏خبر دارى صدا
entries.english: O sturdy beggar, unless you are a lover, you have an echo, like the unconscious mountain.

entries.farsi: كوه را گفتار كى باشد ز خود / عكس غير است آن صدا اى معتمد
entries.english: How should the mountain possess a voice of its own? The echo is reflected from another, O trusty man.

entries.farsi: گفت تو ز آن سان كه عكس ديگرى است / ‏جمله احوالت بجز هم عكس نيست
entries.english: In the same fashion as your speech is the reflection of another, so all your feelings are nothing but a reflection.

entries.farsi: خشم و ذوقت هر دو عكس ديگران / ‏شادى قواده و خشم عوان
entries.english: Both your anger and your pleasure are reflected from others, the joy of the procuress and the rage of the night-patrol.

entries.farsi: آن عوان را آن ضعيف آخر چه كرد / كه دهد او را به كينه زجر و درد
entries.english: Pray, what did that poor fellow do to the night-patrol t hat he should punish and torment him in revenge?

entries.farsi: تا به كى عكس خيال المعه / ‏جهد كن تا گرددت اين واقعه
entries.english: How long the glittering phantom reflected? Strive to make this actual for yourself,

entries.farsi: تا كه گفتارت ز حال تو بود / سير تو با پر و بال تو بود
entries.english: So that your words will be by your immediate feelings, and your flight will be made with your own wings and pinions.

entries.farsi: باز صيد آرد به خود از كوهسار / الجرم شاهش خوراند كبك و سار
entries.english: It is with alien feathers that the arrow captures its prey; consequently it gets no share of the bird’s flesh;

entries.farsi: صيد گيرد تير هم با پر غير / الجرم بى‏بهره است از لحم طير
entries.english: The falcon brings its quarry from the mountains itself; consequently the king lets it eat partridge and starling.

entries.farsi: منطقى كز وحى نبود از هواست / ‏همچو خاكى در هوا و در هباست
entries.english: The speech that is not from inspiration springs from self-will: it is like dust in the air and among the motes.

entries.farsi: گر نمايد خواجه را اين دم غلط / ز اول و النجم بر خوان چند خط
entries.english: If this saying appears to the Khwaja to be erroneous, recite a few lines at the beginning of Wa’l-Najm.

entries.farsi: تا كه ما ينطق محمد عن هوى / ‏ان هو الا بوحى احتوى
entries.english: Down to, Mohammed does not speak from self-will: ’ It is only gained by inspiration.

entries.farsi: احمدا چون نيستت از وحى ياس / ‏جسميان را ده تحرى و قياس
entries.english: O Ahmad, since you despair not of inspiration, leaves investigation and conjecture to the corporealists;

entries.farsi: كز ضرورت هست مردارى حالل / ‏كه تحرى نيست در كعبه‏ى وصال
entries.english: For in case of necessity a carcass is lawful, but there is no need to investigate in the Ka‘ba of union.

entries.farsi: بى‏تحرى و اجتهادات هدى / هر كه بدعت پيشه گيرد از هوا
entries.english: Whoever willfully adopts a heresy without investigation and the utmost efforts to discover the right way,

entries.farsi: همچو عادش بر برد باد و كشد / نه سليمان است تا تختش كشد
entries.english: The wind will lift him up and kill him, like Ad: he is no Solomon that it should waft his throne along.

entries.farsi: عاد را باد است حمال خذول / ‏همچو بره در كف مردى اكول
entries.english: For Ad the wind is a treacherous carrier: as a lamb in the hands of a glutton,

entries.farsi: همچو فرزندش نهاده بر كنار / مى‏برد تا بكشدش قصاب‏وار
entries.english: Which he lays in his lap as though it were his own child and carries away to slaughter like a butcher.

entries.farsi: عاد را آن باد ز استكبار بود / يار خود پنداشتند اغيار بود
entries.english: That wind was (the punishment) for Ad because of their pride: they indeed deemed it a friend, it was a stranger.

entries.farsi: چون بگردانيد ناگه پوستين / ‏خردشان بشكست آن بئس القرين
entries.english: When of a sudden it turned its coat, that evil comrade shattered them piecemeal.

entries.farsi: باد را بشكن كه بس فتنه‏ست باد / پيش از آن كت بشكند او همچو عاد
entries.english: Shatter the wind—for the wind is a great temptation— ere it shatter you, like Ad

entries.farsi: لشكر حق است باد و از نفاق / ‏چند روزى با شما كرد اعتناق
entries.english: Hud admonished them, saying, “O prideful folk, this wind will tear out of your hands the skirt.

entries.farsi: هود دادى پند كاى پر كبر خيل / ‏بر كند از دستتان اين باد ذيل
entries.english: The wind is God’s army, and in hypocrisy has it embraced you for a few days.

entries.farsi: او به سر با خالق خود راست است / ‏چون اجل آيد بر آرد باد دست
entries.english: Secretly it is loyal to its Creator: when the appointed term arrives, the wind will throw up its hands.”

entries.farsi: باد را اندر دهن بين رهگذر / هر نفس آيان روان در كر و فر
entries.english: See how the wind passes through the mouth, coming and going at every moment in advance and retreat.

entries.farsi: حلق و دندانها از او ايمن بود / حق چو فرمايد به دندان در فتد
entries.english: The throat and teeth are in no danger from it; when God commands, it attacks the teeth;

entries.farsi: اين همان باد است كايمن مى‏گذشت / ‏بود جان كشت و گشت او مرگ كشت
entries.english: An atom of wind becomes a mountain and heavy, and toothache keeps him miserable and ill.

entries.farsi: كوه گردد ذره‏ى باد و ثقيل / ‏درد دندان داردش زار و عليل
entries.english: This is the same wind that used to pass by harmlessly: it was the life of the crops and it became the death of the crops.

entries.farsi: دست آن كس كه بكردت دست‏بوس / ‏وقت خشم آن دست مى‏گردد دبوس
entries.english: The hand of the person who kissed your hand— in the moment of anger that hand becomes a mace.

entries.farsi: يا رب و يا رب بر آرد او ز جان / ‏كه ببر اين باد را اى مستعان
entries.english: He cries from his soul, “O Lord! O Lord! Take away this wind, O You whose aid is besought!

entries.farsi: اى دهان غافل بدى زين باد رو / از بن دندان در استغفار شو
entries.english: O mouth, you were heedless of this wind: go and betake yourself to asking pardon of God with utter abasement.”

entries.farsi: چشم سختش اشكها باران كند / منكران را درد اللهَّ خوان كند
entries.english: His hard eye sheds tears like rain: pain causes the unbelievers to call unto God.

entries.farsi: چون دم مردان نپذرفتى ز مرد / وحى حق را هين پذيرا شو ز درد
entries.english: Since you have not received the breath of men from a man, listen; receive the Divine inspiration from pain.

entries.farsi: باد گويد پيكم از شاه بشر / گه خبر خير آورم گه شور و شر
entries.english: The wind says, “I am a messenger from the King of mankind: now I bring good news, now calamitous and bad;

entries.farsi: ز انكه مأمورم امير خود نى‏ام / ‏من چو تو غافل ز شاه خود كى‏ام
entries.english: For I am subject to command, I am not in command of myself: when am I forgetful, like you, of my King?

entries.farsi: گر سليمان‏وار بودى حال تو / چون سليمان گشتمى حمال تو
entries.english: If your state resembled that of Solomon, I should have carried you as I carried Solomon.

entries.farsi: عاريه‏ستم گشتمى ملك كفت / ‏كردمى بر راز خود من واقفت
entries.english: I am lent; I should have become a possession in your hand: I should have made you acquainted with my mystery.

entries.farsi: ليك چون تو ياغيى من مستعار / مى‏كنم خدمت ترا روزى سه چهار
entries.english: But since you are rebellious and I am taken on loan to serve you for three or four days,

entries.farsi: آن زمان خود جملگان مومن شوند / آن زمان خود سركشان بر سر دوند
entries.english: Therefore I will lay you low, like Ad, and dash away in revolt from your army,

entries.farsi: تا به غيب ايمان تو محكم شود / آن زمان كايمانت مايه‏ى غم شود
entries.english: In order that your faith in the Unseen may become firm at the moment when your faith is a source of woe.”

entries.farsi: پس چو عادت سر نگونيها دهم / ‏ز اسپه تو ياغيانه بر جهم
entries.english: At that moment, in truth, all become believers: at that moment even the headstrong run on their heads.

entries.farsi: آن زمان زارى كنند و افتقار / همچو دزد و راه زن در زير دار
entries.english: At that moment they cry piteously and make humble supplication, like robbers and brigands under the gibbet.

entries.farsi: ليك گر در غيب گردى مستوى / ‏مالك دارين و شحنه‏ى خود توى
entries.english: But if you become upright in the Unseen, you are owner of the two worlds and a magistrate over yourself.

entries.farsi: شحنگى و پادشاهى مقيم / ‏نه دو روزه و مستعار است و سقيم
entries.english: The abiding magistracy and kingship is not taken on loan for two days and ailing.

entries.farsi: رستى از بيگار و كار خود كنى / ‏هم تو شاه و هم تو طبل خود زنى
entries.english: You are delivered from strife and can act for yourself: you are king and at the same time beating your own drum.

entries.farsi: چون گلو تنگ آورد بر ما جهان / ‏خاك خوردى كاشكى حلق و دهان
entries.english: When the World squeezes our throats tightly, would that our gullets and mouths had eaten earth!

entries.farsi: اين دهان خود خاك خوارى آمده‏ست / ‏ليك خاكى را كه آن رنگين شده‏ست
entries.english: This mouth, indeed, has been an eater of earth; but an earth that has been coloured.

entries.farsi: اين كباب و اين شراب و اين شكر / خاك رنگين است و نقشين اى پسر
entries.english: This roast-meat and this wine and this sugar are coloured and painted earth, O son.

entries.farsi: چون كه خوردى و شد آنها لحم و پوست / ‏رنگ لحمش داد و اين هم خاك كوست
entries.english: When you have eaten or drunk and they have become flesh and skin, He gives them the colour of flesh, but they are still the earth of street.

entries.farsi: هم ز خاكى بخيه بر گل مى‏زند / جمله را هم باز خاكى مى‏كند
entries.english: It is from a bit of earth that He stitches the clay, and then makes the whole a bit of earth again.

entries.farsi: هندو و قفچاق و رومى و حبش / ‏جمله يك رنگ‏اند اندر گور خوش
entries.english: Hindus, Turks, Greeks and Abyssinians— all have quite the same colour in the grave.

entries.farsi: ا بدانى كان همه رنگ و نگار / جمله رو پوش است و مكر و مستعار
entries.english: So you may know that all those colours and pictures are entirely a mask and deceit and borrowed.

entries.farsi: رنگ باقى صِ بْغَةَ اللهَِّ است و بس / ‏غير آن بر بسته دان همچون جرس
entries.english: The only lasting colour is the dye of Allah: know that all the rest are tied on like a bell.

entries.farsi: رنگ صدق و رنگ تقوى و يقين / ‏تا ابد باقى بود بر عابدين
entries.english: The colour of sincerity and the colour of piety and intuitive faith will endure in the worshippers for evermore;

entries.farsi: رنگ شك و رنگ كفران و نفاق / ‏تا ابد باقى بود بر جان عاق
entries.english: And the colour of doubt and the colour of ingratitude and hypocrisy will endure in the undutiful soul for evermore;

entries.farsi: چون سيه رويى فرعون دغا / رنگ آن باقى و جسم او فنا
entries.english: Like wicked Pharaoh’s blackness of face, the colour whereof is enduring, though his body passes away.

entries.farsi: برق و فر روى خوب صادقين / ‏تن فنا شد و آن بجا تا يوم دين
entries.english: The radiance and glory in the beauteous faces of the sincere: their bodies pass away, but that remains till the Day of Judgement.

entries.farsi: زشت آنزشت است و خوب آنخوب و بس / دايم آن ضحاك و اين اندر عبس
entries.english: The only ugly one is that ugly one; the only beautiful one is that beautiful one: this one is always laughing and that one scowling.

entries.farsi: خاك را رنگ و فن و سنگى دهد / طفل خويان را بر آن جنگى دهد
entries.english: He gives to earth a certain colour and variety and value, and causes childish folk to wrangle over it.

entries.farsi: از خميرى اشتر و شيرى پزند / كودكان از حرص آن كف مى‏گزند
entries.english: A piece of dough is baked in the shape of a camel or lion; children bite their fingers in their greed for it.

entries.farsi: شير و اشتر نان شود اندر دهان / ‏درنگيرد اين سخن با كودكان
entries.english: The lion or camel turns to bread in the mouth, but it is futile to tell this to children.

entries.farsi: طفل را استيزه و صد آفت است / ‏شكر اين كه بى‏فن و بى‏قوت است
entries.english: The child is in an ignorance and fancy and doubt: at any rate, thank God, his strength is little.

entries.farsi: كودك اندر جهل و پندار و شكى است / شكر بارى قوت او اندكى است
entries.english: The child is quarrelsome and very mischievous: thank God for his lack of skill and strength.

entries.farsi: واى از اين پيران طفل نااديب / ‏گشته از قوت بالى هر رقيب
entries.english: Alas for these childish undisciplined elders who in their strength have become an affliction to every guardian!

entries.farsi: چون سالح و جهل جمع آيد بهم / ‏گشت فرعونى جهان سوز از ستم
entries.english: When weapons and ignorance are brought together, he becomes in his tyranny a world-consuming Pharaoh.

entries.farsi: شكر كن اى مرد درويش از قصور / كه ز فرعونى رهيدى و ز كفور
entries.english: O poor man, thank God for your deficiency, for you are delivered from being a Pharaoh and ungrateful.

entries.farsi: شكر كه مظلومى و ظالم نه‏اى / ‏ايمن از فرعونى و هر فتنه‏اى
entries.english: Thank God that you are the oppressed, not the oppressor: you are secure from acting like Pharaoh and from every temptation.

entries.farsi: اشكم تى الف اللهى نزد / كآتشش را نيست از هيزم مدد
entries.english: An empty belly never bragged of Divinity, for it has no faggots to feed its fire.

entries.farsi: اشكم خالى بود زندان ديو / كش غم نان مانع است از مكر و ريو
entries.english: An empty belly is the Devil’s prison, because anxiety for bread prevents him from plotting and deceiving.

entries.farsi: اشكم پر لوت دان بازار ديو / تاجران ديو را در وى غريو
entries.english: Know that a belly full of viands is the Devil’s market, where the Devil’s merchants raise a clamour:

entries.farsi: تاجران ساحر الشى فروش / ‏عقلها را تيره كرده از خروش
entries.english: Merchants who practice sorcery and sell worthless goods and obfuscate wits by vociferation.

entries.farsi: خم روان كرده ز سحرى چون فرس / ‏كرده كرباسى ز مهتاب و غلس
entries.english: By sorcery they cause a vat to run like a horse and make a piece of linen out of moonshine and twilight.

entries.farsi: چون بريشم خاك را بر مى‏تنند / خاك در چشم مميز مى‏زنند
entries.english: They weave earth like silk and throw earth in the eyes of the discerning.

entries.farsi: چندلى را رنگ عودى مى‏دهند / بر كلوخيمان حسودى مى‏دهند
entries.english: They give to a bit of sandal-wood the appearance of a piece of wood; they put in us the envious desire for a clod.

entries.farsi: پاك آن كه خاك را رنگى دهد / همچو كودكمان بر آن جنگى دهد
entries.english: Holy is He who gives earth a colour and causes us to quarrel over it like children.

entries.farsi: دامنى پر خاك ما چون طفلكان / ‏در نظرمان خاك همچون زر كان
entries.english: A skirt full of earth, and we are like little children: in our sight the earth is as gold of the mine.

entries.farsi: طفل را با بالغان نبود مجال / ‏طفل را حق كى نشاند با رجال
entries.english: There is no room for a child beside men: how should God let a child sit with men?

entries.farsi: ميوه گر كهنه شود تا هست خام / ‏پخته نبود غوره گويندش بنام
entries.english: If fruit become old, so long as it is immature and not ripe it is called ghúra (unripe grapes).

entries.farsi: گر شود صد ساله آن خام ترش / ‏طفل و غوره‏ست او بر هر تيز هش
entries.english: Though immature and sour reach the age of a hundred years, he is a child and unripe in the opinion of every sagacious person.

entries.farsi: گر چه باشد مو و ريش او سپيد / هم در آن طفلى خوف است و اميد
entries.english: Though his hair and beard be white, he is still in the childish state of fear and hope,

entries.farsi: كه رسم يا نارسيده مانده‏ام / ‏اى عجب با من كند كرم آن كرم
entries.english: Saying, “Shall I attain, or am I left immature? Oh, I wonder, will the Vine bestow that bounty on me?

entries.farsi: با چنين ناقابلى و دوريى / ‏بخشد اين غوره مرا انگوريى
entries.english: Notwithstanding such an incapacity and remoteness, will He confer on these unripe grapes of mine perfection like that of the ripe grape (angúr)?

entries.farsi: نيستم اوميدوار از هيچ سو / و آن كرم مى‏گويدم ال تيأسوا
entries.english: I have no hopes from any quarter, but that Bounty is saying to me, ‘Do not despair!’”

entries.farsi: دايما خاقان ما كردست طو / گوشمان را مى‏كشد ال تقنطوا
entries.english: Our Khaqan has made a perpetual feast: He is always pulling our ears, “Do not lose hope! ”

entries.farsi: گر چه ما زين نااميدى در گويم / ‏چون صال زد دست اندازان رويم
entries.english: Although we are in the ditch by this despair, let us go dancing along since He has invited us.

entries.farsi: دست اندازيم چون اسبان سيس / ‏در دويدن سوى مرعاى انيس
entries.english: Let us dance like mettlesome horses galloping towards the familiar pasturage.

entries.farsi: گام اندازيم و آن جا گام نى / ‏جام پردازيم و آن جا جام نى
entries.english: Let us toss our feet, though no foot is there; let us drain the cup, though no cup is there,

entries.farsi: ز انكه آن جا جمله اشيا جانى است / ‏معنى اندر معنى اندر معنى است
entries.english: Because all things there are spiritual: It is reality on reality on reality.

entries.farsi: هست صورت سايه معنى آفتاب / ‏نور بى‏سايه بود اندر خراب
entries.english: Form is the shadow, reality is the sun: the shadowless light is in the ruin.

entries.farsi: خشت اگر زرين بود بر كندنى است / ‏چون بهاى خشت وحى و روشنى است
entries.english: When not a brick is left on a brick there, no ugly shadow remains in the moonlight.

entries.farsi: چون كه آن جا خشت بر خشتى نماند / نور مه را سايه‏ى زشتى نماند
entries.english: If the brick be of gold it must be torn away, since the brick is the price paid for inspiration and light.

entries.farsi: كوه بهر دفع سايه مندك است / ‏پاره گشتن بهر اين نور اندك است
entries.english: In order to remove the shadow the mountain is razed to the ground: It is a small matter to fall to pieces for the sake of this light.

entries.farsi: بر برون كه چو زد نور صمد / پاره شد تا در درونش هم زند
entries.english: When the light of the Lord struck on the surface of the mountain, it fell to pieces in order that it should penetrate its interior too.

entries.farsi: گرسنه چون بر كفش زد قرص نان / ‏واشكافد از هوس چشم و دهان
entries.english: As soon as a loaf of bread touches the palm of a hungry man, his eyes and mouth open wide in desire it.

entries.farsi: تا كه نور چرخ گردد سايه سوز / شب ز سايه‏ى تست اى ياغى روز
entries.english: This is worth falling into a hundred thousand pieces: soar up through the heaven, O earth,

entries.farsi: صد هزاران پاره گشتن ارزد اين / ‏از ميان چرخ برخيز اى زمين
entries.english: That the light of heaven may consume your shadow: the night is caused by your shadow, O enemy of Day.

entries.farsi: اين زمين چون گاهواره‏ى طفلكان / بالغان را تنگ مى‏دارد مكان
entries.english: This earth is like a cradle for babes: it cramps the movements of grownup men.

entries.farsi: بهر طفالن حق زمين را مهد خواند / در گواره شير بر طفالن فشاند
entries.english: On account of the babes God has called the earth a cradle, and He has bestowed milk on the babes in their cradle.

entries.farsi: خانه تنگ آمد از اين گهواره‏ها / طفلكان را زود بالغ كن شها
entries.english: The house is crowded with these cradles: let the babes grow up quickly, O King!

entries.farsi: اى گواره خانه را ضيق مدار / تا تواند كرد بالغ انتشار
entries.english: O cradle, do not incommode the house, so that the grown-up man can move freely.

sections.title_fa: وسوسه‏اى كه پادشاه زاده را پيدا شد از سبب استغنايى و كشفى كه از شاه دل او را حاصل شده بود و قصد ناشكرى و سركشى مى‏كرد، شاه را از راه الهام و سر خبر شد، دلش درد كرد، روح او را ‏زخمى زد چنان كه صورت شاه را خبر نبود الى آخره

sections.title_en: The vicious distempered thoughts that arose in the prince in consequence of the self-sufficiency and illumination with which his heart had been endowed by the King: how he proceeded to show ingratitude and rebelliousness, and how the King, being made aware of it in an inspired and mysterious manner, was pained at heart and, though outwardly unconscious,
dealt his spirit a wound, etc.

entries.farsi: چون مسلم گشت بى‏بيع و شرى / ‏از درون شاه در جانش جرى
entries.english: When from the inward nature of the King the allowance was paid over, without sale or purchase, into his soul,

entries.farsi: قوت مى‏خوردى ز نور جان شاه / ‏ماه جانش همچو از خورشيد ماه
entries.english: His moon-like soul was feeding on the light of the King’s soul as the moon on the sun,

entries.farsi: راتبه‏ى جانى ز شاه بى‏نديد / دم به دم در جان مستش مى‏رسيد
entries.english: And the spiritual ration from the peerless King was arriving in his intoxicated soul at every moment.

entries.farsi: آن نه كه ترسا و مشرك مى‏خورند / ز آن غذايى كه ماليك مى‏خورند
entries.english: It was not that which polytheists and Christians eat part of the food which the angels eat.

entries.farsi: اندرون خويش استغنا بديد / گشت طغيانى ز استغنا پديد
entries.english: He felt self-sufficient within himself, and from self-sufficiency emerged a feeling of insolent pride.

entries.farsi: كه نه من هم شاه و هم شه زاده‏ام / ‏چون عنان خود بدين شه داده‏ام
entries.english: “Am not I,” said he, “both a king and a king’s son? How have I let this King take control of me?

entries.farsi: چون مرا ماهى بر آمد با لمع / ‏من چرا باشم غبارى را تبع
entries.english: Now that a resplendent moon has risen for me, why should I be following a cloud of dust?

entries.farsi: آب در جوى من است و وقت ناز / ناز غير از چه كشم من بى‏نياز
entries.english: The water is in my river-bed, and it is time to show disdain: why should I who want nothing endure disdain from another?

entries.farsi: سر چرا بندم چو درد سر نماند / وقت روى زرد و چشم تر نماند
entries.english: Why should I bandage my head when my headache is gone? The time for pale face and tearful eye is past.

entries.farsi: چون شكر لب گشته‏ام عارض قمر / زين منى چون نفس زاييدن گرفت
entries.english: Since my lips have become as sugar and my cheeks as the moon, I must open another shop.”

entries.farsi: ‏صد هزاران ژاژ خاييدن گرفت / صد بيابان ز آن سوى حرص و حسد
entries.english: When his carnal soul began to spawn from this egoism, he began to chew a hundred thousand thistles.

entries.farsi: تا بدان جا چشم بد هم مى‏رسد / بحر شه كه مرجع هر آب اوست
entries.english: Even the evil eye can traverse a hundred deserts to reach the object of its greed and envy:

entries.farsi: ‏چون نداند آن چه اندر سيل و جوست / شاه را دل درد كرد از فكر او
entries.english: How should the sea of the King, to which every water returns, be ignorant of what is in torrent and river?

entries.farsi: ناسپاسى عطاى بكر او / گفت آخر اى خس واهى ادب
entries.english: The King’s heart was pained by his thoughts and the ingratitude for his virgin munificence.

entries.farsi: ‏اين سزاى داد من بود اى عجب / من چه كردم با تو زين گنج نفيس
entries.english: He said, “Please, O base ill-mannered fellow was this bounty deserved? Marvellous!

entries.farsi: ‏تو چه كردى با من از خوى خسيس / من ترا ماهى نهادم در كنار
entries.english: How I have dealt with you in this precious treasure! How you have dealt with me in your mean-spiritedness!

entries.farsi: كه غروبش نيست تا روز شمار / در جزاى آن عطاى نور پاك
entries.english: I have put in your bosom a moon that will never set till the Day of Reckoning,

entries.farsi: ‏تو زدى در ديده‏ى من خار و خاك / من ترا بر چرخ گشته نردبان
entries.english: And in requital for that gift of pure light you have thrown thorns and earth in my eye.

entries.farsi: ‏تو شده در حرب من تير و كمان / درد غيرت آمد اندر شه پديد
entries.english: I have become for you a ladder to Heaven, and you have become a bow and arrow in combat with me.”

entries.farsi: عكس درد شاه اندر وى رسيد / مرغ دولت در عتابش بر طپيد
entries.english: Pangs of jealousy arose in the King: the reflection of the King’s pangs entered into him.

entries.farsi: پرده‏ى آن گوشه گشته بر دريد / چون درون خود بديد آن خوش پسر
entries.english: The bird of his felicity fluttered violently in reproaching him and tore the veil of him who had sought seclusion.

entries.farsi: از سيه كارى خود گرد و اثر / آن وظيفه‏ى لطف و نعمت كم شده
entries.english: When the comely youth felt within himself the dust and effects of his wicked behaviour,

entries.farsi: ‏خانه‏ى شادى او پر غم شده / با خود آمد او ز مستى عقار
entries.english: The allowance of favour and bounty had failed and that the house of his joy was filled with sorrow,

entries.farsi: ز آن گنه گشته سرش خانه‏ى خمار / خورده گندم حله زو بيرون شده
entries.english: He came to himself from the intoxication caused by the wine; in consequence of that sin his head became the abode of crop sickness.

entries.farsi: ‏خلد بر وى باديه و هامون شده / ديد كان شربت و را بيمار كرد
entries.english: He had eaten the wheat, his celestial robe had been stripped off him, and Paradise had become for him a desert and sandy plain.

entries.farsi: زهر آن ما و منيها كار كرد / جان چون طاوس در گلزار ناز
entries.english: He perceived that that draught had made him ill and that the poison of those egoistic pretensions had done its work.

entries.farsi: همچو جغدى شد به ويرانه‏ى مجاز / همچو آدم دور ماند او از بهشت
entries.english: His soul that was like a peacock in the garden of delight became like an owl in the wilderness of unreality.

entries.farsi: ‏در زمين مى‏راند گاوى بهر كشت / اشك مى‏راند او كه اى هندوى زاو
entries.english: Like Adam, he was left far away from Paradise, driving an ox on the earth for the purpose of sowing.

entries.farsi: شير را كردى اسير دم گاو / كردى اى نفس بد بارد نفس
entries.english: He was shedding tears and crying, “O Hindu mighty, you have made the lion a captive of the cow’s tail.

entries.farsi: ‏بى‏حفاظى با شه فريادرس / دام بگزيدى ز حرص گندمى
entries.english: O wicked fleshly soul with your chill breath, you have acted disloyally to the King who answers every call for help.

entries.farsi: ‏بر تو شد هر گندم او كژدمى / در سرت آمد هواى ما و من
entries.english: In your greed for a grain of wheat you have chosen the trap, and every grain of its wheat has become a scorpion to you.

entries.farsi: ‏قيد بين بر پاى خود پنجاه من / نوحه مى‏كرد اين نمط بر جان خويش
entries.english: The vain fancy of egoism came into your head: behold a shackle weighing fifty mann on your foot!”

entries.farsi: ‏كه چرا گشتم ضد سلطان خويش / آمد او با خويش و استغفار كرد
entries.english: In this fashion was he mourning for his soul, saying, “Why did I become the antagonist of my sovereign?”

entries.farsi: با انابت چيز ديگر يار كرد / درد كان از وحشت ايمان بود
entries.english: He came to himself and asked pardon of God, and with his repentance he combined something else.

entries.farsi: رحم كن كان درد بى‏درمان بود / مر بشر را خود مبا جامه‏ى درست
entries.english: The pain that arises from dread of losing one’s faith— take pity, for that is the irremediable pain.

entries.farsi: ‏چون رهيد از صبر در حين صدر جست / مر بشر را پنجه و ناخن مباد
entries.english: May no human being have perfect raiment! As soon as he is delivered from enduring he at once seeks the seat of honour.

entries.farsi: كه نه دين انديشد آن گه نه سداد / آدمى اندر بال كشته به است
entries.english: May no human being possess a fist and nails! Then he never thinks of devotion and righteousness.

entries.farsi: نفس كافر نعمت است و گمره است
entries.english: It is best for a man to be killed in tribulation: the carnal soul is an ingrate and one that has gone astray.

sections.title_fa: خطاب حق به عزرائيل كه ترا رحم بر كه بيشتر آمد از اين خاليق كه جانشان قبض كردى، و جواب دادن عزرائيل حضرت را
sections.title_en: How God addressed Azrael, saying, “Of all these creatures whose souls you have seized,
whom did you pity most?” and the answer given by Azrael to the Lord.

entries.farsi: حق به عزرائيل مى‏گفت اى نقيب / ‏بر كه رحم آمد ترا از هر كئيب
entries.english: God was saying to Azrael, “O marshal, whom of all the miserable ones did you pity?”

entries.farsi: گفت بر جمله دلم سوزد به درد / ليك ترسم امر را اهمال كرد
entries.english: He replied, “My heart burns with grief for them all, but I am afraid to neglect the command,

entries.farsi: تا بگويم كاشكى يزدان مرا / ‏در عوض قربان كند بهر فتى
entries.english: So that I should say, ‘would that God might sacrifice me in exchange for the youth!’”

entries.farsi: گفت بر كى بيشتر رحم آمدت / ‏از كه دل پر ز سوز و بريان‏تر شدت
entries.english: God asked, “For whom did you feel the greatest pity? On account of whom was your heart most filled with flame and grilled?”

entries.farsi: گفت روزى كشتيى بر موج تيز / من شكستم ز امر تا شد ريز ريز
entries.english: “One day,” said he, “by Your command I wrecked a ship on the fierce waves, so that it went to pieces.

entries.farsi: بس بگفتى قبض كن جان همه / ‏جز زنى و غير طفلى ز آن رمه
entries.english: Then You bade me take the souls of them all, except one woman and one child belonging to that company.
