entries.farsi: باز گفتى جان مادر قبض كن / ‏طفل را بگذار تنها ز امر كن
entries.english: The two were left on a plank, and the plank was being driven on by the waves.

entries.farsi: هر دو بر يك تخته‏اى درماندند / تخته را آن موجها مى‏راندند
entries.english: Then You said, ‘Take the mother’s soul and leave the child alone in obedience to the command Be!’

entries.farsi: چون ز مادر بگسليدم طفل را / خود تو مى‏دانى چه تلخ آمد مرا
entries.english: When I parted the child from its mother, You yourself know how bitter It was to me.

entries.farsi: بس بديدم دود ماتمهاى زفت / ‏تلخى آن طفل از فكرم نرفت
entries.english: Often have I seen sighs in great mourning, the bitter grief of that child has never gone from my recollection.”

entries.farsi: گفت حق آن طفل را از فضل خويش / ‏موج را گفتم فگن در بيشه ايش
entries.english: God said, “Of My grace I bade the waves cast that child into a forest—

entries.farsi: بيشه‏ى پر سوسن و ريحان و گل / ‏پر درخت ميوه دار خوش اكل
entries.english: A forest abounding in lilies and sweet basils and roses, full of trees laden with fruit good to eat,

entries.farsi: چشمه‏هاى آب شيرين زالل / ‏پروريدم طفل را با صد دالل
entries.english: And fountains of sweet limpid water. I fostered the child with a hundred endearments.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران مرغ مطرب خوش صدا / اندر آن روضه فگنده صد نوا
entries.english: Myriads of melodious singing-birds poured forth a hundred songs in that garden.

entries.farsi: بسترش كردم ز برگ نسترن / ‏كرده او را ايمن از صدمه‏ى فتن
entries.english: I made for him a couch of wild-rose leaves; I made him secure from the shock of afflictions.

entries.farsi: ابر را گفته بر او باران مريز / برق را گفته بر او مگراى تيز
entries.english: I told the sun not to scorch him; I told the wind to blow on him gently;

entries.farsi: زين چمن اى دى مبر آن اعتدال / ‏پنجه‏اى بهمن بر اين روضه ممال
entries.english: I told the clouds not to rain upon him; I told the lightning not to dart at him.

entries.farsi: گفته من خورشيد را كاو را مگز / باد را گفته بر او آهسته وز
entries.english: I said, ‘O December, do not cut off the mild weather from this orchard; O November, do not let your fist fall on this garden.’”

sections.title_fa: ‏كرامات شيخ شيبان راعى و بيان معجزه‏ى هود عليه السلام
sections.title_en: The miracles of Shayban Ra’i, May God sanctify his venerable spirit!

entries.farsi: همچو شيبان راعى از گرگ عنيد / وقت جمعه بر رعا خط مى‏كشيد
entries.english: Just as Shayban Ra’i, because of the stubborn wolf, used to draw a line round his flock at the hour of the Friday prayers,

entries.farsi: تا برون نايد از آن خط گوسفند / نه در آيد گرگ و دزد با گزند
entries.english: In order that no sheep should go beyond that line, and that no wolf or mischievous robber should come inside.

entries.farsi: بر مثال دايره‏ى تعويذ هود / كاندر آن صرصر امان آل بود
entries.english: It was on the model of Hud’s circle of refuge, in which his followers were safe from the sarsar wind.

entries.farsi: هشت روزى اندر اين خط تن زنيد / و ز برون مثله تماشا مى‏كنيد
entries.english: “Stay quietly within this line for eight days and view the terrible mutilation outside.”

entries.farsi: بر هوا بردى فگندى بر حجر / تا دريدى لحم و عظم از همدگر
entries.english: It lifted into the air and flung them on the stones, so that flesh and bone were torn asunder.

entries.farsi: يك گره را بر هوا در هم زدى / ‏تا چو خشخاش استخوان ريزان شدى
entries.english: One party it hurled against each other in the air, so that their bones crumbled like poppy-seed.

entries.farsi: آن سياست را كه لرزيد آسمان / ‏مثنوى اندر نگنجد شرح آن
entries.english: There is no room in the Masnaví to describe fully that chastisement whereat Heaven trembled.

entries.farsi: گر به طبع اين مى‏كنى اى باد سرد / گرد خط و دايره‏ى آن هود گرد
entries.english: If, O icy wind, you are doing this by nature, try to invade the line and circle drawn by Hud!

entries.farsi: اى طبيعى فوق طبع اين ملك بين / ‏يا بيا و محو كن از مصحف اين
entries.english: O natural philosopher, perceive that this kingdom is above Nature, or else come and wipe out this from the Holy Book!

entries.farsi: مقريان را منع كن بندى بنه / ‏يا معلم را بمال و سهم ده
entries.english: Prohibit those who recite the Qur’an and impose a ban, or punish the teacher and put terror into him!

entries.farsi: عاجزى و خيره كاين عجز از كجاست / ‏عجز تو تابى از آن روز جزاست
entries.english: You are helpless and unable to understand the cause of this helplessness: your helplessness is a reflection of the Day of Retribution.

entries.farsi: عجزها دارى تو در پيش اى لجوج / ‏وقت شد پنهانيان را نك خروج
entries.english: O perverse man, you have many a helpless plight before you: the hour comes, lo, the hidden ones will emerge!

entries.farsi: خرم آن كاين عجز و حيرت قوت اوست / ‏در دو عالم خفته اندر ظل دوست
entries.english: Happy is he whose food is this helplessness and bewilderment and who in both worlds are sleeping in the shadow of the Beloved.

entries.farsi: هم در آخور هم در آخر عجز ديد / مرده شد دين عجايز را گزيد
entries.english: He is conscious of being helpless both in the stable and in the last state: he is dead; he has adopted “the old women’s religion.”

entries.farsi: چون زليخا يوسفش بر وى بتافت / ‏از عجوزى در جوانى راه يافت
entries.english: Like Zalikha, when Joseph beamed upon her, found the way from decrepitude to youth.

entries.farsi: زندگى در مردن و در محنت است / ‏آب حيوان در درون ظلمت است
entries.english: Life depends on dying and on suffering tribulation: the Water of Life is in the Darkness.

sections.title_fa: ‏رجوع كردن به قصه‏ى پروردن حق تعالى نمرود را بى‏واسطه‏ى مادر و دايه در طفلى
sections.title_en: Resuming the Story of the most High God’s bringing up Nimrod in his childhood without the intervention of mother and nurse.

entries.farsi: حاصل آن روضه چو باغ عارفان / ‏از سموم و صرصر آمد در امان
entries.english: “In short, that garden, like the orchard of gnostics, was secure from the simoom and the sarsar wind.

entries.farsi: يك پلنگى طفلكان نوزاده بود / گفتم او را شير ده طاعت نمود
entries.english: A leopardess had newly given birth to cubs: I bade her give milk to him, and she obeyed.

entries.farsi: پس بدادش شير و خدمتهاش كرد / تا كه بالغ گشت و زفت و شير مرد
entries.english: So she gave him milk and tended him till he grew up and became strong and valiant.

entries.farsi: چون فطامش شد بگفتم با پرى / ‏تا در آموزيد نطق و داورى
entries.english: When he was weaned, I told the peris to teach him how to discourse and deal justice.

entries.farsi: پرورش دادم مر او را ز آن چمن / ‏كى به گفت اندر بگنجد فن من
entries.english: I gave him nourishment from that garden: how should My artfulness be contained in words.

entries.farsi: داده من ايوب را مهر پدر / بهر مهمانى كرمان بى‏ضرر
entries.english: I bestowed on Job a father’s love in order that he might entertain the worms hospitably and do them no harm.

entries.farsi: داده كرمان را بر او مهر ولد / بر پدر من اينت قدرت اينت يد
entries.english: I bestowed on the worms love for him like that of children for their father. Look, here is Power, here is Hand!

entries.farsi: مادران را داب من آموختم / ‏چون بود لطفى كه من افروختم
entries.english: I have taught mothers to care: how must be the kindness that I have kindled!

entries.farsi: صد عنايت كردم و صد رابطه / ‏تا ببيند لطف من بى‏واسطه
entries.english: I showed a hundred favours and a hundred ties, that he might experience My kindness directly,

entries.farsi: تا نباشد از سبب در كش مكش / ‏تا بود هر استعانت از منش
entries.english: And not be distracted by any secondary cause, to the end that every call for help should be made by him to Me,

entries.farsi: ور نه تا خود هيچ عذرى نبودش / ‏شكوتى نبود ز هر يار بدش
entries.english: Or at least that he should have no excuse and no occasion to complain of any evil companion.

entries.farsi: اين حضانه ديد با صد رابطه / ‏كه بپروردم و را بى‏واسطه
entries.english: He enjoyed this tender care by a hundred ties, for I fostered him without an intermediary.

entries.farsi: شكر او آن بود اى بنده‏ى جليل / ‏كه شد او نمرود و سوزنده‏ى خليل
entries.english: His thanks, O honoured servant, were this that he became Nimrod and the burner of Khalíl”—

entries.farsi: كه چرا من تابع غيرى شوم / ‏چون كه صاحب ملك و اقبال نوم
entries.english: Just as this prince, in return for the favours of the King, showed arrogance and sought to aggrandise himself,

entries.farsi: همچنان كاين شاه زاده شكر شاه / ‏كرد استكبار و استكثار جاه
entries.english: Saying, “Why should I become the follower of another when I possess empire and new fortune?”

entries.farsi: لطفهاى شه كه ذكر آن گذشت / ‏از تجبر بر دلش پوشيده گشت
entries.english: The King’s favours, of which the tale has been told above, were veiled from his heart by his outrageous insolence—

entries.farsi: همچنان نمرود آن الطاف را / ‏زير پا بنهاد از جهل و عمى
entries.english: “Even so did Nimrod ignorantly and blindly trample underfoot those favours.

entries.farsi: اين زمان كافر شد و ره مى‏زند / كبر و دعوى خدايى مى‏كند
entries.english: Now he has become an infidel and is waylaying: he is acting with arrogance and pretending to Divinity.

entries.farsi: رفته سوى آسمان با جلال / ‏با سه كركس تا كند با من قتال
entries.english: By means of three vultures he has gone towards august Heaven in order to battle with Me,

entries.farsi: صد هزاران طفل بى‏تلويم را / كشته تا يابد وى ابراهيم را
entries.english: And has killed a hundred thousand innocent children that he may find Abraham;

entries.farsi: كه منجم گفت كاندر حكم سال / ‏زاد خواهد دشمنى بهر قتال
entries.english: For the astrologers declared that, according to the forecast for the year, there would be born an adversary to combat him,

entries.farsi: هين بكن در دفع آن خصم احتياط / هر كه مى‏زاييد مى‏كشت از خباط
entries.english: ‘Listen, take precautions to repel that enemy’; in his craziness he would eagerly kill every child that was born.

entries.farsi: كورى او رست طفل وحى كش / ‏ماند خونهاى دگر در گردنش
entries.english: To confound him, the inspired child was saved; the blood of the others remained upon his neck.

entries.farsi: از پدر يابيد آن ملك اى عجب / ‏تا غرورش داد ظلمات نسب
entries.english: Oh, it is wonderful! Did he obtain that empire from his father so that he was befooled by the darkness of noble lineage?

entries.farsi: ديگران را گر ام و اب شد حجاب / ‏او ز ما يابيد گوهرها بجيب
entries.english: If father and mother were an obstacle to others, he derived the jewels in his pocket from Me.”

entries.farsi: گرگ درنده‏ست نفس بد يقين / ‏چه بهانه مى‏نهى بر هر قرين
entries.english: Assuredly your wicked carnal soul is a rapacious wolf: why are you laying the blame on every comrade?

entries.farsi: در ضاللت هست صد كل را كله / ‏نفس زشت كفرناك پر سفه
entries.english: In its misguidedness the foul disbelieving unconscionable carnal soul is a cap for a hundred baldpates.

entries.farsi: زين سبب مى‏گويم اى بنده‏ى فقير / سلسله از گردن سگ بر مگير
entries.english: For this reason, O poor slave, I am always saying, “Do not remove the collar from the neck of the cur.”

entries.farsi: گر معلم گشت اين سگ هم سگ است / ‏باش ذلت نفسه كاو بد رگ است
entries.english: If this cur has become a teacher, it is a cur still: be you one whose carnal soul is abased, for it is evil-natured.

entries.farsi: فرض مى‏آرى بجا گر طايفى / ‏بر سهيلى چون اديم طايفى
entries.english: You will perform your bounden duty if you go round about Suhayl as Tá’if hide,

entries.farsi: تا سهيلت واخرد از شر پوست / ‏تا شوى چون موزه‏اى هم پاى دوست
entries.english: In order that Suhayl may redeem you from the vices of the skin, and that you may fit the foot of the Beloved like a boot.

entries.farsi: جمله قرآن شرح خبث نفسهاست / ‏بنگر اندر مصحف آن چشمت كجاست
entries.english: The entire Qur’an is a description of the viciousness of carnal souls: look into the Holy Book! Where is your eye?

entries.farsi: ذكر نفس عاديان كآلت بيافت / ‏در قتال انبيا مو مى‏شكافت
entries.english: An account of the carnal soul of people like Ad, which found weapons, took the utmost pains to combat the prophets.

entries.farsi: قرن قرن از شوم نفس بى‏ادب / ‏ناگهان اندر جهان مى‏زد لهب
entries.english: From generation to generation, the wickedness of the undisciplined carnal soul was the cause of the world being suddenly set on fire.

sections.title_fa: ‏رجوع كردن بدان قصه كه شاه زاده زخم خورد از خاطر شاه، پيش از استكمال فضايل ديگر از دنيا برفت
sections.title_en: Returning to the Story of the prince who was smitten by a blow from the heart of the King and departed from this world before he was fully endowed with the other excellences.

entries.farsi: قصه كوته كن كه رشك آن غيور / برد او را بعد سالى سوى گور
entries.english: Abridge the tale: after a year the indignation of that jealous one brought him to the grave.

entries.farsi: شاه چون از محو شد سوى وجود / چون به تركش بنگريد آن بى‏نظير
entries.english: When the King emerged from the state of self-effacement into consciousness, his martial eye had wrought that bloodshed.

entries.farsi: ديد كم از تركشش يك چوبه تير / گفت كو آن تير و از حق باز جست
entries.english: When the peerless looked at his quiver he perceived that one arrow was missing from his quiver.

entries.farsi: ‏گفت كاندر حلق او كز تير تست / عفو كرد آن شاه دريادل ولى
entries.english: He said “Where is that arrow?” and requested God. He replied, “In his throat, for it is by your arrow.”

entries.farsi: ‏آمده بد تير او بر مقتلى / كشته شد در نوحه‏ى او مى‏گريست
entries.english: The King, whose heart was like an ocean, pardoned him; but, alas, the arrow had struck a vital spot.

entries.farsi: اوست جمله هم كشنده و هم ولى است / ور نباشد هر دو او پس كل نيست
entries.english: He was slain, and the King wept in mourning for him, he is all: he is both the slayer and the next of kin;

entries.farsi: كان بزد بر جسم و بر معنى نزد / جسم ظاهر عاقبت هم رفتنى است
entries.english: For if he is not both, then he is not all; he is both the slayer of people and a mourner.

entries.farsi: ‏هم كشنده‏ى خلق و هم ماتم كنى است / شكر مى‏كرد آن شهيد زرد خد
entries.english: The pale-cheeked martyr was thanking that it had smitten his body and had not smitten that which is real.

entries.farsi: ‏تا ابد معنى بخواهد شاد زيست / آن عتاب ار رفت هم بر پوست رفت
entries.english: The visible body is doomed to go at last that which is real shall live rejoicing forever.

entries.farsi: ‏دوست بى‏آزار سوى دوست رفت / گر چه او فتراك شاهنشه گرفت
entries.english: If that punishment was inflicted, yet it fell only on the skin: the lover went unscathed to the Beloved.

entries.farsi: ‏آخر از عين الكمال او ره گرفت / و آن سوم كاهلترين هر سه بود
entries.english: Although he laid hold of the Emperor’s saddle-strap, in the end he was admitted by the eye whose glances kill.

entries.farsi: صورت و معنى بكلى او ربود
entries.english: And the third was the laziest of the three: he won completely—the form as well as the reality.

sections.title_fa: ‏وصيت كردن آن شخص كه بعد از من او برد مال مرا از سه فرزند من كه كاهل تر است
sections.title_en: The injunctions given by a certain person that after he died his property should be inherited by whichever of his three sons was the laziest.

entries.farsi: آن يكى شخصى بوقت مرگ خويش / ‏گفته بود اندر وصيت پيش پيش
entries.english: Long ago a certain person, in giving injunctions on his death-bed, had spoken—

entries.farsi: سه پسر بودش چو سه سرو روان / ‏وقف ايشان كرده او جان و روان
entries.english: He had three sons like three moving cypresses: to them he had devoted his soul and his spirit.

entries.farsi: گفت هر چه در كفم كاله و زر است / ‏او برد زين هر سه كاو كاهلتر است
entries.english: He said, “Whichever of these three is the laziest, let him take all the goods and gold in my possession.”

entries.farsi: گفت با قاضى و بس اندرز كرد / بعد از آن جام شراب مرگ خورد
entries.english: He told the cadi and enjoined him strictly: after that, he drained the wine cup of death.

entries.farsi: گفته فرزندان به قاضى كاى كريم / ‏نگذريم از حكم او ما سه يتيم
entries.english: The sons said to the cadi, “O noble sir, we three orphans will not depart from his decision.

entries.farsi: سمع و طاعه مى‏كنيم او راست دست / ‏آن چه او فرمود بر ما نافذ است
entries.english: We accept and obey: control belongs to him: what he has commanded must be executed by us.

entries.farsi: ما چو اسماعيل ز ابراهيم خود / سر نپيچيم ار چه قربان مى‏كند
entries.english: We are like Ishmael: we will not recoil from our Abraham though he is offering us in sacrifice.”

entries.farsi: گفت قاضى هر يكى با عاقليش / ‏تا بگويد قصه‏اى از كاهليش
entries.english: The cadi said, “Let each one, using his intelligence, and give some account of his laziness,

entries.farsi: تا ببينم كاهلى هر يكى / ‏تا بدانم حال هر يك بى‏شكى
entries.english: That I may perceive the laziness of each and know beyond any doubt the case of every one.”

entries.farsi: عارفان از دو جهان كاهلترند / ز آنك بى‏شد يار خرمن مى‏برند
entries.english: The gnostics are the laziest folk in the two worlds, because they get their harvest without ploughing.

entries.farsi: كاهلى را كرده‏اند ايشان سند / كار ايشان را چو يزدان مى‏كند
entries.english: They have made laziness their prop since God is working for them.

entries.farsi: كار يزدان را نمى‏بينند عام / ‏مى‏نياسايند از كد صبح و شام
entries.english: The vulgar do not see God’s working and never rest from toil at morn or eve.

entries.farsi: هين ز حد كاهلى گوييد باز / تا بدانم حد آن از كشف راز
entries.english: “Come,”, “define laziness, so that from the disclosure of the secret I may learn its definition.”

entries.farsi: بى‏گمان كه هر زبان پرده‏ى دل است / ‏چون بجنبد پرده سرها واصل است
entries.english: It is unquestionable that every tongue is a curtain over the heart: when the curtain is moved, the mysteries reach us.

entries.farsi: پرده‏ى كوچك چو يك شرحه‏ى كباب / ‏مى‏بپوشد صورت صد آفتاب
entries.english: A little curtain like a slice of roast-meat conceals the forms of a hundred suns.

entries.farsi: گر بيان نطق كاذب نيز هست / ‏ليك بوى از صدق و كذبش مخبر است
entries.english: Even if the oral explanation is false, yet the scent makes one acquainted with his veracity or falsehood.

entries.farsi: آن نسيمى كه بيايد از چمن / ‏هست پيدا از سموم گولخن
entries.english: The zephyr that comes from a garden is distinct from the simoom of the ash-heap.

entries.farsi: بوى صدق و بوى كذب گول گير / هست پيدا در نفس چون مشك و سير
entries.english: The scents of truth and fool-catching falsehood are apparent in the breath, like musk and garlic.

entries.farsi: بانگ هيزان و شجاعان دلير / هست پيدا چون فن روباه و شير
entries.english: If you cannot distinguish a friend from a double-hearted person, complain of your own rotten sense of smell.

entries.farsi: گر ندانى يار را از ده دله / ‏از مشام فاسد خود كن گله
entries.english: The voices of cowards and brave courageous men are as distinct as the characteristics of the fox and the lion.

entries.farsi: يا زبان همچو سر ديگ است راست / ‏چون بجنبد تو بدانى چه اباست
entries.english: Or, the tongue is just like the lid of a cooking-pot: when it is moved you know what sort of food is inside;

entries.farsi: از بخار آن بداند تيز هش / ‏ديگ شيرينى ز سكباج ترش
entries.english: One whose sense is keen can tell by the vapour whether it is a pot of sweetmeat or sour sikbáj (stew flavoured with vinegar).

entries.farsi: دست بر ديگ نوى چون زد فتى / وقت بخريدن بديد اشكسته را
entries.english: When a man taps a new pot with his hand at the time when he is buying it, he detects the cracked one.

entries.farsi: گفت دانم مرد را در حين ز پوز / ور نگويد دانمش اندر سه روز
entries.english: He said, “I know a man at once by his mouth; and if he do not speak, I know him within three days.”

entries.farsi: و آن دگر گفت ار بگويد دانمش / ‏ور نگويد در سخن پيچانمش
entries.english: The second said, “I know him if he speak, and if he do not speak, I engage him in conversation.”

entries.farsi: گفت اگر اين مكر بشنيده بود / لب ببندد در خموشى در رود
entries.english: He said, “If he has heard of this device, he will close his lips and take refuge in silence.”

sections.title_en: Parable.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه گفت مادر بچه را / گر خيالى آيدت در شب فرا
entries.english: The case is like that of the mother who said to her child, “If a ghost comes to you in the night,

entries.farsi: يا به گورستان و جاى سهمگين / ‏تو خيالى بينى اسود پر ز كين
entries.english: Or if in a graveyard and frightful place you behold a black boogeyman full of rage,

entries.farsi: دل قوى دار و بكن حمله بر او / او بگرداند ز تو در حال رو
entries.english: Keep a stout heart and rush at it, and immediately it will turn its face away from you.”

entries.farsi: گفت كودك آن خيال ديووش / ‏گر بدو اين گفته باشد مادرش
entries.english: “Said the child, “Suppose the devilish boogeyman’s mother has said this to it;

entries.farsi: حمله آرم افتد اندر گردنم / ‏ز امر مادر پس من آن گه چون كنم
entries.english: If I rush at it, by its mother’s orders it will fall on my neck: what shall I do then?

entries.farsi: تو همى‏آموزى‏ام كه چست ايست / ‏آن خيال زشت را هم مادرى است
entries.english: You are teaching me to stand firm, the ugly boogeyman has a mother too.”

entries.farsi: ديو و مردم را ملقن آن يكى است / ‏غالب از وى گردد ار خصم اندكى است
entries.english: The instructor of devils and of mankind is the One: through Him the enemy prevails if he is in small force.

entries.farsi: تا كدامين سوى باشد آن يواش / ‏اللهَّ اللهَّ رو تو هم ز آن سوى باش
entries.english: On whichever side that Gracious One may be, go and for God’s sake, for God’s sake, be you also on that side!

entries.farsi: گفت اگر از مكر نايد در كالم / ‏حيله را دانسته باشد آن همام
entries.english: He said, “Suppose the worthy man is not induced to speak by your device and has perceived the trick,

entries.farsi: سر او را چون شناسى راست گو / گفت من خامش نشينم پيش او
entries.english: Tell me truly, how can you know his hidden nature?” He replied, “I sit before him in silence

entries.farsi: صبر را سلم كنم سوى درج / ‏تا بر آيم صبر مفتاح الفرج
entries.english: And make patience a ladder to climb upwards: patience is the key to success.

entries.farsi: ور بجوشد در حضورش از دلم / ‏منطقى بيرون از اين شادى و غم
entries.english: And if in his presence there should gush from my heart a speech beyond this joy and sorrow,

entries.farsi: من بدانم كاو فرستاد آن به من / ‏از ضمير چون سهيل اندر يمن
entries.english: I know that he has sent it to me from the depths of a soul like Canopus in Yemen.

entries.farsi: در دل من آن سخن ز آن ميمنه است / ‏ز انكه از دل جانب دل روزنه‏ست
entries.english: The speech in my heart comes from that auspicious quarter, for there is a window between heart and heart.”

sections.title_fa: پایان دفتر ششم
